## 2. High-Level Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        MAIN THREAD                               │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐         │
│  │ MainSim    │───▶│ FarmRenderer│───▶│ GPU Instanced  │         │
│  │ (Singleton)│    │             │    │ Draw Calls     │         │
│  │            │    └─────────────┘    └────────────────┘         │
│  │            │                                                   │
│  │            │    ┌─────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐         │
│  │            │───▶│ Workspace   │───▶│ Code Windows   │         │
│  │            │    │ (IDE)       │    │ (N editors)    │         │
│  │            │    └─────────────┘    └────────────────┘         │
│  │            │                                                   │
│  │            │    ┌─────────────┐    ┌────────────────┐         │
│  │            │───▶│ ResearchMenu│    │ Inventory UI   │         │
│  │            │    └─────────────┘    └────────────────┘         │
│  └─────┬──────┘                                                   │
│        │ lock(lockSimulation)                                     │
│        ▼                                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐         │
│  │               BACKGROUND THREAD                      │         │
│  │                                                       │         │
│  │  ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────┐  │         │
│  │  │ Simulation │───▶│ Execution  │───▶│ Program-   │  │         │
│  │  │            │    │            │    │ State(s)   │  │         │
│  │  │            │    │ Apply-     │    │            │  │         │
│  │  │ timers     │    │ SideEffect │    │ ModuleState│  │         │
│  │  │ (Priority  │    │ (Harvest,  │    │ Scope      │  │         │
│  │  │  Queue)    │    │  Move,     │    │ callStack  │  │         │
│  │  │            │    │  Plant...) │    │            │  │         │
│  │  └─────┬──────┘    └─────┬──────┘    └────────────┘  │         │
│  │        │                 │                            │         │
│  │        ▼                 ▼                            │         │
│  │  ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────┐                    │         │
│  │  │ Farm       │    │ Drone(s)   │                    │         │
│  │  │ (items,    │◀──▶│ (pos, hat, │                    │         │
│  │  │  unlocks)  │    │  state)    │                    │         │
│  │  └─────┬──────┘    └────────────┘                    │         │
│  │        │                                              │         │
│  │        ▼                                              │         │
│  │  ┌────────────┐                                      │         │
│  │  │GridManager │                                      │         │
│  │  │(entities,  │                                      │         │
│  │  │ grounds,   │                                      │         │
│  │  │ water,     │                                      │         │
│  │  │ cactus#s)  │                                      │         │
│  │  └────────────┘                                      │         │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Data Flow: Code Execution Lifecycle

```
User writes Python code
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌────────┐
│ Tokenizer    │────▶│ Parser   │────▶│ AST    │
│ (regex lexer)│     │ (recur.  │     │ (Node  │
│              │     │  descent)│     │  tree) │
└──────────────┘     └──────────┘     └───┬────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                                    ┌────────────┐
                                    │ Execution  │
                                    │            │
                                    │ foreach    │
                                    │ node.Exec()│
                                    │ → yields   │
                                    │ SideEffect │
                                    └─────┬──────┘
                                          │
                                          ▼
                              ┌───────────────────────┐
                              │ ApplySideEffect()     │
                              │ switch(sideEffect):   │
                              │  Harvest → drone.Harv │
                              │  Move    → drone.Move │
                              │  Plant   → grid.Set   │
                              │  Swap    → grid.Swap  │
                              │  Water   → drone.Watr │
                              │  Print   → Logger.Log │
                              │  ...39 cases...       │
                              └───────────────────────┘
```

### Data Flow: Research/Progression

```
Harvest crops → Farm.CollectItem() → Items accumulate in ItemBlock
        │
        ▼
Player clicks UnlockBox in ResearchMenu
        │
        ▼
MainSim.UnlockOrUpgrade() → Farm.UnlockOrUpgrade()
        │                       │
        │                       ▼
        │              Items deducted from ItemBlock
        │              Farm.unlocks[name] incremented
        │              Farm.unlockedKeyWords grows
        │                       │
        │                       ▼
        │              New builtins available in Python
        │              (harvest, plant, move, etc.)
        ▼
ResearchMenu.UnlockBox.SetupRec() → UI refreshes tree state
```

---

## 3. Core Architectural Pattern: Singleton Managers

The original source uses a **lightweight singleton pattern** — not a generic base class, but individual static `_instance` fields per manager.

### Singleton Pattern (per-class, not generic)

```csharp
// MainSim.cs
private static MainSim _instance;
public static MainSim Inst => _instance;

void Awake()
{
    if (_instance == null) _instance = this;
    else Destroy(gameObject);
}
```

### Execution Order (Implied)

| Priority | Manager | Reason |
|----------|---------|--------|
| First | `ThemeManager` `[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]` | Color themes needed before any UI renders |
| Early | `SteamManager` | Steam API init, `DontDestroyOnLoad` |
| Early | `TimerManager` | Timer scheduling needed by buttons, animations |
| Normal | `MainSim` | Core game orchestrator — creates Simulation, Farm |
| Normal | `SoundManager` | Sound queueing |
| Normal | `VFXManager` | VFX pooling/queueing |
| Normal | `HatPopup` | Hat unlock popup display |
| Late | `UtilsGameObjects` | Lazy-finds WarningPopup on first access |

### Communication in Original Source

The original source is **remarkably clean** for a decompiled codebase:
- **ZERO `FindObjectOfType` in any MonoBehaviour** (only 1 `FindAnyObjectByType` in `UtilsGameObjects`)
- Communication is primarily through **direct references** (`[SerializeField]` or constructor injection for plain C# classes)
- **`OptionHolder.OnOptionChanged`** serves as the main cross-system event (static event)
- **`ThemeManager.OnThemeChanged`** for theme updates
- **`Logger.OnOutputChanged`** for output display
- Static classes (`ResourceManager`, `Saver`, `Achievements`, `OptionHolder`, `Localizer`, `CodeUtilities`) accessed directly

---

## 4. System-by-System Breakdown

### 4.1 Game Core System

**Files:** `MainSim.cs`, `Simulation.cs`, `Farm.cs`, `GridManager.cs`, `FarmObject.cs`, `FarmObjectSO.cs`, `FarmRenderer.cs`

**Responsibilities:** The beating heart of the game. `MainSim` (MonoBehaviour singleton) owns the `Simulation` which runs on a background thread. The `Simulation` owns `Farm` (items, unlocks, drones) and `Execution` (Python interpreter). `Farm` owns `GridManager` (2D grid of `FarmObject` entities and `Ground` tiles). `FarmRenderer` renders everything via GPU instancing on the main thread.

**Key Code:**
- `MainSim.SimulationLoop()` — runs on background thread, calls `sim.NextExecutionStep()` repeatedly
- `MainSim.SetupSim()` — creates `Simulation`, `Farm`, loads `ResourceManager`, restores save
- `Farm.CollectItems()` — harvests all items from grid, returns `ItemBlock`
- `GridManager.CreateFarmObject()` — uses `Activator.CreateInstance` + type lookup from `ResourceManager`

**Data Flow:** MainSim.Inst → Simulation → Farm → GridManager → FarmObject instances

**Coupling:**
- `MainSim` directly references: Simulation, Farm, Execution, Workspace, Saver, Achievements, ResourceManager, Leaderboard*
- `Farm` directly references: GridManager, Simulation, Drone, ResourceManager, Achievements
- `GridManager` uses reflection (`Activator.CreateInstance`) to create FarmObjects from SO className
- `FarmRenderer` polls `MainSim.Inst` every frame for mesh data

### 4.2 Python Interpreter System

**Files:** `Tokenizer.cs`, `Token.cs`, `TokenType.cs`, `TokenStream.cs`, `Parser.cs`, `Node.cs` + 23 node subclasses, `Execution.cs`, `Program.cs`, `ProgramState.cs`, `ModuleState.cs`, `Scope.cs`, `PyFunction.cs`, `BuiltinFunctions.cs`, `SideEffect.cs`, `ParseException.cs`

**Responsibilities:** A complete Python-subset interpreter. The `Tokenizer` lexes code into `Token` streams, the `Parser` builds an AST of `Node` objects via recursive descent, and `Execution` walks the AST using `IEnumerable<double>` (yield-based coroutine pattern for step-by-step execution). Side effects (move, harvest, plant, etc.) are yielded up to `Execution.ApplySideEffect()` which calls into the Farm/Drone.

**Key Code:**
- `Tokenizer.Tokenize()` — regex-based lexer, produces linked list of Tokens
- `Parser.Parse()` — recursive descent: `Expression → BinaryExpression → UnaryExpression → Call/Index/Dot → Atom`
- `Node.Execute()` — `IEnumerable<double>` (each yield = one operation's time cost in seconds)
- `Execution.Execute()` — main loop: iterates node execution, applies side effects, tracks op count
- `BuiltinFunctions` — ~50 builtin functions mapped to `SideEffect` enum values
- All 23 node types implement `Execute()` for their specific semantics

**AST Node Hierarchy:**
```
Node (abstract base)
├── LiteralNode (True/False/None/numbers/strings)
├── ValueNode (variable reference)
├── AssignmentNode (=, +=, -=, etc.)
├── BinaryExprNode (+, -, *, /, and, or, in, **, etc.)
├── UnaryExprNode (-, +, not)
├── ComparisonNode (==, !=, <, >, in, not in — chains)
├── BranchNode (if/elif/else/while)
├── ForNode (for x in y)
├── CallNode (function call)
├── DefNode (def function definition)
├── FunctionNode (function body execution)
├── ReturnNode (return statement)
├── BreakNode / ContinueNode / PassNode / NoOpNode
├── SequenceNode (statement sequence)
├── ImportNode (import / from x import y)
├── ListNode / DictNode / SetNode / TupleNode (collection literals)
└── BracketNode (parenthesized expressions)
```

**Coupling:** `BuiltinFunctions` and `Execution.ApplySideEffect()` are the main coupling points — they directly reference `Farm`, `Drone`, `GridManager`, `Hat`, `Leaderboard*`, `ResourceManager`, `Logger`. The AST nodes themselves are pure computation with no game dependencies.

### 4.3 Python Type System

**Files:** `IPyObject.cs`, `IPySequence.cs`, `IPyIndexable.cs`, `IPyNameSpace.cs`, `PyNumber.cs`, `PyBool.cs`, `PyString.cs`, `PyList.cs`, `PyDict.cs`, `PyTuple.cs`, `PySet.cs`, `PyRange.cs`, `PyNone.cs`, `PyObjectBox.cs`, `PyCollectionEnumerator.cs`, `PyConstBag.cs`, `PyUnassigned.cs`, `PyDroneHandle.cs`, `PyGridDirection.cs`, `PyModule.cs`, `PyFunction.cs`, `InternalPySequence.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Runtime type system for the Python interpreter. Each type implements `IPyObject` with `DeepCopy()` and `Size()`. Collection types implement `IPySequence` with indexing/slicing via `IPyIndexable`. `PyModule` and `PyConstBag` implement `IPyNameSpace` for namespace lookup (`Entities.Bush`, `Items.Cactus`).

**Key Design:**
- `IPyObject` — base interface: `DeepCopy(Dictionary<object,object>)`, `Size()`
- `IPySequence` extends `IReadOnlyList<IPyObject>`, `IPyIndexable` — ordered collections
- `IPyIndexable` — has default implementation for Python-style slicing `[start:stop:step]`
- `PyObjectBox` — mutable wrapper for dict values (reference semantics)
- `PyNone` — singleton pattern (`static PyNone Instance`)
- `PyDroneHandle` / `PyGridDirection` — game-specific types wrapping drone IDs and grid directions

**Coupling:** Minimal. Types reference each other for conversion/comparison. `CodeUtilities.ToNiceString()` handles repr for all types. Game-specific types (`PyDroneHandle`, `PyGridDirection`) reference `GridDirection`.

### 4.4 Farm Objects & Plants System

**Files:** `FarmObject.cs`, `Growable.cs`, `BushPlant.cs`, `Cactus.cs`, `Sunflower.cs`, `Pumpkin.cs`, `DeadPumpkin.cs`, `PumpkinController.cs`, `TreePlant.cs`, `Apple.cs`, `HedgePlant.cs`, `Treasure.cs`, `Ground.cs`, `Soil.cs`

**Responsibilities:** All grid objects. `FarmObject` is the base class with position, animation, and virtual methods (Harvest, GetMeshes, Measure). `Growable` extends it with growth timing, companion planting, and fertilizer mechanics. Each plant type has unique puzzle logic:

| Plant | Mechanic |
|-------|----------|
| BushPlant | Basic — grow + harvest. Can generate hedge mazes. |
| Cactus | Sorting puzzle — numbered cacti, harvest pops connected sorted chains |
| Sunflower | Number game — 8x yield if harvesting max-number when 10+ sunflowers exist |
| Pumpkin | Square grouping — forms NxN squares via PumpkinController (dynamic programming) |
| TreePlant | Growth penalty — 2x grow time per adjacent tree |
| HedgePlant | Maze walls — 4 directional walls, blocks drone movement |
| Treasure | Maze reward — collectible that repositions within maze |
| Apple | Snake food — part of dinosaur hat snake game |

**Coupling:**
- All plants reference `Simulation`, `GridManager`, `ResourceManager`
- `PumpkinController` tightly coupled to `GridManager` (manages pumpkin groups)
- `BushPlant` creates `HedgePlant` and `Treasure` instances directly
- `Apple` references `Dinosaur` for snake game positioning

### 4.5 Drone & Hat System

**Files:** `Drone.cs`, `DroneSO.cs`, `Hat.cs`, `HatSO.cs`, `HatPopup.cs`, `DinosaurHat.cs`, `Dinosaur.cs`

**Responsibilities:** The drone is the player's avatar on the grid. It moves, harvests, plants, waters, fertilizes, and swaps. Hats modify drone behavior (e.g., DinosaurHat adds a snake game where the drone grows a tail). `Hat.CreateHat()` uses reflection (`Activator.CreateInstance`) to instantiate typed hat subclasses from `HatSO.className`.

**Key Code:**
- `Drone.Move()` — moves drone on grid, calls `hat.OnMove()`, handles wrapping
- `Drone.Harvest()` — harvests entity under drone, collects items
- `Hat.CreateHat()` — factory using `Type.GetType(className)` + `Activator.CreateInstance`
- `DinosaurHat.OnMove()` — extends snake tail, checks apple collision, bone collection

**Coupling:** `Drone` references `Simulation`, `Farm`, `GridManager`, `Hat`, `ResourceManager`, `Achievements`. `DinosaurHat` references `Dinosaur`, `Apple`, `Achievements`.

### 4.6 Economy & Inventory System

**Files:** `ItemSO.cs`, `ItemBlock.cs`, `SerializableItem.cs`, `StringIds.cs`, `Inventory.cs`, `ItemUI.cs`, `ItemEffect.cs`, `PiggyBank.cs`, `TradeSO.cs`

**Responsibilities:** `ItemBlock` is the core currency type — a double array indexed by item ID. `StringIds` maps item names to IDs. `Inventory` is a MonoBehaviour that polls an `ItemBlock` source every frame to sync `ItemUI` elements. `PiggyBank` is a flying collector that follows the cursor/drone for visual feedback on item collection.

**Key Code:**
- `ItemBlock` — `Add(ItemBlock)`, `RemovePartially()`, `Contains()`, `GetNumber()`, `TruncateNumbers()`
- `StringIds.SetItemIds()` — initializes the name→ID mapping from ItemSO resources
- `Inventory.Update()` — polls `getItemBlock()` callback, creates/destroys `ItemUI` instances

**Coupling:** `ItemBlock` is used everywhere (Farm, Drone, UnlockSO, TradeSO, FarmObjectSO). `StringIds` is static, accessed globally.

### 4.7 Research & Progression System

**Files:** `ResearchMenu.cs`, `UnlockBox.cs`, `UnlockSO.cs`, `MultiUnlockDescrMode.cs`, `Achievements.cs`, `AchievementSO.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Research tree UI where players spend items to unlock new features. `UnlockSO` defines a tree structure (`parentUnlock`, `unlocks` list). `ResearchMenu` renders the tree with auto-layout and collision detection. `UnlockBox` represents one node with states (Locked/Unlockable/Unlocked/Upgradable). `Achievements` tracks statistics, golden hat unlocks, and Steam achievements.

**Key Code:**
- `ResearchMenu.Setup()` — tree layout: recursive positioning with collision avoidance
- `UnlockBox.SetupRec()` — recursive state update (unlock state based on parent and cost)
- `Achievements.CollectItem()` — static, thread-safe item tracking with windowed statistics
- `Achievements.UnlockGoldenHats()` / `UnlockFarmHats()` — threshold-based hat unlocks

**Coupling:**
- `ResearchMenu` references `MainSim`, `OptionHolder`, `ThemeManager`
- `UnlockBox` references `MainSim` for unlock state queries
- `Achievements` is static, references `SteamManager`, `MainSim`, `ResourceManager`

### 4.8 Save/Load System

**Files:** `Saver.cs`, `SaveGame.cs`, `OldSaveGame.cs`, `SaveChooser.cs`, `SaveOption.cs`, `SFO.cs`

**Responsibilities:** JSON serialization to disk with rotating backups. `SaveGame` is the data class (items, open windows, unlocks). `Saver` is a static class managing save/load operations plus a `FileSystemWatcher` for live code reload from `.py` files on disk. `SaveChooser` is the UI for managing multiple save slots.

**Key Code:**
- `Saver.Save()` — serializes `SaveGame` to JSON, writes code windows to `.py` files
- `Saver.Load()` — deserializes, handles `OldSaveGame` migration
- `Saver.StartFileWatcher()` — `FileSystemWatcher` on save directory for `.py` changes
- `SaveChooser` — CRUD operations on save directories

**Coupling:** `Saver` references `MainSim`, `Workspace`, `SaveGame`, `OptionHolder`.

### 4.9 IDE / Workspace System

**Files:** `Workspace.cs`, `CodeWindow.cs`, `Window.cs`, `CodeCompleter.cs`, `CodeOption.cs`, `DocsWindow.cs`, `SearchBox.cs`, `MarkdownText.cs`, `OutputText.cs`, `ErrorMessage.cs`, `BlinkManager.cs`, `BreakPointPanel.cs`, `ContainerScaler.cs`, `InputBoss.cs`, `SpoilerWarning.cs`

**Responsibilities:** A full code editor IDE built in Unity. `Workspace` manages multiple `CodeWindow` instances (each a Python file editor with syntax highlighting, breakpoints, error display). `Window` handles docking, minimization, resize. `CodeCompleter` provides autocomplete. `DocsWindow` renders markdown documentation. `SearchBox` does workspace-wide search. `BlinkManager` highlights executing code lines.

**Key Code:**
- `Workspace.OpenCodeWindow()` — creates/focuses code editor windows
- `CodeWindow.Parse()` — tokenizes + parses on background thread, caches program
- `CodeWindow.PressExecuteOrStop()` — starts/stops Python execution for this file
- `Window.DockOnto()` — docking system for window management
- `MarkdownText.Setup()` — markdown → rich text with links, spoilers, images, code blocks
- `InputBoss.Update()` — global keyboard routing (Ctrl+F, Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+S, Escape, etc.)

**Coupling:** Heavy coupling between Workspace ↔ CodeWindow ↔ Window. `CodeWindow` references `Parser`, `Tokenizer`, `ThemeManager`. `InputBoss` references `MainSim`, `Workspace`, `Menu`.

### 4.10 Options & Settings System

**Files:** `OptionSO.cs`, `OptionUI.cs`, `OptionHolder.cs`, `OptionValueType.cs`, `CycleOptionSO.cs`, `CycleOptionUI.cs`, `DropdownOptionSO.cs`, `DropdownOptionUI.cs`, `KeyBindOptionSO.cs`, `KeyBindOptionUI.cs`, `SliderOptionSO.cs`, `SliderOptionUI.cs`, `OptionMenu.cs`, `KeyCombination.cs`

**Responsibilities:** A ScriptableObject-driven options system. `OptionSO` subtypes define option types (cycle, dropdown, slider, keybind). `OptionHolder` is a static class that persists settings to a file and dispatches side effects (graphics quality, resolution, volume, language, theme). `OptionMenu` renders option categories with tabs.

**Key Code:**
- `OptionHolder.SetOption()` — writes to file, applies side effects (volume, language, etc.)
- `OptionHolder.OnOptionChanged` — static event broadcast to all listeners
- Each `OptionUI` subclass handles its specific input type

**Coupling:** `OptionHolder` is a coupling hub — references `FMODSoundManager`, `Localizer`, `ThemeManager`, `WarningPopup`, `SteamManager`, `ResolutionOptionUpdater`.

### 4.11 Theme System

**Files:** `ThemeManager.cs`, `ColorTheme.cs`, `ColorStringAttribute.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Hot-reloadable color theme system. `ThemeManager` loads themes from `ScriptableObject` or JSON `.tfwrTheme` files. `ColorTheme` defines all colors for code syntax, UI elements, buttons, input fields. Subscribes to `OnThemeChanged` fire immediately on subscribe (ensures new subscribers get current theme).

**Key Design:** `ThemeManager.OnThemeChanged` uses custom event add/remove that immediately invokes the new subscriber — elegant pattern for theme-dependent UI.

**Coupling:** `ThemeManager` is referenced by almost every UI script.

### 4.12 Sound & VFX System

**Files:** `SoundManager.cs`, `FMODSoundManager.cs`, `SoundEffectType.cs`, `SoundReferencesSO.cs`, `VFXManager.cs`, `VFXCall.cs`, `VFXType.cs`, `ParticleEffect.cs`, `PopupEffect.cs`, `PrintSign.cs`, `IVisualEffect.cs`

**Responsibilities:** `SoundManager` queues sounds from background threads and plays them on the main thread. `FMODSoundManager` wraps FMOD API calls (VCA volume, event instances). `VFXManager` pools visual effects (particles, text popups, print signs). Both use concurrent queues for thread safety.

**Coupling:** `SoundManager` references `FMODSoundManager`, `SoundReferencesSO`, `OptionHolder`. `VFXManager` references `IVisualEffect`, `OptionHolder`.

### 4.13 Leaderboard System

**Files:** `LeaderboardManager.cs`, `Leaderboard.cs`, `LeaderboardEntry.cs`, `LeaderboardSO.cs`, `LeaderboardType.cs`, `SteamLeaderboard.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Timed farming challenges with Steam leaderboard integration. `LeaderboardManager` manages run state (start/stop timer, speed controls). `SteamLeaderboard` wraps Steam API calls for uploading/downloading scores.

**Coupling:** `LeaderboardManager` references `MainSim`, `SteamLeaderboard`. `SteamLeaderboard` references `SteamManager`, Steam API.

### 4.14 Tooltip System

**Files:** `Tooltip.cs`, `TooltipAccessor.cs`, `TooltipPositioner.cs`, `TooltipUtils.cs`, `TooltipInfo.cs`, `ITooltipHandler.cs`, `DropdownTooltips.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Hover tooltip system. Components implement `ITooltipHandler` to provide tooltip content. `Tooltip` shows markdown-rendered text near cursor. `TooltipUtils` has static factory methods for item/unlock/hat tooltips.

**Coupling:** Clean interface-based design. `ITooltipHandler` is implemented by many UI components.

### 4.15 Camera & Input System

**Files:** `CameraController.cs`, `InputBoss.cs`, `CustomStandaloneInputModule.cs`

**Responsibilities:** `CameraController` handles zoom and positioning for the code editor workspace. `InputBoss` routes keyboard input globally (Escape, Ctrl+F, Ctrl+Z/Y, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+N, WASD).

**Coupling:** `InputBoss` references `MainSim`, `Workspace`, `Menu`.

### 4.16 Localization System

**Files:** `Localizer.cs`, `TMPTextLocalizer.cs`, `StringIds.cs`

**Responsibilities:** `Localizer` loads translations from files, provides `Localize(key)` lookup. `TMPTextLocalizer` auto-localizes TMP text components. Language files loaded per-locale with TMP font fallback support.

**Coupling:** `Localizer` references `OptionHolder`, `TMP_Settings`. Accessed globally as static class.

### 4.17 Timer System

**Files:** `TimerManager.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Simple timer scheduler (sorted list). `StartTimer(callback, delay, loop)` / `StopTimer(callback)`. Used by buttons (hold detection), animations, and UI timing.

### 4.18 Misc / Debug

**Files:** `SteamScript.cs`, `SteamStatsLoop.cs`, `CheatConsole.cs`, `DLCBanner.cs`, `FlashingButton.cs`, `WarningIcon.cs`, `Logger.cs`, `ResourceManager.cs`, `UndoHistory.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Steam integration (stats, rich presence), debug console (cheat codes), DLC promotion, logging system, centralized resource loading.

### 4.19 UI Components (Shared)

**Files:** `ColoredButton.cs`, `Tickbox.cs`, `WarningPopup.cs`, `UIScaler.cs`, `CutoutMaskImg.cs`, `ScaledScrollRect.cs`, `MonoTextSizeFitter.cs`, `ResolutionOptionUpdater.cs`, `ImgFrameAnimator.cs`, `UtilsGameObjects.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Reusable UI components. `ColoredButton` is the primary button with theme colors, sounds, hold detection, and tooltips. `WarningPopup` shows modal dialogs. `UIScaler` applies global UI scale.

### 4.20 Data Structures & Utilities

**Files:** `Deque.cs`, `Duration.cs`, `RangeSet.cs`, `RectExtensions.cs`, `Helper.cs`, `CodeUtilities.cs`, `PriorityQueue.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Generic data structures (double-ended queue, nanosecond duration, non-overlapping range set), number formatting (`NrToText` with k/M/B/T suffixes), Python syntax highlighting (`CodeUtilities`), world size scaling.

### 4.21 TMPro Fork

**Files:** `CodeInputField.cs`, `SetPropertyUtility.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Custom fork of TMP_InputField for code editing features (custom events for text insertion, backspace, caret movement, click, text mesh updates).

---

## 5. Key Design Patterns & Techniques

### Thread-Safe Simulation
The game runs its simulation on a background thread with `lock(lockSimulation)` synchronization. Main thread reads mesh data and queues UI updates; background thread runs the Python interpreter and applies side effects to the farm grid.

### Yield-Based Coroutine Interpreter
AST nodes implement `Execute()` as `IEnumerable<double>` — each yield returns the time cost of an operation. The execution engine iterates these, accumulating time and applying side effects. This allows step-by-step debugging and speed control.

### GPU Instanced Rendering
`FarmRenderer` collects all mesh data from `MainSim` and draws them via `Graphics.DrawMeshInstanced()` in a single batch — no individual GameObjects for farm tiles.

### ConcurrentQueue for Thread Communication
`SoundManager` and `VFXManager` use `ConcurrentQueue` to receive requests from the background thread and process them on the main thread in `Update()`.

### Reflection-Based Factory Pattern
`GridManager.CreateFarmObject()` and `Hat.CreateHat()` use `Type.GetType(className)` + `Activator.CreateInstance` to create instances from ScriptableObject class names. This allows data-driven entity creation without switch statements.

### Static Classes as Service Locators
`ResourceManager`, `Saver`, `Achievements`, `OptionHolder`, `Localizer`, `CodeUtilities`, `Logger` are all static classes accessed globally. This avoids FindObjectOfType but creates implicit dependencies.

### FileSystemWatcher for Live Reload
`Saver.StartFileWatcher()` watches the save directory for `.py` file changes, enabling editing code in external editors with auto-import.

### Custom Event Pattern (ThemeManager)
`ThemeManager.OnThemeChanged` uses custom event add/remove that immediately invokes the new subscriber with the current theme — subscribers always get the initial value.

### PriorityQueue Timer System
`Simulation` uses a `PriorityQueue<Duration, Timer>` for scheduling timed events (plant growth, animations), processed on the background thread.

---

## 6. Coupling Analysis

### FindObjectOfType Calls

| File | Target Type | How to Decouple |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `UtilsGameObjects.cs` | `WarningPopup` | `[SerializeField]` injection |

### Direct Cross-System References

| File A | File B | Nature | How to Decouple |
|--------|--------|--------|-----------------|
| `MainSim` | `Simulation`, `Farm`, `Execution`, `Workspace`, `Saver`, `Achievements`, `Leaderboard*` | God-object orchestrator | Split into focused managers, use GameEvents |
| `Execution.ApplySideEffect()` | `Farm`, `Drone`, `GridManager`, `Hat`, `Leaderboard*`, `Logger` | 39-case switch | Strategy pattern or interface-based dispatch |
| `BuiltinFunctions` | `Farm`, `Drone`, `Simulation`, `ProgramState` | Static function bindings | Inject via interface |
| `Farm` | `GridManager`, `ResourceManager`, `Achievements`, `MainSim` | Direct field access | Constructor injection, GameEvents |
| `GridManager` | `Activator.CreateInstance(Type.GetType(className))` | Reflection | Factory with registered types |
| `ResearchMenu` | `MainSim` | Unlock state queries | Interface or GameEvents |
| `CodeWindow` | `Parser`, `Tokenizer`, `ThemeManager` | Direct use | Fine — same system |
| `InputBoss` | `MainSim`, `Workspace`, `Menu` | Global keyboard | GameEvents for actions |
| `OptionHolder` | `FMODSoundManager`, `Localizer`, `ThemeManager`, `SteamManager` | Side effect dispatch | Event-driven per-option |
| `Saver` | `MainSim`, `Workspace` | Save/load | GameEvents + data serialization |

### Static Instance Access

| File | Singleton | Read or Command |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| `FarmRenderer.Update()` | `MainSim.Inst` | Read (mesh data) |
| `BuiltinFunctions` | `MainSim.Inst` | Read + Command (simulation state) |
| `Achievements` | `MainSim.Inst` | Read (unlock state) |
| Many UI scripts | `ThemeManager.Inst` | Read (subscribe to theme) |
| Sound callers | `SoundManager.Inst` | Command (play sound) |
| VFX callers | `VFXManager.Inst` | Command (play effect) |
| Timer users | `TimerManager.Inst` | Command (start/stop timer) |
| UI scripts | `WarningPopup.Inst` | Command (show popup) |
| `HatPopup` callers | `HatPopup.Inst` | Command (show hat popup) |

---

## 7. God Objects — Split Candidates

| File | ~Lines | Responsibilities | Suggested Split |
|------|--------|-----------------|-----------------|
| `MainSim.cs` | ~600 | Simulation lifecycle, unlock queries, item queries, leaderboard scheduling, sound/VFX forwarding, save/load orchestration | `SimulationManager` (lifecycle) + `UnlockManager` (queries) + GameEvents for sound/VFX |
| `Execution.cs` | ~700 | Program execution loop + `ApplySideEffect()` 39-case switch | `ExecutionEngine` (loop) + `SideEffectDispatcher` (strategy pattern) |
| `Parser.cs` | ~500 | Full recursive descent parser | Fine as-is — single responsibility |
| `CodeWindow.cs` | ~500+ | Code editing, parsing, execution, error display, UI state | `CodeEditorState` (parsing/tokens) + `CodeWindow` (UI only) |
| `Workspace.cs` | ~350 | Window management, zoom, camera, code window factory | Fine — workspace is naturally a coordinator |
| `BuiltinFunctions.cs` | ~300+ | 50 function bindings | Group by category (movement, farming, utility, drone) |
| `ResearchMenu.cs` | ~300 | Tree layout + UI management | `ResearchLayout` (positioning) + `ResearchMenu` (UI) |
| `Growable.cs` | ~280 | Base plant with growth, fertilize, companion, weird, serialization | Fine — base class naturally has more |
| `GridManager.cs` | ~450 | Grid CRUD + world generation + neighbor queries | `GridManager` (CRUD) + `WorldGenerator` (generation) |
| `OptionHolder.cs` | ~320 | Option persistence + side effect dispatch for ALL option types | Fine — static option manager |
| `CodeUtilities.cs` | ~400+ | Syntax highlighting + formatting + deep compare | `SyntaxHighlighter` + `PyFormatter` + `DeepCompare` |
| `ItemBlock.cs` | ~200 | Item collection with arithmetic | Fine — focused data class |

---

## 8. Critique & Thoughts

### What the Original Source Does Well (Preserve These)

1. **Zero FindObjectOfType** — exceptionally clean for a decompiled project. Only 1 `FindAnyObjectByType`.
2. **Thread-safe design** — simulation on background thread with proper locking is rare and impressive.
3. **Yield-based interpreter** — elegant `IEnumerable<double>` pattern for step-by-step execution.
4. **GPU instanced rendering** — no individual GameObjects for 1000+ grid tiles. Excellent performance.
5. **Data-driven design** — `FarmObjectSO`, `UnlockSO`, `HatSO`, `ItemSO` keep data in ScriptableObjects.
6. **PriorityQueue timer** — efficient scheduling on the background thread.
7. **Hot-reloadable themes** — JSON color themes with FileSystemWatcher.
8. **Live code reload** — FileSystemWatcher for external Python editing.
9. **ConcurrentQueue** for cross-thread communication (sound/VFX).
10. **Clean type system** — `IPyObject` hierarchy with proper deep copy, equality, slicing.

### What to Improve (Architecture Transformation Drivers)

1. **Static class proliferation** — `ResourceManager`, `Saver`, `Achievements`, `OptionHolder`, `Localizer`, `CodeUtilities`, `Logger` are all static. Makes testing impossible. → Convert to Singleton<T> managers or inject via constructor.
2. **`MainSim` god-object** — does too much (simulation lifecycle, unlock queries, item queries, leaderboard, sound/VFX forwarding). → Split into focused managers.
3. **`Execution.ApplySideEffect()` 39-case switch** — monolithic handler for all game actions. → Strategy pattern or command dispatch.
4. **Reflection-based factories** — `Activator.CreateInstance(Type.GetType(className))` is fragile and hard to trace. → Registered factory dictionary.
5. **Missing event system** — No GameEvents. Cross-system communication goes through `MainSim.Inst` or static classes. → Add partial GameEvents for decoupling.
6. **Polling in Inventory** — `Inventory.Update()` polls `getItemBlock()` every frame. → Event-driven refresh.
7. **Thread safety concerns** — Many shared data structures accessed from both threads with inconsistent locking. → Review lock granularity.
8. **Missing separation of concerns** — `CodeWindow` handles editing, parsing, execution, and error display. → Extract responsibilities.
9. **`OptionHolder` coupling hub** — dispatches side effects for all option types, directly calling into `FMODSoundManager`, `Localizer`, `ThemeManager`, etc. → Option-specific handlers.
10. **No test infrastructure** — No unit tests, no test scenes, no DataService pattern. → Add DataServices with `GetSnapShotForTest()`.

---

| `TweenAction.cs` | 52 | Enum: LeanTween action types (third-party) |
| `UIScaler.cs` | 25 | Global UI scale adjuster |
| `UndoHistory.cs` | 45 | Per-GameObject undo/redo stack |
| `UtilsGameObjects.cs` | 30 | Singleton: lazy WarningPopup finder |
| `VFXCall.cs` | 20 | VFX request data struct |
| `VFXManager.cs` | 130 | Singleton: thread-safe VFX pool |
| `VFXType.cs` | 12 | Enum: 9 visual effect types |
| `WarningPopup.cs` | 65 | Modal warning dialog |

### Assembly-CSharp/ (Game Code Only)

| File | ~Lines | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `AchievementSO.cs` | 8 | ScriptableObject: achievement mesh + icon text |
| `CreateItemTextures.cs` | 200+ | Editor tool: capture 3D models as texture atlases |

### Third-Party (NOT rebuilt — used as-is or replaced)

| Library | Files | Decision |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Steamworks.NET | ~200 | Use as-is (external DLL) |
| LeanTween | ~22 | Use as-is (tweening library) |

**Total game scripts to rebuild: ~185** (Core 119 + Utils game-code ~64 + Assembly-CSharp 2)

|--------|-------|-------------|-----------|---------|
| TooltipAccessor | G | TooltipSystem | Any ITooltipHandler | Hunter (GetComponent scan) |

**Note:** fwr has minimal bridge usage. Most cross-system communication uses:
- ConcurrentQueue for thread-safe sound/VFX dispatch
- Direct singleton reads (MainSim.Inst, SoundManager.Inst)
- Static method calls (Logger, ResourceManager, Localizer)

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/fwr/GOAL.md ==== -->
# GOAL — Architecture for fwr (The Farmer Was Replaced)

> Every script is typed by hand. This file defines how.

---

## What

Rebuild **fwr** (The Farmer Was Replaced) from its original decompiled/extracted source (`Scripts/Core/`, `Scripts/Utils/`, `Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/`).

**Three goals, in order:**
1. **100% main-source behavior** — every feature the original game has, we have
2. **Clean architecture** — zero tight coupling between systems. Systems communicate via interfaces and GameEvents only. No direct class imports across system boundaries.
3. **Portable systems** — systems designed via `_-Systems/` architecture should work in ANY future Unity project with minimal changes. Some systems will be intentionally game-specific — that's fine.

This document is the architecture bible for fwr. The architecture, rules, conventions, and patterns below are universal — they work for any Unity3D game rebuild.

## Source of Truth

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Logic & behavior | `Scripts/Core/*.cs`, `Scripts/Utils/*.cs`, `Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/*.cs` |
| Architecture rules | `learn/GOAL.md` (this file) |
| Roadmap | `learn/PhaseMap.md` |
| DataService specs | `learn/StructureMap.md` |
| Original analysis | `learn/ARCHITECTURE.md` |
| Reasoning log | `learn/surfer.md` |
| User's actual code | `learn/handTyped(latest)/` — ground truth for coding style |
| Portability map | `learn/SystemPortabilityMap.md` — L0/L1+ per system |
| Cross-system audit | `learn/SystemIsolationAnalysis.md` — interfaces, bridges, events, concrete deps |
| Source coverage | `learn/CoverageMap.md` — every source file → which phase covers it |
| Optional extras | `learn/OptionalFeatures.md` — external package integration |

---

## Core Principle

**Every script's purpose fits in one sentence. If it doesn't, split it until it does.**
**Every script performs ONLY the service it was assigned — purely, nothing else.**

Every file must have a `→ "I do X"` one-liner. That one-liner is the script's contract. If the script does anything outside that sentence, it's doing too much — move it to the script whose sentence covers it.

```
SO_FarmObject          → "I define what a farm object IS (mesh, grow time, item type)"
SO_Item                → "I define what an item IS (name, texture, value)"
SO_Unlock              → "I define a research unlock (cost, prerequisite, description)"
ItemBlock              → "I hold a set of item quantities indexed by ID"
FarmObject             → "I represent one entity placed on the farm grid"
GridManager            → "I manage the 2D grid of farm entities and ground tiles"
Farm                   → "I coordinate grid, drone, items, and side effects"
Execution              → "I run Python AST programs on the background thread"
BuiltinFunctions       → "I map Python function names to C# side effect implementations"
Tokenizer              → "I lex Python source code into a token stream"
Parser                 → "I parse a token stream into an AST node tree"
CodeWindow             → "I manage one code editor window (text, caret, autocomplete)"
Workspace              → "I manage the IDE — open/close/position code windows"
ResearchMenu           → "I display the unlock tree and handle purchases"
Saver                  → "I serialize/deserialize the full game state"
MainSim                → "I am the singleton root — Start/Stop simulation, own Farm+Workspace"
GameEvents             → "I deliver messages between systems"
Singleton              → "I ensure one instance"
```

End goal: **main source (100%) behavior** with this architecture — consistent, decoupled, independently testable via vertical slice tests.

---

## What Makes This Architecture Different

### 1. God-objects → One-sentence scripts

```
Original fwr:   Farm.cs (600+ lines) — grid mgmt, item collection, unlocks, drone, side effects, rendering
This approach:  GridManager.cs (grid state — positions, bounds, neighbors)
                Farm.cs (coordinate grid+drone+items, dispatch side effects)
                FarmRenderer.cs (GPU instanced drawing — meshes, matrices, colors)
                → each fits ONE sentence. Break one? Others still work.
```

### 2. FindObjectOfType → GameEvents + [SerializeField]

```
Original fwr:   Only 1 FindObjectOfType in entire codebase (good!) — UtilsGameObjects → WarningPopup
This approach:  Replace that 1 via [SerializeField] or GameEvents
                → zero FindObjectOfType anywhere
```

### 3. UI logic mixed with data → DataService (pure C#, testable via `new`)

```
Original fwr:   ResearchMenu manages unlock state + UI display in one class
This approach:  UnlockDataService (pure C# — unlock state, afford checks, prerequisite chains)
                ResearchMenu (UI display only — open/close, forward clicks to orchestrator)
                → DataService testable via `new` instance, zero scene
```

### 4. Thread communication → Formalized ConcurrentQueue

```
Original fwr:   ConcurrentQueue<SoundCall> between simulation thread and main thread (good pattern!)
This approach:  Keep the pattern — formalize with GameEvents for main-thread dispatch
                → background thread enqueues, main thread dequeues in Update, fires events
```

---

## Foundation Templates

These two scripts go in `phase-All/0-Core/` and are the foundation for everything.

### Singleton.cs

```csharp
public abstract class Singleton<T> : MonoBehaviour where T : MonoBehaviour
{
    public static T Ins { get; private set; }

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        if (Ins == null) Ins = this as T;
        else if (Ins != this)
        {
            Debug.Log($"{typeof(T)} singleton already exists, destroying duplicate");
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }
}
```

- First instance wins; duplicates destroyed
- Accessed via `Singleton<T>.Ins` (short for Instance)
- All Managers extend this

**Note:** The original fwr source uses `.Inst` (e.g., `MainSim.Inst`, `SoundManager.Inst`). We standardize to `.Ins` for consistency across all projects.

### GameEvents.cs — Partial Class Pattern

Each phase extends GameEvents in its own `0-Core/GameEvents.cs`. **Phase-All** has the core:

```csharp
/// <summary> I deliver messages between systems. Each phase adds events via partial class.
/// Every Raise logs subscriber count for debugging. </summary>
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when menu state changes >>
    public static event Action<bool> OnMenuStateChanged;
    public static void RaiseMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnMenuStateChanged), OnMenuStateChanged);
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged?
            .Invoke(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }
    // << when menu state changes

    // when all sub-managers must close >>
    public static event Action OnCloseAllSubManagers;
    public static void RaiseCloseAllSubManagers()
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnCloseAllSubManagers), OnCloseAllSubManagers);
        GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers?
            .Invoke();
    }
    // << when all sub-managers must close

    /// <summary> Logs how many subscribers an event has — helps catch missing/extra subscriptions. </summary>
    static void LogSubscribersCount(string name, Delegate del)
    {
        int count = del?.GetInvocationList()?.Length ?? 0;
        Debug.Log($"[GameEvents] {name} raised for -> {count} subscribers".colorTag("lime"));
    }
}
```

### DefaultExecutionOrder — Singleton Init Priorities

```csharp
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-1000)]  // Must exist before anything
public class MainSim : Singleton<MainSim>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(-900)]   // Save/load needed early
public class Saver : Singleton<Saver>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]   // Core systems
public class UIManager : Singleton<UIManager>
public class SoundManager : Singleton<SoundManager>
public class VFXManager : Singleton<VFXManager>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(0)]      // Default — most MonoBehaviours
```

---

## Folder Structure

Numbered for Unity project panel sorting. One-liner purpose per folder.

```
Scripts/phase-All/                      → "shared scripts that grow across phases — never duplicated"
├── 0-Core/
│   ├── Singleton.cs                    → "generic singleton base — first instance wins"
│   └── GameEvents.cs                   → "core events shared across ALL phases"
├── 1-Managers/
│   └── UIManager.cs                    → "reports menu state + closes all panels + routes keyboard"
├── 2-Data/
│   └── Enums/
│       └── GlobalEnumsAll.cs           → "TagType enum — grows across phases"
├── 4-Utils/
│   └── Utils.cs                        → "shared extensions (.map(), .gc<T>(), HasTag, etc.)"
└── 7-3D/
    ├── MODEL.md                        → "low-poly GLB mesh guide (crops, hats, farm objects)"
    ├── ANIM.md                         → "minimal — GPU instanced, no per-object animators"
    └── WORLD.md                        → "grid-based world — 2D NxN farm layout"

Scripts/phase-X/
├── _-Systems/                  → "PRIMARY — where the main code lives"
│   └── XxxSystem/              → "self-contained feature: ALL code for one system"
│       ├── SO_XxxDef.cs        → "config blueprint — only fields, zero methods"
│       ├── Field_Xxx.cs        → "display only — SetData/SetState, no onClick"
│       ├── WXxx.cs             → "wraps SO_ with mutable session state"
│       ├── XxxDataService.cs   → "collection service — Build/Get/Add/Remove/Snapshot"
│       ├── XxxOrchestrator.cs  → "wires Field_ to DataService, AddListener"
│       ├── XxxUI.cs            → "SubManager — open/close one UI panel (isFirstEnable)"
│       ├── Interface/          → "contracts this system OWNS — portable with the system"
│       ├── Bridge/             → "bridges that push runtime context TO this system"
│       ├── Test.md             → "vertical slice test for this system"
│       └── Dependency.md       → "what this system imports, implements, owns"
├── 0-Core/                     → "GameEvents.cs (partial per phase) — nothing else"
├── 2-Data/
│   ├── Interface/              → "cross-system contracts NOT owned by any single system (RARE)"
│   └── Enums/                  → "one GlobalEnumsX.cs per phase — all enums"
├── 4-Utils/                    → "PhaseXLOG.cs only (snapshot formatters)"
└── 5-Tests/                    → "standalone tests not inside a _-Systems/ folder"
    └── Manual/                 → ".md test guides for visual/hands-on verification"
```

**The rule: if a script belongs to a feature system, it goes INSIDE `_-Systems/XxxSystem/`.** The numbered folders are ONLY for shared infrastructure that genuinely doesn't belong to any specific system — this is RARE.

### Manager vs MonoBehaviour — The `Singleton<T>` Rule

**If a script extends `Singleton<T>`, it goes in `1-Managers/`.** No exceptions. This takes precedence over PhaseMap.md listings.

---

## Naming

### Prefix / Field Conventions

| Kind | Convention | Example |
|------|-----------|---------|
| ScriptableObject | `SO_` prefix | `SO_FarmObject`, `SO_Item` |
| Display-only prefab handle | `Field_` prefix | `Field_UnlockBox`, `Field_LeaderboardEntry` |
| DataWrapper | `W` prefix | `WUnlock`, `WItem` |
| Interface | `I` prefix | `IPyObject`, `IPySequence` |
| Dictionary lookups | `DOC__` prefix | `DOC__pos_entity`, `DOC__name_window` |
| List/collection fields | `ALL_CAPS` | `CATEGORY`, `ITEM_DEF`, `UNLOCK` |
| `[SerializeField]` fields | `_` prefix, always private | `[SerializeField] float _speed` |
| Private non-serialized | `camelCase` | `items`, `isFirstEnable`, `waterVolume` |
| Boolean fields | `is` prefix | `isFirstEnable`, `isDead`, `isMaze` |
| Constants | `CONSTANT_CASE` | `GRID_SIZE`, `MAX_DRONES` |
| Event handlers | `Handle...` | `HandleSimulationCompleted` |
| GameEvents | `On...` + `Raise...` side by side | `OnSimulationCompleted` / `RaiseSimulationCompleted()` |
| Singleton access | `.Ins` | `Singleton<MainSim>.Ins` |
| Enum values | camelCase | `SideEffect.plantBush`, `GridDirection.north` |

**Prefix = no logic.** `SO_` = pure data. `Field_` = display only. `W` = session wrapper.

### Method Naming Patterns

| Prefix | Meaning | Return | Example |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| `Get...` | Read/query data | value | `GetNumber()`, `GetUnlockCost()` |
| `Set...` | Write/assign data | void | `SetData()`, `SetEntity()` |
| `Try...` | Attempt action (may fail) | bool | `TryAdd()`, `TryMerge()` |
| `Can...` | Check capability | bool | `CanAfford()` |
| `Is...` | Check state | bool | `IsEmpty()`, `IsWithinBounds()` |
| `Has...` | Ownership check | bool | `HasTag()` |
| `Handle...` | Event handler (callback) | void | `HandleSimulationCompleted()` |
| `Raise...` | Fire GameEvent | void | `RaiseSimulationCompleted()` |
| `Build...` | Initialize collections | void | `BuildGrid()`, `BuildUnlocks()` |
| `Refresh...` | Update UI after mutation | void | `RefreshAllRequired()` |

### No C# Property Accessors

`{ get; set; }` forbidden. Use explicit `Get.../Set...()` methods.
- **Exceptions:** `[Serializable]` entities, SO_ (pure data), interface contracts, static collections

---

## Script Structure

`#region` order (MANDATORY):

```
#region Inspector Fields       ← [SerializeField] refs (MonoBehaviours only)
#endregion
#region private API            ← runtime state + internal helpers (combined)
#endregion
#region Public API             ← or subdivide per interface
#endregion
#region Extra                  ← // nice-to-have: features — typed last, skippable
#endregion
#region Unity Life Cycle       ← Awake, Start, OnEnable, Update, etc. — LAST
#endregion
```

- No blank lines between `#endregion` and next `#region`
- **`private API` combines fields + helpers** — single region
- `/// <summary>` on EVERY class and EVERY method — mandatory
- `// →` inline flow markers inside every method body
- `// purpose:` on every `.Raise...()` and `+=` subscription
- Explicit `== false` over `!` prefix for boolean checks

---

## Class Responsibilities — Pure Purpose

### `SO_` — Pure Data Blueprint

Inspector-editable config. **Only public fields. Zero methods.**

```csharp
[CreateAssetMenu(menuName = "SO/SO_FarmObject", fileName = "SO_FarmObject")]
public class SO_FarmObject : ScriptableObject
{
    public string objectName;
    public Mesh mesh;
    public float growTime;
    public int itemId;
    public Material material;
}
```

### `Field_` — Display Only

Set UI visuals. `SetData()` / `SetState()`. **No onClick. No logic.**

### `W` — DataWrapper

Wrap SO_ with mutable session state. **SO_ = what it IS. W = what happened to it.**

### DataService — EVERY collection lives here

Purely C# Collection Service. `Build + Get + Add + Remove + boolean questions + snapshot`.
**Every `List<T>` and `Dictionary<K,V>` that can be tested via `new` MUST live in a DataService.**

### PhaseXLOG — ONE LOG method per collection

Snapshot-format each collection to JSON. `LIST_X__TO__JSON` for lists, `DOC_X__TO__JSON` for dictionaries.

### Orchestrator — Wires Field_ to DataService

Instantiate Field_ prefabs, `AddListener`, Destroy. `DOC__X__Field` tracking. `RefreshAllRequired()` after mutations. Event-driven — **never `Update()`**.

### Managers — Singleton<T>

Owns one domain's state. Minimal query API. Lives in `1-Managers/`.

### SubManagers — `isFirstEnable` pattern

Open/close one UI panel. Creates DataService on first enable. Zero business logic.

```csharp
bool isFirstEnable = true;
private void OnEnable()
{
    if (isFirstEnable)
    {
        GameEvents.OnOpenThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(true);
        GameEvents.OnCloseThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        isFirstEnable = false;
        return;
    }
    GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: true);
}
```

---

## Decoupling

- **GameEvents** for cross-system communication — `partial class`, each phase extends
- **GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes** — `Action<IPyObject>` not `Action<FarmObject>`
- **`[SerializeField]`** for same-GO references — always private
- **Owner chain** for parent-child references
- **Singleton reads ONLY** for queries, NEVER cross-system commands
- **Separate Open/Close events** per UI panel — NOT a toggle
- **ZERO `FindObjectOfType`** in MonoBehaviours
- **`// purpose:`** on EVERY `.Raise...()` and `+=` subscription

### Cross-Phase File Changes

- **`GameEvents` (static class)** → use `partial class`. Each phase extends in own `0-Core/GameEvents.cs`
- **MonoBehaviours with `[SerializeField]`** → must be directly modified. Document in GUIDE.md
- **Rule:** prefer `partial` extend over direct modify

---

## No Defensive Null Checks

Let it crash. The crash is traceable.
**Exception:** `?.Invoke()` on GameEvents (events can have zero subscribers).

---

## Unity Lifecycle Order

```
Awake() → OnEnable() → Start() → Update() → LateUpdate() → OnDisable() → OnDestroy()
```

- Awake + OnEnable fire together BEFORE Start
- Start runs ONCE — never again even if re-enabled
- OnEnable fires EVERY enable
- Execution order across GOs is NOT guaranteed → use `isFirstEnable`

---

## C# Features (Allowed)

- `$""` string interpolation, `?.`, `??`, `=>`, ternary `? :`
- LINQ: `.Select()`, `.Where()`, `.Any()`, `.All()`, `.First()`, `.OrderBy()`, `.ToDictionary()`
- Custom extensions: `.map()`, `.find()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, `.toggle()`, `.getRandom()`
- `var` for obvious types

**Not allowed:** `async/await` (use coroutines), `Span<T>`, `ValueTuple` deconstruction

---

## LEGO Independence

Each system works **standalone first**, connects later via GameEvents. A script must **never** require an unfinished system to compile.

---

## Vertical Slice Tests

Two levels per system:
1. **Data-level** — test DataService logic without UI (`DEBUG_Check` — plain `new` instance)
2. **UI-level** — test full system flow with keyboard shortcuts

Each test is a standalone scene. If a test requires another system, fix the architecture.

---

## 10 Common Pitfalls

1. **SetActive cascading** — parent disables ALL children. Use siblings for independent panels.
2. **Scene instance vs prefab** — drag from Hierarchy, not Project panel.
3. **raycastTarget** — ghost=false, slot bg=true, icon=false.
4. **Swap contents not objects** — swap `.item` field, not entire slot.
5. **Missing layers** — `LayerMask.NameToLayer` returns -1 silently.
6. **LayerMask at Nothing** — defaults to 0, all raycasts return empty.
7. **Cross-phase mods not applied** — compile error or silent failure.
8. **Dual collider setup** — trigger + physical on separate child GOs.
9. **Static lists surviving domain reload** — second Play press has stale null refs.
10. **Execution order attributes** — `[DefaultExecutionOrder]` on managers, don't remove.

---

## 23 Common Agent Mistakes

1. Any `FindObjectOfType` in MonoBehaviours → use `[SerializeField]`, Owner chain, or GameEvents
2. Public methods nobody calls externally → audit every public method
3. Missing `// purpose:` on Raise/Subscribe calls → mandatory on every single one
4. Tight coupling (Script A directly calls Script B across systems) → decouple via GameEvents
5. Defensive null checks on inspector refs → let it crash
6. RefreshAll() in Update() (polling) → make it event-driven
7. DataService that needs Unity physics/lifecycle → question if it should be a DataService
8. Missing `isFirstEnable` pattern on SubManagers → every SubManager MUST use it
9. Toggle instead of separate Open/Close events → every UI panel needs SEPARATE events
10. Using `Input.GetKeyDown` directly in SubManagers → SubManagers don't handle input
11. Methods on SO_ classes → SO_ = pure data, zero methods. Move to consumer
12. Singleton in `3-MonoBehaviours/` instead of `1-Managers/` → Singleton<T> = always 1-Managers/
13. Collections left in MonoBehaviour instead of DataService → extract to DataService
14. Missing PhaseXLOG methods → every collection gets its own LOG method
15. Missing `RefreshAllRequired()` in Orchestrator → after every data mutation, refresh UI
16. Missing `DOC__X__Field` in Orchestrator → must track data→Field_ mapping
17. Not using user's custom extensions → use `.map()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, etc.
18. Bridge script placed on the portable side → Bridges live on the non-portable side
19. `Interface/` subfolder not used when system has 2+ interfaces → use subfolder
20. Using `FindObjectOfType` instead of `FindObjectsByType` → obsolete in Unity 6000.3
21. Wrong bridge pattern chosen → match data-flow direction and frequency
22. `#region Private Fields` instead of `#region private API` → correct name is `private API`
23. Field_ uses `[SerializeField]` instead of public fields → Field_ uses public refs set by Orchestrator

---

## fwr-Specific Patterns

### Thread-Safe Communication

The original fwr architecture uses a background thread for Python execution. Communication between threads:

```
Background thread (Simulation) → ConcurrentQueue<SoundCall> → Main thread (SoundManager.Update dequeues)
Background thread (Simulation) → ConcurrentQueue<VFXCall>  → Main thread (VFXManager.Update dequeues)
Background thread (Simulation) → lock(lockSimulation)       → Main thread (reads grid state safely)
```

**Rule:** GameEvents fire on main thread ONLY. Background thread enqueues to ConcurrentQueue. Main thread dequeues in Update() and fires GameEvents.

### GPU Instanced Rendering

Farm objects are NOT individual GameObjects. `FarmRenderer` uses `Graphics.DrawMeshInstanced` with per-instance matrices and material property blocks. This means:
- No per-object MonoBehaviours on farm tiles
- Grid state is pure C# data (GridManager = DataService-like)
- FarmRenderer reads grid state each frame and submits draw calls

### Python Interpreter Pipeline

```
Source code → Tokenizer (regex) → Token linked list
           → Parser (recursive descent) → AST Node tree
           → Execution (IEnumerable<double> yield-coroutines) → SideEffect enum
           → Farm.ApplySideEffect() → grid/drone state changes
```

All pure C# — testable without Unity. Phase-C tests can parse Python code and verify AST structure without any scene.

---

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Name | Weight | Difficulty | Description |
|-------|------|--------|------------|-------------|
| All | Shared Infrastructure | 5% | Easy | Singleton, GameEvents, UIManager, Utils, enums |
| A | Core Data + Python Interfaces + SOs | 10% | Easy-Medium | All SO_ definitions, IPyObject hierarchy, ItemBlock, enums |
| B | Python Type System | 10% | Medium | PyNumber, PyString, PyList, PyDict, etc. — all IPyObject implementations |
| C | Python Interpreter | 15% | Complex | Tokenizer, Parser, 23 AST Node types, Scope, ProgramState |
| D | Grid, Farm Core, Drone | 12% | Complex | GridManager, Farm, FarmRenderer, Drone, GPU instancing |
| E | Execution Engine + Simulation | 12% | Complex | Execution, BuiltinFunctions, Simulation, MainSim, TimerManager |
| F | Plants & Special Farm Objects | 8% | Medium | Bush, Cactus, Sunflower, Pumpkin (groups), Tree, Hedge/Maze, Dinosaur/Snake |
| G | IDE / Code Editor Workspace | 12% | Complex | CodeWindow, Workspace, CodeCompleter, SearchBox, BreakPoints, Tooltips |
| H | Economy UI, Research, Achievements | 8% | Medium | Inventory, ResearchMenu, UnlockBox, Leaderboard, Steam integration |
| I | Save/Load, Menu, Options, Audio/VFX | 8% | Medium-Complex | Saver, Menu, OptionMenu, ThemeManager, SoundManager, VFXManager |

---

## System-Based Architecture (`_-Systems/`)

`_-Systems/` is the PRIMARY structure. Each feature with 3+ tightly coupled files gets its own folder.

### System Independence Rules

- Systems communicate ONLY via interfaces and GameEvents
- GameEvents use interfaces, not concrete classes
- Portable systems own their interfaces — copy folder → it compiles

### Observable System Shapes

| Shape | Emoji | What It Does | Portable? |
|-------|-------|-------------|-----------|
| **Spider** | 🕷️ | DEFINES interfaces, RECEIVES implementations | ✅ Always |
| **Hunter** | 🔍 | DEFINES interfaces, actively scans via GetComponent | ✅ Always |
| **Adapter** | 🔌 | IMPLEMENTS interfaces defined by others | ✅ If interface-only deps |
| **Broadcaster/Listener** | 📡 | Fires or subscribes to GameEvents only | ✅ Always |
| **Infrastructure** | 🌍 | Used by ALL, doesn't count as dependency | N/A |

### Portability Levels

L0 (zero deps, copy → compiles) → L1+ (each import from another `_-Systems/` = +1)

**FREE (doesn't count as dependency):** GameEvents, Singleton<T>, UIManager, Utils.*, GlobalEnumsX, all phase-All/ infra.

### Cross-System Communication (preference order)

1. **GameEvents** — fire-and-forget, zero coupling
2. **Interface / DIP** — caller-defines abstraction
3. **Bridge** — runtime context push (lives on non-portable side)
4. **[SerializeField]** — same-GO / parent-child only, intra-system

### Anti-patterns (NEVER use)

- ❌ `FindObjectOfType<ConcreteClass>()` in MonoBehaviours
- ❌ `Singleton<X>.Ins.DoSomething()` (commands) — use GameEvents
- ❌ Direct class import across `_-Systems/` folders
- ❌ Concrete class in GameEvent signatures

---

## Public API Obsession

**Before making ANY method public, ask: "does another script ACTUALLY call this?"**
If no → private. If only subclasses → protected.

**After writing every script, audit: can any public method be made private/protected?**

---

## UIManager — Grows Per Phase

Three responsibilities:
1. **Report state** — `isAnyMenuOpen` property
2. **Close all panels** — `CloseAllSubManager()` fires all Close events
3. **Route keyboard** — ESC/Tab priority routing

UIManager NEVER calls SetActive on SubManagers — fires Close events, SubManagers disable themselves.

---

## Tight Coupling Red Flags

```
❌ FindObjectOfType<AnyType>()
❌ Singleton<X>.Ins.DoSomething()        → use GameEvents.Raise...()
❌ Script A calls Script B directly       → use GameEvents
❌ Concrete class in GameEvent signature → use interface
```

---

## Splitting Rules

```
SPLIT — Farm.cs (600+ lines, 4 responsibilities):
  → GridManager (grid state), Farm (coordination), FarmRenderer (GPU drawing)

SPLIT — MainSim.cs (manages everything):
  → MainSim (singleton root), Simulation (background thread), Execution (AST runner)

DON'T SPLIT — small Py* types (20-60 lines, one purpose each):
  → already one sentence

DON'T SPLIT — AST Node types (30-60 lines each):
  → each represents exactly one Python construct
```

---

## fwr User's Coding Style

> To be filled when handTyped(latest)/ is available.

**Custom utility extensions:**
```
.map()           → same as .Select()
.find()          → same as .FirstOrDefault()
.all()           → same as .All()
.sum()           → same as .Sum()
.gc<T>()         → same as .GetComponent<T>()
.toggle(value)   → same as .SetActive(value)
.destroyLeaves() → destroy all child GOs
.getRandom()     → pick random element
.colorTag()      → wrap string in rich text color
.repeat(n)       → repeat char/string n times
.HasTag()        → enum-based CompareTag (TagType)
.SetTag()        → enum-based tag assignment
.ToNSJson()      → JSON serialize
.GetOrCreate()   → dictionary get-or-create
```

# New Agent — Start Here (fwr: The Farmer Was Replaced)

> **Current target: phase-All — Shared Infrastructure**
> Update this line when moving to the next phase.

## First Steps — Before Any Phase

### Step 0: Bootstrap the Learn Folder

```
learn/
├── GOAL.md              → architecture bible for fwr
├── NewAgent.md          → this file — agent instructions + delivery checklist
├── ARCHITECTURE.md      → comprehensive analysis of the original source (21 systems)
├── PhaseMap.md          → 10 phases (All + A through I), ~185 files total
├── StructureMap.md      → DataService specs per phase
├── SystemPortabilityMap.md → L0/L1+ classification per system
├── SystemIsolationAnalysis.md → cross-system audit
├── CoverageMap.md       → every source file → which phase covers it
├── Estimate.md          → timeline calibrated from actual typing speed
├── OptionalFeatures.md  → features outside 100% scope
├── surfer.md            → reasoning log (append after each agent prompt)
├── handTyped(latest)/   → user's ground-truth hand-typed code
└── phase-All/           → shared scripts (Singleton, GameEvents, UIManager, etc.)
```

---

## First Prompt (copy-paste into new conversation)

```
Read these files in this order:
1. learn/GOAL.md — all architecture rules, folder structure, naming conventions, decoupling, vertical slice tests
2. learn/PhaseMap.md — [PHASE_X] section (files, modifications, vertical slice tests)
3. learn/StructureMap.md — DataService specs per phase (exact collections, methods, nested types)
4. learn/handTyped(latest)/ — my ACTUAL hand-typed code (ground truth for coding style)
5. learn/handTyped(latest)/ and its subfolders — reference for architecture patterns
6. Scripts/Core/ + Scripts/Utils/ — original source for [PHASE_X] files
7. Completed phase folders in learn/ — reference for established _-Systems/ architecture

End goal: 100% main-source functionality with clean architecture — systems communicate via interfaces + GameEvents, making them portable to future Unity projects.

Now build [PHASE_X] with:
- 100% main source functionality + portable systems
- _-Systems/ architecture: each feature = self-contained folder with ALL code
- System independence: communicate via interfaces + GameEvents only
- GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes
- Thread-safe: background thread → ConcurrentQueue → main thread → GameEvents
- GPU instanced rendering: grid state = pure C# data, FarmRenderer reads + draws
- Python interpreter: Tokenizer → Parser → AST → Execution → SideEffect → Farm
- [AddComponentMenu("fwr/Category/ClassName")] on every MonoBehaviour
- partial GameEvents in phase-X/0-Core/GameEvents.cs
- Vertical slice tests per system
```

## Documentation Voice & Tone

- **GUIDE.md** — beginner-friendly, conversational
- **FLOW.md** — story-style data flows. **bold** = visible change, *italic* = context, `code` = exact reference
- **Script summaries** — first person "I" (the script talks to you)
- **Test instructions** — assume the reader has never opened Unity

For each phase:

- What it looks like when running

- Header: `# SystemName — L{n} ✅/❌`

- FREE Requirements table

- `#region` blocks in correct order
- `/// <summary>` on every class and method

| `Scripts/Utils/` | Original utility/type source | Always for the current phase |

---

## Key Rules (Quick Reference)

- **One sentence per script.** Split if it doesn't fit.
- **`_-Systems/` is PRIMARY.** ALL feature code goes inside.
- **System independence.** Interfaces + GameEvents only across systems.
- **GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes.**
- **Thread-safe.** Background thread → ConcurrentQueue → main thread dispatch.
- **GPU instanced.** No per-tile GameObjects. GridManager = pure C# data.
- **`[AddComponentMenu("fwr/Category/ClassName")]`** on every MonoBehaviour.
- **No `FindObjectOfType`** — zero tolerance.
- **No C# property accessors** — `Get.../Set...()` only.
- **`isFirstEnable`** on ALL SubManagers.
- **Separate Open/Close events** per UI panel.
- **DataService + PhaseXLOG + GetSnapShotForTest()** for EVERY collection.
- **`// purpose:`** on every Raise and Subscribe.
- **No defensive null checks.** Let it crash.
- **Public API audit** after every script.

---

## Common Mistakes The Agent WILL Make

1. `FindObjectOfType` in MonoBehaviours → use `[SerializeField]`, Owner chain, or GameEvents
2. Public methods nobody calls externally → audit every public
3. Missing `// purpose:` on Raise/Subscribe → mandatory on every one
4. Tight coupling across systems → decouple via GameEvents
5. Defensive null checks on inspector refs → let it crash
6. RefreshAll() in Update() → make it event-driven
7. DataService needing Unity lifecycle → question if it should be DataService
8. Missing `isFirstEnable` on SubManagers → every SubManager MUST use it
9. Toggle instead of Open/Close → separate events
10. Input in SubManagers → SubManagers don't handle input
11. Methods on SO_ → SO_ = pure data, zero methods
12. Singleton in `3-MonoBehaviours/` → Singleton<T> = always `1-Managers/`
13. Collections in MonoBehaviour → extract to DataService
14. Missing PhaseXLOG methods → every collection gets LOG
15. Missing `RefreshAllRequired()` → after every mutation
16. Missing `DOC__X__Field` → Orchestrators must track mapping
17. Not using custom extensions → use `.map()`, `.gc<T>()`, etc.
18. Bridge on portable side → Bridges live on non-portable side
19. `Interface/` subfolder missing → use when 2+ interfaces
20. `FindObjectOfType` vs `FindObjectsByType` → obsolete in Unity 6000.3
21. Wrong bridge pattern → match data-flow direction
22. `#region Private Fields` → correct name is `private API`
23. Field_ with `[SerializeField]` → Field_ uses public refs

---

## MANDATORY Patterns

### `// purpose:` on EVERY Raise and Subscribe

```csharp
// purpose: main thread dispatches queued sound effects
GameEvents.RaiseSoundEffectRequested(soundType);

// purpose: UI updates when simulation completes
GameEvents.OnSimulationCompleted += HandleSimulationCompleted;
```

### `isFirstEnable` on ALL SubManagers

```csharp
bool isFirstEnable = true;
private void OnEnable()
{
    if (isFirstEnable)
    {
        GameEvents.OnOpenThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(true);
        GameEvents.OnCloseThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        isFirstEnable = false;
        return;
    }
    GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: true);
}
```

### Separate Open/Close Events Per UI Panel

```csharp
// phase-X/0-Core/GameEvents.cs
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when research menu opens >>
    public static event Action OnOpenResearchMenu;
    public static void RaiseOpenResearchMenu() { ... }
    // << when research menu opens

    // when research menu closes >>
    public static event Action OnCloseResearchMenu;
    public static void RaiseCloseResearchMenu() { ... }
    // << when research menu closes
}
```

---

## fwr-Specific Architecture Notes

### Thread Model

```
MainSim.Start() → creates Simulation on background thread
Simulation → runs Execution → dispatches SideEffects to Farm
Farm → enqueues SoundCall/VFXCall to ConcurrentQueue
Main thread → SoundManager/VFXManager.Update() dequeues and processes
```

- **All GameEvents fire on main thread ONLY**
- Background thread reads/writes grid state under `lock(lockSimulation)`
- Main thread reads grid state for rendering under same lock

### Python Interpreter

Fully pure C# — no Unity dependencies:
- `Tokenizer` → regex-based lexer
- `Parser` → recursive descent
- `Node` subclasses → AST
- `Execution` → `IEnumerable<double>` yield-based coroutines
- `BuiltinFunctions` → maps Python names to `SideEffect` enum values

All testable via `new` without any scene.

### GPU Instanced Rendering

- No individual GameObjects for crops/tiles
- `FarmRenderer.Update()` calls `Graphics.DrawMeshInstanced(mesh, 0, material, matrices)`
- Grid state lives in `GridManager` (pure C# dictionaries)
- Material property blocks handle per-instance colors

### Multi-Drone Support

- `List<Drone>` managed by `Farm`
- Each drone has independent position, direction, hat
- `Execution` runs separate `ProgramState` per drone
- Python code accesses `get_world_size()`, `get_pos_x()`, `get_pos_y()` per-drone

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/fwr/OptionalFeatures.md ==== -->
# fwr — OptionalFeatures

> Features outside 100% main-source scope. Polish, extras, external packages.
> These are NOT required for main-source fidelity — they enhance the rebuild.

---

## External Package Integration Candidates

### 1. Feel / MMFeedbacks (Nice Games Studio)

**What it replaces:** Manual LeanTween calls for UI feedback, item pickup effects, button press juice.

**Integration points:**
- `ItemEffect.cs` → replace LeanTween scale/alpha with MMFeedbacks
- `FlashingButton.cs` → replace manual color pulse with MMF_Feedback
- `HatPopup.cs` → replace LeanTween popup animation with MMFeedbacks sequence
- `PopupEffect.cs` → replace manual text popup with MMFeedbacks

**Effort:** ~4h to replace all LeanTween UI calls with MMFeedbacks.

**Decision:** Skip unless user wants juice polish. LeanTween works fine.

### 2. Odin Inspector (Sirenix)

**What it replaces:** Custom editor drawers, inspector validation, SO_ editing.

**Integration points:**
- All SO_ types → better inspector editing with Odin attributes
- OptionSO hierarchy → validated inspector with Odin
- ColorTheme.cs → color picker improvements

**Decision:** Skip. Standard Unity inspector is sufficient for fwr's SO_ types.

### 3. UniTask (Cysharp)

**What it replaces:** Coroutines for async operations.

**Integration points:**
- `Saver.cs` → async save/load
- `SteamLeaderboard.cs` → async Steam API calls
- `ResourceManager.cs` → async resource loading

**Decision:** Skip. Coroutines are the established pattern. UniTask adds complexity.

---

## Polish Features (In-Source but Low Priority)

### 1. CreateItemTextures.cs — Editor Texture Capture

**What:** Editor tool that positions 3D models in front of a camera and captures render textures as item icon atlases.

**Priority:** Polish. Items work with manually assigned sprites.

**Effort:** ~2h if needed.

### 2. Steam Rich Presence (SteamStatsLoop.cs)

**What:** Periodic updates to Steam rich presence (shows farm size, items collected in friends list).

**Priority:** Polish. Game works without it.

**Effort:** ~1h (already mapped to phase-I).

### 3. DLC Detection (DLCBanner.cs)

**What:** Checks if Dinosaur Hat DLC is owned via Steam API, shows promotional banner if not.

**Priority:** Polish. Game works without DLC awareness.

**Effort:** ~30min.

### 4. Localization (Localizer.cs + TMPTextLocalizer.cs)

**What:** Font fallback chain for Japanese/Korean/Chinese characters. Not full i18n.

**Priority:** Important for non-Latin scripts. Most text is in-game Python keywords (English).

**Effort:** Already mapped to phase-E. ~1h.

---

## Potential Enhancements Beyond Source

### 1. Test Runner Integration

**What:** NUnit test project that tests DataServices and pure C# classes automatically.

**Integration points:**
- GridDataService → unit tests for grid operations
- ItemBlock → unit tests for arithmetic
- Tokenizer → unit tests for lexing
- Parser → unit tests for parsing

**Effort:** ~8h for comprehensive test suite.

**Decision:** Optional. Manual DEBUG_Check scripts serve the same purpose with more visibility.

### 2. Hot Reload for Python Code

**What:** File watcher that auto-compiles Python code on save (like VS Code live reload).

**Integration points:**
- `Workspace.cs` → watch code files for external changes
- `CodeWindow.cs` → reload on file change

**Effort:** ~4h.

**Decision:** Optional. The original game doesn't have this.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/fwr/PhaseMap.md ==== -->
# fwr — PhaseMap

> All phases, files, modifications, vertical slice tests, gap audit.
> Each phase depends only on earlier phases — never forward.

---

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Domain | Files | Difficulty | Weight% |
|-------|--------|-------|------------|---------|
| phase-All | Shared infrastructure | 8 | Easy | 5% |
| phase-A | Core Data, Python Interfaces, ScriptableObjects | 24 | Easy-Medium | 10% |
| phase-B | Python Type System | 21 | Medium | 10% |
| phase-C | Python Interpreter (Lexer, Parser, AST Nodes) | 25 | Complex | 15% |
| phase-D | Grid, Farm Core, Drone | 18 | Complex | 12% |
| phase-E | Execution Engine, BuiltinFunctions, Simulation | 14 | Complex | 12% |
| phase-F | Plants & Special Farm Objects | 16 | Medium | 8% |
| phase-G | IDE / Code Editor Workspace | 22 | Complex | 12% |
| phase-H | Economy UI, Research, Achievements, Leaderboard | 18 | Medium | 8% |
| phase-I | Save/Load, Main Menu, Options, Theme, Audio/VFX, Polish | 19 | Medium-Complex | 8% |

**Total: ~185 files across 9 phases + phase-All**

---

## Phase Dependency Order (DAG)

```
phase-All ← phase-A ← phase-B ← phase-C ← phase-D ← phase-E ← phase-F
                                                 ↑               ↗
                                           phase-G ← phase-H ← phase-I
```

All arrows point LEFT (to earlier phases). No forward references.

**Verification:**
- phase-A imports from: phase-All only
- phase-B imports from: phase-A (IPyObject interfaces)
- phase-C imports from: phase-A (SideEffect, GridDirection), phase-B (Py* types)
- phase-D imports from: phase-A (FarmObjectSO, ItemBlock, SideEffect, GridDirection), phase-B (IPyObject)
- phase-E imports from: phase-A through phase-D (all prior)
- phase-F imports from: phase-D (FarmObject, Growable, GridManager), phase-A (ItemBlock)
- phase-G imports from: phase-A (IPyObject), phase-B (Py*), phase-C (Parser, Tokenizer, Node)
- phase-H imports from: phase-A (UnlockSO, ItemBlock, ItemSO), phase-D (Farm)
- phase-I imports from: all prior phases (wires everything together)

---

## phase-All — Shared Infrastructure

**Domain boundary:** Foundation scripts ALL phases depend on — never duplicated.

| `GameEvents.cs` | `0-Core/` | Core shared events (OnMenuStateChanged, OnCloseAllSubManagers) |
| `UIManager.cs` | `1-Managers/` | Reports menu state, CloseAllSubManager(), keyboard routing |
| `GlobalEnumsAll.cs` | `2-Data/Enums/` | TagType enum + shared enums (grows across phases) |
