<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/0-Core/GameEvents.cs ==== -->
using System;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I deliver messages between systems. Each phase adds events via partial class.
/// Every Raise logs subscriber count for debugging.
/// Core events: OnMenuStateChanged, OnCloseAllSubManagers. </summary>
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when menu state changes >>
    public static event Action<bool> OnMenuStateChanged;
    public static void RaiseMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        // purpose: tell PlayerMovement, PlayerCamera, UIManager that a menu opened/closed
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnMenuStateChanged), OnMenuStateChanged);
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged?
            .Invoke(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }
    // << when menu state changes

    // when all sub-managers must close >>
    public static event Action OnCloseAllSubManagers;
    public static void RaiseCloseAllSubManagers()
    {
        // purpose: UIManager tells every SubManager to close its panel
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnCloseAllSubManagers), OnCloseAllSubManagers);
        GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers?
            .Invoke();
    }
    // << when all sub-managers must close

    // when game paused >>
    public static event Action OnGamePaused;
    public static void RaiseGamePaused()
    {
        // purpose: freeze game-time systems when pause menu opens
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnGamePaused), OnGamePaused);
        GameEvents.OnGamePaused?
            .Invoke();
    }
    // << when game paused

    // when game unpaused >>
    public static event Action OnGameUnpaused;
    public static void RaiseGameUnpaused()
    {
        // purpose: resume game-time systems when pause menu closes
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnGameUnpaused), OnGameUnpaused);
        GameEvents.OnGameUnpaused?
            .Invoke();
    }
    // << when game unpaused

    /// <summary> Logs how many subscribers an event has — helps catch missing/extra subscriptions. </summary>
    static void LogSubscribersCount(string name, Delegate del)
    {
        int count = del?.GetInvocationList()?.Length ?? 0;
        Debug.Log($"[GameEvents] {name} raised for -> {count} subscribers".colorTag("lime"));
    }
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/0-Core/Singleton.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I am the generic singleton base. First instance wins — duplicates are destroyed.
/// All Managers extend me. Access via Singleton&lt;T&gt;.Ins. </summary>
public abstract class Singleton<T> : MonoBehaviour where T : MonoBehaviour
{
    #region Private Fields

    // no private fields — Ins is static

    #endregion
    #region Unity Lifecycle

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        if (Ins == null) Ins = this as T;
        else if (Ins != this)
        {
            Debug.Log($"{typeof(T)} singleton already exists, destroying duplicate");
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }

    #endregion
    #region Public API

    public static T Ins { get; private set; }

    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/1-Managers/DataManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I hold shared visual data: materials, layer masks, colors.
/// Any phase can read these via Singleton&lt;DataManager&gt;.Ins.
/// Source: UIManager had MoneyTextColor, ResearchTicketsTextColor, GrayscaleImageMaterial. </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("minemgl/Managers/DataManager")]
public class DataManager : Singleton<DataManager>
{
    #region Inspector Fields

    [SerializeField] Color _moneyTextColor;
    [SerializeField] Color _researchTicketsTextColor;
    [SerializeField] Material _grayscaleImageMaterial;

    #endregion
    #region Public API

    public Color GetMoneyTextColor() { return _moneyTextColor; }
    public Color GetResearchTicketsTextColor() { return _researchTicketsTextColor; }
    public Material GetGrayscaleImageMaterial() { return _grayscaleImageMaterial; }

    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/1-Managers/UIManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I report menu state, close all panels, and route keyboard input with priority.
/// Every SubManager calls me to announce open/close. I manage cursor lock and background blur.
/// Events used: GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged, GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers. </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("minemgl/Managers/UIManager")]
public class UIManager : Singleton<UIManager>
{
    #region Inspector Fields

    [SerializeField] GameObject _hudObject;
    [SerializeField] GameObject _backgroundBlur;

    #endregion
    #region Private Fields

    bool _isAnyMenuOpen;
    bool _hudIsHidden;

    #endregion
    #region Unity Lifecycle

    void LateUpdate()
    {
        // → route ESC key
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape))
        {
            if (_isAnyMenuOpen)
            {
                // purpose: close all menus when ESC pressed while in a menu
                GameEvents.RaiseCloseAllSubManagers();
            }
            else
            {
                // purpose: open pause menu when ESC pressed with no menu open
                GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(true);
            }
        }

        // → cursor state
        if (_isAnyMenuOpen)
        {
            Cursor.lockState = CursorLockMode.None;
            Cursor.visible = true;
        }
        else
        {
            Cursor.lockState = CursorLockMode.Locked;
            Cursor.visible = false;
        }

        // → background blur
        _backgroundBlur.SetActive(_isAnyMenuOpen);
    }

    void OnEnable()
    {
        // purpose: track when any SubManager opens/closes a menu
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged += HandleMenuStateChanged;
    }

    void OnDisable()
    {
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged -= HandleMenuStateChanged;
    }

    #endregion
    #region Public API

    public bool GetIsAnyMenuOpen() { return _isAnyMenuOpen; }

    public void CloseAllSubManager()
    {
        // → tells every SubManager to close via event
        // purpose: centralized close-all for ESC, inventory toggle, etc.
        GameEvents.RaiseCloseAllSubManagers();
        SetMenuState(false);
    }

    public void SetHudVisible(bool visible)
    {
        _hudIsHidden = !visible;
        _hudObject.SetActive(visible);
    }

    #endregion
    #region Private API

    void HandleMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        // → update internal state when a SubManager announces menu change
        SetMenuState(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }

    void SetMenuState(bool isOpen)
    {
        _isAnyMenuOpen = isOpen;
        if (isOpen)
        {
            _hudObject.SetActive(false);
        }
        else
        {
            _hudObject.SetActive(!_hudIsHidden);
        }
    }

    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/3-MonoBehaviours/Physics/BasePhysicsObject.cs ==== -->
using System.Collections.Generic;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I am the shared base for all physics objects on conveyors.
/// I cache my Rigidbody, track which ConveyorBelts touch me, and accumulate conveyor velocity.
/// Children: OrePiece, BaseSellableItem, BuildingCrate, etc. </summary>
[AddComponentMenu("minemgl/Physics/BasePhysicsObject")]
public class BasePhysicsObject : MonoBehaviour
{
    #region Inspector Fields

    // no inspector fields — all runtime state

    #endregion
    #region Private Fields

    public const float STANDARD_LINEAR_DAMPING = 0.2f;
    public const float STANDARD_ANGULAR_DAMPING = 0.05f;

    [HideInInspector] public Vector3 SumVelocity;
    [HideInInspector] public float BestY;
    [HideInInspector] public int Count;
    [HideInInspector] public bool RetainY;

    List<ConveyorBelt> _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis = new List<ConveyorBelt>();
    Rigidbody _rb;

    #endregion
    #region Unity Lifecycle

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        // → cache rigidbody and set damping defaults
        _rb = GetComponent<Rigidbody>();
        if (_rb != null)
        {
            _rb.linearDamping = STANDARD_LINEAR_DAMPING;
            _rb.angularDamping = STANDARD_ANGULAR_DAMPING;
        }
    }

    protected virtual void OnEnable()
    {
        // → register with ConveyorBeltManager for batch velocity updates
        ConveyorBeltManager.Register(this);
    }

    protected virtual void OnDisable()
    {
        // → unregister from ConveyorBeltManager
        ConveyorBeltManager.Unregister(this);
    }

    #endregion
    #region Public API

    public Rigidbody GetRb() { return _rb; }

    public void ClearTouchingConveyorBelts()
    {
        // → tell each belt to remove me, then clear my list
        foreach (var belt in _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis)
        {
            belt.RemovePhysicsObject(this);
        }
        _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis.Clear();
    }

    public void AddTouchingConveyorBelt(ConveyorBelt belt)
    {
        _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis.Add(belt);
    }

    public bool IsOnAnyConveyor()
    {
        return _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis.Count > 0;
    }

    public void RemoveTouchingConveyorBelt(ConveyorBelt belt)
    {
        _conveyorBeltsTouchingThis.Remove(belt);
    }

    public void AddConveyorVelocity(Vector3 velocity, bool retainY)
    {
        // → accumulate velocity from multiple overlapping conveyors
        if (Count == 0)
            SumVelocity = velocity;
        else
            SumVelocity += velocity;

        if (velocity.y > BestY) BestY = velocity.y;
        Count++;
        if (retainY) RetainY = true;
    }

    public void ResetAccum()
    {
        SumVelocity = default;
        BestY = 0f;
        Count = 0;
        RetainY = false;
    }

    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/3-MonoBehaviours/Physics/BaseSellableItem.cs ==== -->

/// Children: OrePiece, processed items. </summary>
[AddComponentMenu("minemgl/Physics/BaseSellableItem")]
public class BaseSellableItem : BasePhysicsObject
{
    #region Inspector Fields

    public float BaseSellValue = 1f;

    #endregion
    #region Public API

    public virtual float GetSellValue()
    {
        return BaseSellValue;
    }

    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/4-Utils/TimeSince.cs ==== -->
using System;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> Elapsed-time tracker. Assign 0f to start, then compare: if (timeSince > 2f).
/// Source: direct port from original TimeSince.cs. </summary>
public struct TimeSince : IEquatable<TimeSince>
{
    float _time;

    public float Absolute => _time;
    public float Relative => this;

    public static implicit operator float(TimeSince ts) => Time.time - ts._time;

    public static implicit operator TimeSince(float ts) =>
        new TimeSince { _time = Time.time - ts };

    public static bool operator <(in TimeSince ts, float f) => ts.Relative < f;
    public static bool operator >(in TimeSince ts, float f) => ts.Relative > f;
    public static bool operator <=(in TimeSince ts, float f) => ts.Relative <= f;
    public static bool operator >=(in TimeSince ts, float f) => ts.Relative >= f;

    public static bool operator <(in TimeSince ts, int f) => ts.Relative < (float)f;
    public static bool operator >(in TimeSince ts, int f) => ts.Relative > (float)f;
    public static bool operator <=(in TimeSince ts, int f) => ts.Relative <= (float)f;
    public static bool operator >=(in TimeSince ts, int f) => ts.Relative >= (float)f;

    public override string ToString() => $"{Relative}";

    public static bool operator ==(TimeSince left, TimeSince right) => left.Equals(right);
    public static bool operator !=(TimeSince left, TimeSince right) => !(left == right);

    public override bool Equals(object obj) => obj is TimeSince o && Equals(o);
    public readonly bool Equals(TimeSince o) => _time == o._time;
    public override readonly int GetHashCode() => _time.GetHashCode();
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/4-Utils/TimeUntil.cs ==== -->
using System;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> Countdown timer. Assign duration: timeUntil = 5f. Check: if (timeUntil) means time is up.
/// Also: timeUntil.Fraction for 0→1 progress. Source: direct port from original TimeUntil.cs. </summary>
public struct TimeUntil : IEquatable<TimeUntil>
{
    float _time;
    float _startTime;

    public float Absolute => _time;
    public float Relative => this;
    public float Passed => Time.time - _startTime;
    public float Fraction => Math.Clamp((Time.time - _startTime) / (_time - _startTime), 0f, 1f);

    public static implicit operator bool(TimeUntil ts) => Time.time >= ts._time;
    public static implicit operator float(TimeUntil ts) => ts._time - Time.time;

    public static bool operator <(in TimeUntil ts, float f) => ts.Relative < f;
    public static bool operator >(in TimeUntil ts, float f) => ts.Relative > f;
    public static bool operator <=(in TimeUntil ts, float f) => ts.Relative <= f;
    public static bool operator >=(in TimeUntil ts, float f) => ts.Relative >= f;

    public static bool operator <(in TimeUntil ts, int f) => ts.Relative < (float)f;
    public static bool operator >(in TimeUntil ts, int f) => ts.Relative > (float)f;
    public static bool operator <=(in TimeUntil ts, int f) => ts.Relative <= (float)f;
    public static bool operator >=(in TimeUntil ts, int f) => ts.Relative >= (float)f;

    public static implicit operator TimeUntil(float ts) =>
        new TimeUntil { _time = Time.time + ts, _startTime = Time.time };

    public override string ToString() => $"{Relative}";

    public static bool operator ==(TimeUntil left, TimeUntil right) => left.Equals(right);
    public static bool operator !=(TimeUntil left, TimeUntil right) => !(left == right);

    public override readonly bool Equals(object obj) => obj is TimeUntil o && Equals(o);
    public readonly bool Equals(TimeUntil o) => _time == o._time;
    public override readonly int GetHashCode() => HashCode.Combine(_time);
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/4-Utils/Utils.cs ==== -->
using System;
using System.Collections;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary> I hold ALL shared utility methods and extensions. One file, one static class.
/// HasTag/SetTag for TagType, .map(), .gc&lt;T&gt;(), vector/math/physics helpers, UniqueQueue, TimeUtil.
/// Source: MathExtensions + Vector3Utils + PhysicsUtils + UniqueQueue + TimeUtil merged here. </summary>
public static class Utils
{
    #region TagType Extensions

    public static bool HasTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag)
    {
        return go.CompareTag(tag.ToString());
    }

    public static bool HasTag(this Component comp, TagType tag)
    {
        return comp.CompareTag(tag.ToString());
    }

    public static void SetTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag)
    {
        go.tag = tag.ToString();
    }

    #endregion
    #region Component Shorthand

    /// <summary> Short for GetComponent&lt;T&gt;() </summary>
    public static T gc<T>(this GameObject go) where T : Component
    {
        return go.GetComponent<T>();
    }

    /// <summary> Short for GetComponent&lt;T&gt;() </summary>
    public static T gc<T>(this Component comp) where T : Component
    {
        return comp.GetComponent<T>();
    }

    #endregion
    #region Collection Extensions

    /// <summary> Functional map — transform each element. </summary>
    public static List<TOut> map<TIn, TOut>(this IList<TIn> list, Func<TIn, TOut> selector)
    {
        var result = new List<TOut>(list.Count);
        for (int i = 0; i < list.Count; i++)
            result.Add(selector(list[i]));
        return result;
    }

    #endregion
    #region GameObject Extensions

    /// <summary> Destroy all child GameObjects. </summary>
    public static void destroyLeaves(this Transform parent)
    {
        for (int i = parent.childCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(parent.GetChild(i).gameObject);
    }

    /// <summary> Toggle active state. </summary>
    public static void toggle(this GameObject go)
    {
        go.SetActive(!go.activeSelf);
    }

    #endregion
    #region String Extensions

    /// <summary> Wraps text in a Unity rich-text color tag. </summary>
    public static string colorTag(this string text, string color)
    {
        return $"<color={color}>{text}</color>";
    }

    #endregion
    #region Vector3 Helpers (from Vector3Utils)

    public static bool IsValid(Vector3 v)
    {
        return float.IsFinite(v.x) && float.IsFinite(v.y) && float.IsFinite(v.z);
    }

    #endregion
    #region Math Helpers (from MathExtensions)

    public static Vector3 RoundVector3(Vector3 v, int decimals = 2)
    {
        return new Vector3(
            (float)Math.Round(v.x, decimals),
            (float)Math.Round(v.y, decimals),
            (float)Math.Round(v.z, decimals));
    }

    public static Vector3 TruncateVector3(Vector3 position)
    {
        return new Vector3(
            Truncate(position.x),
            Truncate(position.y),
            Truncate(position.z));

        static float Truncate(float value) => Mathf.Floor(value * 100f) / 100f;
    }

    #endregion
    #region Physics Helpers (from PhysicsUtils)

    public static void SimpleExplosion(Vector3 position, float radius, float force, float upwardsModifier = 0f)
    {
        // → find all colliders in radius and apply explosion force
        Collider[] hits = Physics.OverlapSphere(position, radius);
        for (int i = 0; i < hits.Length; i++)
        {
            Rigidbody rb = hits[i].attachedRigidbody;
            if (rb != null)
                rb.AddExplosionForce(force, position, radius, upwardsModifier, ForceMode.Impulse);
        }
    }

    public static void IgnoreAllCollisions(GameObject object1, GameObject object2, bool ignore)
    {
        Collider[] aCols = object1.GetComponents<Collider>();
        Collider[] bCols = object2.GetComponents<Collider>();
        IgnoreAllCollisions(aCols, bCols, ignore);
    }

    public static void IgnoreAllCollisions(Collider[] aCols, Collider[] bCols, bool ignore)
    {
        foreach (var a in aCols)
            foreach (var b in bCols)
                Physics.IgnoreCollision(a, b, ignore);
    }

    public static void SetLayerRecursively(GameObject obj, int layer)
    {
        if (obj == null) return;
        obj.layer = layer;
        foreach (Transform child in obj.transform)
            SetLayerRecursively(child.gameObject, layer);
    }

    #endregion
    #region Time Display (from TimeUtil)

    public static string GetDisplaySaveTime(string rawTimestamp)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(rawTimestamp))
            return rawTimestamp ?? string.Empty;

        if (!DateTimeOffset.TryParseExact(rawTimestamp, "o", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture,
            DateTimeStyles.RoundtripKind, out var result))
            return rawTimestamp;

        CultureInfo culture = CultureInfo.CurrentCulture;
        string pattern = culture.DateTimeFormat.LongDatePattern;

        // → strip day-of-week names for compact display
        pattern = Regex.Replace(pattern, @"[\p{Ps}(\[]?\s*dddd\s*[\p{Pe})\]]?[,，]?\s*", "");
        pattern = Regex.Replace(pattern, @"[\p{Ps}(\[]?\s*ddd\s*[\p{Pe})\]]?[,，]?\s*", "");
        pattern = Regex.Replace(pattern, @"\s{2,}", " ").Trim();
        pattern = Regex.Replace(pattern, @"\s*,\s*", ", ");
        pattern = pattern.Trim(',', ' ');

        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(pattern))
            pattern = culture.DateTimeFormat.ShortDatePattern;

        string fullPattern = pattern + " " + culture.DateTimeFormat.ShortTimePattern;
        return result.ToLocalTime().ToString(fullPattern, culture);
    }

    #endregion
    #region Money Formatting

    public static string FormatMoney(float amount)
    {
        return "$" + amount.ToString("N2");
    }

    #endregion
}

/// <summary> A queue that only accepts unique items. Enqueue is O(1) with HashSet backing. </summary>
public class UniqueQueue<T> : IEnumerable<T>
{
    readonly HashSet<T> _set = new HashSet<T>();
    readonly Queue<T> _queue = new Queue<T>();

    public int Count => _queue.Count;

    public void Enqueue(T item)
    {
        if (_set.Add(item))
            _queue.Enqueue(item);
    }

    public T Dequeue()
    {
        T val = _queue.Dequeue();
        _set.Remove(val);
        return val;
    }

    public bool Contains(T item) => _set.Contains(item);

    public void Clear()
    {
        _set.Clear();
        _queue.Clear();
    }

    public IEnumerator<T> GetEnumerator() => _queue.GetEnumerator();
    IEnumerator IEnumerable.GetEnumerator() => _queue.GetEnumerator();
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/7-3D/ANIM.md ==== -->
# ANIM.md — minemgl Animation Conventions

> Grows per phase as new animations are identified.

---

## Animation System

- No dedicated `Animations/` folder — animations are embedded in `.glb` models
- Animator Controllers referenced via `[SerializeField] Animator` in scripts
- No `RuntimeAnimatorController` swapping detected in source

## Known Animation Users

| Script | Animation Purpose | Phase |
|--------|------------------|-------|
| PlayerController → PlayerMovement | Walk/run/idle blend tree (via Animator) | C |
| BaseHeldTool | Tool swing/use animation | D |
| ConveyorRenderer | Belt scroll UV animation | B |
| BlastFurnace | Fire/processing visual state | F |
| CastingFurnace | Mold fill animation | F |
| RollingMill / PipeRoller | Roller spin animation | F |
| StartingElevator | Elevator up/down movement | K2 |
| MainMenuElevator | Menu elevator animation | K1 |
| DetonatorExplosion | Explosion sequence | K2 |
| JackOLantern | Holiday pumpkin animation | K2 |

## Animation Event Patterns

- No `AnimationEvent` callbacks detected in source
- Machine state changes are code-driven (coroutines), not animation-event-driven
- Tool attack timing uses `TimeSince` cooldowns, not animation length

## State Machine Patterns

- Simple on/off states (machines: idle → processing → output)
- No complex blend trees beyond player movement
- Machine animations typically controlled by `SetBool` or `SetTrigger`

---

*Last updated: phase-All init*

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/7-3D/MODEL.md ==== -->
# MODEL.md — minemgl 3D Model Conventions

> Grows per phase as new models are identified.

---

## File Format & Location

- All models use `.glb` format (GLTF binary)
- Located in `Assets/PrefabHierarchyObject/` (flat folder — no subfolders)
- ~100+ model files totaling ~200MB+

## Naming Conventions

| Pattern | Example | Meaning |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `big_{resource}-src.glb` | `big_coal-src.glb`, `big_iron-src.glb` | Large ore node models (source meshes) |
| `small_{resource}.glb` | `small_coal.glb` | Small ore piece models |
| `BuildingCrate{dims}.glb` | `BuildingCrate1x1x1.glb`, `BuildingCrate1x2x2.glb` | Building crate variants by grid size |
| `{MachineName}.glb` | `blastfurnace.glb`, `CastingFurnace.glb` | Machine models |
| `{Geode}Geode.glb` / `_Polished` | `AmethystGeode.glb`, `AmethystGeode_Polished.glb` | Raw + polished geode pairs |
| `{Tool}.glb` | `Hammer1.glb`, `Pickaxe1.glb` | Tool models with tier number |

## Scale & Units

- Unity default: 1 unit = 1 meter
- Conveyor belts: standard widths for BasePhysicsObject interaction
- Building grid: integer-based placement (1x1, 1x2, etc.)
- Ore pieces: small-scale (fit on conveyors)

## Prefab Structure Patterns

- Machine prefabs: root GO with script + child colliders (trigger zone + physical body)
- Building prefabs: root BuildingObject + BuildingPlacementNode children
- Ore nodes: root OreNode + child mesh + DamageableOrePiece children
- Tools: viewmodel mesh + held position offset

## LOD Setup

- No LOD groups detected in source code (ConveyorBatchRenderingComponent handles batch rendering instead)
- Future: LOD could be added to conveyor items for large factories

---

*Last updated: phase-All init*

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/phase-All/7-3D/WORLD.md ==== -->
# WORLD.md — minemgl World/Level Design Conventions

> Grows per phase as world systems are built.

---

## Scene Organization

| Scene | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| MainMenu | Title screen, save file selection, elevator animation |
| Gameplay | Main gameplay — cave mining environment |
| DemoCave | Demo/tutorial level variant |

Each scene has baked lightmaps (4-11 lightmap textures per scene) + LightProbes.json.

## Level Scale & Grid

- Underground cave environment — no terrain system
- Building grid: integer-based (1x1, 1x2, 2x2 building footprints)
- BuildingPlacementNode defines valid placement positions
- Conveyor connections snap to building outputs
- DisplacementMeshGenerator: procedural cave wall geometry

## World Structure

- **OreNode** clusters distributed through cave walls
- **BuildingPlacementNode** positions on cave floor/platforms
- **WaterVolume** trigger zones for underwater areas
- **StartingElevator** connects surface to mine
- **BreakableCrate** scattered as loot containers
- **EditableSign** player-placeable text signs
- **DetonatorExplosion** destructible walls (progression gates)

## Lighting

- Baked lightmaps (URP) — no real-time GI
- Jupiter sky system (Pinwheel/JDayNightCycle) for surface areas
- CookieFlipbook for animated light effects (torches, fire)
- ToolMiningHat provides player headlamp

## Layer Usage

Based on source code analysis:

| Layer | Purpose | Used By |
|-------|---------|---------|
| Default | Standard objects | Most GOs |
| Player | Player collider | PlayerController |
| Ore | Mineable ore nodes | OreNode, OrePiece |
| Building | Placed buildings | BuildingObject |
| Conveyor | Belt colliders | ConveyorBelt |
| UI | UI elements | UIManager panels |
| Ignore Raycast | Non-interactive | Particles, decor |

(Exact layer numbers to be verified in Unity project — PhysicsUtils.SetLayerRecursively used throughout)

## Vertex Painting

- VertexPainter.cs: editor tool for painting cave rock surfaces
- Used for blending materials on cave geometry

---

*Last updated: phase-All init*

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/minemgl/surfer.md ==== -->
# surfer.md — minemgl Reasoning Log

> Append after each agent prompt. Tracks decisions, discoveries, and changes.

---

## Prompt 1 — /init Bootstrap

**Asked:** Bootstrap all architecture documentation for minemgl from raw decompiled source.

**Key Discoveries:**
- 294 .cs files total: 268 game code, 24 third-party (Pinwheel/Jupiter sky + AzureNature), 2 generated
- 18+ Singleton managers with [DefaultExecutionOrder] — existing pattern maps cleanly to our architecture
- 31+ FindObjectOfType calls in original — all eliminated in new architecture via GameEvents/SerializeField/Owner chain
- God objects: SavingLoadingManager (~900 lines → 4 scripts), PlayerController (~774 lines → 4 scripts), ComputerShopUI (~283 lines → 3 scripts), PlayerInventory (~392 lines → 3 scripts)
- Machine pipeline is deep: OreNode → OrePiece → Crusher → BlastFurnace → Ingot → RollingMill/PipeRoller/RodExtruder → finished goods → SellerMachine
- CastingFurnace is unique — requires mold tool + coal input + recipe selection (separate from blast furnace pipeline)
- Tool inheritance: BaseHeldTool → 12 tool subclasses. ToolPickaxe → ToolHardHat further. RapidAutoMinerDrillBit is its own branch.
- Quest system has 5 requirement types: Resource, Triggered, Timed, UnlockResearch, ShopItem
- Save system uses ISaveLoadableObject interface + SavableObjectID enum (150+ entries)
- Existing utility code (MathExtensions, Vector3Utils, PhysicsUtils, UniqueQueue, TimeUtil) merged into single Utils.cs
- TimeSince/TimeUntil structs are operator-heavy implicit conversion types — ported directly

**Decisions Made:**
- 14 phases: All, A(Shop/Economy), B(Conveyor/Building), C(Player/Inventory), D(Tools), E(Mining/Deposit), F(Processing), G(Automation), H1(Quests), H2(Research/Contracts), I(Save), J(Audio/Settings/Input), K1(MainMenu/Debug), K2(World/Polish)
- EconomyManager → phase-A (not phase-All) per GOAL-general.md guidance
- SupportSystem stays in phase-B (tightly coupled to BuildingSystem)
- Processing machines grouped in one phase-F despite 15+ scripts (all depend on OrePiece from E)
- Quests (H1) before Research/Contracts (H2) because research unlocks are quest rewards
- Save system (I) late because it implements interfaces from many earlier phases
- 52% of systems are L0 portable, 48% game-specific
- 9 interfaces identified, IInteractable has most implementors (8 across 6 phases)
- BasePhysicsObject + BaseSellableItem placed in phase-All (shared physics hierarchy)
- ConveyorBelt referenced by BasePhysicsObject — forward declaration handled via phase-B providing ConveyorBelt

**What Changed:**
- Created: ARCHITECTURE.md, PhaseMap.md, StructureMap.md, GOAL.md, NewAgent.md, Estimate.md, SystemPortabilityMap.md, CoverageMap.md, OptionalFeatures.md, SystemIsolationAnalysis.md
- Created: phase-All/ scaffolding (11 files): Singleton.cs, GameEvents.cs, UIManager.cs, DataManager.cs, GlobalEnumsAll.cs, BasePhysicsObject.cs, BaseSellableItem.cs, Utils.cs, TimeSince.cs, TimeUntil.cs, MODEL.md, ANIM.md, WORLD.md
- Singleton.Instance renamed to Singleton.Ins per GOAL convention
- GameManager events extracted into static partial class GameEvents
- Original 5 utility classes merged into single Utils.cs + UniqueQueue<T>
- All scripts follow region blocks, class summaries, and // → inline flow markers

---

*Next: Phase-A delivery (ShopSystem + EconomySystem + InteractionSystem)*

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/ARCHITECTURE.md ==== -->
# noimnot — Detailed Architecture Documentation

## 1. Project Overview

### Game Description

**"Not I'm Not"** (noimnot) is a first-person horror/narrative survival game where the player shelters strangers in their house across multiple days and nights. The core loop: characters knock on the door, the player decides whether to let them in or refuse them, engages in branching dialog, manages consumable items, and navigates toward one of 13 possible endings. Some characters are imposters who may murder other residents — the player must identify them through conversation and behavioral signs (eyes, teeth, ears, armpits, hands). The game features a 7-room Soviet-style apartment, a dream/nightmare system, a phone for calling FEMA/couriers, and environmental mechanics like window boarding and basement access.

The game is built on Unity (URP pipeline) with heavy use of **Zenject DI**, **UniTask** for async, **YarnSpinner** for branching dialog, **ECM2** for first-person movement, **Cinemachine** for camera, and **DOTween** for animations. All source is decompiled — method bodies are empty stubs.

### Project Structure

```
Assembly-CSharp/
├── _Code/
│   ├── Characters/                    → Character management, types, sprites, emotions
│   │   ├── Cat/                       → Pet cat pathing
│   │   └── DialogSystem/             → Dialog UI elements (HoverableButton, DialogAnswer)
│   ├── DialogSystem/                  → Core dialog: DialogManager, DialogView, YarnSpinner bridge
│   │   ├── Commands/                  → DialogCommandsInstance (Yarn→events)
│   │   ├── Signs/                     → Character sign/emotion display
│   │   └── Subtitles/                → Subtitle overlay
│   ├── Events/                        → Game events: DingDong, pose changes, time-of-day
│   ├── Infrastructure/                → All managers and subsystems (~280 files)
│   │   ├── ActionableObjects/         → Clickable world objects
│   │   ├── CloseUps/                  → Detailed interaction views (fridge, phone, radio)
│   │   ├── Consumables/               → Inventory items (beer, cigarettes, mushrooms, etc.)
│   │   ├── Cursor/                    → Custom cursor management
│   │   ├── Cutscenes/                 → Cutscene playback
│   │   ├── DayNight/                  → Day/night cycle, energy system
│   │   ├── DI/                        → Zenject installers
│   │   ├── Endings/                   → 10+ ending types + ending display
│   │   ├── GameEvents/                → Time-based character arrival scheduling
│   │   ├── InteractableObjects/       → 12+ interactable types
│   │   ├── Locations/                 → HouseInterior/Basement/Death/PreDeath
│   │   ├── Notepad/                   → In-game notepad
│   │   ├── Pause/                     → Pause menu
│   │   ├── Player/                    → Player service layer
│   │   ├── Randomization/             → Random character generation
│   │   ├── Rooms/                     → Room management + views
│   │   ├── Save/                      → Save data handlers
│   │   ├── Scenes/                    → Scene loading
│   │   ├── Settings/                  → Game settings
│   │   ├── Showcase/                  → Demo mode
│   │   ├── Sound/                     → Music configuration
│   │   ├── StartUp/                   → Game initialization
│   │   ├── StateObjects/              → State machine for world objects
│   │   ├── TriggerObjects/            → Physics trigger zones
│   │   ├── Updatable/                 → Custom update loop
│   │   ├── Windows/                   → Window boarding system
│   │   ├── _NINAH__*/                → "NINAH" subsystems (cat, dream, effects, gacha, etc.)
│   │   └── ResourceMother.cs          → Central config ScriptableObject (12 interfaces!)
│   ├── Language/                       → Language enum
│   ├── Menues/                        → UI menus (HUD, pause, endings, gun, titles)
│   ├── Parallax/                      → Mouse parallax effect
│   ├── Player/                        → Player controller, input, watcher, markers
│   ├── Raycast/                       → Raycast hint system
│   ├── Rooms/                         → Room views (7 rooms + base classes)
│   └── Utils/                         → Utilities, extensions, custom Yarn reader, dev tools
├── _Scripts/
│   ├── Services/
│   │   ├── DataModel/                → Data persistence layer
│   │   └── Sound/                    → Core sound service (reusable)
│   └── Raycast/                      → Reusable raycast system
├── _Other/
│   └── SimpleBalancer/               → SO-based value tuning system
├── Code/Utils/                        → String extensions, enum creator utilities
├── DefaultNamespace/                  → AEnumEventBus (typed event bus)
├── Dream/                            → Dream ball timeline
└── [root files]                      → Steam, input actions, gamepad cursor, Arabic RTL, etc.
```

### Third-Party Dependencies

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Zenject** | Core DI framework — constructor injection everywhere |
| **UniTask (Cysharp)** | Async/await — replaces coroutines throughout |
| **DOTween (DG.Tweening)** | UI animations, fade, camera movement, sound fades |
| **YarnSpinner (Yarn.Unity)** | Branching dialog system — FakeDialogRunner, FakeVariableStorage |
| **Steamworks.NET** | Steam achievements, stats, cloud saves |
| **ECM2** | Easy Character Movement 2 — first-person character controller |
| **Cinemachine** | Virtual cameras (normal, crouched, look-at) |
| **Unity Localization** | Multi-language support |
| **Newtonsoft.Json** | Save data serialization |
| **AYellowpaper.SerializedCollections** | Inspector-editable dictionaries (ResourceMother) |
| **NaughtyAttributes** | Inspector enhancements |
| **RTLTMPro** | Right-to-left text support (Arabic) |
| **TextMeshPro** | Text rendering throughout |
| **Unity Input System** | New Input System (PlayerInput, action maps) |
| **URP** | Universal Render Pipeline with post-processing |

---

## 2. High-Level Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          ZENJECT DI CONTAINER                       │
│  CommonInstaller (project) ← ResourceMother (central config SO)    │
│  GameplaySceneInstaller (scene) ← 24 ViewProviders                 │
└─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
              │ injects via constructor
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     CORE MANAGERS (IInitializable)                   │
│                                                                     │
│  StartUpService ──→ initializes all systems in order                │
│       │                                                             │
│       ├── DayNightController (18 deps) ─── day/night cycle          │
│       ├── GameEventsManager (5 deps) ──── DingDong scheduling       │
│       ├── CharactersManager (5 deps) ──── character lifecycle       │
│       ├── RoomsManager (17 deps) ─────── 7-room house              │
│       ├── DialogManager (17 deps) ────── dialog + Yarn + gun        │
│       ├── InteractablesManager (18 deps)─ interactive objects       │
│       ├── CloseUpsController (18 deps) ── detail views              │
│       ├── GameplayEndingManager (11 deps) endings                   │
│       └── LocationsManager (9 deps) ──── area transitions          │
└─────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
              │ communicates via
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     COMMUNICATION PATTERNS                          │
│                                                                     │
│  1. event Action<T>  ─── cross-system notifications (primary)       │
│  2. event Func<T>    ─── cross-system queries (clever but hard      │
│                          to trace)                                  │
│  3. AEnumEventBus    ─── typed enum event bus (sound/HUD)           │
│  4. Yarn Commands    ─── DialogCommandsInstance → C# events         │
│  5. Interface injection ── Zenject constructor injection             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
              │
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     PLAYER & VIEW LAYER                              │
│                                                                     │
│  PlayerController (ECM2 + Cinemachine) ── first-person movement     │
│  InputHandling (Unity Input System) ───── keyboard + gamepad        │
│  WatcherManager + Markers ─────────────── UI focus tracking         │
│  HUDView / HUDPresenter ──────────────── main HUD overlay          │
│  Room Views (7 rooms) ─────────────────── 2D room UI               │
│  DialogView + FakeDialogRunner ────────── YarnSpinner UI            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
              │
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     DATA / PERSISTENCE LAYER                        │
│                                                                     │
│  DataModelService                                                   │
│  ├── PrefsDataStorage (PlayerPrefs)                                 │
│  ├── EncryptedPrefsDataStorage (AES)                                │
│  └── RamDataStorage (session data)                                  │
│                                                                     │
│  ASavableClass<T> ──→ every manager saves/loads via this base       │
│  SteamCloudSaver ──→ Steam cloud integration                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Core Gameplay Loop

```
┌─── DAY PHASE ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Player walks house → interacts with objects → talks to     │
│ residents → uses consumables → checks windows              │
│ Energy slots limit actions per day                         │
└────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │ DayNightController.Change()
                     ▼
┌─── NIGHT PHASE ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Characters knock (DingDong events) → player goes to door   │
│ → peek/talk → let in or refuse → dialog choices            │
│ Imposters may kill residents overnight                     │
└────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │ DayNightController.DayChanged
                     ▼
┌─── MORNING ────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ RunMorningOfSuicides() → clear corpses → new day events    │
│ Check ending conditions → dream sequences (if triggered)   │
└────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │ loop until ending triggered
                     ▼
┌─── ENDINGS (13 total) ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Basement, BasementWithThem, BasementAlone, KilledBySuper,  │
│ Baby, TheDeath, Cult, Killer, FEMA, Mushroom, Intro, Rage, │
│ Vigilante                                                  │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 3. Core Architectural Pattern: Zenject DI (No Singletons)

Unlike typical Unity projects, noimnot uses **Zenject dependency injection** instead of singletons. Only 4 classes use singleton patterns:

| Class | Pattern | Scope |
|-------|---------|-------|
| `SteamManager` | `_instance` field + `Awake()` | Global (third-party) |
| `SteamAchievements` | `Instance { get; private set; }` | Global |
| `UIRayCaster` | `Instance { get; private set; }` | Scene |
| `DialogCommandsInstance` | `_instance` static field | Scene |

### DI Composition Roots

**`CommonInstaller : MonoInstaller`** — Project-level. Binds `ResourceMother` (central config SO).

**`GameplaySceneInstaller : MonoInstaller`** — Scene-level. Binds **24 ViewProviders**:
ViewProvider, DayNightControllerViewProvider, RoomsViewProvider, UpdaterInstanceProvider, WindowsViewProvider, ActionableObjectsViewProvider, CloseUpsViewProvider, NotepadViewProvider, DialogViewProvider, PlayerViewProvider, PauseMenuViewProvider, RoomDisplayerViewProvider, LocationsViewProvider, InteractablesViewProvider, StateObjectsViewProvider, SoundServiceInstanceProvider, EndingViewProvider, InputHandlerProvider, CutscenesDataProvider, SettingsInstanceProvider, CatViewProvider, TriggerObjectsProvider, CommonViewProvider

**`MainSceneInstaller`** — Main menu scene installer.

**`StrangeMorningSceneInstaller`** — Strange morning dream scene installer.

**`TitlesSceneInstaller`** — Credits/titles scene installer.

### Execution Order

All services implement `IInitializable` (Zenject) and are initialized in binding order. `StartUpService` (12 deps) acts as the orchestrator:

| Priority | Service | Reason |
|----------|---------|--------|
| 1 | DataModelService | Load saved state before anything else |
| 2 | GameEventsManager | Event scheduling needs saved day data |
| 3 | CharactersManager | Character state from save data |
| 4 | DayNightController | Day/time state |
| 5 | RoomsManager | Room state + character placement |
| 6 | DialogManager | Dialog progress |
| 7 | InteractablesManager | Object states |
| 8 | CloseUpsController | Close-up states |
| 9 | LocationsManager | Current location |
| 10 | GameplayEndingManager | Ending progress |
| 11 | StartUpService | Final wiring + kickoff |

---

## 4. System-by-System Breakdown

### 4.1 Dialog System

**Files:** `DialogManager.cs`, `DialogView.cs`, `DialogViewData.cs`, `DialogData.cs`, `DialogSaveData.cs`, `DialogCommandsInstance.cs`, `FakeDialogRunner.cs`, `FakeVariableStorage.cs`, `FakeOptionView.cs`, `DialogSignsView.cs`, `ADialogSignElementView.cs`, `SubtitlesView.cs`, `IDialogManager.cs`, `IDialogViewProvider.cs`, `DialogAnswer.cs`, `HoverableButton.cs`, `IDialogUI.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Branching dialog via YarnSpinner, character sign display (eyes/teeth/ears/armpits/hands), gun mechanics during dialog, death sequences, subtitle overlays, cutscene triggering, ending triggering.

**God Object: `DialogManager` (~1108 lines, 34 events, 17 injected deps)**
- Manages dialog flow, subtitles, gun show/hide/shoot, death sequences, cutscene triggers, ending triggers, cat feeding, consumable usage, pose changes
- **Split candidates:** GunManager (gun show/hide/shot), SubtitleManager (subtitle overlay), CutsceneTriggerHandler (cutscene triggering), EndingTriggerHandler (ending conditions from dialog)

**Data flow:**
```
YarnSpinner dialog → FakeDialogRunner → DialogCommandsInstance (commands)
                                     → DialogManager (event dispatch)
                                     → DialogView (UI display)
                                     → DialogSignsView (character signs)
```

### 4.2 Day/Night System

**Files:** `DayNightController.cs`, `DayNightControllerSOData.cs`, `IDayNightController.cs`, `IDayNightControllerSODataProvider.cs`, `DayNightControllerViewProvider.cs`, `DayNightSaveData.cs`, `MockDayNightController.cs`, `ETimeOfDay.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Day/night cycle management, energy/action-point system, morning/evening transitions, TV watching, body eater appearances, dream triggering, bed mechanic.

**God Object: `DayNightController` (~400 lines, 18 deps, 8 events)**
- Manages day counter, energy slots, time-of-day transitions, TV watching, body eater, dreams, pose changes for tomorrow
- **Split candidates:** EnergyManager (action points), BodyEaterController (body eater appearances), BedController (sleeping/waking)

### 4.3 Room System

**Files:** `RoomsManager.cs`, `IRoomsManager.cs`, `IRoomsViewProvider.cs`, `RoomsSaveData.cs`, `RoomDisplayer.cs`, `IRoomDisplayerViewProvider.cs`, `ARoom.cs`, `EntranceRoom.cs`, `BigRoom.cs`, `Bedroom.cs`, `Bathroom.cs`, `KitchenRoom.cs`, `OfficeRoom.cs`, `PantryRoom.cs`, `ARoomObjectView.cs`, `CharacterRoomObjectView.cs`, `ObjectRoomObjectView.cs`, `NarrativeRoomObject.cs`, `TestRoomObjectView.cs`, `RoomObjectState.cs`, `UIRayCaster.cs`, `UIButton.cs`, `ERoom.cs`, `ERoomPeopleState.cs`, `ERoomTestObjectStates.cs`, `EObjectsRoomObject.cs`, `ENarrativeObject.cs`

**Responsibilities:** 7-room house management, character placement in rooms, room entering/leaving (async transitions), corpse clearing, TV watching, whisper system, body eater visuals.

**God Object: `RoomsManager` (~210 lines, 17 deps)**
- **Split candidates:** WhisperController (whisper system), CorpseManager (corpse clearing)

### 4.4 Character System

**Files:** `CharactersManager.cs`, `ICharactersManager.cs`, `ICharactersSODataProvider.cs`, `CharacterSOData.cs`, `CharacterVisitData.cs`, `CharactersManagerSaveData.cs`, `ECharacterType.cs` (75 entries!), `ECharacterPlace.cs`, `ECharacterSign.cs`, `ACharacterSpriteByEmotion.cs`, `CharacterAnimatedSpriteByEmotion.cs`, `CharacterEyeData.cs`, `BinPackager.cs`, `CatPathController.cs`

**Responsibilities:** 75 character types, let-in/refuse/kill/exile lifecycle, imposter detection, random generation, morning suicides, FEMA exile, povistka (summons), room assignment.

### 4.5 Game Events (Scheduling) System

**Files:** `GameEventsManager.cs`, `IGameEventsManager.cs`, `IGameEventsManagerSODataProvider.cs`, `GameEventsManagerSOData.cs`, `GameEventsManagerSaveData.cs`, `AGameEvent.cs`, `CharacterDingDongEvent.cs`, `CharacterChangePoseEvent.cs`, `GrowingBellyEvent.cs`, `GameEventConditions.cs`, `VisualsByTimeOfDay.cs`, `ActionView.cs`

**Responsibilities:** Time-based event scheduling, DingDong (doorbell) generation per day, character arrival timing, event condition checking, completion tracking.

### 4.6 Ending System

**Files:** `GameplayEndingManager.cs`, `IGameplayEndingManager.cs`, `GameplayEndingManagerSaveData.cs`, `AGameplayEnding.cs`, `StayAliveEnding.cs`, `StayAliveAloneEnding.cs`, `StayAliveWithThemEnding.cs`, `BabyEnding.cs`, `KillerEnding.cs`, `MushroomEnding.cs`, `CultistsEnding.cs`, `DeathEnding.cs`, `FemaEnding.cs`, `VigilanteEnding.cs`, `EndingShower.cs`, `IEndingShower.cs`, `EndingView.cs`, `IEndingDataProvider.cs`, `IEndingSODataProvider.cs`, `EndingViewSOData.cs`, `EndingsSOData.cs`, `EEnding.cs`

## 5. Key Design Patterns & Techniques

### 5.1 Zenject Dependency Injection
Every major service is injected via constructor. ViewProviders are MonoBehaviours bound in scene installers. The pattern eliminates `FindObjectOfType` entirely — only 1 occurrence exists (in firstpass assembly, not game code).

### 5.2 ASavableClass<T> Pattern
Every manager that persists state inherits `ASavableClass<TSaveData>`. Save data classes inherit `ASavableData`. The `DataModelService` manages three storage layers (Prefs, EncryptedPrefs, RAM).

### 5.3 ViewProvider Pattern
MonoBehaviours that hold scene references are wrapped in `*ViewProvider` classes. These are bound to `I*ViewProvider` interfaces in Zenject, separating scene wiring from logic. Example: `DialogViewProvider` provides `DialogView`, `SubtitlesView`, `DialogCommandsInstance` to `DialogManager`.

### 5.4 Custom Update Loop
`UpdaterService` + `IUpdateable` replaces scattered `Update()` methods. Systems implement `IUpdateable.OnUpdateAction()` and are registered with the updater. Also uses Zenject's `ITickable` for some systems.

### 5.5 Event Func<T> Pattern
Cross-system queries use `event Func<T>` — systems expose query events that other systems subscribe to, returning data without direct references. Example: `CharactersManager.GetRoom` is `Func<ERoom, ARoom>` subscribed by `RoomsManager`.

### 5.6 AEnumEventBus Pattern
`AEnumEventBus<TEnum>` provides a typed event bus using enum keys. `CommonEnumEventBus : AEnumEventBus<ECommonEvents>` handles cross-cutting concerns like `HudFadeIn`, `PlaySound`, `StopSound`.

### 5.7 Mock Classes for Testability
Several mock implementations exist: `MockDialogManager`, `MockDayNightController`, `MockPauseController`, `MockCursorController`, `MockHudPresenter`, `MockSoundService`.

### 5.8 ResourceMother Central SO
Single ScriptableObject implementing 11+ interfaces — serves all configuration data. Bound in `CommonInstaller` and each interface is resolved separately by consumers.

---

## 6. Coupling Analysis

### Direct Cross-System References (Zenject Constructor Injection)

| Class | # Dependencies | Top Dependencies |
|-------|---------------|------------------|
| InteractablesManager | 18 | ILocationsManager, IHUDPresenter, ICloseUpsController, IDialogManager |
| CloseUpsController | 18 | IHUDPresenter, IDayNightController, IDialogManager, IConsumablesController |
| DayNightController | 18 | IGameEventsManager, ICharactersManager, IDialogManager, IHUDPresenter |
| DialogManager | 17 | ICharactersManager, IGameEventsManager, ICutscenesManager, IConsumablesController |
| RoomsManager | 17 | IDayNightController, IDialogManager, IGameEventsManager, ICharactersManager |
| PauseController | 14 | Multiple |
| ActionableObjectsManager | 14 | Multiple |
| StartUpService | 12 | All core services |
| GameplayEndingManager | 11 | IDayNightController, ICharactersManager, IDialogManager |
| LocationsManager | 9 | Multiple |
| CharactersManager | 5 | Lightest among managers |
| GameEventsManager | 5 | Lightest among managers |

### Most-Referenced Interfaces (Dependency Hotspots)

| Interface | Referenced By (count) |
|-----------|----------------------|
| `IDialogManager` | 10+ (RoomsManager, DayNightController, PauseController, etc.) |
| `INotAHumanSoundService` | ~15 (nearly every manager) |
| `IHUDPresenter` | 8+ |
| `IDayNightController` | 8+ |
| `ICharactersManager` | 6+ |
| `IDataModelService` | ~15 (nearly every saveable class) |
| `ICursorController` | 10+ |
| `IConsumablesController` | 6+ |
| `IInputHandlerProvider` | 8+ |

### FindObjectOfType Usage

**Only 1 occurrence** — in Assembly-CSharp-firstpass (not game code). The Zenject DI pattern successfully eliminates this anti-pattern.

### Static Instance Access

| Class | Pattern | Usage |
|-------|---------|-------|
| `SteamManager` | `_instance` | Steam API access |
| `SteamAchievements` | `Instance` | Achievement tracking |
| `UIRayCaster` | `Instance` | 2D UI raycasting in rooms |
| `DialogCommandsInstance` | `_instance` | Yarn command bridge |

---

## 7. God Objects — Split Candidates

| File | Lines | Responsibilities | Suggested Split |
|------|-------|------------------|-----------------|
| `DialogManager` | ~1108 | Dialog flow, subtitles, gun mechanics, death, cutscenes, endings, cat feeding, consumables, poses, signs | GunController, SubtitleController, DialogEventDispatcher |
| `DayNightController` | ~400 | Day/night cycle, energy, TV, body eater, dreams, poses, bed | EnergyController, BodyEaterController, BedController |
| `RoomsManager` | ~210 | 7 rooms, characters, corpses, TV, whispers, body eater | WhisperController, CorpseManager |
| `InputHandling` | ~390 | 32 input properties, device detection, UI counter | Could stay unified — input is inherently centralized |
| `GameEventsManager` | ~315 | Event scheduling, DingDong generation, conditions | EventScheduler, DingDongGenerator |
| `InteractablesManager` | ~107 | 12+ interactable types | Already relatively focused |
| `CloseUpsController` | ~105 | 5 close-up views | Already relatively focused |
| `ResourceMother` | ~60 | 11 interfaces, 12 SerializeFields | Split into per-system SOs |

---

## 8. Critique & Thoughts

### What the Original Source Does Well (Preserve These)
1. **Interface-first architecture** — every service has an `I` interface counterpart, enabling mock testing
2. **Zenject DI** — eliminates FindObjectOfType entirely, clean composition root
3. **ViewProvider pattern** — clean separation of scene references from logic
4. **ASavableClass<T>** — consistent save/load pattern across all systems
5. **Mock classes** — MockDialogManager, MockDayNightController, etc. show testing awareness
6. **Custom update loop** — IUpdateable/UpdaterService avoids scattered Update() methods
7. **Event Func<T> pattern** — clever query mechanism without direct references
8. **AEnumEventBus** — typed event system for cross-cutting concerns
9. **Three-layer persistence** — PlayerPrefs, encrypted prefs, RAM storage
10. **YarnSpinner integration** — professional dialog system with custom reader

### What to Improve (Drive the Architecture Transformation)
1. **Massive constructor parameter lists** (14-19 deps) — split god objects to reduce fan-out
2. **DialogManager is a 1108-line god object** — handles dialog, gun, death, endings, cutscenes, cat, consumables, poses, signs — needs splitting into focused services
3. **DayNightController** manages too many concerns (energy, TV, body eater, dreams, bed)
4. **ResourceMother** implements 11 interfaces — should be split into per-system SOs
5. **Event Func<T>** is clever but hard to trace — consider explicit interface methods instead
6. **34 events on DialogManager** — too many responsibilities exposed as events
7. **Decompiled empty stubs** — method bodies are missing, limiting static analysis
8. **Some naming issues** — `EGaypadType` naming, inconsistent folder naming (`Menues`)
9. **Missing clear event bus** — cross-system events are ad-hoc `event Action` fields, not centralized
10. **Zenject coupling** — the architecture is deeply tied to Zenject; our rebuild will use Singleton<T> + GameEvents pattern instead

---

| 48 | ActionableObjectsManager.cs | ~50 | Actionable world objects |
| 49 | NotepadController.cs | ~30 | In-game notepad |
| 50 | GameplayRandomizer.cs | ~40 | Random character generation |
| 51 | LocalizationManager.cs | ~30 | Language switching |
| 52 | UpdaterService.cs | ~25 | Custom update loop |
| 53 | ScenesChanger.cs | ~15 | Static scene loader |
| 54 | PlayerInputActions.cs | ~500 | Auto-generated input actions |
| 55 | ECharacterType.cs | ~80 | 75-entry character enum |
| 56+ | ~530 remaining files | varies | Interfaces, enums, data classes, SO data, view providers, markers, etc. |

**Total: ~585 .cs files across Assembly-CSharp**

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/Estimate.md ==== -->
# Estimate — noimnot (Not I'm Not)

> Timeline calibrated from complexity tiers. All estimates assume hand-typing.

---

## Complexity Tiers

| Tier | Examples | Avg Time |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Simple** (~15 min) | Enums, interfaces, SO_ (pure data), save data classes, entities, stubs | 15 min |
| **Medium** (~30 min) | DataService, DataWrapper, Field_, PhaseXLOG, Utils additions, test scripts | 30 min |
| **Complex** (~60 min) | MonoBehaviours, Orchestrators, Managers, Player scripts, base classes | 60 min |

---

## Phase Breakdown

### phase-All — Shared Foundation
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 6 | 2 (enums, 3D docs) | 2 (Utils.cs, DataManager) | 2 (Singleton, UIManager, GameEvents) | 2.5h |

### phase-A — Core Data + Events + Persistence
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 23 | 10 (interfaces, enums, simple data) | 8 (DataService, storage, handlers) | 5 (DataModelService, GameSaveDataHandler, AesCrypter) | 8.0h |

### phase-B — Character Data + Room Framework + Cursor
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 23 | 8 (interfaces, data classes, state) | 8 (SO_ files, cursor, raycast) | 7 (room bases, character data, cursor controller) | 8.5h |

### phase-C — Characters + Rooms + State Objects
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 26 | 6 (interfaces, save data, enums) | 6 (window views, state objects) | 14 (7 rooms, CharactersManager, RoomsManager, WindowsManager, StateObjectController) | 13.0h |

### phase-D — Dialog System + YarnSpinner
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 26 | 8 (interfaces, enums, data classes) | 8 (Field_DialogOption, dialog data, custom reader) | 10 (DialogManager, FakeDialogRunner, FakeVariableStorage, DialogSignsView, SubtitlesView) | 12.0h |

### phase-E — Player Controller + Input + HUD
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 6 (interfaces, enums, save data) | 7 (watcher, gamepad, footstep, HUD views) | 12 (PlayerController, InputHandling, HUDPresenter, PlayerService, WatcherManager) | 12.5h |

### phase-F — Interactables + Close-Ups
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 24 | 4 (interfaces, save data) | 6 (close-up views, base interactable) | 14 (12 interactable types, InteractablesManager, CloseUpsController) | 13.0h |

### phase-G — Day/Night + Game Events + Consumables
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 8 (interfaces, SO_ configs, enums, save data) | 7 (event types, consumables, randomizer data) | 10 (DayNightController, GameEventsManager, ConsumablesController, GameplayRandomizer) | 11.5h |

### phase-H — Endings + Locations
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 8 (interfaces, SO_ configs, save data) | 7 (ending types — mostly similar) | 10 (EndingManager, 10 ending implementations, LocationsManager) | 11.0h |

### phase-I — Settings + Pause + Menus
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 8 (interfaces, data, popup) | 10 (settings views, notepad, pause menu) | 7 (SettingsManager, PauseController, StartUpService, ActionableObjectsManager) | 9.5h |

### phase-J — Sound + Effects
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 24 | 8 (interfaces, enums, SO_ configs, mock) | 8 (sound list, source, emitter, fog) | 8 (SoundService, NotAHumanSoundService, EffectsController, RandomSoundsEmitter) | 10.0h |

### phase-K — Cat + Dream + Gacha + Steam + Titles
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 8 (interfaces, data, save data) | 9 (Field_ cards, gacha, titles, cat position) | 8 (CatController, DreamController, SteamManager, SteamAchievements) | 10.0h |

### phase-L — DI Adaption + Remaining Utilities
| Scripts | Simple | Medium | Complex | Raw Hours |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|-----------|
| 25 | 10 (triggers, simple MonoBehaviours) | 10 (parallax, visuals, view providers) | 5 (LocalizationManager, ResourceMother split, UpdaterService) | 8.0h |

---

## Summary

| Phase | Scripts | Raw Hours | With Buffer (+25%) |
|-------|---------|-----------|-------------------|
| All | 6 | 2.5h | 3.1h |
| A | 23 | 8.0h | 10.0h |
| B | 23 | 8.5h | 10.6h |
| C | 26 | 13.0h | 16.3h |
| D | 26 | 12.0h | 15.0h |
| E | 25 | 12.5h | 15.6h |
| F | 24 | 13.0h | 16.3h |
| G | 25 | 11.5h | 14.4h |
| H | 25 | 11.0h | 13.8h |
| I | 25 | 9.5h | 11.9h |
| J | 24 | 10.0h | 12.5h |
| K | 25 | 10.0h | 12.5h |
| L | 25 | 8.0h | 10.0h |
| **TOTAL** | **302** | **129.5h** | **162.0h** |

**Agent generation saves ~30%** vs designing from scratch — code is ready to reference, GUIDE.md has typing order.

---

## Notes

- Complex phases (C, D, E, F) have the most interconnected systems — budget extra review time
- Phase-D (Dialog) is the highest-risk phase due to YarnSpinner integration complexity
- Phase-L is mostly reference/adaptation — the DI installers don't need to be rebuilt, just documented
- Buffer accounts for: scene setup, testing, debugging, inspector wiring, cross-phase integration
