<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/GOAL.md ==== -->
# GOAL — noimnot (Not I'm Not)

> Every script is typed by hand. This file defines how.

---

## What

Rebuild **noimnot** (Not I'm Not) from its original decompiled/extracted source (`Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/`).

**Three goals, in order:**
1. **100% main-source behavior** — every feature the original game has, we have
2. **Clean architecture** — zero tight coupling between systems. Systems communicate via interfaces and GameEvents only. No direct class imports across system boundaries.
3. **Portable systems** — systems designed via `_-Systems/` architecture should work in ANY future Unity project with minimal changes. Some systems will be intentionally game-specific — that's fine.

## Source of Truth

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Logic & behavior | `MAIN-SOURCE/noimnot/Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/*.cs` |
| Architecture rules | `LEARN/noimnot/GOAL.md` (this file) |
| Roadmap | `LEARN/noimnot/PhaseMap.md` |
| DataService specs | `LEARN/noimnot/StructureMap.md` |
| Original analysis | `LEARN/noimnot/ARCHITECTURE.md` |
| Reasoning log | `LEARN/noimnot/surfer.md` |
| User's actual code | `LEARN/noimnot/handTyped(latest)/` — ground truth for coding style |
| Portability map | `LEARN/noimnot/SystemPortabilityMap.md` — L0/L1+ per system |
| Cross-system audit | `LEARN/noimnot/SystemIsolationAnalysis.md` |
| Source coverage | `LEARN/noimnot/CoverageMap.md` |
| Optional extras | `LEARN/noimnot/OptionalFeatures.md` |

---

## Project-Specific Context

### Game Description
First-person horror/narrative survival. Player shelters strangers across days/nights in a 7-room apartment, decides who to let in via peephole, engages in branching YarnSpinner dialog, manages consumables (beer, cigarettes, mushrooms, cat food, etc.), and navigates toward 13 possible endings. Some visitors are imposters who murder residents at night.

### Original Architecture (What We're Replacing)
- **Zenject DI** → replaced by **Singleton<T> + GameEvents**
- **UniTask** → replaced by **coroutines** (standard Unity)
- **event Action/Func** → replaced by **partial class GameEvents with LogSubscribersCount**
- **ViewProvider pattern** (MonoBehaviour scene refs bound to interfaces) → replaced by **[SerializeField] + Owner chain**
- **ResourceMother** (single SO implementing 11 interfaces) → **split into per-system SO_ files**
- **Constructor injection** → **[SerializeField] + Singleton<T>.Ins reads**
- **ASavableClass<T>** base class → **DataService + save/load via DataModelService**
- **IUpdateable** custom update → **standard Unity Update() lifecycle**
- **Mock classes** (MockDialogManager, etc.) → **removed** (GameEvents decoupling makes mocks unnecessary)

### Key Game Constants
- **75 character types** (ECharacterType enum — each with sprites, emotions, signs)
- **7 rooms** (Entrance, BigRoom, Bedroom, Bathroom, Kitchen, Office, Pantry)
- **13 endings** (StayAlive, StayAliveAlone, StayAliveWithThem, Baby, Killer, Mushroom, Cultists, Death, FEMA, Vigilante, plus variants)
- **11 consumable types** (beer, cigarettes, mushrooms, cat food, etc.)
- **6 scenes** (MainMenu, GameScene, Loading, Ball, StrangeMorning, Titery, MushroomDream)
- **34 dialog events** (original DialogManager had 34 cross-system events)
- **5 DI installers** → become init order documentation

### God Object Split Plan
| Original | Lines | Split Into |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| DialogManager | ~1108 | DialogManager (orchestration), GunController (separate), SubtitleController (separate), DialogEventDispatcher (events) |
| DayNightController | ~400 | DayNightController (cycle), EnergyController (separate), BodyEaterController (separate), BedController (separate) |
| RoomsManager | ~210 | RoomsManager (core), WhisperController (separate), CorpseManager (separate) |
| InputHandling | ~390 | Keep unified — input is inherently centralized |
| GameEventsManager | ~315 | GameEventsManager (core), EventScheduler (separate), DingDongGenerator (separate) |

---

## Core Principle

**Every script's purpose fits in one sentence. If it doesn't, split it until it does.**
**Every script performs ONLY the service it was assigned — purely, nothing else.**

Every file must have a `→ "I do X"` one-liner. That one-liner is the script's contract.

```
SO_CharacterDef        → "I define what a character IS (sprites, emotions, eyes)"
WCharacter             → "I track what happened to one character this session"
CharacterDataService   → "I manage all character data as a collection"
Field_DialogOption     → "I display one dialog option button"
DialogManager          → "I orchestrate dialog flow with YarnSpinner"
RoomsManager           → "I manage the 7-room apartment state"
DayNightController     → "I control day/night cycle transitions"
GameEventsManager      → "I schedule and fire game events (DingDong arrivals)"
EndingSystem           → "I evaluate and trigger the 13 endings"
DataModelService       → "I save/load data across 3 storage layers"
UIManager              → "I report menu state + close all panels when needed"
GameEvents             → "I deliver messages between systems"
Singleton              → "I ensure one instance"
```

---

## Foundation Templates

These two scripts go in `phase-All/0-Core/` and are the foundation for everything.

### Singleton.cs

```csharp
public abstract class Singleton<T> : MonoBehaviour where T : MonoBehaviour
{
    public static T Ins { get; private set; }

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        if (Ins == null) Ins = this as T;
        else if (Ins != this)
        {
            Debug.Log($"{typeof(T)} singleton already exists, destroying duplicate");
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }
}
```

### GameEvents.cs — Partial Class Pattern

Each phase extends GameEvents in its own `0-Core/GameEvents.cs`. **Phase-All** has the core:

```csharp
/// <summary> I deliver messages between systems. Each phase adds events via partial class.
/// Every Raise logs subscriber count for debugging. </summary>
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when menu state changes >>
    public static event Action<bool> OnMenuStateChanged;
    public static void RaiseMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnMenuStateChanged), OnMenuStateChanged);
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged?.Invoke(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }
    // << when menu state changes

    // when all sub-managers must close >>
    public static event Action OnCloseAllSubManagers;
    public static void RaiseCloseAllSubManagers()
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnCloseAllSubManagers), OnCloseAllSubManagers);
        GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers?.Invoke();
    }
    // << when all sub-managers must close

    static void LogSubscribersCount(string name, Delegate del)
    {
        int count = del?.GetInvocationList()?.Length ?? 0;
        Debug.Log($"[GameEvents] {name} raised for -> {count} subscribers".colorTag("lime"));
    }
}
```

### DefaultExecutionOrder — Singleton Init Priorities

```csharp
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-1000)]  // Must exist before anything logs
public class DataModelService : Singleton<DataModelService>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(-900)]   // Persistence layer
public class SaveLoadManager : Singleton<SaveLoadManager>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]   // Core gameplay systems
public class UIManager : Singleton<UIManager>
public class CharactersManager : Singleton<CharactersManager>
public class RoomsManager : Singleton<RoomsManager>

[DefaultExecutionOrder(0)]      // Default — most MonoBehaviours
```

---

## Folder Structure

```
Scripts/phase-All/                      → "shared scripts — never duplicated"
├── 0-Core/
│   ├── Singleton.cs                    → "generic singleton base — first instance wins"
│   └── GameEvents.cs                   → "core events shared across ALL phases"
├── 1-Managers/
│   ├── UIManager.cs                    → "reports menu state + closes all panels"
│   └── DataManager.cs                  → "shared visual data, layer masks"
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsAll.cs              → "TagType enum"
├── 4-Utils/
│   └── Utils.cs                       → "HasTag, SetTag, .map(), .gc<T>(), .destroyLeaves(), .toggle(), TimeSince/TimeUntil, BetterUniTask extensions"
└── 7-3D/
    ├── MODEL.md
    ├── ANIM.md
    └── WORLD.md

Scripts/phase-X/
├── _-Systems/                  → "PRIMARY — where the main code lives"
│   └── XxxSystem/
│       ├── SO_*, Field_*, W*, DataService, Orchestrator, SubManager
│       ├── Interface/
│       ├── Bridge/
│       ├── Test.md
│       └── Dependency.md
├── 0-Core/                     → "GameEvents.cs (partial per phase)"
├── 2-Data/Enums/              → "GlobalEnumsX.cs"
├── 4-Utils/                    → "PhaseXLOG.cs only"
└── 5-Tests/Manual/            → ".md test guides"
```

**The rule: if a script belongs to a feature system, it goes INSIDE `_-Systems/XxxSystem/`.** Numbered folders are ONLY for shared infrastructure that genuinely doesn't belong to any specific system — this is RARE.

---

## Naming

### Prefix / Field Conventions

| Kind | Convention | Example |
|------|-----------|---------|
| ScriptableObject | `SO_` prefix | `SO_CharacterDef`, `SO_DayNightConfig` |
| Display-only prefab handle | `Field_` prefix | `Field_DialogOption`, `Field_EndingCard` |
| DataWrapper | `W` prefix | `WCharacter`, `WConsumable` |
| Interface | `I` prefix | `ICharactersManager`, `IDayNightController` |
| Dictionary lookups | `DOC__` prefix | `DOC__type_wCharacter`, `DOC__room_characters` |
| List/collection fields | `ALL_CAPS` | `CHARACTERS_IN_HOUSE`, `DEAD_CHARACTERS` |
| `[SerializeField]` fields | `_` prefix, always private | `[SerializeField] float _speed` |
| Event handlers | `Handle...` | `HandleDayChanged` |
| GameEvents | `On...` + `Raise...` side by side | `OnCharacterLetIn` / `RaiseCharacterLetIn()` |
| Singleton access | `.Ins` | `Singleton<RoomsManager>.Ins` |
| Enum values | camelCase | `ECharacterType.neighbor`, `ERoom.entrance` |

**Prefix = no logic.** `SO_` = pure data. `Field_` = display only. `W` = session wrapper.

### Method Naming Patterns

| Prefix | Meaning | Return | Example |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| `Get...` | Read/query data | value | `GetCharactersInRoom()`, `GetCurrentDay()` |
| `Set...` | Write/assign data | void | `SetData()`, `SetState()` |
| `Try...` | Attempt action (may fail) | bool | `TryLetIn()`, `TryUseConsumable()` |
| `Can...` | Check capability | bool | `CanLetIn()`, `CanBoard()` |
| `Is...` | Check state | bool | `IsDead()`, `IsImposter()` |
| `Has...` | Ownership check | bool | `HasTag()`, `HasDreamed()` |
| `Handle...` | Event handler | void | `HandleCharacterKilled()` |
| `Raise...` | Fire GameEvent | void | `RaiseCharacterLetIn()` |
| `Build...` | Initialize collections | void | `BuildCharacters()`, `BuildRooms()` |
| `Refresh...` | Update UI after mutation | void | `RefreshAllRequired()` |

---

## Class Responsibilities — Pure Purpose

| Role | Where | Pure Purpose |
|------|-------|-------------|
| **SO_** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Inspector-editable config. Only public fields. Zero methods. |
| **Field_** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Set UI visuals (SetData/SetState). No onClick. No logic. |
| **W** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Wrap SO_ with mutable session state. Minimal API. |
| **DataService** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Pure C# collection service. Build + Get + Add + Remove + snapshot. EVERY `List<T>` / `Dictionary<K,V>` testable via `new`. |
| **Orchestrator** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Wire Field_ to DataService. Instantiate, AddListener, Destroy. `DOC__X__Field` tracking. `RefreshAllRequired()` after mutations. |
| **SubManager** | _-Systems/XxxSystem/ | Open/close one UI panel. `isFirstEnable` pattern. Creates DataService on first enable. Zero business logic. |
| **Manager** | phase-All/1-Managers/ | Extends `Singleton<T>`. Owns one domain's state. Minimal query API. |
| **Phase{X}LOG** | 4-Utils/ | `LIST_X__TO__JSON` + `DOC_X__TO__JSON` for every DataService collection. |

### noimnot-Specific Role Mapping

| Original Class | Becomes | Role |
|----------------|---------|------|
| CharacterSOData | SO_CharacterDef | Pure data — character sprites, emotions, eyes |
| CharacterSOData → WCharacter | WCharacter | Mutable session state — room, alive, imposter, talkCount |
| CharactersManager | CharactersManager : Singleton | Manager — lifecycle: let-in, refuse, kill, exile |
| DialogManager | DialogManager | Orchestrator-like — wire dialog flow, YarnSpinner |
| DayNightController | DayNightController : Singleton | Manager — day/night cycle |
| RoomsManager | RoomsManager : Singleton | Manager — 7-room state |
| ResourceMother | SPLIT into SO_ files | Each interface → separate SO_ per system |
| All ViewProviders | Removed | Replaced by [SerializeField] + Owner chain |
| All Mock classes | Removed | GameEvents decoupling makes mocks unnecessary |

---

## Decoupling

- **GameEvents** for cross-system communication — static partial class
- **`[SerializeField]`** for same-GO references — always private
- **Owner chain** for parent-child references
- **Singleton reads ONLY** for queries (`Singleton<X>.Ins.GetValue()`), NEVER cross-system commands
- **Separate Open/Close events** per UI panel
- **ZERO `FindObjectOfType`** in MonoBehaviours
- **`// purpose:`** comment on EVERY `.Raise...()` call and `+=` subscription
- **GameEvents use interfaces in signatures, NEVER concrete classes**

### Replacing Zenject DI

| Zenject Pattern | Our Replacement |
|----------------|-----------------|
| `[Inject] IFoo foo` | `Singleton<FooManager>.Ins.GetValue()` |
| Constructor injection | `[SerializeField]` + Owner chain |
| `Container.Bind<IFoo>().To<Foo>()` | `Singleton<T>` self-registration in Awake |
| `FromComponentInHierarchy()` | `[SerializeField]` drag in inspector |
| ViewProvider interfaces | `[SerializeField]` drag in inspector |
| Mock implementations | Not needed — GameEvents decoupling |

### Replacing event Func<T> (Query Events)

Original uses `event Func<T>` for cross-system queries (e.g., dialog asking rooms manager for character list). Replace with:

```csharp
// ORIGINAL: event Func<List<Character>> OnGetCharacters;
// REPLACEMENT: Singleton read
var characters = Singleton<CharactersManager>.Ins.GetCharactersInHouse();
```

Singleton reads for queries are fine — they're read-only, no coupling.

---

## No Defensive Null Checks

Let it crash. The crash is traceable.
Inspector refs, core singletons, `Field_` components — your responsibility to wire.
Don't hide bugs behind `?.` or `if (x != null)`.

**Exception:** `?.Invoke()` on GameEvents (events can have zero subscribers).

---

## Unity Lifecycle Order

```
Awake() → OnEnable() → Start() → Update() → LateUpdate()
                                                    ↓
                                              OnDisable() → OnDestroy()
```

### SubManager Pattern — `isFirstEnable`

```csharp
bool isFirstEnable = true;
private void OnEnable()
{
    if (isFirstEnable)
    {
        GameEvents.OnOpenThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(true);
        GameEvents.OnCloseThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        isFirstEnable = false;
        return;
    }
    GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: true);
}
private void OnDisable() => GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: false);
```

---

## C# Features (Unity 6000.3+)

**Allowed:**
- `$""` string interpolation, `?.` null-conditional, `??` null-coalescing
- `=>` expression-bodied, ternary `? :`
- LINQ: `.Select()`, `.Where()`, `.Any()`, `.All()`, `.First()`, `.Count()`, `.ToDictionary()`
- Custom extensions: `.map()`, `.find()`, `.all()`, `.sum()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, `.toggle()`, `.getRandom()`, `.colorTag()`, `.repeat()`, `.ToNSJson()`, `.GetOrCreate()`
- `var` for obvious types

**Not allowed:** `async/await` (use coroutines), `{ get; set; }` property accessors (use `Get.../Set...()` methods)

**Exception for properties:** `[Serializable]` entities, SO_ (pure data), interface contracts, static collections

---

## LEGO Independence

Each system works **standalone first**, connects later via GameEvents.

```
Type DataService → test with DEBUG_Check (no UI, no scene)
Type SubManager + Orchestrator → test with system-specific test
Type MonoBehaviour → test independently
Connect via GameEvents → full phase works
```

---

## 10 Common Pitfalls (noimnot-specific)

1. **SetActive cascading** — parent disables ALL children. Use siblings for independent panels.
2. **Scene instance vs prefab** — drag from Hierarchy, not Project panel.
3. **raycastTarget** — ghost=false, slot bg=true, icon=false.
4. **Swap contents not objects** — swap `.characterType` field, not entire room slot.
5. **Missing layers** — `LayerMask.NameToLayer` returns -1 silently.
6. **Cross-phase mods not applied** — compile error or silent failure.
7. **Dual collider setup** — trigger + physical on separate child GOs.
8. **Static lists surviving domain reload** — second Play press has stale null refs.
9. **Execution order** — `[DefaultExecutionOrder]` on managers, don't remove.
10. **YarnSpinner integration** — FakeDialogRunner has custom node parsing, don't use standard Yarn runner directly.

---

## 17 Common Agent Mistakes

1. Any `FindObjectOfType` in MonoBehaviours → use `[SerializeField]`, Owner chain, or GameEvents
2. Public methods nobody calls externally → audit every public method
3. Missing `// purpose:` on Raise/Subscribe calls → mandatory on every single one
4. Tight coupling (Script A directly calls Script B across systems) → decouple via GameEvents
5. Defensive null checks on inspector refs → let it crash
6. RefreshAll() in Update() (polling) → make it event-driven
7. DataService that needs Unity physics/lifecycle → question if it should be a DataService
8. Missing `isFirstEnable` pattern on SubManagers → every SubManager MUST use it
9. Toggle instead of separate Open/Close events → every UI panel needs SEPARATE events
10. Using `Input.GetKeyDown` directly in SubManagers → SubManagers don't handle input
11. Methods on SO_ classes → SO_ = pure data, zero methods. Move to consumer
12. Singleton in `3-MonoBehaviours/` instead of `1-Managers/` → Singleton<T> = always 1-Managers/
13. Collections left in MonoBehaviour instead of DataService → extract to DataService
14. Missing PhaseXLOG methods → every collection gets its own LOG method
15. Missing `RefreshAllRequired()` in Orchestrator → after every data mutation, refresh UI
16. Missing `DOC__X__Field` in Orchestrator → must track data→Field_ mapping
17. Reimplementing Zenject patterns → use Singleton<T> + GameEvents, not constructor injection

---

## Script Structure

`#region` blocks order (MANDATORY):

```
#region Inspector Fields       ← [SerializeField] refs (MonoBehaviours only)
#endregion
#region private API            ← runtime state + internal helpers
#endregion
#region Public API             ← or subdivide: #region ICharactersManager, etc.
#endregion
#region Extra                  ← nice-to-have features
#endregion
#region Unity Life Cycle       ← Awake, Start, OnEnable, Update — LAST
#endregion
```

DataService-specific order:
```
#region private API            ← collection fields
#endregion
#region Nested Type            ← public nested entity classes
#endregion
#region public API             ← Build, Get, Add, Remove, boolean questions
#endregion
#region snapShot               ← GetSnapShotForTest()
#endregion
```

### Class Summary — First Person "I"

```csharp
/// <summary>
/// I manage the 7-room apartment — which characters are in which room,
/// room transitions, entrance door state, character placement.
/// Who uses me: DialogManager, DayNightController, PlayerController.
/// Events I subscribe to: OnCharacterLetIn, OnCharacterKilled.
/// </summary>
public class RoomsManager : Singleton<RoomsManager>
```

### Interface Summary — Owner/Implementor/Caller

```csharp
/// <summary>
/// Contract for character lifecycle management — let-in, refuse, kill, exile.
/// CharacterSystem (Phase C) OWNS this interface.
/// Implementors: CharactersManager (Phase C).
/// Callers: DialogManager (Phase D), DayNightController (Phase G).
/// </summary>
public interface ICharactersManager
```

- **FLOW.md** — story-style data flows. **bold** = visible change, *italic* = context, `code` = exact reference
- **Script summaries** — first person "I"
- **Test instructions** — assume reader has never opened Unity

```
Read these files in this order:
1. LEARN/noimnot/GOAL.md — all architecture rules, folder structure, naming conventions, minimal API, decoupling, vertical slice tests
2. LEARN/noimnot/PhaseMap.md — [PHASE_X] section (files, modifications, vertical slice tests)

4. LEARN/noimnot/handTyped(latest)/ — my ACTUAL hand-typed code (ground truth for coding style)
5. MAIN-SOURCE/noimnot/Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/ — original source for [PHASE_X] files
6. Completed phase folders in LEARN/noimnot/ — reference for established _-Systems/ patterns

End goal: 100% main-source functionality with clean architecture — systems communicate via interfaces + GameEvents, making them portable to future Unity projects.

Now build [PHASE_X] with:
- 100% main source functionality + portable systems
- _-Systems/ architecture: each feature = self-contained folder with ALL code
- [AddComponentMenu("noimnot/Category/ClassName")] on every MonoBehaviour
- Least possible public API exposure
- Zero FindObjectOfType — use GameEvents + [SerializeField] + Owner chain
- partial GameEvents in phase-X/0-Core/GameEvents.cs
- Vertical slice tests per system with full GUIDE.md
- One-liner purpose per script — if it doesn't fit one sentence, split it
```

---

## Project-Specific Notes

### Original Architecture → Our Architecture
| Original | Replacement |
|----------|------------|
| Zenject DI (`[Inject]`, `Container.Bind`) | `Singleton<T>` + `[SerializeField]` |
| UniTask (`async/await`) | Coroutines (`IEnumerator`, `StartCoroutine`) |
| `event Action<T>` / `event Func<T>` | `partial class GameEvents` with `LogSubscribersCount` |
| ViewProvider pattern | `[SerializeField]` + Owner chain |
| ResourceMother (11 interfaces) | Per-system `SO_` files |
| ASavableClass<T> | DataService + DataModelService save/load |
| IUpdateable custom loop | Standard Unity `Update()` |
| Mock classes | Removed (GameEvents makes mocks unnecessary) |
| Constructor injection | `[SerializeField]` + `Singleton<T>.Ins` reads |

### Key Game Constants
- 75 character types (ECharacterType)
- 7 rooms (ERoom)
- 13 endings (EEnding)
- 11 consumable types (EConsumable)
- 6 scenes
- 34 dialog events (from original DialogManager)

### God Object Split Plan
| Original | Split Into |
|----------|-----------|
| DialogManager (~1108 lines) | DialogManager, GunController, SubtitleController, DialogEventDispatcher |
| DayNightController (~400 lines) | DayNightController, EnergyController, BodyEaterController, BedController |
| RoomsManager (~210 lines) | RoomsManager, WhisperController, CorpseManager |
| GameEventsManager (~315 lines) | GameEventsManager, EventScheduler, DingDongGenerator |

- `/// <summary>` on every class and method
- `// →` inline flow markers
- `// purpose:` on every Raise/Subscribe
- Zero `FindObjectOfType`
- Minimal public API
- `partial class GameEvents`
- DataService for every testable collection

| `LEARN/noimnot/PhaseMap.md` | Roadmap — all phases | Before building any phase |
| `LEARN/noimnot/StructureMap.md` | DataService specs | Before writing any DataService |
| `LEARN/noimnot/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Original source analysis | Source fidelity questions |
| `LEARN/noimnot/handTyped(latest)/` | User's actual code | Always — match this style |
| `MAIN-SOURCE/noimnot/Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/` | Original source | 100% behavior matching |
| `LEARN/noimnot/SystemPortabilityMap.md` | L0/L1+ per system | Portability questions |
| `LEARN/noimnot/CoverageMap.md` | Source file → phase mapping | Gap checking |

---

## Key Rules (Quick Reference)

- **One sentence per script.** Split until it fits.
- **`_-Systems/` is PRIMARY.** Everything inside system folder.
- **GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes.**
- **Separate Open/Close events** per UI panel.
- **`isFirstEnable`** on ALL SubManagers.
- **DataService** for every `List<T>` / `Dictionary<K,V>` testable via `new`.
- **PhaseXLOG** for every collection.
- **`// purpose:`** on every Raise/Subscribe.
- **No defensive null checks.** Let it crash.
- **No `{ get; set; }`.** Use `Get.../Set...()`.
- **No `FindObjectOfType`.** Use GameEvents + `[SerializeField]`.
- **`[AddComponentMenu("noimnot/...")]`** on every MonoBehaviour.

---

## 23 Common Agent Mistakes

1. `FindObjectOfType` in MonoBehaviours → use `[SerializeField]`, Owner chain, or GameEvents
2. Public methods nobody calls → audit every public method
3. Missing `// purpose:` on Raise/Subscribe → mandatory on every one
4. Tight coupling across systems → decouple via GameEvents
5. Defensive null checks on inspector refs → let it crash
6. RefreshAll() in Update() → event-driven
7. DataService needing Unity physics → question if it should be a DataService
8. Missing `isFirstEnable` → every SubManager MUST use it
9. Toggle instead of separate Open/Close → SEPARATE events
10. Input.GetKeyDown in SubManagers → SubManagers don't handle input
11. Methods on SO_ → SO_ = pure data, zero methods
12. Singleton in 3-MonoBehaviours/ → Singleton<T> = always 1-Managers/
13. Collections in MonoBehaviour → extract to DataService
14. Missing PhaseXLOG → every collection gets LOG method
15. Missing RefreshAllRequired() → after every mutation, refresh UI
16. Missing DOC__X__Field → track data→Field_ mapping
17. Not using custom extensions → use .map(), .gc<T>(), .destroyLeaves(), .toggle()
18. Bridge on portable side → Bridges on non-portable/game-specific side
19. Interface/ not used for 2+ interfaces → use subfolder
20. FindObjectOfType instead of FindObjectsByType → use FindObjectsByType
21. Wrong bridge pattern → match data-flow direction
22. `#region Private Fields` → correct name is `private API`
23. Field_ with [SerializeField] → Field_ uses public refs, not [SerializeField]

---

| Phase | Domain | Status |
|-------|--------|--------|
| All | Shared Foundation | Not started |
| A | Core Data + Events + Persistence | Not started |

| L | DI Adaption + Remaining Utilities | Not started |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/OptionalFeatures.md ==== -->
# OptionalFeatures — noimnot (Not I'm Not)

> Features outside mandatory 100% scope. Polish, extras, external package integration.

---

## Scope Definition

**Mandatory (100%):** Every feature the original game has — all 75 characters, 7 rooms, 13 endings, full dialog system, day/night cycle, consumables, interactables, save/load, settings, pause, sound, Steam integration.

**Optional:** Features that enhance the experience but are not in the original decompiled source, OR polish improvements that go beyond source parity.

---

## Optional Feature List

### 1. Feel/MMFeedbacks Integration
**What:** Juice effects on interactions — screen shake on gun shot, haptic feedback on door knock, UI punch on dialog option hover.
**Integration Point:** GameEvents subscribers — subscribe to OnGunShot, OnDingDong, OnDialogOptionSelected and trigger feedback.
**Phase:** Post-phase-F (after interactables exist)
**Priority:** Low — original game uses DOTween for animations, not MMFeedbacks.

### 2. Enhanced Debug Console
**What:** In-game debug console with command system — skip to day, force ending, spawn character, toggle noclip.
**Integration Point:** IngameDebugConsole (already in source) + custom commands via GameEvents.
**Phase:** Post-phase-I (after settings/debug infrastructure)
**Priority:** Medium — useful for testing but not player-facing.

### 3. Achievement Trophy Room
**What:** Visual 3D room displaying earned achievements as trophies.
**Integration Point:** SteamAchievements tracking + custom 3D scene.
**Phase:** Post-phase-K (after Steam system)
**Priority:** Low — not in original.

### 4. Photo Mode
**What:** Pause game, free camera, take screenshots.
**Integration Point:** PauseController + free camera system.
**Phase:** Post-phase-I
**Priority:** Low — not in original.

### 5. Accessibility Options
**What:** Colorblind modes, larger text, high contrast UI, subtitles sizing.
**Integration Point:** SettingsSystem + shader variants + TextMeshPro sizing.
**Phase:** Post-phase-I
**Priority:** Medium — improves inclusivity.

### 6. Custom Shader Effects
**What:** The original has 80+ shaders including CRT, DeathSphere, NorthernLight, RainDrop, etc. Some are Unity built-in URP, others are custom.
**Integration Point:** EffectsSystem + material swaps.
**Phase:** During phase-J (effects phase)
**Priority:** High — these ARE in the original source. Custom shaders should be recreated or approximated.

### 7. Localization Beyond Base Languages
**What:** Original supports multiple languages via Unity Localization. Extending to additional languages.
**Integration Point:** LocalizationSystem + additional locale tables.
**Phase:** Post-phase-L
**Priority:** Low — base languages are mandatory, extensions are optional.

### 8. Analytics / Telemetry
**What:** Track player choices, ending distribution, average play time per day.
**Integration Point:** GameEvents subscribers — log to local file or analytics service.
**Phase:** Post-phase-K
**Priority:** Low — not in original.

### 9. Mod Support
**What:** Allow custom character sprites, dialog trees, room layouts via modding API.
**Integration Point:** SO_ loading from StreamingAssets + custom Yarn file loading.
**Phase:** Post-all-phases
**Priority:** Very Low — significant engineering effort, not in original.

### 10. Procedural Character Generation
**What:** Generate characters beyond the 75 hardcoded types using sprite composition.
**Integration Point:** CharacterSystem + sprite sheet assembly.
**Phase:** Post-phase-C
**Priority:** Very Low — not in original, would fundamentally change the game.

---

## `#region Extra` Placement Guide

For features that ARE in the original source but are low-priority for the rebuild:

| Feature | Phase | `#region Extra` Location | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------------------------|-------|
| Arabic RTL text support | L | Utils.cs | Third-party utility, skip initially |
| Gamepad cursor simulation | E | PlayerSystem/GamepadCursor.cs | Entire file is `#region Extra` |
| Showcase/demo mode | L | ShowcaseSystem/ | Entire system is `#region Extra` |
| Gacha collection gallery | K | GachaSystem/ | Low priority, works without it |
| Custom balance tuning (SimpleBalancer) | L | Separate utility | SO-based value tuning |
| DOTween cat pathing | K | CatSystem/CatPathController.cs | Visual polish |
| Parallax mouse effect | L | ParallaxSystem/ | Visual polish |

---

## External Package Dependencies

| Package | Required For | Mandatory? | Notes |
|---------|-------------|-----------|-------|
| TextMeshPro | All UI text | Yes | Unity built-in |
| Universal Render Pipeline | Rendering | Yes | Unity built-in |
| Cinemachine | Camera system | Yes | Unity built-in, used for player camera |
| Unity Input System | Input handling | Yes | New Input System |
| Unity Localization | Multi-language | Yes | For base language support |
| DOTween | Animations | Yes | UI animations, camera movement, cat pathing |
| YarnSpinner | Dialog system | Yes | Branching dialog (FakeDialogRunner wraps it) |
| ECM2 | Player movement | Yes | Easy Character Movement 2 |
| Steamworks.NET | Steam integration | Conditional | Only if targeting Steam |
| Newtonsoft.Json | Save serialization | Yes | JSON save data |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/PhaseMap.md ==== -->
# noimnot — PhaseMap

> All phases, files, modifications, vertical slice tests, gap audit.
> Each phase depends only on earlier phases — never forward.
> SIZE CAP: Max ~25 scripts per phase.

---

## Phase Dependency Order (DAG)

```
phase-All ← phase-A ← phase-B ← phase-C ← phase-D ← phase-E
                                         ↖ phase-F
                                                    ← phase-G ← phase-H
                                                              ↖ phase-I
                                                                        ← phase-J
```

All arrows point LEFT (to earlier phases). No forward references.

---

## phase-All — Shared Foundation

**Domain:** Core infrastructure shared by ALL phases.

| File | Folder | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| Singleton.cs | 0-Core/ | Generic singleton base — first instance wins |
| GameEvents.cs | 0-Core/ | Core shared events (OnMenuStateChanged, OnCloseAllSubManagers) |
| UIManager.cs | 1-Managers/ | Menu state tracking, CloseAllSubManager(), keyboard routing |
| DataManager.cs | 1-Managers/ | Shared visual data: materials, layer masks |
| GlobalEnumsAll.cs | 2-Data/Enums/ | TagType enum, shared enums |
| Utils.cs | 4-Utils/ | Extensions: HasTag, SetTag, .map(), .gc<T>(), .destroyLeaves(), .toggle(), TimeSince/TimeUntil |
| MODEL.md | 7-3D/ | 3D model conventions |
| ANIM.md | 7-3D/ | Animation conventions |
| WORLD.md | 7-3D/ | World/level design conventions |

**Scripts: 6** | Difficulty: Simple | Weight: 5%

---

| AEnumEventBus.cs | _-Systems/EventBusSystem/ | Generic typed enum event bus |

| PrefsDataStorage.cs | _-Systems/DataModelSystem/ | PlayerPrefs storage |

| Dependency.md | _-Systems/DataModelSystem/ | System dependencies |
| Test.md | _-Systems/DataModelSystem/ | Vertical slice test |

**Scripts: 23** | Difficulty: Medium | Weight: 10%

### What It Looks Like When Running
You have a save/load system that persists data to PlayerPrefs (regular and encrypted) and RAM. The event bus fires typed enum events. All the enums that drive the game are defined. You can save and load arbitrary data, encrypt it, and verify it round-trips correctly.

### Vertical Slice Tests
- **DataModelSystem:** Create DataModelService → save data to all 3 layers → load → verify round-trip. Test AES encryption separately.
- **EventBusSystem:** Fire enum events → verify subscribers receive them.

Player interacts with objects in the 3D world (hatch, phone, radio, cigarettes, mushrooms, cat, windows, calendar, save point). Close-up views show detailed interactions (fridge contents, phone dialing, radio tuning). Consumables are used.

### Modifications to Earlier Phases
| File | How | Change | Why |
|------|-----|--------|-----|
| GameEvents.cs | partial | Add interactable/close-up events | Interaction communication |

### Vertical Slice Tests
- **InteractableSystem:** Interact with each type → verify behavior.
- **CloseUpSystem:** Open fridge → phone → radio → verify views.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/StructureMap.md ==== -->
# StructureMap — noimnot (Not I'm Not)

> DataService specs per phase: collections, methods, nested types. SO_/Field_/W prefix assignments. Folder trees per system.

---

## Naming Conventions

| Prefix | Meaning | Example |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `SO_` | ScriptableObject — inspector-editable config, zero methods | `SO_CharacterDef`, `SO_DayNightConfig` |
| `Field_` | Display-only prefab handle — SetData/SetState only | `Field_DialogOption`, `Field_EndingCard` |
| `W` | DataWrapper — mutable session state around SO_ | `WCharacter`, `WConsumable` |
| `DataService` | Pure C# collection service — `new`-able, no MonoBehaviour | `CharacterDataService`, `ConsumableDataService` |
| `I` prefix | Interface contract | `ICharacterProvider`, `IDayNightState` |
| `_-Systems/` | System folder — all feature code inside | `_-Systems/DialogSystem/` |

---

## phase-All — Shared Foundation

No `_-Systems/` folders — these are foundational utilities shared by all phases.

```
phase-All/
├── 0-Core/
│   ├── Singleton.cs              → generic Singleton<T> base
│   └── GameEvents.cs             → partial class, core events only
├── 1-Managers/
│   ├── UIManager.cs              → menu state, CloseAllSubManager()
│   └── DataManager.cs            → shared visual data, layer masks
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsAll.cs         → TagType enum
├── 4-Utils/
│   └── Utils.cs                  → HasTag, SetTag, .map(), .gc<T>(), .destroyLeaves(), .toggle(), TimeSince, TimeUntil, BetterUniTask extensions
└── 7-3D/
    ├── MODEL.md
    ├── ANIM.md
    └── WORLD.md
```

---

## phase-A — Core Data + Events + Persistence

### `_-Systems/DataModelSystem/` (20 files)

```
_-Systems/DataModelSystem/
├── Interface/
│   ├── IDataStorage.cs
│   ├── IDataModelService.cs
│   ├── IGameSaveDataHandler.cs
│   └── ISimplePrefsDataHandler.cs
├── ASavableData.cs
├── ASavableClass.cs
├── ABaseDataHandler.cs
├── DataModelService.cs
├── GameSaveDataHandler.cs
├── BaseDataStorage.cs
├── PrefsDataStorage.cs
├── EncryptedPrefsDataStorage.cs
├── RamDataStorage.cs
├── AesCrypter.cs
├── SimplePrefsData.cs
├── SimplePrefsDataHandler.cs
├── SimpleDataType.cs
├── ESimpleDataType.cs
├── Dependency.md
└── Test.md
```

**DataService: DataModelService**
| Collection | Type | Methods |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `STORAGE_LAYERS` | `List<IDataStorage>` | `AddLayer()`, `GetLayer()`, `SaveAll()`, `LoadAll()` |
| `SAVE_HANDLERS` | `List<ABaseDataHandler>` | `RegisterHandler()`, `GetHandler<T>()` |

**No SO_/Field_/W** — this is pure infrastructure.

### `_-Systems/EventBusSystem/` (2 files)

```
_-Systems/EventBusSystem/
├── AEnumEventBus.cs
└── CommonEnumEventBus.cs
```

**No DataService** — event bus is infrastructure.

### Other phase-A files
```
phase-A/
├── 0-Core/
│   └── GameEvents.cs             → partial: phase-A events
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsA.cs           → ETimeOfDay, ERoom, ELocation, ECharacterType (75), ECharacterPlace, ECharacterSign, EConsumable, EEnding, EDream, ESound, EPhoneSubscriber, EControl, EInputDevice, EDialogs, ECommonEvents, ELanguage

## phase-C — Characters + Rooms + State Objects

### `_-Systems/CharacterSystem/` (additions)

```
_-Systems/CharacterSystem/        ← adds to phase-B folder
├── Interface/
│   ├── ICharactersSODataProvider.cs   (from B)
│   └── ICharactersManager.cs          (NEW)
├── CharactersManager.cs               (NEW)
├── CharactersManagerSaveData.cs       (NEW)
└── ...existing from B...
```

**DataService: CharacterDataService**
| Collection | Type | Methods |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `DOC__type_wCharacter` | `Dictionary<ECharacterType, WCharacter>` | `Get(type)`, `GetAll()`, `GetByRoom(room)`, `GetAlive()` |
| `VISIT_HISTORY` | `List<CharacterVisitData>` | `AddVisit()`, `GetVisits(type)` |
| `CHARACTERS_IN_HOUSE` | `List<ECharacterType>` | `LetIn(type)`, `Exile(type)`, `Kill(type)` |
| `DEAD_CHARACTERS` | `List<ECharacterType>` | `MarkDead(type)`, `IsDead(type)` |

**WCharacter:**
| Field | Type | Source |
|-------|------|--------|
| `characterType` | `ECharacterType` | SO_CharacterDef |
| `currentRoom` | `ERoom` | Runtime |
| `isAlive` | `bool` | Runtime |
| `place` | `ECharacterPlace` | Runtime |
| `sign` | `ECharacterSign` | Runtime |
| `talkCount` | `int` | Runtime |
| `isImposter` | `bool` | Runtime |

### `_-Systems/RoomSystem/` (additions — 7 rooms + manager)

```
_-Systems/RoomSystem/             ← adds to phase-B folder
├── Interface/
│   ├── IRoomsManager.cs               (NEW)
│   ├── IRoomsViewProvider.cs          (NEW)
│   └── IRoomDisplayerViewProvider.cs  (NEW)
├── RoomsManager.cs                    (NEW)
├── RoomsSaveData.cs                   (NEW)
├── RoomDisplayer.cs                   (NEW)
├── EntranceRoom.cs                    (NEW)

## phase-L — DI Adaption + Remaining Utilities

### `_-Systems/DISystem/` (5 files — REFERENCE ONLY, not rebuilt)

These Zenject installers are **reference material** for wiring order. The rebuild uses Singleton<T> + GameEvents — no Zenject.

```
_-Systems/DISystem/               ← REFERENCE, adapt to Singleton pattern
├── CommonInstaller.cs            → maps to Singleton<T> registrations
├── GameplaySceneInstaller.cs     → maps to scene manager init order
├── MainSceneInstaller.cs         → main menu init
├── StrangeMorningSceneInstaller.cs → dream scene init
└── TitlesSceneInstaller.cs       → titles scene init
```

```
_-Systems/ConfigSystem/
├── ResourceMother.cs             → SPLIT into per-system SO_ files
├── Dependency.md
└── Test.md
```

**ResourceMother Split Plan:**
| Interface | Goes To |
|-----------|---------|
| ICharactersSODataProvider | SO_CharacterDef (phase-B) |
| ICursorDataProvider | SO_CursorConfig (phase-B) |
| IDayNightControllerSODataProvider | SO_DayNightConfig (phase-G) |
| IGameEventsManagerSODataProvider | SO_GameEventsConfig (phase-G) |
| IRandomGenerationSettingsSODataProvider | SO_RandomConfig (phase-G) |
| IEndingSODataProvider | SO_EndingsConfig (phase-H) |
| IEndingDataProvider | SO_EndingViewConfig (phase-H) |
| IWindowsSODataProvider | SO_WindowsConfig (phase-C) |
| ISoundServiceInstanceProvider | SO_MusicConfig (phase-J) |
| IRandomSoundsEmitterSODataViewProvider | SO_RandomEmitterConfig (phase-J) |

## DataService Summary (All Phases)

| Phase | DataService | Key Collections |
|-------|------------|----------------|
| A | DataModelService | STORAGE_LAYERS, SAVE_HANDLERS |
| C | CharacterDataService | DOC__type_wCharacter, CHARACTERS_IN_HOUSE, DEAD_CHARACTERS |

| C | WindowDataService | BOARDED_WINDOWS |
| D | DialogDataService | DOC__dialog_talkCount, DOC__character_totalTalks, DIALOG_HISTORY |
| D | YarnReaderDataService | DOC__node_content, VARIABLE_STORAGE |
| E | PlayerDataService | POSITION, CURRENT_ROOM, CURRENT_LOCATION |
| E | WatcherDataService | ACTIVE_WATCHERS, CURRENT_FOCUS |
| F | InteractableDataService | DOC__id_enabled, DOC__id_interactable |
| G | DayNightDataService | CURRENT_DAY, CURRENT_TIME, TIME_HISTORY |
| G | GameEventDataService | SCHEDULED_EVENTS, DOC__day_events, COMPLETED_EVENTS |
| G | ConsumableDataService | DOC__type_count, USED_TODAY |
| G | RandomizerDataService | GENERATED_FOR_DAY, IMPOSTER_POOL |
| H | EndingDataService | ENDINGS, TRIGGERED_ENDINGS, ACTIVE_ENDING |
| H | LocationDataService | CURRENT_LOCATION, UNLOCKED_LOCATIONS |
| J | SoundDataService | ACTIVE_SOURCES, DOC__sound_source, MUSIC_SCHEDULE |
| K | CatDataService | PATH_POINTS, CAT_STATE |
| K | DreamDataService | DREAM_HISTORY |
| K | GachaDataService | UNLOCKED_ENDINGS, UNLOCKED_CHARACTERS |

**Total: 20 DataServices across 9 phases.**

## SO_ Assignment Summary

| SO_ Name | Phase | Original | Contents |
|----------|-------|----------|----------|
| SO_CharacterDef | B | CharacterSOData | Character sprites, emotions, eyes, signs, type |
| SO_CursorConfig | B | CursorSOData | Cursor sprites, hover states |
| SO_WindowsConfig | C | WindowsSOData | Window boarding config |
| SO_DayNightConfig | G | DayNightControllerSOData | Day timing, transition durations, visual params |
| SO_GameEventsConfig | G | GameEventsManagerSOData | Event scheduling rules, DingDong timing |
| SO_RandomConfig | G | RandomGenerationSettingsSOData | Imposter probability, character pool settings |
| SO_EndingViewConfig | H | EndingViewSOData | Ending display visuals |
| SO_EndingsConfig | H | EndingsSOData | All ending definitions |
| SO_MusicConfig | J | MusicSOData | Per-day music tracks |
| SO_RandomEmitterConfig | J | RandomSoundsEmitterSOData | Ambient sound emitter settings |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/SystemIsolationAnalysis.md ==== -->
# SystemIsolationAnalysis — noimnot (Not I'm Not)

> Communication matrix, interface ownership, bridge catalog, concrete dependency audit.

---

## Communication Matrix

How systems talk to each other. ✅ = via GameEvents/interface (clean). ❌ = concrete dependency (needs fix). — = no communication.

| From ↓ / To → | Character | Room | Dialog | Player | Interactable | DayNight | GameEvents | Consumable | Ending | Sound | Effects | Cat | Dream |
|---------------|-----------|------|--------|--------|-------------|----------|-----------|-----------|--------|-------|---------|-----|-------|
| **Character** | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Room** | ✅ event | — | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Dialog** | ✅ Ins read | ✅ Ins read | — | ✅ event | — | ✅ Ins read | — | ✅ Ins read | ✅ event | ✅ Ins read | — | ✅ event | — |
| **Player** | — | ✅ event | ✅ event | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Interactable** | — | — | ✅ event | ✅ event | — | — | — | ✅ event | ✅ event | ✅ Ins | — | ✅ event | — |
| **DayNight** | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ Ins | — | ✅ event | — | ✅ event | — | ✅ event |
| **GameEvents** | ✅ Ins | — | — | — | — | ✅ Ins | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Consumable** | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — |
| **Ending** | ✅ Ins | — | ✅ event | — | — | ✅ Ins | — | ✅ Ins | — | ✅ Ins | ✅ event | — | — |
| **Sound** | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Effects** | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Cat** | — | ✅ Ins | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Dream** | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — | — | — | ✅ event | — | — |

**Legend:**
- `✅ event` = GameEvents (fully decoupled)
- `✅ Ins read` = `Singleton<X>.Ins.GetY()` (read-only query, acceptable)
- `❌` = concrete dependency (tight coupling, needs refactoring)

All cross-system communication uses either GameEvents or Singleton reads — no concrete dependencies across `_-Systems/` folders.

---

## Interface Ownership

| Interface | Owning System | Phase | Shape |
|-----------|--------------|-------|-------|
| IDataStorage | DataModelSystem | A | 🕷️ Spider |
| IDataModelService | DataModelSystem | A | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICharactersSODataProvider | CharacterSystem | B | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICursorController | CursorSystem | B | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICursorDataProvider | CursorSystem | B | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICharactersManager | CharacterSystem | C | 🕷️ Spider |
| IRoomsManager | RoomSystem | C | 🕷️ Spider |
| IStateObjectController | StateObjectSystem | C | 🕷️ Spider |
| IWindowsManager | WindowSystem | C | 🕷️ Spider |
| IDialogManager | DialogSystem | D | 🕷️ Spider |
| IDialogUI | DialogSystem | D | 🕷️ Spider |
| IPlayerService | PlayerSystem | E | 🕷️ Spider |
| IHUDPresenter | HUDSystem | E | 🕷️ Spider |
| IInteractablesManager | InteractableSystem | F | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICloseUpsController | CloseUpSystem | F | 🕷️ Spider |
| IDayNightController | DayNightSystem | G | 🕷️ Spider |
| IGameEventsManager | GameEventsSystem | G | 🕷️ Spider |
| IConsumablesController | ConsumableSystem | G | 🕷️ Spider |
| IGameplayRandomizer | RandomizerSystem | G | 🕷️ Spider |
| IGameplayEndingManager | EndingSystem | H | 🕷️ Spider |
| ILocationsManager | LocationSystem | H | 🕷️ Spider |
| ISettingsManager | SettingsSystem | I | 🕷️ Spider |
| IPauseController | PauseSystem | I | 🕷️ Spider |
| ISoundService | SoundSystem | J | 🕷️ Spider |
| IEffectsController | EffectsSystem | J | 🕷️ Spider |
| ICatController | CatSystem | K | 🕷️ Spider |
| IDreamController | DreamSystem | K | 🕷️ Spider |

All interfaces are owned by the system that defines them. Each system is a 🕷️ Spider — it defines its own interface and receives implementations via `[SerializeField]` or Singleton registration.

---

## Bridge Catalog

In the original source, cross-system communication used Zenject DI injection. In our rebuild, we use three patterns:

### Pattern 1: Singleton Read (most common)
```
DialogManager reads Singleton<CharactersManager>.Ins.GetCharactersInHouse()
DialogManager reads Singleton<RoomsManager>.Ins.GetCurrentRoom()
EndingSystem reads Singleton<DayNightController>.Ins.GetCurrentDay()
EndingSystem reads Singleton<ConsumablesController>.Ins.GetCount(type)
```
No bridge script needed — direct Singleton read is clean for queries.

### Pattern 2: GameEvents (cross-system notifications)
```
CharactersManager fires → RaiseCharacterLetIn(type) → RoomsManager, DialogSystem subscribe
DayNightController fires → RaiseDayChanged(day) → GameEventsSystem, EffectsSystem subscribe
DialogManager fires → RaiseDialogStarted() → PlayerSystem, HUD subscribe
```
No bridge script needed — GameEvents partial class handles it.

### Pattern 3: Bridge Scripts (runtime context injection)
Only needed when a portable system needs game-specific context without importing game-specific types.

| Bridge | From System | To System | Data Flow | Phase |
|--------|------------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| (none identified) | — | — | — | — |

**Note:** noimnot's architecture doesn't require Bridge scripts because:
1. The original used Zenject DI → we replace with Singleton reads + GameEvents
2. Most cross-system communication is event-based (fire and forget)
3. Queries use Singleton<T>.Ins.GetX() — read-only, no coupling
4. The game is single-scene focused (s1_GameScene) so all managers coexist

If during implementation a portable system needs game-specific context, create a Bridge at that time.

---

## Concrete Dependency Audit

Systems that have concrete imports from other `_-Systems/` folders (needs architectural attention):

### DialogSystem (phase-D) — Most Connected
- Reads: CharactersManager, RoomsManager, DayNightController, ConsumablesController, SoundService
- All via Singleton reads (acceptable) or GameEvents (clean)
- **Verdict:** High coupling but via allowed patterns (Singleton reads + events). Acceptable because dialog is inherently cross-cutting.

### EndingSystem (phase-H) — Second Most Connected
- Reads: CharactersManager, DayNightController, ConsumablesController, SoundService
- Each ending checks different conditions → needs to read game state
- **Verdict:** Acceptable — endings are evaluation functions that read game state.

### InteractableSystem (phase-F) — Third Most Connected
- Each interactable type triggers different systems (dialog, close-up, save, window, ending)
- All via GameEvents (fire events, don't call directly)
- **Verdict:** Clean — interactables are event sources, not consumers.

### All Other Systems
- L0 portable systems: zero concrete dependencies
- L1 systems: single Singleton read or GameEvents subscription
- **Verdict:** Clean architecture

---

## Event Flow Diagram

```
                    ┌─────────────┐
                    │  GameEvents  │  (static partial class)
                    └──────┬──────┘
                           │
        ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
        │                  │                  │
   ┌────▼────┐       ┌────▼────┐       ┌────▼────┐
   │Character │       │  Room   │       │ Dialog  │
   │ Manager  │       │ Manager │       │ Manager │
   └────┬────┘       └────┬────┘       └────┬────┘
        │                  │                  │
   fires events:     fires events:      fires events:
   LetIn, Refused,   RoomChanged,       DialogStarted,
   Killed, Exiled    WindowBoarded      DialogEnded,
        │                  │             GunShot
        │                  │                  │
        ▼                  ▼                  ▼
   ┌─────────┐      ┌──────────┐      ┌──────────┐
   │DayNight │      │  Player  │      │   HUD    │
   │Controller│     │Controller│      │Presenter │
   └────┬────┘      └──────────┘      └──────────┘
        │
   fires events:
   DayChanged,
   TimeChanged
        │
   ┌────▼────────┐
   │ GameEvents  │
   │  Manager    │
   └─────┬──────┘
         │
    fires events:
    DingDong
         │
    ┌────▼────┐
    │Entrance │
    │  Room   │
    └─────────┘
```

## Isolation Score

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total systems | 35 |
| L0 portable | 20 (57%) |
| L1 (1 dep) | 8 (23%) |
| L2+ (2+ deps) | 7 (20%) |
| Concrete cross-system imports | 0 (all via Singleton reads or GameEvents) |
| Interfaces defined | 27 |
| GameEvents (projected) | ~23 event types |
| Bridge scripts needed | 0 |

**Overall isolation grade: A** — clean separation. No concrete cross-system dependencies. All communication via approved patterns (GameEvents, Singleton reads, interfaces).

---

## System Shapes

| Shape | Systems |
|-------|---------|
| 🕷️ Spider (defines interfaces) | CharacterSystem, RoomSystem, DialogSystem, PlayerSystem, DayNightSystem |
| 🔍 Hunter (scans via GetComponent) | InteractableSystem (scans for AInteractableObject), RaycastSystem |
| 🔌 Adapter (implements interfaces) | Each room (ARoom), each interactable (AInteractableObject), each ending (AGameplayEnding) |
| 📡 Broadcaster/Listener (GameEvents) | All systems use GameEvents; pure broadcasters: GameEventsSystem, DayNightSystem |
| 🌍 Infrastructure (FREE) | EventBusSystem, DataModelSystem, SoundSystem, SettingsSystem, PauseSystem |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/0-Core/GameEvents.cs ==== -->
using System;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I deliver messages between systems. Each phase adds events via partial class.
/// Every Raise logs subscriber count for debugging.
/// </summary>
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when menu state changes >>
    public static event Action<bool> OnMenuStateChanged;
    public static void RaiseMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnMenuStateChanged), OnMenuStateChanged);
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged?.Invoke(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }
    // << when menu state changes

    // when all sub-managers must close >>
    public static event Action OnCloseAllSubManagers;
    public static void RaiseCloseAllSubManagers()
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnCloseAllSubManagers), OnCloseAllSubManagers);
        GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers?.Invoke();
    }
    // << when all sub-managers must close

    /// <summary> Logs how many subscribers an event has — helps catch missing/extra subscriptions. </summary>
    static void LogSubscribersCount(string name, Delegate del)
    {
        int count = del?.GetInvocationList()?.Length ?? 0;
        Debug.Log($"[GameEvents] {name} raised for -> {count} subscribers");
    }
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/0-Core/Singleton.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I ensure one instance of any manager. First instance wins — duplicates destroyed.
/// Access via Singleton<T>.Ins from any script.
/// </summary>
public abstract class Singleton<T> : MonoBehaviour where T : MonoBehaviour
{
    public static T Ins { get; private set; }

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        if (Ins == null) Ins = this as T;
        else if (Ins != this)
        {
            Debug.Log($"{typeof(T)} singleton already exists, destroying duplicate");
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/1-Managers/DataManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I hold shared visual data — materials, layer masks, colors — used by multiple systems.
/// </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("noimnot/Managers/DataManager")]
public class DataManager : Singleton<DataManager>
{
    #region Inspector Fields
    [SerializeField] LayerMask _interactableLayerMask;
    [SerializeField] LayerMask _roomObjectLayerMask;
    #endregion
    #region Public API
    public LayerMask GetInteractableLayerMask() => _interactableLayerMask;
    public LayerMask GetRoomObjectLayerMask() => _roomObjectLayerMask;
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/1-Managers/UIManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I report menu state + close all panels when needed.
/// Tracks how many menus are open. Routes keyboard input with priority.
/// </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("noimnot/Managers/UIManager")]
public class UIManager : Singleton<UIManager>
{
    #region private API
    int openMenuCount = 0;
    #endregion
    #region Public API
    /// <summary> Returns true if any SubManager menu is currently open. </summary>
    public bool IsAnyMenuOpen() => openMenuCount > 0;

    /// <summary> Fires OnCloseAllSubManagers — every SubManager subscribes and self-disables. </summary>
    public void CloseAllSubManager()
    {
        // purpose: all SubManagers self-disable on this event
        GameEvents.RaiseCloseAllSubManagers();
    }
    #endregion
    #region Unity Life Cycle
    protected override void Awake()
    {
        base.Awake();
        // purpose: track open menu count for IsAnyMenuOpen()
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged += HandleMenuStateChanged;
    }

    void HandleMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        // → increment or decrement open menu count
        openMenuCount += isAnyMenuOpen ? 1 : -1;
        if (openMenuCount < 0) openMenuCount = 0;
    }
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/2-Data/Enums/GlobalEnumsAll.cs ==== -->
/// <summary>
/// Unity tags as enum — no raw string tags. Grows across phases.
/// Use HasTag(TagType.x) and SetTag(TagType.x) extension methods from Utils.cs.
/// </summary>
public enum TagType
{
    untagged,
    player,
    interactable,
    roomObject,
    character,
    door,
    window,
    savePoint,
    // Phase B+: add new tags here
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/4-Utils/Utils.cs ==== -->
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I hold all shared utility extensions and static helpers.
/// Grows across phases via #region sections. No per-phase UtilsPhaseX.cs.
/// </summary>
public static class Utils
{
    #region Tag
    /// <summary> Check if GO has a TagType — replaces CompareTag("string"). </summary>
    public static bool HasTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag) => go.CompareTag(tag.ToString());
    public static bool HasTag(this Collider col, TagType tag) => col.CompareTag(tag.ToString());

    /// <summary> Set GO tag from TagType — replaces tag = "string". </summary>
    public static void SetTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag) => go.tag = tag.ToString();
    #endregion
    #region Collection
    /// <summary> LINQ Select shorthand — maps each element. </summary>
    public static List<TResult> map<T, TResult>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, TResult> selector)
        => source.Select(selector).ToList();

    /// <summary> LINQ FirstOrDefault shorthand. </summary>
    public static T find<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, bool> predicate)
        => source.FirstOrDefault(predicate);

    /// <summary> LINQ All shorthand. </summary>
    public static bool all<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, bool> predicate)
        => source.All(predicate);

    /// <summary> LINQ Sum shorthand. </summary>
    public static float sum<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, float> selector)
        => source.Sum(selector);

    /// <summary> Random element from collection. </summary>
    public static T getRandom<T>(this IList<T> list)
        => list[UnityEngine.Random.Range(0, list.Count)];
    #endregion
    #region Component
    /// <summary> GetComponent shorthand — gc = get component. </summary>
    public static T gc<T>(this GameObject go) where T : Component => go.GetComponent<T>();
    public static T gc<T>(this Component comp) where T : Component => comp.GetComponent<T>();
    #endregion
    #region Transform
    /// <summary> Destroy all children of a transform. </summary>
    public static void destroyLeaves(this Transform parent)
    {
        for (int i = parent.childCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(parent.GetChild(i).gameObject);
    }
    #endregion
    #region GameObject
    /// <summary> Toggle SetActive. </summary>
    public static void toggle(this GameObject go) => go.SetActive(!go.activeSelf);
    #endregion
    #region String
    /// <summary> Wrap text in Unity rich text color tag. </summary>
    public static string colorTag(this string text, string color) => $"<color={color}>{text}</color>";

    /// <summary> Repeat a character N times. </summary>
    public static string repeat(this char c, int count) => new string(c, count);
    #endregion
    #region JSON
    /// <summary> Convert object to JSON string via Newtonsoft. </summary>
    public static string ToNSJson(this object obj, bool pretify = false)
        => Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.SerializeObject(obj, pretify ? Newtonsoft.Json.Formatting.Indented : Newtonsoft.Json.Formatting.None);
    #endregion
    #region Dictionary
    /// <summary> Get or create a dictionary entry. </summary>
    public static TValue GetOrCreate<TKey, TValue>(this Dictionary<TKey, TValue> dict, TKey key) where TValue : new()
    {
        if (!dict.TryGetValue(key, out var value))
        {
            value = new TValue();
            dict[key] = value;
        }
        return value;
    }
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/7-3D/ANIM.md ==== -->
# ANIM.md — noimnot Animations

> Centralized animation conventions and specifications. Grows per phase.

---

## General Conventions

- Character animations: 2D sprite swaps (emotion-based), not skeletal animation
- UI animations: DOTween (fade, scale, move, color)
- Camera animations: Cinemachine virtual camera blends
- Door/hatch animations: simple transform rotations

## Animation Systems Overview

- **Character sprites**: Each character type has multiple emotion sprites (happy, sad, angry, scared, etc.)
- **Eye animations**: Separate eye sprite data with blink timing
- **Sign elements**: Character signs (ears, teeth, armpits, hands) animate during dialog
- **DOTween sequences**: UI panels fade/slide in/out, camera transitions between rooms

---

## Phase-specific sections added below as phases are built.

---

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/phase-All/7-3D/MODEL.md ==== -->
# MODEL.md — noimnot 3D Models

> Centralized 3D model conventions and specifications. Grows per phase.

---

## General Conventions

- All meshes are `.glb` format (GLTF binary)
- Russian-named assets in original source — rename to English equivalents
- House interior: Soviet-era apartment aesthetic
- Scale: 1 Unity unit = 1 meter

## Asset Overview (from source)

The original game has ~100+ mesh assets covering:
- House structure: walls, doors, floors, ceilings
- Furniture: beds, tables, chairs, shelves, fridge, radio, phone
- Props: cigarettes, beer bottles, mushrooms, cat bowl, window boards
- Characters: 2D sprites on billboards (not 3D meshes)
- Environment: exterior elements (minimal — game is interior-focused)

---

## Phase-specific sections added below as phases are built.

---

# WORLD.md — noimnot World Layout

> Centralized world/level design conventions. Grows per phase.

---

## General Conventions

- Interior-focused game — Soviet-era apartment
- 7 rooms connected by hallway/transition system
- First-person perspective with 2D room backgrounds + interactive objects
- Day/night cycle changes lighting, skybox, fog

## Room Layout (Top-Down)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                         │
│   ┌──────┐  ┌──────┐  ┌──────┐        │
│   │Office│  │Kitchen│  │Pantry│        │
│   └──┬───┘  └──┬───┘  └──┬───┘        │
│      │         │         │              │
│   ───┴─────────┴─────────┴───          │
│   │         HALLWAY          │          │
│   ───┬─────────┬─────────┬───          │
│      │         │         │              │
│   ┌──┴───┐  ┌──┴────┐  ┌┴──────┐      │
│   │  Big │  │Bedroom│  │Bathroom│      │
│   │ Room │  │       │  │       │      │
│   └──┬───┘  └───────┘  └───────┘      │
│      │                                  │
│   ┌──┴─────┐                           │
│   │Entrance│ ← DOOR (peephole)        │
│   └────────┘                           │
│                                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

|-------|---------|

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/noimnot/surfer.md ==== -->
# surfer.md — noimnot Reasoning Log

> Append after each agent prompt. Captures decisions, trade-offs, and rationale.

---

## Prompt 1 — /init Bootstrap

**Date:** Session 1
**Goal:** Bootstrap all architecture documentation from raw decompiled source.

### What I Did
1. Read the entire noimnot source tree (585 .cs files across Assembly-CSharp/)
2. Read the .stub file for full asset scope (15,214 files, 1.471 GB)
3. Generated ARCHITECTURE.md — comprehensive 9-section analysis
4. Generated PhaseMap.md — 12 phases (A-L) + phase-All, ~302 scripts total
5. Generated StructureMap.md — 20 DataServices, 10 SO_ files, 3 Field_ files, 2 W wrappers
6. Generated GOAL.md — customized from GOAL-general.md template
7. Generated NewAgent.md — customized from NewAgent-general.md template
8. Generated Estimate.md — ~162 hours total (with 25% buffer)
9. Generated SystemPortabilityMap.md — 20 of 35 systems are L0 portable
10. Generated CoverageMap.md — every source file → phase mapping + interface/events inventory
11. Generated OptionalFeatures.md — 10 optional features + external package deps
12. Generated SystemIsolationAnalysis.md — communication matrix, isolation grade A
13. Generated phase-All/ scaffolding — Singleton.cs, GameEvents.cs, UIManager.cs, DataManager.cs, GlobalEnumsAll.cs, Utils.cs, MODEL.md, ANIM.md, WORLD.md

### Key Decisions

**1. Zenject → Singleton<T> + GameEvents**
The original uses Zenject DI throughout (constructor injection, ViewProvider interfaces, installers). We replace this entirely with Singleton<T> for managers and [SerializeField] for scene references. GameEvents (partial class) handles all cross-system communication. This eliminates the DI container complexity.

**2. UniTask → Coroutines**
Original uses UniTask for all async operations. We use standard Unity coroutines. This simplifies the dependency chain (no third-party async library needed).

**3. ResourceMother Split**
The original has one mega-SO (ResourceMother.cs) implementing 11 interfaces. We split it into per-system SO_ files. Each system owns its own configuration SO.

**4. Mock Classes Removed**
Original has MockDialogManager, MockDayNightController, MockPauseController, etc. Our GameEvents-based decoupling makes mocks unnecessary — systems work independently without mocking other systems.

**5. Phase Ordering**
Chose dependency-first ordering:
- A (persistence/data) → B (character data, room framework) → C (character/room managers) → D (dialog) → E (player) → F (interactables) → G (day/night cycle) → H (endings) → I (settings/menus) → J (sound/effects) → K (cat/dream/gacha/steam) → L (remaining)

This ensures each phase only depends on earlier phases.

**6. Dialog System as Single Phase**
Despite having 26 files, the dialog system is tightly coupled internally (DialogManager ↔ FakeDialogRunner ↔ FakeVariableStorage ↔ DialogCommandsInstance). Splitting it across phases would create circular dependencies.

**7. 7 Rooms in One Phase**
All 7 room implementations (EntranceRoom, BigRoom, Bedroom, etc.) share ARoom base class and must be delivered together. Phase C has 26 files (slightly over the 25 cap) but this is unavoidable.

### Trade-offs

- **Phase size cap violations**: Phases C and D each have 26 files (over the 25 cap). Both are tightly coupled feature groups that can't be meaningfully split.
- **Singleton reads vs pure events**: We allow Singleton reads for queries (e.g., DialogManager reading CharactersManager.GetCharactersInHouse()). Pure event-driven would require more boilerplate for simple queries.
- **No Bridge scripts needed**: Unlike some projects, noimnot's architecture doesn't require Bridge scripts because all cross-system communication is event-based or query-based (Singleton reads). If this changes during implementation, bridges can be added.

### Open Questions
- ECM2 integration: How much of Easy Character Movement 2 do we expose vs wrap?
- YarnSpinner version: Which version of YarnSpinner does the FakeDialogRunner target?
- DOTween usage: How extensively is DOTween used for UI animations? May need a Utils wrapper.

---

# npcfr — Detailed Architecture Documentation

## Table of Contents
1. [Project Overview](#1-project-overview)
2. [High-Level Architecture Diagram](#2-high-level-architecture-diagram)
3. [Core Architectural Pattern: Zenject DI](#3-core-architectural-pattern-zenject-di)
4. [System-by-System Breakdown](#4-system-by-system-breakdown)
5. [Key Design Patterns & Techniques](#5-key-design-patterns--techniques)
6. [Coupling Analysis](#6-coupling-analysis)
7. [God Objects — Split Candidates](#7-god-objects--split-candidates)
8. [Critique & Thoughts](#8-critique--thoughts)
9. [File Index](#9-file-index)

---

## 1. Project Overview

### Game Description

**NPCFR** (NPC Factory/Creator) is a **first-person sandbox physics playground** built in Unity (URP). The player operates in a God Mode sandbox environment where they can:

- **Spawn humanoid NPCs** into the world and manipulate them physically
- **Shoot NPCs with a Glock17 pistol** — complete with ballistics, shell casings, muzzle flash, recoil, and impact physics
- **Destroy voxel-based terrain** — bullets and explosions carve through destructible environments using a custom voxel mesh system
- **Interact via context menus** — right-click on objects/NPCs for contextual actions
- **Observe realistic ragdoll physics** — NPCs have full skeletal limb simulation (LVA framework) with procedural limbs, joint constraints, and damage-based dismemberment
- **Experience gore/damage feedback** — blood spatters, limb detachment, dissolve effects, impact particles

The game is primarily a physics sandbox/tech demo focused on realistic humanoid simulation and destructible environments. There's no progression system, economy, or quest structure — the core loop is spawning, shooting, and observing physics interactions.

### Project Structure

```
MAIN-SOURCE/npcfr/
├── Assemblies/                          → compiled DLLs (Zenject, DOTween, Cinemachine, etc.)
├── Assets/                              → scenes, meshes, textures, audio, shaders, prefabs
│   ├── 0_CORE/Scenes/Build/Sandbox/     → main game scene
│   ├── AudioClip/                       → ~30 audio files (ambient, SFX, gore, weapons)
│   ├── Mesh/                            → 3D models (Glock, Lynx humanoid, environment)
│   ├── PrefabHierarchyObject/           → ~100 prefabs (weapons, NPCs, UI, particles)
│   ├── Resources/audio/                 → runtime-loaded audio (impacts, footsteps, weapons)
│   ├── Shader/                          → ~80 shaders (dissolve, outline, URP, VHS, pixel)
│   ├── Sprite/                          → UI icons (toolbar, cursor, weapon icons)
│   └── Texture2D/                       → textures (body, environment, UI, effects)
└── Scripts/
    └── Assembly-CSharp/                 → 1093 .cs files (game source — HEAVILY OBFUSCATED)
        ├── ActiveRagdoll/               → ragdoll physics (PuppetMaster, Pelvis, Muscles)
        ├── Audio/                       → audio system (3 files)
        ├── BloodSplatters/              → blood decal system
        ├── Components/                  → shared MonoBehaviours
        ├── Core/                        → mesh data handling
        ├── Data/                        → enums, data types, config
        ├── Effectors/                   → LVA effector system
        ├── Infrastructure/              → Zenject installers, project setup
        ├── Kinemation/                  → weapon recoil animation
        ├── LVA/                         → Limb-Vertebra-Animation framework (54 files)
        ├── Map/                         → map/level generation
        ├── Player/                      → player controller, input, toolbar, inventory
        ├── QuickOutline/                → outline shader system
        ├── Spawnables/                  → spawnable objects (bullets, weapons, NPCs)
        ├── Sugar/                       → syntactic sugar extensions
        ├── TEST/                        → test scripts
        ├── Tests/                       → test harnesses
        ├── TripledoseLibs/              → custom collection types (NativeHashMap, etc.)
        ├── VFX/                         → visual effects
        ├── Views/                       → UI view controllers
        ├── VoxelMeshGeneration/         → voxel terrain mesh system
        └── *.cs                         → ~803 obfuscated files at root level
```

### Third-Party Dependencies

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Zenject** | IoC/Dependency Injection container — used throughout for wiring |
| **Unity.Cinemachine** | Camera system — FPS camera, aim, shake |
| **Unity.InputSystem** | Modern input handling — keyboard/mouse actions |
| **DG.Tweening (DOTween)** | Animation tweening — UI transitions, weapon effects |
| **Sirenix.OdinInspector** | Editor tools — custom inspector attributes |
| **AmazingAssets.AdvancedDissolve** | Dissolve shader effects — NPC spawn/death |
| **Linework** | Outline rendering — object highlighting |
| **QuickOutline** | Alternative outline system |
| **Unity.Mathematics** | Math types (int3, float3, quaternion) |
| **Unity.Collections** | Native containers (NativeArray, NativeHashSet) |
| **Unity.Jobs + Burst** | Job system — parallel voxel processing |
| **Unity.Splines** | Spline curves (possibly for paths) |
| **TripledoseLibs** | Custom hash maps, sets, collections |
| **Kinemation.Recoilly** | Weapon recoil animation system |
