- **Vertical Slice Tests** — beginner-friendly step-by-step for each `.cs` test:
  - Conversational intro: what this test proves (1-2 sentences)
  - "What you need to type first" / "What you DON'T need"
  - Step-by-step scene setup (numbered: create GO, add component, wire fields with `| Field | Drag From |` tables)
  - "How to test" table: `| Key | What it does | What you should see |`
  - "Full test flow" for complex tests (ordered steps: do X → expect Y → do Z)
  - Checklist: pass/fail items
- **Art & Scene Work (Non-Script)** — animations, audio, shaders, prefabs, layers/tags, SO assets
- **Scene Setup** (full — GOs, components, wiring checklist)
- **Modifications to Earlier Phases** (table: File | How | Change | Why)
- **Source vs Phase diff** (what original did vs what we changed)
- **Systems & Testability** (at end): Individual Systems table + Testability Matrix + final count

### 1b. FLOW.md — System Connections

- **System Map** — ASCII box diagram: all systems, what each owns, connections via GameEvents/SerializeField
- **Data Flows** — one per major user action. Written in **conversation-style plain English** with `code refs`, **bold** for key moments, *italics* for context. NOT swim lanes or ASCII tables — readable prose.
- **Event Registry** — table: every GameEvent in that phase, who fires it, who subscribes
- **Portability Diagram** — which systems are L0, which are L1+, dependency arrows with scorecard
- Every connection = GameEvent or [SerializeField]. Direct cross-system calls = tight coupling = refactor.

### 1c. Update `phase-All/7-3D/` — Centralized Asset Docs (grows per phase)

If this phase introduces new 3D models, animations, or world setup:
- **`MODEL.md`** — add a `## Phase X` section with every 3D mesh: name, shape, dimensions, component hierarchy tree, vertex count, variants
- **`ANIM.md`** — add a `## Phase X` section with every animation clip (name, duration, keyframes), AnimatorController state machines (ASCII graph), parameters as `AnimParamType` enum (never raw strings), transition rules, wiring
- **`WORLD.md`** — add a `## Phase X` section with world layout: ASCII top-down map, prefab placement, lighting, camera, "what the player sees"
- If no 3D/asset requirements for this phase, skip this step entirely.

### 1d. `Dependency.md` Per System — Mandatory

Inside each `_-Systems/XxxSystem/` folder, create a `Dependency.md`:
- **Rewrite from scratch** every time this system is built or modified. Never patch — always regenerate.
- **Requires in-depth analysis first:** read every `.cs` in the system, trace every `using`, `Singleton<X>.Ins`, `GetComponent<I>()`, `GameEvents.On/Raise`. Cross-reference GOAL.md portability rules.
- **Sections (all mandatory):**
  - Header: `# SystemName — L{n} ✅/❌ Portable/Game-Specific`
  - Identity table: Phase, Level, Scripts, Owns Interface, External _-Systems/ Deps
  - FREE Requirements table: GameEvents, UIManager, GlobalEnums, Utils, shared infra
  - Internal File Dependency Graph: 3-zone left-border ASCII diagram (INTERNAL / BOUNDARY / EXTERNAL)
  - Future Phase Modifications table
  - Verdict: COMPLETE / MOSTLY COMPLETE / PARTIAL / PERMANENTLY COMPLETE
  - Checklist: pass/fail items for portability + architecture rules
- **Diagram format** — see GOAL.md `_-Systems/` section for the 3-zone left-border-only format.

### 2. All Scripts Following GOAL.md Rules

- `#region` blocks in MANDATORY order: Inspector Fields → private API → Public API → Extra → Unity Life Cycle (lifecycle is LAST, not third)
- **`private API` combines fields + helpers** — don't split into separate "Private Fields" and "Private API" regions. Single `#region private API`.
- Subdivide `#region public API — IInterfaceName` when a class implements multiple interfaces
- **Region suffix annotations** explain context: `#region public API — Owner chain (read by tools)`, `#region private API — drag-drop`, `#region Protected State — subclasses use ownerCam for raycast`
- `#region Extra` for `// nice-to-have:` features (typed last, skippable)
- No blank lines between `#endregion` and next `#region`
- **Pure data classes** (InventorySlot, CartItem) with only public fields — **omit regions entirely** (too small)
- **`/// <summary>` on EVERY class and EVERY method — mandatory, no exceptions:**
  - **Class (short form)**: one-sentence summary for simple scripts (SubManagers, Field_, simple Managers)
  - **Class (narrative form)**: multi-paragraph first-person "I'm the..." for complex classes (Orchestrators, base classes, PlayerMovement). Include: (1) what I do, (2) how I work, (3) who uses me, (4) events I fire/subscribe
  - **Public method**: 2-line English explaining the full effect (what happens inside, not just the method name)
  - **Private method**: same — describe what actually happens, not just the method name being called
  - **Unity lifecycle**: 2-line explaining what THIS script does in this hook — don't just say "called by Unity"
  - **Interface**: design intent + decoupling rationale + who implements + who calls + which phase
  - **Enum**: what each value means in context (which system uses it, what changes per value)
  - **Exception**: one-liner Get.../Set...() that just return/set a field — name is self-documenting
  - **Exception**: Field_ setter methods (SetData, SetState) — name + params tell the story
- **`// →` inline flow markers** inside every method body
- **`// >> text` / `// << text` block delimiters** for multi-step logical blocks in Orchestrators
- `// purpose:` on every `.Raise...()` and `+=` subscription
- **`// Phase X:` comments** in enums that grow across phases
- **Explicit `== false`** over `!` prefix for boolean checks
- Zero `FindObjectOfType`
- Minimal public API — private by default
- `partial class GameEvents` in own `0-Core/GameEvents.cs`
- **GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes.** `Action<IMyInterface>` not `Action<ConcreteClass>`.
- **Actively create DataServices** for any pure C# logic. **EVERY `List<T>` and `Dictionary<K,V>` that can be tested via `new` MUST live in a DataService**, not in a MonoBehaviour. DataService has: `Build + Get + Add + Remove + boolean questions + GetSnapShotForTest()`. Use `ALL_CAPS` for list fields, `DOC__x_y` for dictionary fields. Nested types (e.g. `CartItem`) live inside the DataService. Ask: "Can I test this via `new`?" If yes → DataService.
- **PhaseXLOG for EVERY collection.** Every collection in a DataService gets its own LOG method: `LIST_X__TO__JSON` for lists, `DOC_X__TO__JSON` for dictionaries. All use `.map()` to anonymous type + `.ToNSJson(pretify: true)`. DataService's `GetSnapShotForTest()` calls ALL LOG methods.
- **Actively extract reusable logic into phase-All/4-Utils/Utils.cs.** Extension methods (e.g. `formatMoney(this float)`) + static helpers. If logic appears in 2+ scripts, it goes in Utils.cs (phase-All). No per-phase UtilsPhaseX.cs — per-phase 4-Utils/ only has PhaseXLOG.cs.
- **Orchestrator patterns:** `DOC__X__Field` dictionary mapping data → Field_ instance. `.destroyLeaves()` before repopulating. `.gc<T>()` after Instantiate. `AddListener` ONLY in Orchestrator. `RefreshAllRequired()` after every data mutation (event-driven, never Update).
- **Reduce verbosity** — `?.`, `??`, `=>`, LINQ, custom extensions (`.map()`, `.find()`, `.all()`, `.sum()`, `.getRandom()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, `.toggle()`, `.colorTag()`)
- **Protected helpers** for repeated patterns shared across subclasses. Put in base class, not duplicated in each concrete class.
- See GOAL.md "Pure Purpose with Live Code" for exact code patterns of every role.

### 3. Test Scripts

- Summary comment: prerequisites, NOT required, "How to test" steps, controls
- `// purpose:` on every Raise/Subscribe
- Console logging via GameEvents subscription
- Minimal bootstrap — systems handle their own Update()
- Fire GameEvents to trigger actions (never call methods directly)

### 4. Manual Test Guides (`5-Tests/Manual/*.md`)

**Decision question for every phase:** "Does this system have UI panels, animations, physics visuals, or dense inspector setup?" If yes → create a `Manual/*.md` for each system that needs it. Analyse the main source.

For systems needing visual/hands-on verification:
- **Prerequisites** — singletons, prefabs, test scripts needed
- **Setup Guide** — beginner-level step-by-step:
  - Every GO: name, parent, components, wiring (`| Field | Drag From |` table)
  - Prefab hierarchies: every child with RectTransform, Image, tag, layer, defaults
  - Final hierarchy tree
- **How It Works (System Flow)** — the heart of the manual test. Before DO/EXPECT steps, explain the system's **end-to-end data flow** in conversation-style plain English:
  - Break into labelled paragraphs per action (e.g. "Scene loads:", "Tool pickup:", "Drag-drop:")
  - Each paragraph traces: which script method → which GameEvent → which subscriber → which GO `SetActive` → which field changes → what player sees
  - Teaches WHY things happen, not just WHAT the reader sees
  - The reader should understand the full architecture by reading the manual test
- **Manual Test Flow** — numbered DO/EXPECT steps:
  - One action per step, EXPECT: **bold** for visual, `code` for console
  - Also explain behind the scenes: which method runs, which event fires, which GOs activate/deactivate
  - Cover: initial state → primary actions → edge cases → error conditions
- **Summary Checklist** — pass/fail items
- **Self-contained** — no "see GUIDE.md" shortcuts. Assumes zero prior knowledge.
- **Pitfall Checks** — every Manual/*.md must warn about applicable items from the 10 Common Pitfalls list in GOAL.md (SetActive cascading, scene vs prefab, raycastTarget, missing layers, etc.)

### Manual Test Pitfall Checklist

Every Manual/*.md MUST include a "Pitfall Checks" section at the end. Review each item — if it applies to THIS system, add a warning with the specific GO/component affected:

| # | Pitfall | Check |
|---|---------|-------|
| 1 | **SetActive cascading** — parent.SetActive(false) disables ALL children. Independent panels = siblings, not children. | Does this system have nested panels that toggle independently? |
| 2 | **Scene instance vs prefab** — drag from Hierarchy (scene), not Project panel (asset). | Does the test require wiring scene references? |
| 3 | **raycastTarget** — ghost Image = false, slot background = true, icon on top = false. | Does this system have UI with overlapping Images? |
| 4 | **Missing layers** — `LayerMask.NameToLayer` returns -1 silently if layer doesn't exist. | Does this system use custom layers? |
| 5 | **LayerMask at Nothing** — defaults to 0, all raycasts return empty. Must set in inspector. | Does this system use LayerMask fields? |
| 6 | **Dual collider setup** — trigger + physical on SEPARATE child GOs, never both on same GO. | Does this system use both trigger and physical colliders? |
| 7 | **Static lists surviving domain reload** — second Play press has stale null refs. Clear in OnEnable or use `[RuntimeInitializeOnLoadMethod]`. | Does this system have static collections? |
| 8 | **Execution order** — `[DefaultExecutionOrder]` on managers. Don't remove or reorder. | Does this test depend on manager init order? |
| 9 | **isFirstEnable** — SubManager must self-disable on first OnEnable, not Start/Awake. | Does this system have SubManagers? |
| 10 | **Cross-phase mods not applied** — if this phase modifies earlier files, compile error or silent failure. | Does this phase modify earlier phase scripts? |

---

## Reference Files

| File | What it is | Read when |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| `learn/GOAL.md` | Architecture bible — all rules | Always first |
| `learn/NewAgent.md` | Agent instructions + delivery checklist + common mistakes | Always — before building any phase |
| `learn/PhaseMap.md` | Roadmap — all phases, files, modifications | Before building any phase |
| `learn/StructureMap.md` | DataService specs — exact collections, methods, nested types per phase | Before writing any DataService |
| `learn/handTyped(latest)/` | User's ACTUAL hand-typed code — ground truth for style (isFirstEnable, Open/Close events) | Always — match this, not generic C# |
| `learn/Estimate.md` | Timeline + hours | For planning |
| `learn/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Original source analysis | When source fidelity questions arise |
| `learn/surfer.md` | Reasoning history | Optional — decisions captured in GOAL.md |
| `Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/` | Original source (all files) | To match 100% behavior |
| `learn/SystemPortabilityMap.md` | L0/L1+ classification per system | When assessing portability |
| `learn/SystemIsolationAnalysis.md` | Cross-system audit — interfaces, bridges, events, concrete deps | When writing Dependency.md |
| `learn/CoverageMap.md` | Every source file → which phase covers it | Gap checking |
| `learn/OptionalFeatures.md` | Features outside 100% scope (polish, external packages) | When deciding what to include |
| Completed phase folders | Reference for established _-Systems/ patterns, bridges, interface ownership | Before building later phases |

---

## Key Rules (Quick Reference)

- **Check Gap Audit before building any phase.** PhaseMap.md and StructureMap.md have "Gap Audit" sections listing missing files per phase. Include all **Critical** and **Important** items. **Polish** items go in `#region extra` blocks.
- **PhaseMap/StructureMap are NOT exhaustive.** Always cross-reference every original source file for the phase. If the source has functionality not listed, **include it anyway**. The main source is the ultimate source of truth.
- **MANDATORY: Post-delivery self-audit.** Method-by-method comparison against original source. Non-negotiable.
- **One sentence per script.** If it doesn't fit, split it.
- **`_-Systems/` is the PRIMARY structure.** Each feature with 3+ files gets its own folder with code + Test.md + Dependency.md. **SO_, Field_, DataService, DataWrapper, Orchestrator, SubManager, Interface, Bridge — ALL go inside the system folder.** Numbered folders (2-Data/, 3-MonoBehaviours/) are ONLY for shared cross-system infra (RARE).
- **System independence.** Systems communicate ONLY via interfaces and GameEvents. No direct class imports across `_-Systems/` folders.
- **GameEvents use interfaces, NEVER concrete classes.** `Action<IMyInterface>` not `Action<ConcreteClass>`.
- **Portable systems own their interfaces.** Copy folder → it compiles and works. Bridges live in the consumer (game-specific side).
- **Observable System Shapes:**
  - 🕷️ Spider — defines interfaces, receives implementations (always portable)
  - 🔍 Hunter — defines interfaces, actively scans via `GetComponent<IXxx>()` (always portable)
  - 🔌 Adapter — implements interfaces defined by Spiders/Hunters (portable if interface-only deps)
  - 📡 Broadcaster/Listener — fires or subscribes to GameEvents only (always portable)
  - 🌍 Infrastructure — used by ALL, doesn't count as dependency
  - Hybrids are normal — most systems combine shapes
- **Portability levels:** L0 (zero deps) → L1+ (interface deps = portable ✅, concrete deps = game-specific ❌). Maximize L0.
- **`[AddComponentMenu("npcfr/Category/ClassName")]`** on every MonoBehaviour.
- **Unity 6000.3: filename MUST match classname** for inspector-added MonoBehaviours.
- **GlobalEnumsX.cs** — all enums for a phase go in ONE file. Enum values use **camelCase**.
- **TagType enum — no raw string tags.** Use `HasTag(TagType.x)` and `SetTag(TagType.x)`.
- **`phase-All/`** — shared scripts: Singleton, UIManager, DataManager, Utils.cs, TimeSince/TimeUntil. Never duplicated.
- **Prefix = no logic.** `SO_` = pure data. `Field_` = display only. `W` = session wrapper.
- **GameEvents for cross-system.** `[SerializeField]` for same-GO. Owner chain for parent-child.
- **DataService = pure C#.** EVERY collection testable via `new` → DataService.
- **partial GameEvents.** Each phase extends in its own `0-Core/GameEvents.cs`.
- **SubManagers self-init.** `isFirstEnable` pattern. OnEnable/OnDisable announce menu state.
- **No defensive null checks.** Let it crash — the crash is traceable.
- **Least possible public API.** Default private. Promote only when another script calls it. **After writing every script, audit:** can any public method be made private/protected?
- **`[SerializeField]` is ALWAYS private.** External access via explicit `Get...()` / `Set...()` methods.
- **No C# property accessors.** Never `{ get; set; }`. Always `Get.../Set...()` methods.
- **`// purpose:`** on every Raise call and every += subscription.
- **Vertical slice = standalone scene.** If a test needs another system, fix the architecture.
- **Separate Open/Close events per UI panel** — NOT a toggle. `OnOpenXView` + `OnCloseXView`.
- **Bridge pattern** for cross-system runtime context — static accessor, inspector-wired provider, consumer never imports provider's concrete class.
- **DataManager** in phase-All — shared visual data (materials, layer masks). FREE infra like UIManager.
- **Utils.cs** single static class in phase-All — ALL utility code here. No per-phase UtilsPhaseX.cs. Per-phase 4-Utils/ only has PhaseXLOG.cs.
- **TimeSince/TimeUntil** structs in phase-All — implicit float conversion, drop-in for float timers.
- **SO_ extension pattern** — if SO_ needs helper methods for 2+ consumers, create co-located static extension class `SO_XxxExt` in same file. SO stays pure data.

---

## Common Mistakes The Agent WILL Make

Catch these before delivering:

1. **Any `FindObjectOfType` in MonoBehaviours** → use `[SerializeField]`, Owner chain, or GameEvents.
2. **Public methods nobody calls externally** → audit every public method. "Who calls this?"
3. **Missing `// purpose:` on Raise/Subscribe calls** → mandatory on every single one.
4. **Tight coupling** (Script A directly calls Script B across systems) → decouple via GameEvents.
5. **Defensive null checks on inspector refs** → let it crash.
6. **RefreshAll() in Update()** (polling) → make it event-driven.
7. **DataService that needs Unity physics/lifecycle** → question if it should be a DataService at all.
8. **Missing `isFirstEnable` pattern on SubManagers** → every SubManager MUST use it. No exceptions.
9. **Toggle instead of separate Open/Close events** → every UI panel needs SEPARATE events.
10. **Using `Input.GetKeyDown` directly in SubManagers** → SubManagers don't handle input. They subscribe to events.
11. **Methods on SO_ classes** → SO_ = pure data, zero methods. Move to consumer.
12. **Singleton in `3-MonoBehaviours/` instead of `1-Managers/`** → Singleton<T> = always 1-Managers/.
13. **Collections left in MonoBehaviour instead of DataService** → any `List<T>` / `Dictionary<K,V>` testable via `new` MUST be in a DataService. No exceptions.
14. **Missing PhaseXLOG methods** → every collection in every DataService gets its own LOG method. `GetSnapShotForTest()` calls them all.
15. **Missing `RefreshAllRequired()` in Orchestrator** → after every data mutation, refresh the UI. Never poll in Update().
16. **Missing `DOC__X__Field` in Orchestrator** → Orchestrators that create Field_ instances MUST track them in a `DOC__` dictionary for refresh/destroy.
17. **Not using user's custom extensions** → use `.map()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, `.toggle()`, `.colorTag()`, `C.method(this)`, `LOG.AddLog()` etc. Don't reinvent with standard API.
18. **Bridge script placed on the portable side** → Bridges live on the non-portable / game-specific side, regardless of consumer/provider role. A portable system should have zero game-specific imports.
19. **`Interface/` subfolder not used when system has 2+ interfaces** → single interface = root of system folder. Two or more interfaces = `Interface/` subfolder. Check after writing every system.
20. **Using `FindObjectOfType` instead of `FindObjectsByType`** → `FindObjectOfType` is obsolete in Unity 6000.3. Use `FindObjectsByType<T>(FindObjectsSortMode.None)` — ONLY inside Bridge scripts for push-to-all pattern, never in normal MonoBehaviours.
21. **Wrong bridge pattern chosen** → 5 variants exist (event-push, event-response, push-to-all, event-chain, static-accessor). Match the data-flow direction and frequency. See GOAL.md Bridge Pattern Catalog.
22. **`#region Private Fields` instead of `#region private API`** → the correct region name is `private API` (lowercase). `Private Fields` is NOT a valid region name in this architecture.
23. **Field_ uses `[SerializeField]` instead of public fields** → Field_ scripts use **public** references (set by Orchestrator at runtime via `.gc<T>()`), not `[SerializeField]`. Only MonoBehaviours wired in the inspector use `[SerializeField]`.

---

## MANDATORY Patterns — Do NOT Skip

### `// purpose:` on EVERY Raise and Subscribe

**After writing each script, ctrl+F for `GameEvents.Raise` and `GameEvents.On`. Every single one MUST have a `// purpose:` comment.** No exceptions.

```csharp
// purpose: cursor lock/unlock for player controller
GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: true);

// purpose: HUD updates display
GameEvents.RaiseResourceChanged(amount);

// purpose: log when item is processed
GameEvents.OnItemProcessed += (value) => Debug.Log($"processed for {value}");
```

### `isFirstEnable` on ALL SubManagers

Every SubManager MUST use this pattern:

```csharp
bool isFirstEnable = true;
private void OnEnable()
{
    if (isFirstEnable)
    {
        // subscribe + build + self-disable
        GameEvents.OnOpenThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(true);
        GameEvents.OnCloseThisView += () => this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        this.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        isFirstEnable = false;
        return;
    }
    GameEvents.RaiseMenuStateChanged(isAnyMenuOpen: true);
}
```

Do NOT use Start() for subscriptions. Do NOT use Awake(). See GOAL.md "Why isFirstEnable".

### Separate Open/Close Events Per UI Panel

Every UI panel gets TWO events, not a toggle:

```
OnOpenShopView / OnCloseShopView
OnOpenInventoryView / OnCloseInventoryView
OnOpenSettingsView / OnCloseSettingsView
```

SubManagers subscribe to both. `UIManager.CloseAllSubManager()` fires all Close events.

### DataService + PhaseXLOG + GetSnapShotForTest — For EVERY Collection

**After writing any system with collections, verify:**

1. **Every `List<T>` / `Dictionary<K,V>`** that can be tested via `new` lives in a DataService
2. **Every collection has a PhaseXLOG method:** `LIST_X__TO__JSON` or `DOC_X__TO__JSON`
3. **DataService has `GetSnapShotForTest()`** that calls ALL LOG methods with `=` header
4. **DEBUG_Check tests it** via `new DataService()` → Build → mutate → `LOG.AddLog(snapshot, "json")`

```csharp
// DataService
#region snapShot
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header = "when something happened")
{
    return $@"
{'='.repeat(4) + header + '='.repeat(4)}
// ITEMS
{PhaseXLOG.LIST_ITEMS__TO__JSON(ITEMS)}
// DOC__category_items
{PhaseXLOG.DOC_CATEGORY_ITEMS__TO__JSON(DOC__category_items)}";
}
#endregion
```

### Orchestrator DOC__ + RefreshAllRequired

**Every Orchestrator that creates Field_ instances MUST:**

1. Track them in `DOC__X__Field` dictionary (data → Field_ mapping)
2. Call `RefreshAllRequired()` after every data mutation
3. Use `.destroyLeaves()` before repopulating
4. Use `.gc<T>()` after Instantiate
5. Wire `AddListener` ONLY inside Orchestrator — Field_ never wires its own clicks

---

## After First Delivery

The user will audit your scripts. Expect:
- "Is the public API minimal?"
- "Any tight coupling?"
- "Does every Raise have a // purpose: comment?"
- "Can this be tested independently in a standalone scene?"

Fix what the user flags. You'll calibrate to their strictness level after 1-2 corrections.

---

## Don't Forget

- **Check if the user has custom utility extensions** (like `.map()`, `.find()`, `.all()`, `.sum()`, `.getRandom()`, `.gc<T>()`, `.destroyLeaves()`, `.toggle()`, `.colorTag()`, `.repeat()`, `.ToNSJson()`, `C.method(this)`, `INPUT.K.InstantDown()`, `LOG.AddLog()`, `.flatMap()`, `.forEach()`, `.formatMoneyShort()`, `.parseInt()`). Use them, don't reinvent.
- **Using import block order:** System → UnityEngine → TMPro → SPACE_UTIL. Blank line between groups. Omit unused namespaces.
- **Naming conventions (verify in every script):**
  - Class suffixes: `XxxDataService`, `XxxOrchestrator`, `XxxUI` (SubManager), `XxxManager`, `XxxBridge`, `BaseXxx` (base class)
  - Method prefixes: `Get.../Set...` (explicit, no properties), `Try...` (returns bool), `Can.../Is.../Should.../Has...` (predicates), `Handle...` (event callbacks), `Raise...` (GameEvents), `Build...` (init), `Refresh...` (UI update), `Orchestrate...`/`Subscribe...`/`Wire...` (event setup)
  - Compound method names are fine: `InitBuildOrchestrateAndSubscribe()`, `BuildAndOrchestrateCategoryView()`
  - Enum values: **camelCase** (CRITICAL — `TagType.grabbable` not `Grabbable`). Exception: ID enums with explicit int values may use PascalCase.
  - `[SerializeField]` fields: `_camelCase` always private. Prefab refs: `_pfXxx`
  - Lists: `ALL_CAPS`. Dicts: `DOC__key_value`. Constants: `CONSTANT_CASE`.
  - Boolean fields: `is` prefix (`isFirstEnable`, `isDead`)
- **Multiline SerializeField grouping:** When 4+ fields share a type, group under one `[SerializeField]` attribute with type on separate line:
  ```csharp
  [SerializeField]
  Color
      _canAffordColor = Color.limeGreen,
      _cannotAffordColor = Color.red * 0.8f;
  ```
- **Context injection pattern:** Use `Set...Context()` methods called by Bridges to push data between systems. The consumer stores only what it needs (transforms, not the owner class).
- **Explicit `== false`** over `!` prefix for boolean checks.
- **Attributes (verify in every MonoBehaviour):**
  - `[AddComponentMenu("npcfr/Category/ClassName")]` — MANDATORY on every MB. Categories: `Tools/`, `Managers/`, `Bridge/`, `Mining/`, `Inventory/`, `UI/`, `Test/`, etc.
  - `[CreateAssetMenu(menuName = "SO/SO_XxxDef", fileName = "SO_XxxDef")]` on every SO_
  - `[DefaultExecutionOrder]` on 2-3 critical managers per phase (not overdone)
  - `[Header]`, `[Range]`, `[TextArea]`, `[Tooltip]` — use for inspector clarity
- **File naming: filename MUST match classname** for inspector-added MonoBehaviours (Unity 6000.3)
- **Append surfer.md** after completing the phase with critical decisions made.
- **Read `learn/handTyped(latest)/`** to see the user's ACTUAL coding style — match it.

---

## New Project Quickstart Checklist

When starting a **brand new** Unity3D game rebuild:

```
□ 1. Extract/decompile original source into Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/
□ 2. Read every file. Understand all systems and their connections.
□ 3. Create learn/ARCHITECTURE.md (full source analysis)
□ 4. Create learn/GOAL.md (copy from GOAL-general.md, add project-specific examples)
□ 5. Create learn/NewAgent.md (copy from NewAgent-general.md, customize first prompt)
□ 6. Break game into phases → learn/PhaseMap.md
□ 7. Define DataService specs → learn/StructureMap.md
□ 8. Estimate timeline → learn/Estimate.md
□ 9. Build phase-All/ (Singleton, GameEvents, UIManager, EconomyManager/CurrencyManager)
□ 10. Build Phase 1 → hand-type → test → iterate
□ 11. Build Phase 2+ → repeat
□ 12. Each phase: GUIDE.md + FLOW.md + scripts + tests + manual tests
□ 13. Post-delivery self-audit per phase (mandatory)
□ 14. Append surfer.md with reasoning per prompt
```

**The architecture is the same every time.** Only the game content changes — different SO_ types, different MonoBehaviours, different GameEvents. The folder structure, naming conventions, decoupling rules, test strategy, and documentation format are universal.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/OptionalFeatures.md ==== -->
# npcfr — OptionalFeatures

> Features outside mandatory 100% scope. Each has specific integration points and code patterns.

---

## Visual Polish

### Screen Shake on Weapon Fire — Nice
**Phase:** D (WeaponSystem)
**Effort:** Simple (~15min)
**Integration point:** `Glock17.cs` → `Fire()` method
**SerializeField refs needed:** `[SerializeField] float _fireShakeIntensity = 0.1f;`
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// In Glock17.cs, Fire():
// purpose: camera kick on shot
GameEvents.RaiseCameraShake(_fireShakeIntensity, 0.1f);
```
**Why optional:** Core firing works without it — pure visual feedback.

### Bullet Impact Particles — Nice
**Phase:** D→H (VFXSystem subscribes to WeaponSystem events)
**Effort:** Medium (~30min)
**Integration point:** `VFXSystem` subscribes to `OnBulletImpact`
**SerializeField refs needed:** `[SerializeField] ParticleSystem _impactSparks, _impactDust;`
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// In VFXController.cs, HandleBulletImpact():
var ps = (hitVoxel) ? _impactDust : _impactSparks;
ps.transform.position = hitPoint;
ps.transform.rotation = Quaternion.LookRotation(hitNormal);
ps.Play();
```
**Why optional:** Bullet damage works without visual particles.

### Advanced Dissolve on NPC Death — Cool
**Phase:** H (VFXSystem)
**Effort:** Medium (~30min)
**Integration point:** `DissolveController.cs` subscribes to `OnHumanoidDied`
**SerializeField refs needed:** `[SerializeField] Material _dissolveMaterial; [SerializeField] float _dissolveSpeed = 1f;`
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// In DissolveController.cs:
// purpose: visual death dissolve effect
GameEvents.OnHumanoidDied += (humanoid) => StartCoroutine(DissolveCoroutine(humanoid));
```
**Why optional:** Uses AmazingAssets.AdvancedDissolve third-party — requires asset import.

### QuickOutline on Hover — Nice
**Phase:** H (VFXSystem)
**Effort:** Simple (~15min)
**Integration point:** `OutlineController.cs` — raycasts from camera, adds/removes QuickOutline
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// In OutlineController.cs, Update():
if (Physics.Raycast(cam.position, cam.forward, out hit, _maxDist))
    hit.collider.gc<Outline>()?.SetOutlineWidth(_hoverWidth);
```
**Why optional:** Pure visual feedback. Player can interact without outlines.

---

## Audio

### Ambient Audio System — Cool
**Phase:** H (AudioSystem)
**Effort:** Medium (~30min)
**Integration point:** `AmbientAudioController.cs` — plays looping ambient based on player position
**SerializeField refs needed:** `[SerializeField] AudioClip _ambientLoop; [SerializeField] float _fadeSpeed;`
**Why optional:** Game works in silence. Ambient adds atmosphere but not gameplay.

### Weapon-Specific SFX Variations — Nice
**Phase:** H (AudioSystem)
**Effort:** Simple (~15min)
**Integration point:** `WeaponSFXMapper.cs` — maps WeaponSFXType → AudioClip[]
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// In AudioManager.cs, HandleWeaponSFX():
var clips = _weaponSFXMapper.GetClips(sfxType);
PlayOneShot(clips.getRandom(), hitPoint);
```
**Why optional:** Single fire SFX works. Variations add polish only.

---

## Advanced Physics

### Voxel Chunk Physics After Separation — Stretch
**Phase:** E (VoxelSystem)
**Effort:** Complex (~60min)
**Integration point:** `VoxelSeparator.cs` → after detecting isolated chunks, add Rigidbody to chunk GO
**Why optional:** Complex physics simulation. Core voxel destruction works without floating chunks falling.

### Ragdoll Pose Matching — Stretch
**Phase:** F (RagdollSystem)
**Effort:** Complex (~60min)
**Integration point:** `PuppetMaster.cs` — blend between animation pose and ragdoll physics
**Why optional:** Basic ragdoll activation works. Pose matching adds smoother transitions.

---

## UI Polish

### Damage Numbers Floating Text — Nice
**Phase:** G (HealthSystem / VFXSystem)
**Effort:** Medium (~30min)
**Integration point:** Subscribe to `OnDamageDealt`, spawn world-space text at hit point
**SerializeField refs needed:** `[SerializeField] GameObject _pfDamageNumber;`
**Call pattern:**
```csharp
// purpose: visual damage feedback
GameEvents.OnDamageDealt += (humanoid, amount, pos) =>
{
    var dmgNum = Instantiate(_pfDamageNumber, pos, Quaternion.identity).gc<Field_DamageNumber>();
    dmgNum.SetData(amount);
};
```
**Why optional:** Health system works without visible numbers.

### Crosshair Dynamic Spread — Nice
**Phase:** D (WeaponSystem)
**Effort:** Simple (~15min)
**Integration point:** UI crosshair scales based on recoil/movement state
**Why optional:** Aiming works without dynamic crosshair.

---

## Performance

### Object Pooling for Bullets/Shells — Cool
**Phase:** D (WeaponSystem)
**Effort:** Medium (~30min)
**Integration point:** Replace `Instantiate`/`Destroy` in `Glock17.cs` with pool `Get`/`Release`
**Why optional:** Game works with Instantiate/Destroy. Pool reduces GC pressure at high fire rates.

### LOD for Voxel Meshes — Stretch
**Phase:** E (VoxelSystem)
**Effort:** Complex (~60min)
**Integration point:** `VoxelMeshBuilder.cs` — generate lower-detail meshes for distant chunks
**Why optional:** Complex. Acceptable performance without LOD for sandbox scale.

---

## Summary

| Priority | Count | Total Effort |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Nice | 7 | ~2.5 hrs |
| Cool | 3 | ~1.5 hrs |
| Stretch | 3 | ~3 hrs |
| **Total** | **13** | **~7 hrs** |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/PhaseMap.md ==== -->
# npcfr — PhaseMap

> All phases, files, modifications, vertical slice tests, gap audit.
> Each phase depends only on earlier phases, never forward.

---

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Domain | Files | Weight | Difficulty |
|-------|--------|-------|--------|------------|
| phase-All | Shared foundation | 8 | 5% | Simple |
| phase-A | Core + Input + UI Framework | 22 | 12% | Medium |
| phase-B | Camera + First-Person Controller | 18 | 10% | Medium |
| phase-C | Toolbar + Inventory + Context Menu | 24 | 15% | Complex |
| phase-D | Weapon System + Ballistics | 20 | 12% | Complex |
| phase-E | Voxel Terrain + Mesh Generation | 25 | 15% | Complex |
| phase-F | NPC Humanoid Core (LVA + Skeleton) | 25 | 15% | Complex |
| phase-G | NPC Limbs + Ragdoll + Damage | 24 | 12% | Complex |
| phase-H | Audio + VFX + Polish | 18 | 8% | Medium |
| phase-I | Debug + Demo + Final Assembly | 12 | 6% | Simple |

**Total: ~196 files** (down from 1093 originals — many stubs/interfaces collapse, Zenject removed, obfuscated helpers merged)

---

## Phase Dependency Order

```
phase-All ← phase-A ← phase-B ← phase-C ← phase-D
                                    ↑         ↑
                               phase-E ← phase-F ← phase-G
                                                      ↑
                                              phase-H ← phase-I
```

All arrows point LEFT (to earlier phases). No forward references.

---

## phase-All — Shared Foundation

**Domain boundary:** Scripts that ALL phases depend on — never duplicated, only extended.

### File List

| File | Folder | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| Singleton.cs | 0-Core/ | Generic singleton base — first instance wins |
| GameEvents.cs | 0-Core/ | Core shared events (OnMenuStateChanged, OnCloseAllSubManagers) |
| UIManager.cs | 1-Managers/ | Reports menu state + closes all panels |
| DataManager.cs | 1-Managers/ | Shared visual data: materials, layer masks |
| GlobalEnumsAll.cs | 2-Data/Enums/ | TagType enum + shared enums (grows across phases) |
| Utils.cs | 4-Utils/ | Shared extensions: HasTag, SetTag, .map(), .gc<T>(), etc. |
| MODEL.md | 7-3D/ | 3D model conventions |
| ANIM.md | 7-3D/ | Animation conventions |
| WORLD.md | 7-3D/ | World/level design conventions |

**File count: 6 scripts + 3 docs = 9 items**

---

- **GameStateSystem** (5 files): GameStateCoordinator, CursorManager, ICursorState, Test.md, Dependency.md
- **PauseSystem** (3 files): PauseUI, Test.md, Dependency.md

### Modifications to Earlier Phases

None — this is the first phase.

### Vertical Slice Tests

**InputSystem Test:**
- Prerequisites: phase-All (Singleton, GameEvents, UIManager)
- Create GO with InputManager, wire InputActionAsset
- Press WASD → console logs movement values
- Press LMB/RMB/Q/R/T → console logs button state
- ESC → triggers pause event

**GameStateSystem Test:**
- Cursor locked on play, ESC unlocks, ESC again relocks
- Menu state changes propagate via GameEvents

### What It Looks Like When Running

You start in a scene with nothing visible except a locked cursor. Press ESC — cursor unlocks, game pauses. Press ESC again — cursor locks, game resumes. WASD keys log movement values to console. Mouse movement logs look deltas. All button presses (LMB, RMB, Q, R, T, Z, X, Tab) log their state. The foundation for everything else.

**Difficulty: Medium | Weight: 12%**

- Isolated chunks separate and fall with physics
- Raycast picks correct voxel face

### What It Looks Like When Running

The world has voxel-based terrain/walls. Shoot the terrain — voxels get destroyed where bullets hit. The mesh regenerates in real-time. If you carve enough to isolate a chunk, it separates and falls with physics. The RGBAtlas textures map correctly to voxel faces.

| PickupLimb.cs | _-Systems/LimbSystem/ | Separation/pickup (was yr) |
| LimbAnimationDriver.cs | _-Systems/LimbSystem/ | Bone animation (was ql) |
| ILimb.cs | _-Systems/LimbSystem/Interface/ | Limb contract |
| ILimbDamage.cs | _-Systems/LimbSystem/Interface/ | Limb damage interface |
| Test.md | _-Systems/LimbSystem/ | Limb vertical slice test |
| Dependency.md | _-Systems/LimbSystem/ | Limb dependencies |
| BloodDecalPool.cs | _-Systems/GoreSystem/ | Blood decal object pool |
| BloodParticles.cs | _-Systems/GoreSystem/ | Blood particle effects |

**File count: 24 (3 shared + 8 HealthSystem + 10 LimbSystem + 3 GoreSystem)**

### Modifications to Earlier Phases

| File | How | Change | Why |
|------|-----|--------|-----|
| phase-D/WeaponSystem/PistolBullet.cs | direct | Add NPC damage on collision | Bullets damage NPC limbs |
| phase-F/HumanoidSystem/HumanoidPuppeteer.cs | direct | Add ragdoll transition on death | NPC goes ragdoll when HP=0 |
| phase-F/EffectorSystem/EffectorBase.cs | direct | Add damage signal propagation | Effectors process damage signals |

### Vertical Slice Tests

**HealthSystem Test:**
- Create HealthPool → take damage → events fire
- HealthTracker aggregates multiple pools
- Fall damage calculates from collision velocity

**LimbSystem Test:**
- Shoot NPC limb → damage applied to that limb
- Enough damage → limb detaches
- Blood particles spawn at impact
- NPC transitions to ragdoll on death

### What It Looks Like When Running

Shoot an NPC with the Glock17. Bullet hits a specific limb — blood spawns at impact, limb takes damage. Keep shooting — the limb detaches and falls. When the NPC's total health reaches zero, they go full ragdoll and crumple. Blood decals appear on surfaces behind the NPC.

**Difficulty: Complex | Weight: 12%**

---

## phase-H — Audio + VFX + Polish

**Domain boundary:** Sound effects, ambient audio, particle systems, dissolve effects, outline highlighting, visual polish.

### File List

| File | Folder | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| GameEvents.cs | 0-Core/ | partial — OnSFXPlay, OnAmbientChange |
| GlobalEnumsH.cs | 2-Data/Enums/ | ToolsSFXType, AudioZoneType |
| PhaseHLOG.cs | 4-Utils/ | Snapshot formatters for phase-H |
| SO_AudioConfig.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Volume, mixer, distance curves |
| AudioManager.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Audio playback manager |
| AudioTypeMapper.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Enum → AudioResource mapping (was bhe<T>) |
| ImpactSFXMapper.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Impact SFX mapping (was bhg) |
| WeaponSFXMapper.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Weapon SFX mapping (was bhl) |
| SingleShotAudioSource.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | One-shot audio player |
| AmbientAudioController.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Positional ambient (was PlayerYPosAudio) |
| IAudioPlayer.cs | _-Systems/AudioSystem/Interface/ | Audio playback interface |
| Test.md | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Audio vertical slice test |
| Dependency.md | _-Systems/AudioSystem/ | Audio dependencies |
| ParticleSystemController.cs | _-Systems/VFXSystem/ | Extended particle system (was bg) |
| MuzzleFlashEffect.cs | _-Systems/VFXSystem/ | Muzzle flash VFX |

| OutlineController.cs | _-Systems/VFXSystem/ | Object outline highlighting |
| Dependency.md | _-Systems/VFXSystem/ | VFX dependencies |

**File count: 18 (3 shared + 10 AudioSystem + 5 VFXSystem)**

### Modifications to Earlier Phases

| File | How | Change | Why |
|------|-----|--------|-----|
| phase-D/WeaponSystem/Glock17.cs | direct | Add weapon SFX calls | Gun sounds on fire/reload |
| phase-G/GoreSystem/BloodParticles.cs | direct | Add impact SFX | Blood splatter sounds |
| phase-F/HumanoidSystem/HumanoidSpawner.cs | direct | Add dissolve spawn effect | NPC spawn dissolve VFX |

### Vertical Slice Tests

**AudioSystem Test:**
- Fire weapon → weapon SFX plays
- Walk → footstep SFX plays
- Move to different Y position → ambient changes

### What It Looks Like When Running

All gameplay now has audio. Fire the Glock and hear the shot + shell casing sound. Footsteps play as you walk. NPCs spawning have a dissolve visual effect. Impact sparks have accompanying sounds. Ambient atmosphere changes based on player position. Objects highlight with outlines when hovered.

**Difficulty: Medium | Weight: 8%**

---

## phase-I — Debug + Demo + Final Assembly

**Domain boundary:** Debug harness, slow motion, demo scene setup, final integration, settings.

### File List

| File | Folder | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| GameEvents.cs | 0-Core/ | partial — OnSlowMotion, OnDebugToggle |
| GlobalEnumsI.cs | 2-Data/Enums/ | DebugFlag |
| PhaseILOG.cs | 4-Utils/ | Snapshot formatters for phase-I |
| DebugHarness.cs | _-Systems/DebugSystem/ | Debug controls, spawn tests (was br) |
| SlowMotionController.cs | _-Systems/DebugSystem/ | Slow motion toggle (was TimeScaler extended) |
| StrangeSoundPlayer.cs | _-Systems/DebugSystem/ | Atmospheric sound player |
| Test.md | _-Systems/DebugSystem/ | Debug vertical slice test |
| Dependency.md | _-Systems/DebugSystem/ | Debug dependencies |
| SO_SceneConfig.cs | _-Systems/SceneSystem/ | Scene setup references |
| SceneBootstrap.cs | _-Systems/SceneSystem/ | Bootstrap scene initialization |
| SandboxSetup.cs | _-Systems/SceneSystem/ | Sandbox scene objects |
| Dependency.md | _-Systems/SceneSystem/ | Scene dependencies |

**File count: 12 (3 shared + 5 DebugSystem + 4 SceneSystem)**

### Modifications to Earlier Phases

None — this phase consumes all earlier systems.

### Vertical Slice Tests

**Full Integration Test:**
- Scene loads with terrain, toolbar, weapon
- Spawn NPCs → shoot them → damage/ragdoll/blood
- Destroy terrain → voxel separation
- Pause menu works
- All audio/VFX present
- Slow motion toggle

### What It Looks Like When Running

The complete game sandbox. You spawn in a voxel environment with a toolbar. Select the Glock17, shoot the terrain — it destructs. Spawn NPCs, shoot them — they take damage, bleed, lose limbs, go ragdoll. Ambient atmosphere sounds play. Everything from all phases working together. Debug controls let you spawn test objects and toggle slow motion.

**Difficulty: Simple | Weight: 6%**

## Phase Dependency Order (DAG Verification)

```
phase-All ← phase-A ← phase-B ← phase-C ← phase-D
                                    ↑         ↑
                               phase-E   phase-F ← phase-G
                                                      ↑
                                              phase-H ← phase-I
```

**Verification per phase:**

| Phase | Imports From | All Earlier? |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| phase-A | phase-All | ✅ Yes |
| phase-B | phase-All, phase-A | ✅ Yes |
| phase-C | phase-All, phase-A, phase-B | ✅ Yes |
| phase-D | phase-All, phase-A, phase-B, phase-C | ✅ Yes |
| phase-E | phase-All, phase-A, phase-B, phase-D | ✅ Yes |
| phase-F | phase-All, phase-A, phase-B, phase-C | ✅ Yes |
| phase-G | phase-All, phase-A through phase-F | ✅ Yes |
| phase-H | phase-All, phase-A through phase-G | ✅ Yes |
| phase-I | phase-All, all prior | ✅ Yes |

No forward references. All arrows point LEFT.

# npcfr — StructureMap

> DataService specs per phase — exact collections, methods, nested types, folder trees.

## phase-All — Shared Foundation

```
phase-All/
├── 0-Core/
│   ├── Singleton.cs
│   └── GameEvents.cs
├── 1-Managers/
│   ├── UIManager.cs
│   └── DataManager.cs
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsAll.cs
├── 4-Utils/
│   └── Utils.cs
└── 7-3D/
    ├── MODEL.md
    ├── ANIM.md
    └── WORLD.md
```

**No DataService in phase-All** — only shared infrastructure.

## phase-A — Core + Input + UI Framework

```
phase-A/
├── _-Systems/
│   ├── InputSystem/
│   │   ├── SO_InputConfig.cs
│   │   ├── InputActionHandler.cs
│   │   ├── InputManager.cs
│   │   ├── MovementInputProvider.cs
│   │   ├── ButtonInputProvider.cs
│   │   ├── UnifiedInputManager.cs
│   │   ├── Interface/
│   │   │   ├── IInputProvider.cs
│   │   │   ├── IMovementInput.cs
│   │   │   └── IButtonInput.cs
│   │   ├── Test.md
│   │   └── Dependency.md
│   ├── GameStateSystem/
│   │   ├── GameStateCoordinator.cs
│   │   ├── CursorManager.cs
│   │   ├── Interface/
│   │   │   └── ICursorState.cs
│   │   ├── Test.md
│   │   └── Dependency.md
│   └── PauseSystem/
│       ├── PauseUI.cs
│       ├── Test.md
│       └── Dependency.md
├── 0-Core/
│   └── GameEvents.cs
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsA.cs
└── 4-Utils/
    └── PhaseALOG.cs
```

### InputSystem — No DataService Needed

Input system is stateless — it reads current input state from Unity InputSystem and exposes it via interfaces. No collections to manage.

### GameStateSystem — No DataService Needed

Game state is a simple boolean (isPaused, isCursorLocked). Managed by GameStateCoordinator directly.

### SO_ / Field_ / W Assignments

| Original | New Role | New Name |
|----------|----------|----------|
| le | Manager | InputManager |
| le.ld | Helper | InputActionHandler |
| kv | Provider | MovementInputProvider |
| kw | Provider | ButtonInputProvider |
| lc | Manager | UnifiedInputManager |
| lm | Manager | GameStateCoordinator |

---

## phase-B — Camera + First-Person Controller

```
phase-B/
├── _-Systems/
│   ├── CameraSystem/
│   │   ├── SO_CameraConfig.cs
│   │   ├── CameraController.cs
│   │   ├── CameraHandler.cs
│   │   ├── CameraServiceData.cs
│   │   ├── CameraShakeService.cs
│   │   ├── Interface/
│   │   │   ├── ICameraService.cs
│   │   │   └── ICameraShake.cs
│   │   ├── Test.md
│   │   └── Dependency.md
│   └── MovementSystem/
│       ├── SO_MovementConfig.cs
│       ├── PlayerMovement.cs
│       ├── CharacterControllerAdapter.cs
│       ├── Interface/
│       │   └── IMovementState.cs
│       ├── Test.md
│       └── Dependency.md
├── 0-Core/
│   └── GameEvents.cs
├── 2-Data/Enums/
│   └── GlobalEnumsB.cs
└── 4-Utils/
    └── PhaseBLOG.cs
```

### CameraSystem — No DataService Needed

Camera state (position, rotation, FOV) is runtime-only and stored in CameraServiceData as simple fields. No collection operations.

### MovementSystem — No DataService Needed

Movement is frame-by-frame computation — velocity, ground state, gravity. No collections.

### ToolbarDataService

**Collections:**
```csharp
List<ToolbarSlot> SLOT;                    // all toolbar slots
Dictionary<int, IToolbarItem> DOC__index_item; // slot index → item mapping

public static int HOTBAR_SIZE = 9;
```

**Nested Types:**
```csharp
public class ToolbarSlot
{
    public int index;
    public IToolbarItem item;      // null if empty
    public bool isSelected;
}
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
public void BuildSlots(int count)                // Initialize empty slots
public ToolbarSlot GetSlot(int index)             // Get slot at index
public ToolbarSlot GetSelectedSlot()              // Get currently selected slot
public IToolbarItem GetSelectedItem()             // Get item in selected slot
public void SelectSlot(int index)                 // Select slot, deselect previous
public void SetItem(int index, IToolbarItem item) // Place item in slot
public void ClearSlot(int index)                  // Remove item from slot
public int GetSelectedIndex()                     // Current selection index
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header)   // Snapshot via PhaseCLOG
```

### ItemHolderDataService

**Collections:**
```csharp
IHoldableItem currentItem;    // currently held item (null if empty)
IHoldableItem previousItem;   // previously held (for transition)
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
public void EquipItem(IHoldableItem item)    // Set current held item
public void UnequipItem()                     // Clear current item
public IHoldableItem GetCurrentItem()         // Query current
public bool IsHolding()                       // Has item?
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header)
```

### ContextMenuDataService

**Collections:**
```csharp
List<ContextMenuEntry> ENTRY;           // menu entries
bool isOpen;                             // menu state
```

**Nested Types:**
```csharp
public class ContextMenuEntry
{
    public string label;
    public Action onClick;
    public ContextMenuEntryType entryType; // simple, toggle, slider
    public float sliderValue;
    public bool toggleState;
}
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
public void BuildEntries(List<ContextMenuEntry> entries)
public List<ContextMenuEntry> GetEntries()
public void Clear()
public void SetOpen(bool open)
public bool IsOpen()
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header)
```

### VoxelMeshData (Not a DataService — data structure)

This is a data structure, not a DataService. Stores voxel grid as native containers for Job System compatibility.

```csharp
// Core data:
NativeHashMap<int3, Voxel> voxels;     // position → voxel data
NativeHashSet<int3> enabledVoxels;     // set of active voxel positions
int3 dimensions;                        // grid dimensions

public struct Voxel
{
    public int3 position;
    public bool isEnabled;
    public byte materialIndex;
    public byte albedoIndex;
    public byte metallicIndex;
    public byte emissionIndex;
}
```

### VoxelOperations (Static utility — not DataService)

```csharp
public static void DestroyVoxelsInRadius(VoxelMeshData data, int3 center, float radius)
public static void DestroyVoxel(VoxelMeshData data, int3 position)
public static bool IsVoxelEnabled(VoxelMeshData data, int3 position)
public static List<int3> GetNeighbors(int3 position)
public static bool IsChunkIsolated(VoxelMeshData data, HashSet<int3> chunk)
```

### HealthPool (DataService)

**Collections:**
```csharp
float currentHP;
float maxHP;
float minHP;
bool isDead;
```

**Events:**
```csharp
Action<float> OnDamaged;    // (amount)
Action<float> OnHealed;     // (amount)
Action OnDied;
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
public void Initialize(float maxHP)
public void TakeDamage(float amount)
public void Heal(float amount)
public float GetCurrentHP()
public float GetMaxHP()
public float GetHPPercent()
public bool IsDead()
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header)
```

### HealthTracker (DataService)

**Collections:**
```csharp
List<HealthPool> POOL;                    // all health pools on creature
Dictionary<string, HealthPool> DOC__name_pool; // named pools (head, torso, etc.)
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
public void RegisterPool(string name, HealthPool pool)
public float GetTotalHP()
public float GetTotalMaxHP()
public float GetOverallPercent()
public HealthPool GetPool(string name)
public bool IsAnyDead()
public string GetSnapShotForTest(string header)
```

```
└── 4-Utils/
    └── PhaseILOG.cs
```

### No DataService Needed

Debug and scene systems are setup/utility — no persistent collection management.

---

## Decision Trees

### Is it a Manager or MonoBehaviour?

```
Does it extend Singleton<T>? → YES → 1-Managers/ (UIManager, DataManager, AudioManager)
Does it own shared state others query? → YES → likely Manager, put in 1-Managers/
Does it only manage its own GO? → YES → MonoBehaviour inside _-Systems/
```

### Does it need DataService extraction?

```
Does the class have List<T> or Dictionary<K,V>?
  → YES → Can the collection be tested via `new`?
    → YES → Extract to DataService
    → NO (needs Rigidbody, Collider, etc.) → Keep in MonoBehaviour
  → NO → No DataService needed
```

### Where does the interface go?

```
Does only ONE system define AND use it? → Interface/ inside that system
Do 2+ systems use it but ONE defines it? → Interface/ inside the defining system (Spider pattern)
Do 3+ systems SHARE it with no clear owner? → phase-X/2-Data/Interface/ (RARE)
```

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/SystemIsolationAnalysis.md ==== -->
# npcfr — SystemIsolationAnalysis

> Bird's-eye view of ALL system relationships.
> Communication matrix, interface ownership, bridge catalog, events flow, isolation tiers.

## Communication Matrix

| From \ To | InputSys | GameStateSys | PauseSys | CameraSys | MovementSys | ToolbarSys | ItemHolderSys | ContextMenuSys | WeaponSys | RecoilSys | VoxelSys | TextureSys | HumanoidSys | EffectorSys | RagdollSys | HealthSys | LimbSys | GoreSys | AudioSys | VFXSys | DebugSys |
|-----------|----------|-------------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|--------------|---------------|-----------|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|---------|---------|----------|--------|----------|
| InputSys | — | Event | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| GameStateSys | | — | Event | Event | Event | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| PauseSys | | Event | — | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| CameraSys | Interface | Event | | — | | | | | | Interface | | | | | | | | | | | |
| MovementSys | Interface | Event | | | — | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| ToolbarSys | | | | | | — | Event | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| ItemHolderSys | | | | | | Event | — | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| ContextMenuSys | | | | | | | | — | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| WeaponSys | | | | | | | Interface | | — | | | | | | | | | | Event | Event | |
| RecoilSys | | | | Interface | | | | | Event | — | | | | | | | | | | | |
| VoxelSys | | | | | | | | | | | — | Read | | | | | | | Event | Event | |
| TextureSys | | | | | | | | | | | | — | | | | | | | | | |
| HumanoidSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | — | Event | Event | | | | | | |
| EffectorSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interface | — | | | | | | | |
| RagdollSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interface | Event | — | | | | | | |
| HealthSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interface | | | — | Event | Event | Event | Event | |
| LimbSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interface | | | Interface | — | Event | Event | | |
| GoreSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Event | Event | — | | | |
| AudioSys | | | | | | | | | Event | | Event | | Event | | | Event | Event | | — | | |
| VFXSys | | | | | | | | | Event | | Event | | | | | Event | | Event | | — | |
| DebugSys | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | — |

**Legend:** Event = GameEvents, Interface = implements/uses I*, Read = Singleton.Ins.Get*(), Bridge = pushes context via Bridge class

---

## Interface Ownership Map

| Interface | Owner System | Phase | Implementors |
|-----------|-------------|-------|--------------|
| IInputProvider | InputSystem | A | InputManager(A) |
| IMovementInput | InputSystem | A | MovementInputProvider(A) — consumed by CameraSystem(B), MovementSystem(B) |
| IButtonInput | InputSystem | A | ButtonInputProvider(A) |
| ICursorState | GameStateSystem | A | GameStateCoordinator(A) |
| ICameraService | CameraSystem | B | CameraController(B) |
| ICameraShake | CameraSystem | B | CameraShakeService(B) — consumed by RecoilSystem(D) |
| IMovementState | MovementSystem | B | PlayerMovement(B) |
| IToolbarService | ToolbarSystem | C | ToolbarOrchestrator(C) |
| IToolbarItem | ToolbarSystem | C | Glock17(D) implements — toolbar stores generic items |
| IItemHolder | ItemHolderSystem | C | ItemHolderOrchestrator(C) |
| IHoldableItem | ItemHolderSystem | C | Glock17(D) implements — item holder equips generic items |
| IWeapon | WeaponSystem | D | Glock17(D) |
| IBulletImpact | WeaponSystem | D | VoxelSystem(E) implements, HealthSystem(G) implements |
| IRecoilProvider | RecoilSystem | D | RecoilController(D) |
| IVoxelTerrain | VoxelSystem | E | VoxelMeshBuilder(E) |
| IVoxelRaycast | VoxelSystem | E | VoxelRaycaster(E) |
| IHumanoid | HumanoidSystem | F | HumanoidPuppeteer(F) — consumed by EffectorSystem(F), RagdollSystem(F), HealthSystem(G), LimbSystem(G) |
| ISpawnable | HumanoidSystem | F | HumanoidSpawner(F) |
| IEffector | EffectorSystem | F | EffectorBase(F) |
| IEffectorHost | EffectorSystem | F | EffectorCollection(F) |
| IHealth | HealthSystem | G | HealthPool(G) |
| IDamageable | HealthSystem | G | DamageLimb(G), VoxelSystem(E) |
| ILimb | LimbSystem | G | AbstractLimb(G), DamageLimb(G), BloodDrainLimb(G), etc. |
| ILimbDamage | LimbSystem | G | DamageLimb(G) |
| IAudioPlayer | AudioSystem | H | AudioManager(H) |

---

## Bridge Pattern Catalog

| Bridge | Pattern | From → To | Phase | Purpose |
|--------|---------|-----------|-------|---------|
| InputContextBridge | Event-Push | InputSystem → CameraSystem | A→B | Pushes input axis values to camera on input mode change |
| ToolbarEquipBridge | Event-Push | ToolbarSystem → ItemHolderSystem | C | Pushes selected item to holder on selection change |
| BulletImpactBridge | Event-Response | WeaponSystem → VoxelSystem, HealthSystem | D→E,G | Routes bullet impact events to appropriate damageable systems |
| HumanoidDamageBridge | Event-Push | HealthSystem → LimbSystem | G | Routes damage to specific limbs based on hit data |
| AudioEventBridge | Event-Push | Multiple → AudioSystem | Various→H | Translates game events into SFX type requests |

---

## GameEvents Flow (ALL phases)

| Event | Phase Defined | Raiser(s) | Subscriber(s) |
|-------|--------------|-----------|---------------|
| OnMenuStateChanged(bool) | All | PauseUI(A), ContextMenuUI(C) | UIManager(All), InputManager(A), CameraController(B), PlayerMovement(B) |
| OnCloseAllSubManagers | All | UIManager(All) | PauseUI(A), ContextMenuUI(C) |
| OnInputModeChanged(InputType) | A | InputManager(A) | CameraController(B), MovementSystem(B) |
| OnPauseToggled(bool) | A | GameStateCoordinator(A) | PlayerMovement(B), CameraSystem(B) |
| OnCursorStateChanged(bool) | A | GameStateCoordinator(A) | CameraController(B) |
| OnToolbarSelectionChanged(int) | C | ToolbarOrchestrator(C) | ItemHolderSystem(C), WeaponSystem(D) |
| OnItemEquipped(IHoldableItem) | C | ItemHolderOrchestrator(C) | CameraSystem(B), WeaponSystem(D) |
| OnItemUnequipped | C | ItemHolderOrchestrator(C) | CameraSystem(B) |
| OnContextMenuRequested | C | Various | ContextMenuOrchestrator(C) |
| OnContextMenuClosed | C | ContextMenuUI(C) | Various |
| OnWeaponFired | D | Glock17(D) | AudioSystem(H), VFXSystem(H), RecoilSystem(D) |
| OnBulletImpact(Vector3, IBulletImpact) | D | PistolBullet(D) | VoxelSystem(E), HealthSystem(G), GoreSystem(G), VFXSystem(H) |
| OnReloadStarted | D | Glock17(D) | AudioSystem(H) |
| OnReloadFinished | D | Glock17(D) | — |
| OnVoxelDestroyed(int3) | E | VoxelOperations(E) | AudioSystem(H), VFXSystem(H) |
| OnVoxelChunkSeparated | E | VoxelSeparator(E) | — |
| OnHumanoidSpawned(IHumanoid) | F | HumanoidSpawner(F) | EffectorSystem(F), RagdollSystem(F), HealthSystem(G), LimbSystem(G) |
| OnHumanoidDied(IHumanoid) | G | HealthSystem(G) | RagdollSystem(F), GoreSystem(G), AudioSystem(H) |
| OnRagdollActivated(IHumanoid) | F | RagdollSystem(F) | EffectorSystem(F) |
| OnDamageDealt(IHumanoid, float, Vector3) | G | DamageCalculator(G) | HealthSystem(G), GoreSystem(G), AudioSystem(H), VFXSystem(H) |
| OnLimbDetached(ILimb) | G | DamageLimb(G) | GoreSystem(G), AudioSystem(H) |
| OnHealthChanged(IHumanoid, float) | G | HealthPool(G) | — |
| OnPlaySFX(ImpactSFXType, Vector3) | H | Various | AudioManager(H) |
| OnPlayWeaponSFX(WeaponSFXType) | H | Glock17(D) | AudioManager(H) |

---

## Isolation Tiers Summary

| Tier | Systems | Copy-paste portable? |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| L0 (zero deps) | InputSystem, GameStateSystem, PauseSystem, ToolbarSystem, ContextMenuSystem, VoxelSystem, TextureSystem, VoxelJobSystem, HumanoidSystem, AudioSystem, VFXSystem, DebugSystem, SceneSystem | ✅ Yes — copy folder, compiles |
| L1 (1 dep) | CameraSystem, MovementSystem, ItemHolderSystem, WeaponSystem, RecoilSystem, EffectorSystem, RagdollSystem, GoreSystem | ✅ With 1 interface stub |
| L2 (2 deps) | LimbSystem | ⚠️ Needs 2 interface stubs |

---

## Coupling Hotspots

### Integration Hubs (3+ incoming connections)
- **HealthSystem** — 4 incoming: WeaponSystem (bullet impact), HumanoidSystem (spawn), LimbSystem (limb damage), EffectorSystem (force feedback)
- **AudioSystem** — 5 incoming: WeaponSystem, VoxelSystem, HealthSystem, LimbSystem, GoreSystem
- **VFXSystem** — 4 incoming: WeaponSystem, VoxelSystem, HealthSystem, GoreSystem

### Dependency-Heavy Systems (3+ outgoing connections)
- **WeaponSystem** — 3 outgoing: RecoilSystem, AudioSystem, VFXSystem
- **HealthSystem** — 3 outgoing: GoreSystem, AudioSystem, VFXSystem

### Recommendations
- All hotspot connections are EVENT-BASED (GameEvents) — no concrete coupling. Architecture is healthy.
- AudioSystem and VFXSystem are pure listeners (📡 Listener shape) — they only subscribe, never import game-specific types.
- HealthSystem is the main integration hub — all damage flows converge here. Its interfaces (IHealth, IDamageable) are the key contracts.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/SystemPortabilityMap.md ==== -->
# npcfr — SystemPortabilityMap

> L0/L1+ classification per system. Shapes, deps, FREE items.
> L0 = zero deps, copy folder → compiles. L1+ = each external _-Systems/ import = +1.

---

## FREE Items (don't count as dependency)

- GameEvents (partial static class)
- Singleton<T> (base class)
- UIManager (panel routing)
- DataManager (shared visual data)
- Utils.* (extensions)
- TimeSince / TimeUntil (time helpers)
- GlobalEnumsX (shared enums)
- All phase-All/ infra

---

| System | Level | Shape | Portable? | Interfaces Owned | External Deps | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|-----------|-----------------|---------------|-------|
| InputSystem | L0 | 🕷️ Spider + 📡 Broadcaster | ✅ | IInputProvider, IMovementInput, IButtonInput | — | Wraps Unity InputSystem. Defines input contracts. |
| GameStateSystem | L0 | 📡 Listener | ✅ | ICursorState | — | Subscribes to menu events, manages cursor. |

| System | Level | Shape | Portable? | Interfaces Owned | External Deps | Notes |

| System | Level | Shape | Portable? | Interfaces Owned | External Deps | Notes |

|--------|-------|-------|-----------|-----------------|---------------|-------|

| SceneSystem | L0 | 🌍 Infrastructure | ✅ | — | — | Scene bootstrapping. |
