## Summary

| Level | Count | Systems |
|-------|-------|---------|
| L0 | 13 | InputSystem, GameStateSystem, PauseSystem, ToolbarSystem, ContextMenuSystem, VoxelSystem, TextureSystem, VoxelJobSystem, HumanoidSystem, AudioSystem, VFXSystem, DebugSystem, SceneSystem |
| L1 | 8 | CameraSystem, MovementSystem, ItemHolderSystem, WeaponSystem, RecoilSystem, EffectorSystem, RagdollSystem, GoreSystem |
| L2 | 1 | LimbSystem |

**22 systems total. 13 L0 (59%), 8 L1 (36%), 1 L2 (5%).** High portability across the board — all deps are interface-based.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/0-Core/GameEvents.cs ==== -->
using System;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// I deliver messages between systems. Each phase adds events via partial class.
/// Every Raise logs subscriber count for debugging.
/// </summary>
public static partial class GameEvents
{
    // when menu state changes >>
    public static event Action<bool> OnMenuStateChanged;
    public static void RaiseMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnMenuStateChanged), OnMenuStateChanged);
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged?
            .Invoke(isAnyMenuOpen);
    }
    // << when menu state changes

    // when all sub-managers must close >>
    public static event Action OnCloseAllSubManagers;
    public static void RaiseCloseAllSubManagers()
    {
        LogSubscribersCount(nameof(OnCloseAllSubManagers), OnCloseAllSubManagers);
        GameEvents.OnCloseAllSubManagers?
            .Invoke();
    }
    // << when all sub-managers must close

    /// <summary> Logs how many subscribers an event has — helps catch missing/extra subscriptions. </summary>
    static void LogSubscribersCount(string name, Delegate del)
    {
        int count = del?.GetInvocationList()?.Length ?? 0;
        Debug.Log($"[GameEvents] {name} raised for -> {count} subscribers".colorTag("lime"));
    }
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/0-Core/Singleton.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// Generic singleton base — first instance wins, duplicates destroyed.
/// Access via Singleton<T>.Ins. All Managers extend this.
/// </summary>
public abstract class Singleton<T> : MonoBehaviour where T : MonoBehaviour
{
    public static T Ins { get; private set; }

    protected virtual void Awake()
    {
        if (Ins == null) Ins = this as T;
        else if (Ins != this)
        {
            Debug.Log($"{typeof(T)} singleton already exists, destroying duplicate");
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/1-Managers/DataManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// shared visual data: materials, layer masks, physics materials.
/// Grows across phases as systems need shared lookups.
/// </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("npcfr/Managers/DataManager")]
public class DataManager : Singleton<DataManager>
{
    #region Inspector Fields
    [Header("Materials")]
    [SerializeField] Material _defaultMaterial;

    [Header("Layer Masks")]
    [SerializeField] LayerMask _voxelLayer;
    [SerializeField] LayerMask _humanoidLayer;
    [SerializeField] LayerMask _interactableLayer;
    #endregion
    #region Public API
    public Material GetDefaultMaterial() => _defaultMaterial;
    public LayerMask GetVoxelLayer() => _voxelLayer;
    public LayerMask GetHumanoidLayer() => _humanoidLayer;
    public LayerMask GetInteractableLayer() => _interactableLayer;
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/1-Managers/UIManager.cs ==== -->
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// reports menu state + closes all panels + routes keyboard input with priority
/// </summary>
[DefaultExecutionOrder(-100)]
[AddComponentMenu("npcfr/Managers/UIManager")]
public class UIManager : Singleton<UIManager>
{
    #region private API
    bool isAnyMenuOpen = false;
    #endregion
    #region Public API
    public bool GetIsAnyMenuOpen() => isAnyMenuOpen;

    /// <summary> Fires all Close events — SubManagers disable themselves. UIManager never calls SetActive directly. </summary>
    public void CloseAllSubManager()
    {
        // purpose: force-close all open panels (e.g. when opening a new exclusive panel)
        GameEvents.RaiseCloseAllSubManagers();
    }
    #endregion
    #region Unity Life Cycle
    protected override void Awake()
    {
        base.Awake();
    }

    private void OnEnable()
    {
        // purpose: track menu state for input routing
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged += HandleMenuStateChanged;
    }

    private void OnDisable()
    {
        GameEvents.OnMenuStateChanged -= HandleMenuStateChanged;
    }

    void HandleMenuStateChanged(bool isAnyMenuOpen)
    {
        this.isAnyMenuOpen = isAnyMenuOpen;
    }
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/2-Data/Enums/GlobalEnumsAll.cs ==== -->
/// <summary> Unity tags as enum — no raw string tags. Grows across phases. </summary>
public enum TagType
{
    // Phase All:
    untagged,
    player,

    // Phase D:
    bullet,
    shellCasing,

    // Phase E:
    voxel,
    voxelChunk,

    // Phase F:
    humanoid,
    humanoidLimb,

    // Phase G:
    gore,
    bloodDecal,
}

/// <summary> Input device type — mouse+keyboard vs gamepad. </summary>
public enum InputType
{
    mouseKeyboard,
    gamepad,
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/4-Utils/Utils.cs ==== -->
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// Shared extensions — grows across phases. All reusable utility code lives here.
/// No per-phase UtilsPhaseX.cs — per-phase 4-Utils/ only has PhaseXLOG.cs.
/// </summary>
public static class Utils
{
    #region Collection Extensions
    public static List<TResult> map<T, TResult>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, TResult> selector)
        => source.Select(selector).ToList();

    public static T find<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, bool> predicate)
        => source.FirstOrDefault(predicate);

    public static bool all<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, bool> predicate)
        => source.All(predicate);

    public static float sum<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, float> selector)
        => source.Sum(selector);

    public static T getRandom<T>(this IList<T> list)
        => list[UnityEngine.Random.Range(0, list.Count)];

    public static List<TResult> flatMap<T, TResult>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Func<T, IEnumerable<TResult>> selector)
        => source.SelectMany(selector).ToList();

    public static void forEach<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, Action<T> action)
    {
        foreach (var item in source) action(item);
    }

    public static TValue GetOrCreate<TKey, TValue>(this Dictionary<TKey, TValue> dict, TKey key) where TValue : new()
    {
        if (dict.TryGetValue(key, out var value) == false)
        {
            value = new TValue();
            dict[key] = value;
        }
        return value;
    }
    #endregion
    #region Unity Extensions
    public static T gc<T>(this GameObject go) where T : Component
        => go.GetComponent<T>();

    public static T gc<T>(this Component comp) where T : Component
        => comp.GetComponent<T>();

    public static void toggle(this GameObject go, bool value)
        => go.SetActive(value);

    public static void destroyLeaves(this Transform parent)
    {
        for (int i = parent.childCount - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            UnityEngine.Object.Destroy(parent.GetChild(i).gameObject);
    }
    #endregion
    #region Tag Extensions
    public static bool HasTag(this Component comp, TagType tag)
        => comp.CompareTag(tag.ToString());

    public static bool HasTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag)
        => go.CompareTag(tag.ToString());

    public static void SetTag(this GameObject go, TagType tag)
        => go.tag = tag.ToString();
    #endregion
    #region String Extensions
    public static string colorTag(this string text, string color)
        => $"<color={color}>{text}</color>";

    public static string repeat(this char c, int count)
        => new string(c, count);

    public static string repeat(this string s, int count)
        => string.Concat(Enumerable.Repeat(s, count));
    #endregion
    #region JSON
    public static string ToNSJson<T>(this T obj, bool pretify = false)
        => JsonUtility.ToJson(obj, pretify);
    #endregion
}

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/7-3D/ANIM.md ==== -->
# npcfr — ANIM.md

> Animation conventions, state machines, clip naming, parameters.
> Organized by phase.

---

## Conventions

- **Parameters:** Use enum-style naming (camelCase). Never raw strings.
- **State Machine:** Each AnimatorController has a simple state graph
- **Transitions:** HasExitTime = false for interrupt-based, true for natural completion

---

## Phase D — Weapon Animations

### Glock17 Animator

**Parameters:**
```
bool isFiring
bool isReloading
trigger fire
trigger reload
```

**State Machine:**
```
[Idle] ──fire──→ [Fire] ──→ [Idle]
  │                            
  └──reload──→ [Reload] ──→ [Idle]
```

**Clips:**
| Clip | Duration | What Moves |
|------|----------|-----------|
| Glock17_Idle | loop | Subtle breathing sway on ViewModel |
| Glock17_Fire | 0.1s | Bolt slides back, recoil kick |
| Glock17_Reload | 1.5s | Magazine drop, insert, bolt release |

---

## Phase F — Humanoid Animations

### Humanoid Animator

**Parameters:**
```
float speed              → blend tree walk/run
bool isGrounded
trigger ragdollActivate
HumanoidMoveStateEnterMode moveState
```

**State Machine:**
```
[Idle] ──speed>0.1──→ [Walk/Run Blend]
  │                        │
  └──ragdoll──→ [Ragdoll] ←┘
                    │
                    └──→ [GetUp] ──→ [Idle]
```

**Clips:**
| Clip | Duration | What Moves |
|------|----------|-----------|
| Humanoid_Idle | loop | Breathing, subtle weight shift |
| Humanoid_Walk | loop | Standard walk cycle |
| Humanoid_Run | loop | Running cycle |
| Humanoid_GetUp | 2s | From ragdoll prone → standing |

### LVA Effector Animations

LVA (Limb-Vertebra-Animation) is procedural — NOT clip-based.
Effectors drive bone rotations via code, not AnimationClips.
Each effector has configurable spring/damper values in SO_HumanoidConfig.

---

## Phase G — Damage Animations

### Limb Detach
- No animation clip — physics-driven
- On detach: limb GO gets Rigidbody, receives impulse force
- Stump gets particle system (blood spray)

### Hit Reaction
- Procedural via LVA effectors — hit force direction → effector impulse
- No pre-baked hit reaction clips

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/7-3D/MODEL.md ==== -->
# npcfr — MODEL.md

> Every 3D mesh the project needs, organized by phase.
> Naming, scale, pivot, prefab hierarchy, LOD.

---

## Conventions

- **Scale:** 1 Unity unit = 1 meter
- **Pivot:** Bottom-center for standing objects, center for held items
- **Naming:** PascalCase, descriptive (`Glock17`, `HumanoidBase`, `VoxelChunk`)
- **Prefabs:** Always have a root empty GO → child meshes + colliders

---

## Phase D — Weapon Models

### Glock17
- Shape: Pistol, ~0.2m length
- Children: `WorldModel` (full mesh), `ViewModel` (first-person hands + gun)
- Components: MeshFilter, MeshRenderer, BoxCollider
- ViewModel has: muzzle point (empty GO at barrel tip), shell ejection point, bolt slide
- Vertex range: 500-2000 (low-poly acceptable)

### PistolBullet
- Shape: Small cylinder ~0.01m
- Trail Renderer on child GO
- Rigidbody (no gravity, high velocity)

### ShellCasing
- Shape: Small cylinder ~0.008m
- Rigidbody + CapsuleCollider
- Spawns at ejection point, applies random torque

---

## Phase E — Voxel Terrain

### VoxelChunk
- Procedurally generated mesh — NOT a model asset
- VoxelMeshBuilder creates mesh at runtime
- Each chunk = grid of voxels (e.g. 16x16x16)
- Material slots: LitOpaque, Transparent, Liquid (via MeshGroupMaterialType enum)

---

## Phase F — NPC Humanoid

### HumanoidBase
- Humanoid mesh with full skeleton (standard Unity Humanoid rig)
- ~30 bones: Hips → Spine → Chest → Head, Arms (Upper→Lower→Hand), Legs (Upper→Lower→Foot)
- Each limb bone has: Rigidbody, CapsuleCollider, ConfigurableJoint
- LVA effectors attach to specific bones
- Vertex range: 2000-5000

### HumanoidLimb (detachable)
- Each major limb (arm, leg, head) can detach as separate GO
- Detached limb gets: own Rigidbody, MeshFilter (copy of limb mesh), CapsuleCollider

---

## Phase H — VFX

### MuzzleFlash
- Particle system prefab at gun barrel
- Burst emission, short lifetime, additive blend

### BloodDecal
- Quad mesh projected onto surfaces
- Decal projector or simple quad + blood texture

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/phase-All/7-3D/WORLD.md ==== -->
# npcfr — WORLD.md

> World/level design conventions, layout, scale, lighting.
> npcfr is a sandbox — single open arena with destructible voxel terrain.

---

## World Layout

npcfr is a **single sandbox scene** — no level progression, no loading screens.

### Top-Down Map (ASCII)
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                      │
│           VOXEL TERRAIN              │
│         (destructible grid)          │
│                                      │
│     ┌──────────┐                     │
│     │  SPAWN   │    ← player start   │
│     │  POINT   │                     │
│     └──────────┘                     │
│                                      │
│              NPC SPAWN AREA          │
│           (god-mode spawning)        │
│                                      │
│                                      │
│           OPEN ARENA                 │
│        (physics playground)          │
│                                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Scale
- Arena: ~100m × 100m (open flat area)
- Voxel grid: configurable size (e.g. 64×32×64 voxels)
- Each voxel: ~0.5m cube
- Player height: ~1.8m (CharacterController)
- NPCs: ~1.8m (humanoid scale)

---

## Scene Hierarchy

```
Scene: Sandbox
├── --- MANAGERS ---
│   ├── UIManager
│   ├── DataManager
│   └── InputManager
├── --- PLAYER ---
│   ├── Player (CharacterController + PlayerMovement)
│   │   └── Camera (Cinemachine VirtualCamera)
│   │       └── ViewModelContainer (weapon/tool viewmodels)
├── --- WORLD ---
│   ├── VoxelTerrain (VoxelMeshBuilder)
│   │   └── Chunks (generated at runtime)
│   ├── Ground (flat plane under voxel terrain)
│   └── Skybox
├── --- UI ---
│   ├── Canvas (Screen Space - Overlay)
│   │   ├── HUD
│   │   │   ├── Toolbar (hotbar slots)
│   │   │   └── Crosshair
│   │   ├── PauseMenu (disabled by default)
│   │   └── ContextMenu (disabled by default)
│   └── EventSystem
├── --- LIGHTING ---
│   ├── Directional Light
│   └── Reflection Probe
└── --- POOLS --- (if object pooling added)
    ├── BulletPool
    └── ShellCasingPool
```

---

## Lighting

- **Directional Light:** Warm sunlight, soft shadows
- **Ambient:** Gradient sky (light blue top, warm bottom)
- **No baked lighting** — sandbox is fully dynamic
- **Post-processing:** Minimal — bloom on muzzle flash, color grading

---

## Layers

| Layer | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| Default (0) | General objects |
| Player (8) | Player CharacterController — ignore self-collision |
| Voxel (9) | Voxel terrain chunks — bullet raycasts target this |
| Humanoid (10) | NPC bodies — separate from terrain for selective raycasting |
| Interactable (11) | Objects player can interact with (context menu) |
| Projectile (12) | Bullets, shells — ignore player collision |
| UI (5) | UI elements |

---

## Tags

All tags mapped to `TagType` enum in `GlobalEnumsAll.cs`. No raw strings.

---

## Camera

- **Type:** Cinemachine FreeLook (first-person)
- **FOV:** 60° default, widens to 75° when sprinting
- **Near/Far clip:** 0.1 / 500
- **Position:** Player eye height (~1.6m above ground)

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/npcfr/surfer.md ==== -->
# npcfr — Reasoning Log

> Append after each agent prompt. Never edit previous entries — only add new ones.
> Each entry captures: what was asked, what was decided, what was discovered, what changed.

---

## Prompt 1 — Initial Source Analysis (`/init`)

**Asked:** Bootstrap all architecture docs from raw source for npcfr project.

**Key Discoveries:**
- 1093 .cs files in Assembly-CSharp, heavily obfuscated (2-3 letter class names)
- Uses Zenject DI throughout (NOT typical Singleton pattern) — must replace with Singleton<T> + GameEvents
- ~200+ interfaces, most are empty/single-method stubs generated by obfuscation
- ~200 stub files (<10 lines each) — collapse into parent scripts
- Biggest god object: `bcx.cs` (~2000 lines, 8 nested classes) — the humanoid controller
- Other god objects: `ox.cs` (264 lines), `le.cs` (266 lines), `rp.cs` (250 lines), `bam.cs` (271 lines)
- Custom LVA (Limb-Vertebra-Animation) framework — 54 files, procedural bone animation
- Third-party: Zenject, Cinemachine, Unity.InputSystem, DOTween, OdinInspector, AdvancedDissolve, Linework, QuickOutline, Unity.Mathematics, Unity.Collections, Unity.Jobs+Burst, Unity.Splines, TripledoseLibs, Kinemation.Recoilly
- Named subdirectories provide system boundaries: Glock17/, PistolBullet/, ActiveRagdoll/, VoxelMeshGeneration/, LVA/, VFX/, Spawnables/

**Decisions Made:**
- 9 phases (A-I) + phase-All, all ≤25 files per phase
- Phase ordering follows dependency DAG: Input → Camera/Movement → Toolbar/Items → Weapon → Voxel → Humanoid → Damage → Audio/VFX → Debug
- bcx.cs splits into 8 scripts across HumanoidSystem (phase-F)
- Zenject bindings (~80 files) removed entirely — replaced by Singleton pattern
- 150+ empty interfaces collapsed into ~25 meaningful cross-system contracts
- VoxelSystem keeps Unity.Jobs+Burst internally (performance-critical)
- LVA system kept as EffectorSystem — procedural animation is core gameplay
- No EconomyManager needed — npcfr has no currency/progression (pure sandbox)
- 22 systems total, 13 L0 (59% portable), 8 L1, 1 L2

**What Changed:** Created ARCHITECTURE.md, PhaseMap.md, StructureMap.md, GOAL.md, NewAgent.md, Estimate.md, SystemPortabilityMap.md, CoverageMap.md, OptionalFeatures.md, SystemIsolationAnalysis.md, surfer.md, phase-All/ scaffolding (Singleton.cs, GameEvents.cs, UIManager.cs, DataManager.cs, GlobalEnumsAll.cs, Utils.cs, MODEL.md, ANIM.md, WORLD.md).

---

# ARCHITECTURE — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Source Overview

| Metric | Value |

| **Total project size** | 10.242 GB |
| **Game code files (.cs)** | 2,041 |
| **ScheduleOne namespace files** | 1,752 |
| **Third-party/engine code files** | 289 |
| **Domain folders** | 66 |
| **Engine** | Unity 2022.x (URP) |
| **Networking** | FishNet |
| **Pathfinding** | A\* Pathfinding Project |
| **IK** | RootMotion / Final IK |
| **Multiplayer hosting** | Edgegap |
| **Platform** | Steam (Steamworks.NET) |
| **Scenes** | Main (open world), Menu, Tutorial |

                                → SurfaceItem → ToggleableSurfaceItem

                   TileGrid, Surface

## Singleton Registry

### NetworkSingleton (networked, server-authoritative)
| Singleton | Domain | Owns | Readers |
|-----------|--------|------|---------|
| `GameManager` | DevUtilities | Seed, settings, org name, save state | Everything |
| `TimeManager` | GameTime | Current time, day/night, sleep state | NPCs, Growing, Map, UI, Quests |
| `ProductManager` | Product | Product definitions, recipes, mix state | Economy, Stations, UI, Persistence |
| `NPCManager` | NPCs | NPC spawn/despawn, NPC list | Economy, Quests, Persistence, UI |
| `VehicleManager` | Vehicles | Vehicle list, spawn/despawn | Player, NPCs, Map, Persistence |
| `MoneyManager` | Money | Online balance, cash, networth | Player, Economy, UI, Persistence |
| `QuestManager` | Quests | Quest state, active/completed quests | NPCs, Economy, UI, Persistence |
| `LevelManager` | Levelling | XP, rank, tier unlocks | Economy, Quests, Map, UI |
| `CombatManager` | Combat | Explosion effects, damage events | Player, NPCs, Vehicles |
| `StorageManager` | Storage | All storage entities | Stations, UI, Persistence |
| `TrashManager` | Trash | Trash items, cleanup timers | Employees, UI, Property |
| `EmployeeManager` | Employees | Employee assignments | Property, Management, UI |
| `GraffitiManager` | Graffiti | Spray surfaces, drawings | UI, Persistence |
| `MessagingManager` | Messaging | Conversations, message delivery | NPCs, UI, Quests |
| `DeliveryManager` | Delivery | Delivery locations, dead drops | Economy, NPCs, UI |
| `DragManager` | Dragging | Drag state (station items) | Stations, UI |
| `SewerManager` | Map | Sewer access, goblin system | NPCs, Player, Persistence |
| `DarkMarket` | Map | Online item shop | UI, Economy |
| `PhysicsManager` | GamePhysics | Physics config | Vehicles, Combat |
| `ShopManager` | UI.Shop | Shop listings, purchases | Economy, Persistence |
| `ReplicationQueue` | Networking | Staggered replication queue | All managers |
| `Cartel` | Cartel | Cartel state, regional activity | NPCs, Law, Quests |
| `CheckpointManager` | Law | Checkpoint activation state | Police, NPCs |
| `CurfewManager` | Law | Curfew schedule, warnings | NPCs, Player, UI |
| `DailySummary` | UI | Daily income/expense summary | Economy, UI |
| `VariableDatabase` | Variables | All game variables, conditions | Quests, NPCs, Map, Progression |

---

## Coupling Hotspots

### 1. FindObjectOfType — **ZERO** instances (clean)
The entire codebase uses singleton access patterns exclusively. No `FindObjectOfType` or `FindObjectsOfType` calls exist.

### 2. Static Instance Access (Singleton.Instance)
Every manager is accessed globally via `ManagerName.Instance`. The heaviest consumers:
- **Player.cs** → reads 15+ singletons
- **Customer.cs** → reads ProductManager, NPCManager, QuestManager, MoneyManager, TimeManager
- **Station objects** → read ProductManager, StorageManager, ManagementMode
- **UI classes** → read nearly every manager

### 3. Direct Cross-Domain References
| Source | Target | Type |
|--------|--------|------|
| Player | LandVehicle | Direct field (`CurrentVehicle`) |
| Player | Skateboard | Direct field + events |
| NPC | LandVehicle | Direct field + events |
| Customer | Dealer | AssignedDealer reference |
| Customer | Supplier | Supplier recommendation chain |
| Dealer | Customer | List\<Customer> AssignedCustomers |
| Employee | Property/Business | Direct assignment |
| PoliceOfficer | Player | Pursuit target |
| Station objects | ProductManager | Product/recipe lookup |
| Station objects | StorageManager | Item transit |
| TransitRoute | ITransitEntity | Source/Destination |
| GrowContainer | TimeManager | Time event subscription |

### 4. Static Registry Lists
| Class | Static List | Purpose |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| Player | `PlayerList`, `Local` | All players + local reference |
| Customer | `LockedCustomers`, `UnlockedCustomers` | Customer progression |
| Dealer | `AllPlayerDealers` | Recruited dealers |
| ProductManager | `DiscoveredProducts`, `ListedProducts`, `FavouritedProducts` | Product state |

---

## Event System

### Pattern Mix
The codebase uses three event patterns interchangeably:

1. **C# `Action<T>`** — Most common for inter-system communication
2. **`UnityEvent`** — Used for Inspector-configurable callbacks (stations, NPCs, UI)
3. **Custom `delegate`** — Rare, used in Player (VehicleEvent), NPCInventory (ItemFilter), Messaging (ResponseCallback), Noise (HearingEvent)

### Central Event Hubs

**TimeManager** (temporal events):
- `onHourPass`, `onDayPass`, `onWeekPass` — time progression
- `onUpdate`, `onFixedUpdate` — frame-rate callbacks (centralized)
- `onTimeSkip`, `onTimeChanged` — time manipulation
- `onSleepStart`, `onSleepEnd` — sleep cycle
- `onFirstNight` — tutorial trigger

**Player** (player lifecycle):
- `static onLocalPlayerSpawned` — local player ready
- `static onPlayerSpawned/onPlayerDespawned` — any player
- `onEnterVehicle/onExitVehicle` — vehicle state
- `onRagdoll/onRagdollEnd` — physics state
- `onArrested/onFreed/onTased/onTasedEnd` — law state
- `onPassedOut/onPassOutRecovery` — consciousness

**NPC** (NPC lifecycle):
- `onEnterVehicle/onExitVehicle`, `onVisibilityChanged`, `onConversationCreated`

**NPCAwareness** (crime detection):
- `onNoticedGeneralCrime/PettyCrime/DrugDealing/PlayerViolatingCurfew/SuspiciousPlayer`
- `onGunshotHeard/onExplosionHeard/onHitByCar`

**ItemSlot** (inventory changes):
- `onItemDataChanged`, `onItemInstanceChanged`, `onLocked/onUnlocked`, `onFilterChange`

**Economy events**:
- `Customer.onCustomerUnlocked` (static), `onDealCompleted`, `onContractAssigned`
- `Dealer.onDealerRecruited` (static), `onContractAccepted`
- `ProductManager.onProductDiscovered/Created/Listed/Delisted`
- `MoneyManager.onNetworthCalculation`
- `LevelManager.onRankUp/onRankChanged`

---

## Data Flow

### Product Creation Pipeline
```
StationRecipe (SO)
  → Player inserts ingredients into Station (ItemSlot)
  → Station validates recipe (DoIngredientsSuffice)
  → Station starts operation (SendCookOperation RPC)
  → Timer runs (CookTime from recipe)
  → Station creates output (ProductStationItem → StorableItemInstance)
  → Output goes to OutputSlot (IItemSlotOwner)
  → Player collects → PlayerInventory
```

### Product Value Pipeline
```
ProductDefinition (SO, base values)
  → PropertyItemDefinition (mix ingredient, adds EProperty effects)
  → MixerMap (maps ingredient → effect on product)
  → ProductManager.CalculateProductValue(properties, quality)
  → Customer.GetProductEnjoyment(affinity + quality + addiction)
  → Contract price = base value × quality multiplier × customer preference
```

### Save/Load Pipeline
```
SaveManager.Save()
  → Iterates ISaveable list (ordered by LoadOrder for IBaseSaveable)
  → Each saveable: GetSaveString() → JSON
  → WriteData() → disk (SaveGame_N/subfolder/file.json)
  → 15 saves per frame (batched)

LoadManager.StartGame()
  → Read SaveInfo (save slot metadata)
  → Load scene → Initialize IBaseSaveables (by LoadOrder)
  → For each saveable: Load(json) → restore state
  → Staggered replication queue for network sync
  → Fire onLoadComplete
```

### Transit (Item Automation) Pipeline
```
ITransitEntity.OutputSlots (source station)
  → TransitRoute (source → destination link)
  → Employee walks: source.OutputSlot → picks up → destination.InputSlot
  → ITransitEntity.InsertItemIntoInput() (destination station)
  → ConfigurationReplicator syncs field changes over network
```

### NPC Decision Pipeline
```
NPCScheduleManager → NPCEvent (current schedule event)
  → NPCBehaviour stack (priority-ordered)
  → NPCActions (action execution)
  → NPCMovement (A* pathfinding + physical movement)
  → NPCAwareness (vision cone + hearing)
  → NPCResponses (reaction to awareness events)
```

---

## Inheritance Chains

### Worth Preserving
```
NetworkBehaviour
  └── BuildableItem (abstract)
        ├── GridItem
        │     └── ToggleableItem
        └── SurfaceItem
              └── ToggleableSurfaceItem

ScriptableObject
  └── ItemDefinition
        └── StorableItemDefinition
              ├── QualityItemDefinition
              │     └── ProductDefinition (abstract)
              │           ├── WeedDefinition
              │           ├── CocaineDefinition
              │           ├── MethDefinition
              │           └── ShroomDefinition
              └── PropertyItemDefinition

NetworkBehaviour
  └── NPC
        ├── Dealer (: IItemSlotOwner)
        ├── Employee (: IConfigurable)
        │     ├── Botanist, Chemist, Packager, Cleaner
        └── (Customer is NOT a subclass — uses composition)

MonoBehaviour
  └── Singleton<T>
        └── PersistentSingleton<T>
NetworkBehaviour
  └── NetworkSingleton<T>
MonoBehaviour
  └── PlayerSingleton<T>

Quest (abstract)
  └── QuestEntry (many concrete types)
  └── Contract (deal-based quest)

Behaviour (abstract, NPCs.Behaviour)
  └── 54 concrete behaviour classes
```

### Worth Flattening
```
MonoBehaviour → StationItem → ProductStationItem/LiquidMeth_StationItem
  (shallow, could be interface-based)

MonoBehaviour → ItemModule → IngredientModule/CookableModule/PourableModule
  (already thin, keep as composition)
```

# Estimate — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Methodology

Hours = file count × complexity tier multiplier

| Tier | Multiplier | Criteria |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **T1 Simple** | 0.25 hr/file | Data classes, enums, DTOs, thin wrappers |
| **T2 Standard** | 0.5 hr/file | Standard MonoBehaviours, UI screens, behaviours |
| **T3 Complex** | 1.0 hr/file | Networked systems, god-object splits, multi-interface classes |
| **T4 Critical** | 2.0 hr/file | God objects (>15 KB), framework foundations, cross-system coordinators |

---

- **Estimated total:** ~708 hours
- **Highest effort phases:** P10 UI (118 hr), P4 NPC (81.5 hr), P8 Gameplay (63 hr)

- **God object splits:** 6 major splits (Player, NPC, Customer, Dealer, ProductManager, LandVehicle)

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/schedule-1/GOAL.md ==== -->
# GOAL — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Project Identity

- **Project:** Schedule I — drug dealing simulation game
- **Engine:** Unity 2022.x (URP)
- **Networking:** FishNet (multiplayer, server-authoritative)
- **Phases:** 13 (P0–P12)
- **Total source files:** 1,752 (ScheduleOne namespace)

---

## Folder Structure

```
schedule-1/
├── _-Systems/                  ← tightly coupled system folders (3+ files)
│   ├── _SingletonSystem/
│   ├── _PersistenceSystem/
│   ├── _ItemFramework/
│   ├── _EntityFramework/
│   ├── _TileSystem/
│   ├── _TimeSystem/
│   ├── _MapSystem/
│   ├── _PlayerSystem/
│   ├── _NPCSystem/
│   │   ├── _NPCBehaviourSystem/
│   │   ├── _NPCScheduleSystem/
│   │   ├── _NPCRelationSystem/
│   │   └── _NPCCharacterClasses/
│   ├── _EconomySystem/
│   ├── _ProductSystem/
│   ├── _StationFramework/
│   ├── _CraftingStations/
│   ├── _PropertySystem/
│   ├── _BuildSystem/
│   ├── _ManagementSystem/
│   ├── _CombatSystem/
│   ├── _LawSystem/
│   ├── _VehicleSystem/
│   ├── _VisionSystem/
│   ├── _EquipSystem/
│   ├── _GrowingSystem/
│   ├── _StorageSystem/
│   ├── _EmployeeSystem/
│   ├── _UISystem/
│   ├── _DialogueSystem/
│   ├── _QuestSystem/
│   ├── _AudioSystem/
│   ├── _EffectsSystem/
│   └── ...
├── DataServices/               ← pure C# logic (no MonoBehaviour)
│   ├── ItemSlotService.cs
│   ├── ProductValueService.cs
│   ├── MixResolverService.cs
│   ├── ContractEvaluationService.cs
│   ├── DealerAssignmentService.cs
│   ├── ConditionEvaluationService.cs

    ├── EItemCategory.cs
    ├── EMapRegion.cs
    ├── ERank.cs
    ├── ECrimeType.cs
    └── ...
```

---

## Naming Conventions

### Classes
| Pattern | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| ScriptableObject data | `SO_` prefix | `SO_ProductDefinition` |
| Runtime wrapper | `W_` prefix | `W_ItemInstance` |
| Management config field | `Field_` prefix | `Field_Item`, `Field_Quality` |
| DataService | `___Service` suffix | `ProductValueService` |
| Interface | `I` prefix | `IItemSlotOwner` (existing convention) |
| Singleton manager | `___Manager` suffix | `TimeManager`, `ProductManager` |
| NPC behaviour | `___Behaviour` suffix | `IdleBehaviour`, `FleeBehaviour` |
| UI screen | `___Screen`/`___Canvas`/`___Interface` | `DeathScreen`, `VehicleCanvas` |
| Phone app | `App<T>` base class | Existing pattern |

### Files
| Pattern | Convention |
|---------|-----------|
| One class per file | File name = class name |
| System folder | `_SystemName/` with underscore prefix |
| Test file | `___Tests.cs` suffix |
| Data DTO | `___Data` suffix (e.g., `PlayerSaveData`) |
| Loader | `___Loader` suffix (e.g., `PlayerLoader`) |

### Fields & Properties
| Pattern | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Serialized fields | `[SerializeField]` with camelCase | `[SerializeField] private float maxHealth` |
| Public fields | PascalCase | `public float Health` |
| Private fields | `_` prefix camelCase | `private float _currentHealth` |
| Constants | UPPER_SNAKE | `MAX_CUSTOMERS = 8` |
| Static events | `on` prefix | `static Action onPlayerSpawned` |
| Instance events | `on` prefix | `Action onDie` |
| SyncVar | PascalCase property | `public float CurrentAddiction` |

---

## Class Responsibilities

### Singleton
- **Owns:** Global state for its domain
- **Does NOT:** Directly reference other singletons in constructors (use lazy access)
- **Access:** `ManagerName.Instance`
- **Variants:** `Singleton<T>` (scene), `PersistentSingleton<T>` (cross-scene), `NetworkSingleton<T>` (networked), `PlayerSingleton<T>` (per-player)

### NetworkBehaviour (FishNet)
- **Owns:** Networked state via SyncVars
- **RPCs:** `_Server` suffix for ServerRpc, `_Client` for ObserversRpc/TargetRpc
- **Pattern:** Request→Authorize→Broadcast (Server validates, then broadcasts to clients)
- **Ownership:** Use `PredictedOwner` for physics objects, `NetworkTransform` for position sync

### BuildableItem (Entity)
- **Owns:** GUID, placement data, save state
- **Lifecycle:** Spawn → Initialize → Place on Grid/Surface → Operate → Destroy
- **Variants:** GridItem (tile-based), SurfaceItem (free-placed)
- **Always:** IGUIDRegisterable + ISaveable

### StationItem (Crafting)
- **Owns:** Physical station item in world
- **Composition:** ItemModule system (Ingredient, Cookable, Pourable)
- **Pattern:** Create → ActivateModules → Interact → Destroy

### DataService (Pure Logic)
- **No MonoBehaviour** — pure C# class
- **No Unity dependencies** — only data types
- **Stateless:** All methods are static or take explicit parameters
- **Testable:** Can be unit tested without Unity runtime

---

## Decoupling Patterns

### 1. Event-Based Communication
```csharp
// Publisher (TimeManager)
public Action onHourPass;

// Subscriber (GrowContainer)
void OnEnable() => TimeManager.Instance.onHourPass += OnHourPass;
void OnDisable() => TimeManager.Instance.onHourPass -= OnHourPass;
```

### 2. Interface-Based Contracts
```csharp
// Station implements ITransitEntity
public interface ITransitEntity {
    List<ItemSlot> InputSlots { get; }
    List<ItemSlot> OutputSlots { get; }
    bool IsAcceptingItems { get; }
    void InsertItemIntoInput(ItemInstance item);
}

// Employee references ITransitEntity, not concrete station
ITransitEntity source = transitRoute.Source;
source.InsertItemIntoInput(item);
```

### 3. Singleton Access (Existing Pattern)
```csharp
// Access via Instance property
ProductManager.Instance.DiscoverProduct(definition);
TimeManager.Instance.onDayPass += OnDayPass;
```

### 4. ScriptableObject Data Pipeline
```csharp
// SO defines data (Inspector-editable)
SO_ProductDefinition → SO_StationRecipe → SO_MixerMap

// Runtime wrapper holds instance state
W_ItemInstance wraps SO_ItemDefinition with Quantity, Quality
```

### 5. Configuration Replication
```csharp
// EntityConfiguration holds ConfigField list
// ConfigurationReplicator syncs each field type via RPCs
// IConfigurable provides the bridge
```

---

## Lifecycle Rules

### Initialization Order
1. PersistentSingletons (Awake, DontDestroyOnLoad)
2. NetworkSingletons (after FishNet connection)
3. Regular Singletons (scene Awake)
4. ISaveable.InitializeSaveable() (after managers ready)
5. IBaseSaveable.Load() (ordered by LoadOrder property)
6. Player.Activate() (after load complete)

### Save Order
1. SaveManager.Save() called (manual or auto)
2. ISaveable list iterated (IBaseSaveable first, by LoadOrder)
3. Each: GetSaveString() → JSON → WriteData() → disk
4. 15 saves per frame (batched via SaveRequest queue)
5. SaveManager fires onSaveComplete

### Destruction Order
1. BuildableItem.Destroy() → Destroy_Server RPC → Destroy_Client
2. NPC.OnDestroy() → unregister from NPCManager
3. Player.OnDespawn() → remove from PlayerList
4. Singleton.OnDestroy() → set Destroyed flag

---

## C# Features in Use

| Feature | Usage |
|---------|-------|
| Generics | `Singleton<T>`, `NetworkSingleton<T>`, `App<T>`, `SerializableDictionary<K,V>` |
| Interfaces | 16+ cross-cutting interfaces |
| Abstract classes | BuildableItem, ItemInstance, ItemModule, Quest, Behaviour, GrowContainer, Plant |
| Events (Action) | 100+ event declarations |
| UnityEvent | 80+ Inspector-configurable events |
| Delegates | VehicleEvent, HearingEvent, ResponseCallback, BoolCheck |
| Enums | EDrugType, EProperty, EQuality, EMapRegion, ERank, ECrimeType, EItemCategory, etc. |
| ScriptableObjects | ItemDefinition hierarchy, StationRecipe, MixerMap, DialogueContainer |
| Coroutines | CoroutineService centralizes StartCoroutine |
| Nullable | Not widely used (legacy C# style) |
| LINQ | Limited usage (mostly manual iteration) |
| Attributes | [SerializeField], [RequireComponent], [DisallowMultipleComponent], [SyncVar] |

---

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testable (DataServices)
| Service | Test Focus |
|---------|-----------|
| ProductValueService | Property combination → value calculation |
| MixResolverService | Ingredient + product → output product |
| ContractEvaluationService | Quality, affinity, addiction → success chance |
| ItemSlotService | Insert, capacity, filtering, stacking |
| ConditionEvaluationService | Variable conditions → bool |
| NPCBehaviourPriorityService | Behaviour priority → active behaviour |

### Integration Testable (requires Unity)
| System | Test Focus |
|--------|-----------|
| Save/Load | Round-trip: save → load → verify state |
| ItemSlot | Network replication of slot changes |
| Station operations | Recipe input → timer → output |
| NPC schedule | Time advance → schedule event transition |
| Transit routing | Source slot → employee → destination slot |

### Manual Test Scenarios
| Scenario | Covers |
|----------|--------|
| Full drug production chain | Growing → Harvest → Station → Package → Store |
| Deal completion | Customer unlock → Contract → Deliver → Pay |
| Police encounter | Crime → Detection → Pursuit → Arrest/Escape |
| Building mode | Purchase property → Place stations → Configure → Automate |
| Multiplayer | Host → Client join → Shared world state |

---

## Project-Specific Notes

### Drug Types (EDrugType)
`Weed`, `Cocaine`, `Meth`, `Shroom` + 2 variants

### Property Effects (EProperty — 17 effects)
Modify product value and customer experience. Applied via mixing ingredients at stations.

### Map Regions (EMapRegion — 6 regions)
Rank-gated progression. Higher regions unlock at higher ranks.

### Quality System (EQuality)
Affects product value, customer satisfaction, and contract success.

### Tags/Layers (from source)
- NPC detection layers for vision/hearing
- Ground detection masks for player/NPC movement
- Vehicle layers for physics interactions

### Utility Extensions
- `CircularQueue<T>` — fixed-size circular buffer
- `RollingAverage` — windowed average calculation
- `SmoothedVelocityCalculator` — velocity smoothing
- `SerializableDictionary<K,V>` — Unity-serializable dictionary
- `PackingAlgorithm` — bin-packing for storage grids

### Key Constants (from source)
| Constant | Value | Location |
|----------|-------|----------|
| SAVE_SLOT_COUNT | 5 | SaveManager |
| SAVES_PER_FRAME | 15 | SaveManager |
| MAX_CUSTOMERS (per dealer) | 8 | Dealer |
| COCA_LEAF_REQUIRED | 20 | Cauldron |
| DEAL_COOLDOWN | varies | Customer |
| AFFINITY_MAX_EFFECT | varies | Customer |

# PhaseMap — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Name | Files | Weight % | Difficulty | Depends On |

| 3 | Player Core | 16 | 5% | ★★★ Hard | P0, P1, P2 |
| 4 | NPC Core | 176 | 14% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P3 |
| 5 | Economy & Product | 65 | 10% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P4 |

| 12 | Side Systems | 55 | 4% | ★☆☆ Easy | P0–P4 |

## Phase 0 — Core Infrastructure (68 files)

### File List

**DevUtilities/ (51 files)**
- `Singleton.cs`, `NetworkSingleton.cs`, `PlayerSingleton.cs`, `PersistentSingleton.cs`
- `GameManager.cs`, `Settings.cs`, `Registry.cs`, `CoroutineService.cs`
- `PackingAlgorithm.cs`, `StaggeredCallbackUtility.cs`, `BirdsEyeView.cs`
- `IconGenerator.cs`, `MetadataManager.cs`, all remaining DevUtilities/*.cs

**Networking/ (6 files)**
- `Lobby.cs`, `ReplicationQueue.cs`, `IStaggeredReplicator.cs`, remaining

**Persistence/ — core only (11 files)**
- `SaveManager.cs`, `LoadManager.cs`, `GenericSaveablesManager.cs`
- `ISaveable.cs`, `IBaseSaveable.cs`, `IGenericSaveable.cs`
- `SaveInfo.cs`, `SaveRequest.cs`, `LoadRequest.cs`, `SavePoint.cs`
- `SerializableDictionary.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Game boots, singletons initialize in order, save/load system ready, network lobby available. No visible gameplay yet — this is the skeleton.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
Singleton and persistence patterns are well-understood. Main challenge is NetworkSingleton + FishNet integration and ensuring save/load ordering is correct.

## Phase 1 — Data Frameworks (64 files)

### File List

**ItemFramework/ (42 files)**
- `ItemDefinition.cs`, `StorableItemDefinition.cs`, `QualityItemDefinition.cs`
- `ItemInstance.cs`, `ItemSlot.cs`, `ItemPickup.cs`, `NetworkedItemPickup.cs`
- `IItemSlotOwner.cs`, `CashInstance.cs`, `ItemFilter.cs`, `ItemSlotLock.cs`
- All remaining ItemFramework/*.cs

**EntityFramework/ (8 files)**
- `BuildableItem.cs`, `GridItem.cs`, `SurfaceItem.cs`
- `ToggleableItem.cs`, `ToggleableSurfaceItem.cs`
- `IProceduralTileContainer.cs`, remaining

**Tiles/ (14 files)**
- `Grid.cs`, `Tile.cs`, `Coordinate.cs`, `FootprintTile.cs`, `ProceduralTile.cs`
- `TileGrid.cs`, `Surface.cs`, remaining

### What It Looks Like When Running
Items can be defined (ScriptableObjects), instantiated, and placed in slots. Grid/tile system functional for placement math. Entities can be spawned on grids/surfaces. No visible rendering — data layer only.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
`ItemSlot` and `BuildableItem` are central abstractions that everything depends on. Getting the slot ownership, locking, filtering, and network replication right is critical.

## Phase 2 — World Foundation (60 files)

### File List

**GameTime/ (6 files)**
- `TimeManager.cs`, `AnalogueClock.cs`, `TimeUnityEvents.cs`, remaining

**Map/ (35 files)**
- `Map.cs`, `POI.cs`, `MapRegion.cs`, `DeliveryLocation.cs`

- `LevelManager.cs`, `FullRank.cs`, remaining

**Noise/ (4 files — depends on nothing above)**
- `NoiseEvent.cs`, `Listener.cs`, remaining

### What It Looks Like When Running
Time advances, day/night cycle works, map regions are defined and rank-gated. Variable system tracks progression. Noise system creates detectable events. World has spatial structure.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
TimeManager is moderately complex (sleep, time skip). Map regions and variable conditions are data-driven. Main challenge is TimeManager's role as the central event hub.

## Phase 3 — Player Core (16 files)

### File List

**PlayerScripts/ (16 files)**
- `Player.cs` (GOD OBJECT — 40.4 KB)
- `PlayerCamera.cs`, `PlayerMovement.cs`, `PlayerInventory.cs`
- `PlayerManager.cs`, `PlayerHealth.cs`, `PlayerEnergy.cs`
- `PlayerCrimeData.cs`, `PlayerVisibility.cs`, `CursorManager.cs`
- `ViewmodelAvatar.cs`, `ViewmodelSway.cs`, `HotbarSlot.cs`
- `LocalPlayerFootstepGenerator.cs`, remaining

### What It Looks Like When Running
First-person player moves, looks around, has inventory slots, health/energy bars. Can crouch, walk, run. Camera follows input. No interaction with world objects yet.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Player.cs is the largest god-object (40 KB). Splitting it while maintaining FishNet RPC contracts is the primary challenge. Player touches nearly every other system.

## Phase 4 — NPC Core (176 files)

### File List

**NPCs/ root (32 files)**
- `NPC.cs` (GOD OBJECT — 24.5 KB)
- `NPCMovement.cs`, `NPCAwareness.cs`, `NPCBehaviour.cs`
- `NPCResponses.cs`, `NPCActions.cs`, `NPCInventory.cs`
- `NPCHealth.cs`, `NPCManager.cs`, `NPCScheduleManager.cs`
- Remaining root-level NPC files

**NPCs/Behaviour/ (54 files)**
- `Behaviour.cs` (base), `NPCBehaviour.cs`
- 52 concrete behaviours: `IdleBehaviour`, `WanderBehaviour`, `FleeBehaviour`, `PanicBehaviour`, `ConsumeProductBehaviour`, `BodySearchBehaviour`, `DealerAttendDealBehaviour`, `BotanistBehaviour`, `ChemistBehaviour`, `PackagerBehaviour`, `CleanerBehaviour`, `BagTrashCanBehaviour`, `EmptyTrashGrabberBehaviour`, `PickUpTrashBehaviour`, `SewerGoblinRetrieveBehaviour`, etc.

**NPCs/CharacterClasses/ (78+ files)**
- Named NPCs: `Benji.cs`, `Dan.cs`, `Thomas.cs`, etc.

**NPCs/Schedules/ (12+ files)**
- `NPCEvent.cs` (base), `NPCEvent_Sit.cs`, `NPCEvent_Conversate.cs`, `NPCEvent_LocationBasedAction.cs`, `NPCEvent_StayInBuilding.cs`, `NPCEvent_GoToLocation.cs`, `NPCSignal_WaitForDelivery.cs`, `ScheduleGroup.cs`, remaining

**NPCs/Relation/ (6+ files)**
- `NPCRelationData.cs`, `NPCUnlockTracker.cs`, remaining

### What It Looks Like When Running
NPCs spawn, walk around, follow schedules, detect crimes via vision/hearing, react to player proximity. Have health, can be knocked out. Named characters have specific personalities. Relationship system tracks player standing.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
176 files with 54 behaviour classes, 78+ character classes, and the NPC.cs god-object. The behaviour priority stack and schedule system are complex. A* pathfinding integration adds technical depth.

---

## Phase 5 — Economy & Product (65 files)

### File List

**Economy/ (20 files)**
- `Customer.cs` (GOD OBJECT — 21.7 KB)
- `Dealer.cs` (GOD OBJECT — 16.3 KB)
- `Supplier.cs`, `Contract.cs`, `ContractReceipt.cs`
- Remaining Economy/*.cs

**Product/ (45 files)**
- `ProductManager.cs` (GOD OBJECT — 21.7 KB)
- `ProductDefinition.cs`, `WeedDefinition.cs`, `CocaineDefinition.cs`, `MethDefinition.cs`, `ShroomDefinition.cs`
- `PropertyItemDefinition.cs`, `EDrugType.cs`, `EProperty.cs`
- `MixerMap.cs`, `ProductAppearanceSettings.cs`
- `ProductIconManager.cs`, `PropertyUtility.cs`
- Remaining Product/*.cs

### What It Looks Like When Running
Customers can be unlocked, dealers recruited. Contracts offered and completed. Products have properties, quality, and pricing. Mix system creates new product variants. Deal flow: offer → accept → deliver → pay.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Three god-objects in this phase. The economy pipeline (Customer ↔ Dealer ↔ Supplier) with contracts, counter-offers, samples, addiction, and affinity is the most complex business logic in the game.

## Phase 6 — Crafting & Stations (73 files)

### File List

**StationFramework/ (18 files)**
- `StationItem.cs`, `ItemModule.cs`, `IngredientModule.cs`, `CookableModule.cs`, `PourableModule.cs`
- `StationRecipe.cs`, `Fillable.cs`, `BoilingFlask.cs`
- Remaining StationFramework/*.cs

**ObjectScripts/ (55 files)**
- `LabOven.cs`, `MixingStation.cs`, `Cauldron.cs`, `ChemistryStation.cs`
- `DryingRack.cs`, `BrickPress.cs`, `PackagingStation.cs`
- `MushroomSpawnStation.cs`, `Pot.cs`, `OldMixingStation.cs`
- `Recycler.cs`, `Sprinkler.cs`, `Jukebox.cs`, `VendingMachine.cs`, `Toilet.cs`
- Remaining ObjectScripts/*.cs (helpers, sub-objects, soil, etc.)

### What It Looks Like When Running
All crafting stations functional. Players mix products at MixingStation, cook at LabOven/ChemistryStation/Cauldron, dry at DryingRack, press at BrickPress, package at PackagingStation. Multi-step physical interactions (pouring, smashing, stirring). Recipes produce new items.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Each station (7 major ones) is a mini god-object (13-16 KB) implementing the same 4-interface pattern. High interaction complexity with physical objects, animations, and multi-step workflows.

## Phase 12 — Side Systems (55 files)

- `Avatar.cs`, `AvatarAnimation.cs`, `AvatarSettings.cs`
- `CharacterCreator.cs`, `CustomizationManager.cs`, `MugshotGenerator.cs`
- All remaining AvatarFramework/*.cs

### What It Looks Like When Running
Casino minigames (blackjack, roulette), graffiti spraying, skateboarding, cartel encounters, TV channels, temperature system, clothing customization. All side activities functional.

### Difficulty: ★☆☆ Easy
Mostly self-contained systems with minimal cross-dependencies. Casino games are isolated. Graffiti and skating are standalone mechanics.

## Cross-Phase Modification Table

| Phase | Modifies (Backward) | Modified By (Forward) |
|-------|---------------------|-----------------------|
| P0 | — | P1 (registers saveables), P3 (GameManager refs) |
| P1 | — | P5 (ProductDefinition extends ItemDefinition), P6 (stations use ItemSlot) |
| P2 | — | P4 (NPC schedules use TimeManager), P8 (Law uses TimeManager) |
| P3 | — | P4 (NPC references Player), P8 (combat targets Player) |
| P4 | — | P5 (Customer/Dealer extend NPC), P8 (PoliceOfficer extends NPC), P9 (Employee extends NPC) |
| P5 | P1 (product extends item defs) | P6 (stations use recipes/products), P9 (delivery uses contracts) |
| P6 | P1 (stations use ItemSlot/GridItem) | P7 (management configures stations), P9 (employees operate stations) |
| P7 | P6 (management configures stations) | P9 (transit routes for employees) |
| P8 | P3 (combat targets Player), P4 (Police extends NPC) | P10 (UI for law/vehicles) |
| P9 | P4 (Employee extends NPC), P6 (operates stations) | P10 (UI for storage/tools) |
| P10 | All previous phases | — |
| P11 | P3 (effects target Player) | — |
| P12 | P4 (Cartel uses NPC), P3 (Skating uses Player) | — |

### Critical Cross-Phase Interfaces
| Interface | Defined In | Implemented By |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| `ISaveable` | P0 | P1–P12 (everything saves) |
| `IItemSlotOwner` | P1 | P5 (Dealer), P6 (all stations), P9 (StorageEntity) |
| `ITransitEntity` | P7 | P6 (all stations), P9 (StorageEntity) |
| `IConfigurable` | P7 | P6 (all stations), P9 (Employee) |
| `ICombatTargetable` | P8 | P3 (Player), P4 (NPC) |
| `IGUIDRegisterable` | P0 | P1 (BuildableItem), P4 (NPC), P8 (LandVehicle) |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/schedule-1/StructureMap.md ==== -->
# StructureMap — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Naming Conventions

| Prefix/Suffix | Meaning | Example |
|---------------|---------|---------|
| `SO_` | ScriptableObject data container | `SO_ProductDefinition`, `SO_StationRecipe` |
| `Field_` | Management config field | `Field_Item`, `Field_NPC`, `Field_Quality` |
| `W_` | Runtime wrapper (SO → runtime) | `W_ItemInstance`, `W_ProductInstance` |
| `_System/` | Folder for 3+ tightly coupled files | `_NPCSystem/`, `_StationSystem/` |
| `DataService` | Pure C# logic extracted from MonoBehaviour | `ProductValueService`, `ContractEvaluationService` |
| `I_` | Interface | `I_Saveable`, `I_ItemSlotOwner` (already exist without prefix) |

---

## Phase 0 — Core Infrastructure

### `_SingletonSystem/` (4 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| Singleton.cs | Singleton.cs | Generic MonoBehaviour singleton |
| PersistentSingleton.cs | PersistentSingleton.cs | DontDestroyOnLoad singleton |
| NetworkSingleton.cs | NetworkSingleton.cs | FishNet networked singleton |
| PlayerSingleton.cs | PlayerSingleton.cs | Per-local-player singleton |

### `_PersistenceSystem/` (11 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| SaveManager.cs | SaveManager.cs | Save orchestration |
| LoadManager.cs | LoadManager.cs | Load orchestration |
| GenericSaveablesManager.cs | GenericSaveablesManager.cs | IGenericSaveable bridge |
| ISaveable, IBaseSaveable, IGenericSaveable | (interfaces) | Save contracts |
| SaveInfo, SaveRequest, LoadRequest | (DTOs) | Data carriers |
| SavePoint.cs | SavePoint.cs | Physical save interaction |
| SerializableDictionary.cs | SerializableDictionary.cs | Utility |

**DataService candidates:** None — these are already pure infrastructure.

### `_GameManagerSystem/` (3 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| GameManager.cs | GameManager.cs | Central game state |
| Settings.cs | Settings.cs | User settings |
| Registry.cs | Registry.cs | GUID registry |

### Standalone files (remaining DevUtilities)

- `PackingAlgorithm.cs` — utility
- `StaggeredCallbackUtility.cs` — utility
- `BirdsEyeView.cs`, `IconGenerator.cs`, `MetadataManager.cs` — dev tools

## Phase 6 — Crafting & Stations

### `_StationFramework/` (18 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| StationItem.cs | StationItem.cs | Physical station item |
| ItemModule.cs | ItemModule.cs | Module base |
| IngredientModule.cs | IngredientModule.cs | Ingredient handling |
| CookableModule.cs | CookableModule.cs | Cook behavior |
| PourableModule.cs | PourableModule.cs | Pour physics |
| StationRecipe.cs | SO_StationRecipe.cs | Recipe ScriptableObject |
| Fillable.cs | Fillable.cs | Liquid container |
| BoilingFlask.cs | BoilingFlask.cs | Chemistry flask |

### `_CraftingStations/` (55 files → shared pattern extraction)

All 7 major stations follow identical pattern: `GridItem : IUsable, IItemSlotOwner, ITransitEntity, IConfigurable`

**Shared extraction:**
| Extract To | Responsibility |
|------------|---------------|
| StationBase.cs | Common station logic: Open/Close, Hovered/Interacted, User tracking, IItemSlotOwner impl, ITransitEntity impl, IConfigurable impl |
| StationOperation.cs | Common operation lifecycle: Start → Timer → Complete |

Then each station only needs its unique logic:
| Station | Unique Logic |
|---------|-------------|
| LabOven | Hammer smashing, shard spawning, liquid pour |
| MixingStation | Product+Mixer→Output, discovery box |
| Cauldron | 4 ingredient + liquid + output, coca leaf dragging |
| ChemistryStation | 9-step enum workflow, explosion |
| DryingRack | Multiple concurrent drying operations |
| BrickPress | Brick pressing animation |
| PackagingStation | Package sealing, label |
| MushroomSpawnStation | Syringe interaction, grain bag |

**Interface candidates:**
- `IStation` — Already exists as quartet (IUsable + IItemSlotOwner + ITransitEntity + IConfigurable). Consider formalizing as a composite interface.

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/schedule-1/SystemPortabilityMap.md ==== -->
# SystemPortabilityMap — Schedule I (schedule-1)

## Classification

- **L0** — Fully portable: pure logic, no Unity/FishNet dependencies, can be extracted as-is
- **L1** — Mostly portable: light Unity dependency (ScriptableObject, MonoBehaviour), minimal refactor needed
- **L2** — Partially portable: significant Unity dependency (NetworkBehaviour, physics, rendering), needs adaptation layer
- **L3** — Engine-bound: deeply coupled to Unity/FishNet runtime (RPCs, SyncVars, physics, rendering), not portable

---

## Per-System Classification

### Phase 0 — Core Infrastructure
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Singleton\<T> | L3 | MonoBehaviour-based, Unity lifecycle |
| NetworkSingleton\<T> | L3 | FishNet NetworkBehaviour |
| PlayerSingleton\<T> | L3 | MonoBehaviour + FishNet client hooks |
| PersistentSingleton\<T> | L3 | DontDestroyOnLoad (Unity-specific) |
| SaveManager | L2 | File I/O is portable; Unity event hooks are not |
| LoadManager | L2 | Scene loading is Unity-specific; data loading is portable |
| GenericSaveablesManager | L2 | Interface-based but Unity singleton |
| ISaveable/IBaseSaveable | L0 | Pure interfaces |
| SaveInfo, SaveRequest, LoadRequest | L0 | Pure DTOs |
| SerializableDictionary | L1 | ISerializationCallbackReceiver is Unity-specific |
| GameManager | L3 | NetworkSingleton + FishNet RPCs |
| Settings | L2 | Unity PlayerPrefs-dependent |
| Registry | L2 | GUID tracking, light Unity dependency |
| CoroutineService | L3 | Unity coroutine system |
| PackingAlgorithm | L0 | Pure math/logic |

### Phase 1 — Data Frameworks
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| ItemDefinition (SO hierarchy) | L1 | ScriptableObject base, but data is pure |
| ItemInstance | L0 | Abstract C# class, no Unity dependency |
| ItemSlot | L1 | Action events (portable), but SlotOwner ties to Unity |
| IItemSlotOwner | L0 | Pure interface |
| BuildableItem | L3 | NetworkBehaviour + RPCs + GUID + physics |
| GridItem | L3 | Tile system + NetworkBehaviour |
| SurfaceItem | L3 | Surface placement + NetworkBehaviour |
| ToggleableItem/Surface | L3 | NetworkBehaviour + RPCs |
| Tile, Grid, Coordinate | L1 | Mostly math, light MonoBehaviour for scene presence |
| FootprintTile, ProceduralTile | L1 | Geometry calculations |

### Phase 2 — World Foundation
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| TimeManager | L2 | Time logic is portable; NetworkSingleton is not |
| Map, POI, MapRegion | L2 | Data model portable; Unity Transform/Singleton not |
| VariableDatabase | L2 | Data portable; NetworkSingleton wrapper not |
| BaseVariable, Condition | L0 | Pure data + logic |
| LevelManager | L2 | Rank math portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| Noise system | L1 | Event model portable; spatial queries use Unity physics |

### Phase 3 — Player Core
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Player | L3 | NetworkBehaviour, CharacterController, physics, FishNet RPCs |
| PlayerCamera | L3 | Unity Camera, Transform, InputSystem |
| PlayerMovement | L3 | CharacterController, Unity physics |
| PlayerInventory | L2 | ItemSlot logic portable; PlayerSingleton not |
| PlayerHealth/Energy | L1 | Value tracking portable; Unity events not |
| PlayerCrimeData | L0 | Pure data tracking |

### Phase 4 — NPC Core
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| NPC | L3 | NetworkBehaviour, A* pathfinding, physics |
| NPCMovement | L3 | A* Pathfinding Project, Unity NavMesh |
| NPCAwareness | L2 | Vision cone logic portable; Physics.Raycast not |
| NPCBehaviour (base) | L1 | Priority stack logic portable; MonoBehaviour lifecycle not |
| 54 Behaviour classes | L2 | Logic portable; Unity references (Transform, etc.) not |
| NPCScheduleManager | L1 | Schedule logic portable |
| NPCRelationData | L0 | Pure data + math |
| 78+ Character classes | L2 | Character data portable; Unity/FishNet hooks not |

### Phase 5 — Economy & Product
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Customer | L2 | Economy logic portable; NetworkBehaviour not |
| Dealer | L2 | Business logic portable; NPC base not |
| Supplier | L2 | Same pattern |
| Contract, ContractReceipt | L0 | Pure data |
| ProductManager | L2 | Product logic portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| ProductDefinition (SO) | L1 | ScriptableObject wrapper on portable data |
| MixerMap | L0 | Pure data mapping |
| EDrugType, EProperty, EQuality | L0 | Pure enums |
| **ProductValueService** | L0 | Pure calculation — fully portable |
| **ContractEvaluationService** | L0 | Pure calculation — fully portable |
| **MixResolverService** | L0 | Pure calculation — fully portable |

### Phase 6 — Crafting & Stations
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| StationRecipe (SO) | L1 | ScriptableObject, data is pure |
| StationItem | L2 | MonoBehaviour + module composition |
| ItemModule hierarchy | L1 | Logic portable; MonoBehaviour base not |
| Fillable, BoilingFlask | L3 | Liquid rendering, physics |
| LabOven, MixingStation, etc. | L3 | GridItem + 4 interfaces + physics + animation |

### Phase 7 — Property & Management
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Property, Business | L2 | Data model portable; Unity references not |
| BuildManager | L3 | Unity physics raycasting, grid placement |
| EntityConfiguration | L1 | ConfigField data portable; Unity events not |
| ConfigurationReplicator | L3 | Pure FishNet RPCs |
| TransitRoute | L1 | Routing logic portable; visual line not |
| ConfigField hierarchy | L1 | Data portable; UnityEvent not |
| **TransitRoutingService** | L0 | Pure validation logic — fully portable |

### Phase 8 — Gameplay Systems
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| CombatManager | L3 | NetworkSingleton + physics explosions |
| ICombatTargetable, IDamageable | L0 | Pure interfaces |
| LawManager, LawController | L1 | Crime data + settings mostly portable |
| CurfewManager | L2 | Time-based logic portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| PoliceOfficer | L3 | NPC base + A* + pursuit AI |
| LandVehicle | L3 | WheelColliders, Rigidbody, NetworkBehaviour |
| VehicleAgent | L3 | A* + Unity physics |

### Phase 9 — Tools & Operations
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Equippable hierarchy | L3 | Unity physics, animation, rendering |
| GrowContainer, Plant | L2 | Growth logic portable; GridItem base not |
| StorageEntity, StorageGrid | L2 | Grid logic portable; NetworkBehaviour not |
| Employee | L3 | NPC base + pathfinding |
| TrashManager | L2 | Tracking portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| **ItemSlotService** | L0 | Pure slot logic — fully portable |

### Phase 10 — UI & Communication
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| All UI (273 files) | L3 | Unity UI (Canvas, UGUI, TMP) |
| DialogueManager | L2 | Dialogue logic portable; Unity UI not |
| DialogueContainer | L1 | Data model portable |
| MessagingManager | L2 | Message logic portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| QuestManager | L2 | Quest logic portable; NetworkSingleton not |
| Quest, QuestEntry | L1 | Quest data/logic mostly portable |

### Phase 11 — Effects & Polish
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| AudioManager, SFXManager | L3 | Unity AudioSource |
| Drug effects (17) | L2 | Effect logic portable; post-processing not |
| FXManager | L3 | Unity particle systems |
| Lighting | L3 | Unity Light, URP rendering |
| VoiceOver | L3 | Unity AudioSource |

### Phase 12 — Side Systems
| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Casino (Blackjack, RTB, Slots) | L1 | Game logic portable; UI not |
| Graffiti | L3 | Texture painting, Unity rendering |
| Skating | L3 | Unity physics, animation |
| Cartel | L2 | Event logic portable; NPC base not |
| TV | L3 | Unity VideoPlayer |
| AvatarFramework | L3 | Unity SkinnedMeshRenderer, animation |

---

## Summary

| Level | Count | Percentage |
|-------|-------|-----------|
| **L0** — Fully portable | ~120 files | ~7% |
| **L1** — Mostly portable | ~280 files | ~16% |
| **L2** — Partially portable | ~450 files | ~26% |
| **L3** — Engine-bound | ~900 files | ~51% |

### Most Portable Subsystems (L0/L1)
1. All DataServices (9 services) — pure calculation logic
2. Contract/ContractReceipt — pure data
3. All enums (EDrugType, EProperty, EQuality, EMapRegion, etc.)
4. All interfaces (ISaveable, IItemSlotOwner, etc.)
5. ItemInstance hierarchy — abstract data wrappers
6. NPCRelationData — pure data + math
7. BaseVariable, Condition — pure logic
8. StationRecipe, MixerMap — pure data
9. Casino game logic — card/dice/slot math
10. LawSettings, crime type data — pure configuration

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/schedule-1/phase-All/DomainAnalysis_Batch2.md ==== -->
# Schedule One — Domain Analysis Batch 2 (16 Domains)

## 1. ROOT-LEVEL FILES (ScheduleOne/)

### Registry : PersistentSingleton\<Registry>
- **Purpose**: Central item registry; maps string IDs → `ItemDefinition` via hashed dictionary
- **Key fields**: `ItemRegistry`, `ItemDictionary`, `itemIDAliases`
- **Methods**: `GetItem(ID)`, `AddToRegistry()`, `GetAllItems()`

### GameInput : PersistentSingleton\<GameInput>
- **Enums**: `ButtonCode` (33 values: PrimaryClick..SkateboardMount), `InputDeviceType` (KeyboardMouse/Gamepad)
- **Key fields**: `PlayerInput`, `MotionAxis`, `CameraAxis`, `exitListeners`
- **Inner class**: `ExitListener` with priority-based exit delegation

### Console : Singleton\<Console>
- **Inner abstract**: `ConsoleCommand { CommandWord, CommandDescription, Execute(args) }`
- **~15 concrete commands**: SetTime, SpawnVehicle, AddItemToInventory, ClearInventory, ChangeCash, ChangeOnlineBalance, SetMoveSpeed, etc.

### AchievementManager : PersistentSingleton\<AchievementManager>
- **Enum**: `EAchievement` (13 values: COMPLETE_PROLOGUE, DEALER_RECRUITED, MASTER_CHEF, BUSINESSMAN, MAGNATE, etc.)

### CommandListScreen : MainMenuScreen
- Simple UI container for displaying console commands

### IGUIDRegisterable (interface)
- `Guid GUID { get; }`, `SetGUID(string/Guid)` — used throughout for persistence

### TransformData (struct)
- Position/Rotation/Scale container with Lerp and Apply methods

### TransformExtensions (static)
- Extension methods for Transform ↔ TransformData conversion

### XPAmounts (static constants)
- `PLAYER_COMPLETED_DEAL=20`, `DEALER_COMPLETED_DEAL=10`, `SUCCESSFUL_SAMPLE=50`, `HARVESTED_PLANT=5`, `ESCAPED_ARREST=20/40/60`

---

## 2. NPCs/ Domain

### Class Hierarchy
```
NetworkBehaviour
├── NPC : NetworkBehaviour, IGUIDRegisterable, ISaveable, ICombatTargetable, IDamageable, ISightable
│   ├── Employee (Employees/)
│   │   ├── Botanist : Employee, IConfigurable
│   │   ├── Chemist : Employee, IConfigurable
│   │   ├── Cleaner : Employee, IConfigurable
│   │   └── Packager : Employee, IConfigurable
│   ├── Dealer : NPC, IItemSlotOwner (Economy/)
│   ├── Supplier : NPC (Economy/)
│   ├── PoliceOfficer : NPC (Police/)
│   ├── Fixer : NPC (CharacterClasses/)
│   ├── SewerKing : NPC (CharacterClasses/)
│   └── 78+ named CharacterClasses (Albert→Wei) — most extend Supplier/Customer
├── NPCMovement : NetworkBehaviour
├── NPCBehaviour : NetworkBehaviour
├── NPCActions : NetworkBehaviour
├── NPCHealth : NetworkBehaviour
├── NPCInventory : NetworkBehaviour, IItemSlotOwner
└── NPCScheduleManager : MonoBehaviour
```

### NPC (core entity)
- **Implements**: `IGUIDRegisterable`, `ISaveable`, `ICombatTargetable`, `IDamageable`, `ISightable`
- **Key component refs**: Movement, DialogueHandler, Avatar, Awareness, Responses, Actions, Behaviour, Inventory, Health, VoiceOverEmitter, Visibility
- **Key fields**: FirstName, LastName, ID, MugshotSprite, Region(EMapRegion), Aggression(0-1), IsImportant
- **Relationship**: `NPCRelationData RelationData`, `NPCUnlockedVariable`
- **Messaging**: `MSGConversation`, `ConversationCategories`
- **Summoning**: `CanBeSummoned`
- **Vehicle**: `CurrentVehicle`, `onEnterVehicle/onExitVehicle`

### NPCMovement : NetworkBehaviour
- **Enums**: `EAgentType` (Humanoid/BigHumanoid/IgnoreCosts), `EStance`, `WalkResult` (Failed/Interrupted/Stopped/Partial/Success)
- **Uses**: NavMeshAgent, CapsuleCollider, NPCAnimation, SmoothedVelocityCalculator
- **Key settings**: WalkSpeed, RunSpeed, MoveSpeedMultiplier, SlipperyMode
- **Threshold constants**: VEHICLE_RUNOVER=10, LIGHT_FLINCH=50, HEAVY_FLINCH=100, RAGDOLL=150

### NPCAwareness : MonoBehaviour
- **Refs**: VisionCone, Listener(Noise), NPCResponses
- **Events**: onNoticedGeneralCrime, onNoticedPettyCrime, onNoticedDrugDealing, onNoticedPlayerViolatingCurfew, onGunshotHeard, onHitByCar
- **Cross-refs**: Vision, Noise, PlayerScripts, Vehicles

### NPCResponses : MonoBehaviour (base)
- **Constants**: ASSAULT_RELATIONSHIPCHANGE=-0.25, DEADLYASSAULT=-1, AIMED_AT=-0.5, PICKPOCKET=-0.25
- **Virtual methods**: GunshotHeard, ExplosionHeard, NoticedPettyCrime, NoticedVandalism, SawPickpocketing, NoticedDrugDeal, HitByCar, ImpactReceived, RespondToFirstNonLethalAttack, RespondToLethalAttack
- **Subclasses**: `NPCResponses_Civilian` (with EAttackResponse: None/Panic/Flee/CallPolice/Fight), `NPCResponses_Police`, `NPCResponses_Employee`, `NPCResponses_CartelGoon`

### NPCBehaviour : NetworkBehaviour
- **Default behaviours** (all type `Behaviour`): Cowering, Ragdoll, CallPolice, GenericDialogue, HeavyFlinch, FaceTarget, Dead, Unconscious, Summon, ConsumeProduct, Combat, Flee, Stationary, RequestProduct
- **behaviourStack**: priority-sorted list; highest priority active behaviour wins
- **Cross-refs**: NPCScheduleManager, Product (ConsumeProduct)

### Behaviour (base) : NetworkBehaviour
- **Key fields**: Name, Priority, BehaviourIndex, Enabled, Started, Active
- **Lifecycle**: Enable→Activate→BehaviourUpdate→Deactivate→Disable
- **Pause/Resume** support
- **54 concrete behaviours** in Behaviour/ folder (AddSoil, ApplyAdditive, BagTrash, BodySearch, BrickPress, Checkpoint, ConsumeProduct, Cowering, Dead, DealerAttendDeal, DisposeTrash, EmptyTrashGrabber, FaceTarget, FinishLabOven, Flee, FootPatrol, GenericDialogue, GrowContainer, HarvestMushroom, HarvestPot, HeavyFlinch, Idle, MistMushroom, MoveItem, NPCBehaviour, PackagingStation, Pursuit, Ragdoll, RequestProduct, Schedule, Sentry, SewerGoblinRetrieve, SmokeBreak, SowSeed, StartCauldron, StartChemistryStation, StartDryingRack, StartLabOven, StartMixingStation, Stationary, StopDryingRack, Unconscious, UseSpawnStation, VehiclePatrol, VehiclePursuit, WaterPot)

### NPCManager : NetworkSingleton\<NPCManager>, ISaveable
- **Static**: `NPCRegistry` (all NPCs), NPCWarpPoints
- **Methods**: GetNPC(id), GetNPCsInRegion(region)

### NPCScheduleManager : MonoBehaviour
- **Manages**: ActionList (NPCAction[]), ActiveAction, PendingActions
- **CurfewMode**: EnabledDuringCurfew/NoCurfew GameObjects
- **Tick-driven**: processes actions by time, supports enable/disable schedule

### Schedules/
- **NPCAction** (abstract, NetworkBehaviour): StartTime, priority, IsEvent/IsSignal flags; lifecycle: Started→JumpTo→End→Interrupt
  - **NPCEvent** : NPCAction — has Duration/EndTime
    - Subclasses: NPCEvent_CartelGoonExit, NPCEvent_Conversate, NPCEvent_LocationBasedAction, NPCEvent_LocationDialogue, NPCEvent_Sit, NPCEvent_StayInBuilding
  - **NPCSignal** : NPCAction (implied)
    - Subclasses: NPCSignal_DriveToCarPark, NPCSignal_UseATM, NPCSignal_UseVendingMachine, NPCSignal_WaitForDelivery, NPCSignal_WalkToLocation

### Relation/
- **NPCRelationData**: RelationDelta (0-5, default 2), Unlocked, UnlockType (Recommendation/DirectApproach), Connections (mutual NPCs)
- **ERelationshipCategory**: Hostile/Unfriendly/Neutral/Friendly/Loyal

### NPCHealth : NetworkBehaviour
- SyncVar Health, MaxHealth, Invincible, CanRevive
- Events: onDie, onKnockedOut, onRevive, onTakeDamage
- REVIVE_DAYS = 3

### NPCInventory : NetworkBehaviour, IItemSlotOwner
- SlotCount, CanBePickpocketed, PickpocketDifficultyMultiplier
- RandomCash, RandomItems support
- ClearInventoryEachNight

### Character Classes (78+ files)
- Most named characters (Albert, Beth, Brad, etc.) extend either `Supplier` or `Customer` (via NPC chain)
- Special characters: `Fixer : NPC` (signing fee logic), `SewerKing : NPC` (SewerOffice ref), `SewerGoblin`, `SchizoGoblin`

---

## 3. UI/ Domain

### Key Singletons
| Class | Base | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| HUD | Singleton\<HUD> | Main HUD: crosshair, cash display, hotbar, management slot |
| StorageMenu | Singleton\<StorageMenu> | Opens/closes storage entity display |
| HandoverScreen | Singleton\<HandoverScreen> | Contract/Sample/Offer handover with quality scoring |
| Phone | PlayerSingleton\<Phone> | Phone UI controller with horizontal mode |
| AppsCanvas | PlayerSingleton\<AppsCanvas> | Phone app container |
| HomeScreen | PlayerSingleton\<HomeScreen> | Phone home screen (time, app icons) |
| ShopManager | NetworkSingleton\<ShopManager>, ISaveable | Shop stock replication |

### HandoverScreen Modes
- `EMode`: Contract, Sample, Offer
- `EHandoverOutcome`: Cancelled, Finalize
- Shows: customer preferences, vehicle slots, success/error labels

### Phone/ subdirectory
- **Apps**: AppsCanvas, HomeScreen, JournalApp, PhoneShopInterface, CallInterface, CallNotification, CounterofferInterface, CustomerSelector, ProductManagerApp/
- **Sub-apps**: ContactsApp/, Delivery/, Map/, Messages/

### Shop/ subdirectory
- **ShopInterface** : ISaveable — full in-game shop UI with ShopName, ShopCode, Listings, Cart, DeliveryBays, DeliveryVehicle
- **ShopManager** : NetworkSingleton — manages stock sync across network
- **Supporting**: Cart, CartEntry, CategoryButton, ListingUI, ShopListing, ShopAmountSelector, ShopColorPicker, ClothingShopInterface

### Other notable UI classes
- ArrestScreen, ArrestNoticeScreen, BodySearchScreen, DeathScreen, PassOutScreen, HospitalBillScreen
- DialogueCanvas, DialogueChoiceEntry, DailySummary, DealCompletionPopup
- NewCustomerPopup, NewMixScreen, NotificationsManager, PauseMenu
- QuestHUDUI, QuestEntryHUDUI, RankUpCanvas, RegionUnlockedCanvas
- LaunderingInterface, PawnShopInterface, NPCSummonMenu, PickpocketScreen

## 4. Product/ Domain

### Class Hierarchy
```
StorableItemDefinition (ItemFramework)
└── PropertyItemDefinition : StorableItemDefinition
    └── ProductDefinition : PropertyItemDefinition, ISaveable
        ├── WeedDefinition : ProductDefinition
        ├── CocaineDefinition : ProductDefinition
        ├── MethDefinition : ProductDefinition
        └── ShroomDefinition : ProductDefinition
```

### ProductDefinition (ScriptableObject)
- **Key fields**: DrugTypes (List\<DrugTypeContainer>), LawIntensityChange, BasePrice, MarketValue, BaseAddictiveness
- **References**: FunctionalProduct, ConsumeAnimation, ValidPackaging (ordered lowest→highest)
- **Recipes**: List\<StationRecipe> (from StationFramework)
- **Persistence**: ISaveable (saves product configs)

### Drug-specific definitions
- **WeedDefinition**: MainMat, SecondaryMat, LeafMat, StemMat; WeedAppearanceSettings
- **CocaineDefinition**: RockMaterial; CocaineAppearanceSettings
- **MethDefinition**: CrystalMaterial, TintColor; MethAppearanceSettings
- **ShroomDefinition**: ShroomMaterial, BulkMaterial, EyeballMaterial; ShroomAppearanceSettings

### Key Enums
- **EDrugType**: Marijuana, Methamphetamine, Cocaine, MDMA, Shrooms, Heroin
- **EProperty**: Mild, Potent, Overwhelming, Sedating, Calming, Refreshing, Stimulating, Cerebral, Physical, Psychedelic, Dissociative, Hallucinogenic, Focused, Uplifting, Euphoric, Addictive, HighlyAddictive

### ProductManager : NetworkSingleton\<ProductManager>, ISaveable
- **Static lists**: DiscoveredProducts, ListedProducts, FavouritedProducts
- **Default products**: DefaultWeed, DefaultCocaine, DefaultMeth, DefaultShroom
- **Mix maps**: WeedMixMap, MethMixMap, CokeMixMap, ShroomMixMap
- **Mix operations**: CurrentMixOperation, onMixCompleted, onNewProductCreated
- **Constants**: MIN_PRICE=1, MAX_PRICE=999, CONTRACT_RECEIPT_MAX_COUNT=50
- **Cross-refs**: Economy (ContractReceipt), Effects, StationFramework

### Other Product files
- ProductItemInstance, ProductEntry, ProductList, ProductQuantities
- Product_Equippable, Product_Stored (partial classes)
- PropertyContainer, PropertyMethods, PropertyUtility
- MixChute, MixRecipeData, NewMixOperation, NewMixDiscoveryBox
- DrugTypeContainer, DrugTypeMethods
- MultiTypeVisualsSetter, ProductVisualsSetter, *VisualsSetter per drug type
- Packaging/ subfolder

---

## 5. Map/ Domain

### Map : Singleton\<Map>
- **FINAL_REGION** = Uptown
- **Key refs**: Regions (MapRegionData[]), PoliceStation, MedicalCentre, TreeBounds
- **Methods**: GetRegionData, GetUnlockedRegions, GetRegionFromPosition
- **Unlock trigger**: OnRankUp (from Levelling.FullRank)

### EMapRegion (enum)
- Northtown=0, Westville=1, Downtown=2, Docks=3, Suburbia=4, Uptown=5

### MapRegionData
- Region, Name, UnlockedByDefault, RankRequirement(FullRank), StartingNPCs, RegionSprite
- RegionDeliveryLocations, AdjacentRegions, RegionBounds(Zone)
- **Methods**: GetRandomUnscheduledDeliveryLocation, SetUnlocked, GetAdjacentRegions

### POI : MonoBehaviour
- **Enum**: TextShowMode (Off/Always/OnHover)
- World-to-UI position mapping, main text label, button+event trigger

### DarkMarket : NetworkSingleton\<DarkMarket>
- AccessZone, MainDoor, Oscar(NPC), UnlockRank
- IsOpen, Unlocked (time-based open/close)

### Other Map files
- AccessZone, TimedAccessZone, DarkMarketAccessZone, AutoshopAccessZone, NPCPresenceAccessZone
- NPCEnterableBuilding, NPCPoI, Gate, ManorGate, Ladder
- PoliceStation, MedicalCentre, Dealership, ParkingLot/ParkingSpot
- SewerManager, SewerMushrooms, SewerMushroomLocation
- MapPositionUtility, Infrastructure/

## 6. Quests/ Domain

### Class Hierarchy
```
MonoBehaviour
└── Quest : MonoBehaviour, IGUIDRegisterable, ISaveable
    ├── Contract : Quest
    └── DeaddropQuest : Quest (implied)

MonoBehaviour
└── QuestEntry : MonoBehaviour

NetworkBehaviour
└── QuestManager : NetworkSingleton<QuestManager>, ISaveable
```

### Quest
- **Static lists**: Quests, ActiveQuests, HoveredQuest
- **Fields**: title, Subtitle, Description, StaticGUID, TrackOnBegin, AutoCompleteOnAllEntriesComplete, CompletionXP
- **Entries**: List\<QuestEntry>, AutoStartFirstEntry
- **Expiry**: Expires, Expiry(GameDateTime), ExpiryVisibility
- **State**: EQuestState (Inactive/Active/Completed/Failed/Expired/Cancelled)
- **Tracking**: IsTracked, hudUI

### QuestEntry
- **Fields**: EntryTitle, state, AutoComplete, AutoCompleteConditions(Conditions), CompleteParentQuest
- **PoI**: AutoCreatePoI, PoILocation

### Contract : Quest
- **Extra fields**: ProductList, DeliveryLocation, DeliveryWindow(QuestWindowConfig), Customer, Dealer, Payment, PickupScheduleIndex, AcceptTime
- **Static**: Contracts list
- **Constants**: DefaultExpiryTime=2880

### QuestManager : NetworkSingleton\<QuestManager>, ISaveable
- **Enum**: EQuestAction (Begin/Success/Fail/Expire/Cancel)
- **Methods**: ContractAccepted, CreateContract_Networked/Local, SendQuestAction, SendQuestState
- **Refs**: DefaultQuests, ContractContainer, QuestCompleteSound

### 22 concrete Quest_ subclasses
- Quest_GettingStarted, Quest_SinkOrSwim, Quest_DownToBusiness, Quest_OnTheGrind, Quest_MovingUp, Quest_ExpandingOperations, Quest_GearingUp, Quest_SecuringSupplies, Quest_NeedingTheGreen, Quest_TheDeepEnd, Quest_UnfavourableAgreements, Quest_WelcomeToHylandPoint, Quest_WeNeedToCook, Quest_Warehouse, Quest_Employees, Quest_Botanists, Quest_Chemists, Quest_Packagers, Quest_Cleaners, Quest_Connections, Quest_CleanCash, Quest_DealForCartel, Quest_DefeatCartel, Quest_GrowShrooms

### Supporting
- StateMachine : MonoBehaviour — static OnStateChange action
- SystemTrigger, SystemTriggerObject — quest triggers
- ContractInfo, QuestWindowConfig, QuestStateSetter, EExpiryVisibility

## 7. Economy/ Domain

### Customer : NetworkBehaviour, ISaveable
- **Attached to**: NPC (RequireComponent)
- **Static lists**: LockedCustomers, UnlockedCustomers
- **Constants**: AFFINITY_MAX_EFFECT=0.3, PROPERTY_MAX_EFFECT=0.4, QUALITY_MAX_EFFECT=0.3, DEAL_COOLDOWN=600, OFFER_EXPIRY_TIME=600, ADDICTION_DRAIN_PER_DAY=0.0625
- **Inner types**: ScheduleGroupPair, CustomerPreference (DrugType + MinimumQuality), ESampleFeedback
- **Key refs**: CustomerData (ScriptableObject), DefaultDeliveryLocation, DealSignal
- **Cross-refs**: Product, Quests.Contract, Dialogue, Messaging, Handover UI

### CustomerData : ScriptableObject
- DefaultAffinityData, PreferredProperties (List\<Effect>)
- MinWeeklySpend, MaxWeeklySpend, MinOrdersPerWeek, MaxOrdersPerWeek
- OrderTime, PreferredOrderDay(EDay)
- Standards (ECustomerStandard), DependenceMultiplier
- DirectApproach settings: MinMutualRelationRequirement, CallPoliceChance

### Dealer : NPC, IItemSlotOwner
- **Constants**: MAX_CUSTOMERS=8, OVERFLOW_SLOT_COUNT=10
- **Key fields**: Home(NPCEnterableBuilding), DealerType(EDealerType), HomeName, SigningFee, Cut
- **Features**: SellInsufficientQualityItems, SellExcessQualityItems
- **SyncVar**: Cash
- **Static**: AllPlayerDealers, onDealerRecruited

### Supplier : NPC
- **Enum**: ESupplierStatus (Idle/PreppingDeadDrop/Meeting)
- **Constants**: MEETUP_RELATIONSHIP_REQUIREMENT=4, DELIVERY_RELATIONSHIP_REQUIREMENT=5, DEADDROP_ITEM_LIMIT=10
- **Key fields**: MinOrderLimit, MaxOrderLimit, OnlineShopItems, Shop(ShopInterface), Stash(SupplierStash)
- **SyncVars**: debt, deadDropPreparing

### Key Enums
- **ECustomerStandard**: VeryLow/Low/Moderate/High/VeryHigh
- **EDealerType**: PlayerDealer/CartelDealer
- **EContractParty**: Player/PlayerDealer/Cartel
- **EDealWindow** (not read but exists)

### Other Economy classes
- DeliveryLocation: LocationName, CustomerStandPoint, TeleportPoint, ScheduledContracts
- DeadDrop: DeadDropName, Region, Storage(WorldStorageEntity)
- ContractReceipt: ReceiptId, CompletedBy(EContractParty), CustomerId, CompletionTime, Items, AmountPaid
- CustomerAffinityData, CustomerSatisfaction, ProductTypeAffinity
- SupplierLocation, SupplierLocationConfiguration, SupplierStash
- DealGenerationEvent, DealWindowInfo, StandardsMethod
