<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/twFactory/GOAL.md ==== -->
# GOAL — Tower Factory (twFactory)

## Project Identity

- **Project Name:** Tower Factory
- **Engine:** Unity 6 (URP, Input System, Addressables, Localization, Cinemachine)
- **Genre:** Tower Defense + Factory Automation hybrid
- **Phases:** 12 (Core → Grid → Character → Tower → Effects → Enemy → Factory → Buildings → Flow → Player → UI → Audio)

---

## Target Architecture Rules

### 1. Folder Structure

```
twFactory-reimpl/
├── _-StatsSystem/           # Stat, StatModifier, StatsComponent
├── _-StorageSystem/         # Storage<T>, Storage_ResourceData
├── _-GameplayObjectBase/    # GameplayObject, GameplayObjectData, PlacementComponent
├── _-GridSystem/            # Grid, GridCell, Tile, PathTile, Path
├── _-MapGeneratorSystem/    # MapGenerator, PathGenerator, GroundGenerator, EnvironmentGenerator
├── _-FogOfWarSystem/        # FogOfWarController, FogOfWarArea
├── _-CharacterSystem/       # Character, Controller, MovementComponent, AnimationComponent
├── _-CombatSystem/          # CombatComponent, TowerCombatComponent, EnemyCombatComponent
├── _-AbilitySystem/         # Ability, ActiveAbility, AutoAttack, AbilityManager, AbilityQueue
├── _-FSMSystem/             # FSMComponent, FSMState, FSMCondition, FSMTask, FSMTransition
├── _-PerceptionSystem/      # PerceptionAI, PerceptionSense variants
├── _-GameplayEffectBase/    # GameplayEffect, GameplayEffectData, GameplayEffectsComponent
├── _-TowerSystem/           # Tower, TowerController, TowerAutoAttack, PlayerTower
├── _-AttackVariants/        # AutoAttack_directDamage, _projectile, _overTime, etc.
├── _-TargetProviderSystem/  # TowerTargetProvider + 10 concrete strategies
├── _-ProjectileSystem/      # Projectile, ProjectileMovement variants
├── _-AreaEffectSystem/      # AreaEffect, AreaEffectModule variants
├── _-GameplayEffects/       # All GE_ pairs (30+ files, flat folder)
├── _-GemSystem/             # GemsComponent, GemData, GemUI
├── _-EnemySystem/           # Enemy, EnemyData, EnemyMovement, EnemyController
├── _-EnemyAbilitySystem/    # EnemyAbility variants, EnemyAICondition variants
├── _-SpawnerSystem/         # Spawner, WaveSpawner, LTSpawner, CycleSpawners, SpawnersManager
├── _-ExtractorSystem/       # Extractor, AreaExtractor, audio, UI
├── _-ProcessorSystem/       # Processor, EssenceFactory, Recipe
├── _-ConveyorSystem/        # ConveyorBelt variants, ConveyorBeltGroup, ConveyorBeltSystem
├── _-IdleSystem/            # IdleDetector variants, IdleManager
├── _-BeaconSystem/          # Beacon hierarchy (Circle, Cone, Squared, Beacon_01-04)
├── _-SnowfallSystem/        # SnowfallController, SnowfallBeacon, VFX
├── _-GameManagerSystem/     # GameManager, LTGameManager variants, LTFunctionLibrary
├── _-SaveSystem/            # SaveSystem, SaveProfile, SessionChecker
├── _-ProgressionSystem/     # PlayerUpgrade, PlayerUpgradesManager, LevelsProgressionManager
├── _-InputModeSystem/       # InputMode, Standard/Edit/BuyModeInputMode
├── _-CameraSystem/          # Isometric, Top, SpringArm, Cinemachine, CameraShake
├── _-HotbarSystem/          # HotbarController, HotbarAction variants
├── _-HUDSystem/             # HUD, InGameHUD, LTHUD, MainMenuHUD
├── _-StoreSystem/           # StoreUI, StoreElementUI, info panels
├── _-SelectableUISystem/    # SelectableUI + 15 concrete variants
├── _-TooltipSystem/         # TooltipManager, TooltipUI/Component variants
├── _-MenuSystem/            # MainMenu, Pause, Settings, Profile, Credits
├── _-AudioSystem/           # AudioSystem, MusicManager, AmbienceManager
├── DataServices/            # Pure C# logic services (no MonoBehaviour)
├── Interfaces/              # Cross-system interfaces
├── SO/                      # ScriptableObject definitions
└── Enums/                   # All shared enums
```

### 2. Naming Conventions

| Prefix | Type | Example |
|--------|------|---------|
| `SO_` | ScriptableObject asset | `SO_GameplayObjectData`, `SO_LevelData` |
| `Field_` | Serialized data field | `Field_EStats`, `Field_ResourceType` |
| `W_` | Runtime wrapper | `W_GameplayEffect`, `W_SpawnerConfig` |
| `DS_` | DataService (pure C#) | `DS_ConveyorTransport`, `DS_Economy` |
| `I` | Interface | `ISelectable`, `ISavable`, `IDamageable` |
| `_-` | System folder | `_-TowerSystem/`, `_-ConveyorSystem/` |
| `GE_` | Gameplay effect | `GE_Splash`, `GE_Dot`, `GE_ProjectileBounce` |
| `TTP_` | Tower target provider | `TTP_Nearest`, `TTP_HighestHealth` |

### 3. Class Responsibilities

**MonoBehaviour classes:**
- Unity lifecycle only (Awake, Start, Update, OnDestroy)
- Delegate logic to DataServices
- Wire events in OnEnable, unwire in OnDisable
- No business logic in Update — call DataService methods

**DataService classes:**
- Pure C# (no MonoBehaviour inheritance)
- Stateless where possible, or explicitly manage state
- Testable without Unity runtime
- Injected via constructor or singleton registry

**ScriptableObject assets:**
- Configuration data only
- No runtime state
- Shared across instances via asset references

### 4. Decoupling Patterns

**Current Problem → Target Solution:**

| Current Pattern | Count | Target Pattern |
|-----------------|-------|---------------|
| `LTFunctionLibrary.GetXxx()` (service locator) | ~100+ calls | Keep as central accessor — acceptable pattern for this scale |
| `AudioSystem.Instance` (direct singleton) | ~50+ calls | Route through `LTFunctionLibrary.GetAudioSystem()` for consistency |
| `FogOfWarController.instance` (direct singleton) | ~10 calls | Route through `LTFunctionLibrary` |
| `ConveyorBeltSystem.instance` (direct singleton) | ~5 calls | Route through `LTFunctionLibrary` |
| `SaveSystem.instance` (direct singleton) | ~8 calls | Keep direct — persistence is cross-cutting |
| God objects >400 lines | 10 files | Split into DataService + thin MonoBehaviour |

**Acceptable static access (keep as-is):**
- `LTFunctionLibrary` service locator — centralized facade is the intended pattern
- `SaveSystem.instance` — persistence is legitimately cross-cutting
- `MatchInfo.instance` — match config is globally relevant

**Must decouple:**
- God objects: `LTPlayerController` (844 lines), `EnvironmentGenerator` (786 lines), `CycleSpawners` (737 lines), `SaveSystem` (692 lines), `LTGameManager` (666 lines)
- Direct cross-domain references that bypass LTFunctionLibrary

### 5. Lifecycle Rules

**Game Session Lifecycle (critical path):**

```
1. MainMenu → SelectLevel → MatchInfo configured
2. GameScene loaded
3. LTGameManager.Awake() → register singletons
4. MapGenerator.Generate() → PathGenerator → GroundGenerator → EnvironmentGenerator
5. PlayerData initialized (buildings, inventory, upgrades)
6. LTPlayerController.Start() → StandardInputMode active
7. LTGameManager.onGameStarted fires
8. CyclesManager begins (cycle 1, day mode)
9. [GAMEPLAY LOOP]:
   a. Day phase: build towers, place extractors/processors, connect conveyors
   b. CyclesManager.onCycleChanged → night phase
   c. SpawnersManager activates spawners
   d. Enemies spawn, walk paths, attack PlayerTower
   e. Towers detect, target, fire, apply GE effects
   f. Factory loop: Source → Extractor → Belt → Processor → Inventory
   g. Cycle ends → day phase → repeat
10. Victory: all waves cleared → VictoryAnimation → BossReward
11. Game Over: PlayerTower HP ≤ 0 → GameOverAnimation
12. SaveSystem persists progression
```

**Building Placement Lifecycle:**
```
1. Player selects building from Hotbar/Store
2. BuyModeInputMode activates preview ghost
3. Grid cell validation (occupancy, adjacency, resources)
4. Conveyor auto-connection check (if belt type)
5. Cost deducted via LTGameManager.Pay()
6. GameplayObject instantiated
7. PlacementComponent.Place() → onPlace fires
8. Subscribers react: Tower enables combat, Extractor finds sources, etc.
```

**Tower Combat Lifecycle:**
```
1. TowerController.PerceptionAI detects enemies in range
2. TowerTargetProvider selects best target (e.g., nearest, highest HP)
3. AbilityManager.UseAutoAttack() queues attack
4. AutoAttack variant executes (projectile, direct, over-time)
5. TowerCombatComponent.DoDamageToEnemy() applies damage
6. onDamageEnemy fires → GE_ subscribers trigger (splash, bounce, DoT, etc.)
7. Enemy.CombatComponent.DoDamage() reduces HP
8. If HP ≤ 0: onDie fires → SpawnersManager tracks, resources awarded
```

### 6. C# Feature Guidelines

**Use:**
- `readonly` for fields set once
- `sealed` on classes not intended for inheritance
- Pattern matching for type checks
- String interpolation over concatenation
- `using` declarations for IDisposable
- `Dictionary<K,V>` over `Hashtable`
- `List<T>` over `ArrayList`
- Generic `Storage<T>` pattern (already in source)

**Avoid:**
- `public` fields on MonoBehaviours (use `[SerializeField] private`)
- Allocation in Update/FixedUpdate (pool objects)
- String concatenation in hot paths (use StringBuilder)
- `BinaryFormatter` for new code (security risk — replace with JSON or custom binary)
- `foreach` on non-generic collections in tight loops

**Existing patterns to preserve:**
- `Storage<T>` generic container — clean and reusable
- `GameplayEffect` + `GameplayEffectData` paired pattern — all 25+ effects follow it
- `StatsComponent` + `StatModifier` event-driven stats — well-designed
- `PlacementComponent` event bus — `onPlace`/`onUnplace` is the building lifecycle hook
- `TowerTargetProvider` strategy pattern — clean extension point
- `ProjectileMovement` strategy pattern — clean extension point
- `ConveyorBeltGroup` chain management — complex but well-isolated

### 7. Project-Specific Elements

**Tower Types (13 types × 3 tiers):**
Ballista, Catapult, Bombard, Rockets, SawThrower, LightRay, TeslaCoil, Shockwave, Spikes, AcornCannon, ResinBarrel, SporeCannon, FlyingCannon

**Enemy Types (~27):**
Skeleton, Mushroom, SoldierMushroom, Boss_mushroom, Bat, Bee, BeeWarrior, BattleBee, Boss_bee, Fishman, CrabMesh, HermitCrab, MantaRay, Salamander, BigPenguin, FlyingPenguin, Boss_penguin, BigSnowman, Boss_snowman, Yeti, BlackKnight, LizardWarrior, RatAssasin, Specter, Werewolf, Boss_naga, Boss_orc

**Resource Types:**
Wood, Stone, Iron, Gold (and processed variants via recipes)

**Building Types:**
- Extractors: Wood (T1/T2), Stone (T1/T2)
- Processors: Sawmill, Stonemason, KilnFurnace, Forge, Workshop, EssenceFactory
- Conveyors: Straight, Curve, Splitter, Combiner, Crossing, Underground (all T1/T2)
- Special: 4 Beacon variants, PerkBeacon, SnowfallBeacon, CrystalAltar, CrystalFinder, Obelisk

**Gameplay Effects (25+ types):**
GE_StatModifier, GE_Slow, GE_SlowResistance, GE_Dot, GE_Splash, GE_SplashDamage, GE_ProjectileBounce, GE_ProjectileSplit, GE_DirectDamageChain, GE_GameplayEffectApplier, GE_SplashGameplayEffectApplier, GE_StacksConsumer, GE_StacksConsumerDamage, GE_DisableTower, GE_LifeBasedDamage, GE_Heal, GE_HealLightTower, GE_GiveResources, GE_GiveEffectToBuilding, GE_GiveEffectToEnemy, GE_AddGemSlot, GE_UnlockBuilding, GE_AreaEffectSpawner, GE_Descriptor

**Input Modes:**
Standard (select/inspect/sell), Edit (rearrange), Buy (place new buildings)

**Match Modes:**
Campaign, Endless

### 8. Testing Strategy

**Unit Testable (DataServices):**
- `DS_StatCalculator` — modifier stacking, operation order (Add → Multiply → Override)
- `DS_ConveyorTransport` — belt chain traversal, resource distribution, loop detection
- `DS_SpawnerBalance` — LpS auto-balancing formula correctness
- `DS_Economy` — cost evaluation, refund calculation, resource sufficiency
- `DS_RecipeEvaluator` — input matching, output quantity
- `DS_TowerExperience` — XP thresholds, stat scaling per level
- `DS_PlacementValidator` — grid occupancy, adjacency rules
- `DS_Serializer` — save/load round-trip (replace BinaryFormatter with testable format)
- `DS_EffectStacking` — stack count, duration refresh, max cap
- `DS_PathLayout` — procedural path generation determinism

**Integration Testable (MonoBehaviour):**
- Tower place → detect enemy → fire → damage → GE trigger chain
- Extractor → Conveyor belt chain → Processor → Inventory
- Spawner cycle → wave composition → enemy spawn → path follow → die
- Building placement → cost deduction → grid occupation → PlacementComponent events
- Save → load → verify all ISavable state restored

**Manual Test Scenarios:**
- Build complete factory: extract wood → belt → sawmill → belt → inventory
- Tower upgrade path: place ballista → level up → tier 2 → tier 3
- Full combat cycle: day build → night defend → boss fight → victory
- Snowfall mechanic: controller intensity increases → beacon placement counters
- Fog of war: beacon placement → area reveal → enemy tower discovery
- Trader interaction: wait for trader → buy item → resources deducted

### 9. Key Metrics

| Metric | Current | Target |
|--------|---------|--------|
| God objects (>400 lines) | 10 | 0 (split into DataService + thin MonoBehaviour) |
| Direct singleton access (bypass LTFunctionLibrary) | ~70+ | ≤10 (AudioSystem, SaveSystem acceptable) |
| Public fields on MonoBehaviour | Hundreds | All `[SerializeField] private` |
| Untested business logic | ~100% | DataServices: 80%+ unit test coverage |
| `BinaryFormatter` usage | 1 (SaveSystem) | 0 (replace with JSON or custom binary) |
| FindObjectOfType calls | 0 | 0 (maintain clean pattern) |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/twFactory/PhaseMap.md ==== -->
# PhaseMap — Tower Factory (twFactory)

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Name | Files | Weight % | Difficulty | Depends On |
|-------|------|-------|----------|------------|------------|
| 0 | Core Framework & Data | ~75 | 8% | ★★☆ Medium | — |
| 1 | Grid & Map Generation | ~25 | 6% | ★★★ Hard | P0 |
| 2 | Character & Combat Framework | ~35 | 8% | ★★★ Hard | P0 |
| 3 | Tower System | ~45 | 10% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P2 |
| 4 | Gameplay Effects | ~35 | 9% | ★★☆ Medium | P0, P2, P3 |
| 5 | Enemy & Spawner System | ~45 | 10% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P2, P4 |
| 6 | Factory & Economy | ~40 | 10% | ★★★ Hard | P0, P1 |
| 7 | Special Buildings & Map Objects | ~35 | 8% | ★★☆ Medium | P0, P1, P4, P6 |
| 8 | Game Manager & Flow | ~30 | 9% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P7 |
| 9 | Player & Input | ~30 | 8% | ★★★ Hard | P0–P8 |
| 10 | UI Layer | ~130 | 10% | ★★☆ Medium | P0–P9 |
| 11 | Audio & Polish | ~30 | 4% | ★☆☆ Easy | P0 |
| **Total** | | **~554** | **100%** | | |

---

## Phase 0 — Core Framework & Data (~75 files)

### File List

**Enums & Constants (~20 files)**
- `EStats.cs`, `DamageType.cs`, `EDamageMultiplier.cs`, `EOrientation.cs`
- `EGameplayObjectType.cs`, `EComparison.cs`, `ECycleMode.cs`, `EInputMode.cs`
- `EMatchMode.cs`, `ENotificationType.cs`, `EValueMode.cs`, `EGameDifficulty.cs`
- `ModifierOperation.cs`, `ETargetProviderType.cs`, `EProjectileMovementType.cs`
- `ECombatState.cs`, `EControllerState.cs`, `ETowerUpgradeState.cs`, `EResourceType.cs`

**Stats System (~8 files)**
- `Stat.cs`, `StatConfig.cs`, `StatModifier.cs`, `StatsComponent.cs`
- `StatConfigVariable.cs`, `StatConfigObject.cs`, `ISampleableData.cs`

**Data Types & Containers (~12 files)**
- `Cost.cs`, `ResourceData.cs`, `FDamageData.cs`, `FDamageReport.cs`
- `RandomizableFloat.cs`, `RandomizableInt.cs`, `WeightedRandomSelector.cs`
- `Timer.cs`, `StorableData.cs`, `Storable.cs`
- `GameplayObjectData.cs`, `GameplayObjectDataGroup.cs`

**Base Classes (~8 files)**
- `GameplayObject.cs`, `Storage.cs` (generic), `Storage_ResourceData.cs`
- `PlacementComponent.cs`, `ISelectable.cs`, `ISavable.cs`

**Utility Libraries (~12 files)**
- `FunctionLibrary.cs` (586 lines — GOD OBJECT), `ExtensionMethods.cs`, `RectExtensions.cs`
- `LTAssetsReferences.cs`, `SavableAttribute.cs`, `SaveComponent.cs`
- Serialization surrogates (`Vector2`, `Vector3`, `Quaternion`)

**Serialization (~5 files)**
- Serialization surrogate selectors, custom formatters

**Third-Party Wrappers (~10 files)**
- `DOTween/` references (external DLL — no source), `HighlightPlus/` (~25 files — read-only third-party)

### What It Looks Like When Running
Stats can be created, modified, and queried. Resources and costs can be evaluated. Storage containers hold typed data. Gameplay objects have placement and serialization. Utility functions available. No visible game yet — pure data backbone.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
Well-defined data types and enums. `FunctionLibrary` (586 lines) is the only god object here. Stats system (`StatsComponent` + `StatModifier`) is event-heavy but cleanly designed with `onStatChanged` delegates.

---

## Phase 1 — Grid & Map Generation (~25 files)

### File List

**Grid Core (~6 files)**
- `Grid.cs`, `GridCell.cs`
- `LightTower/Tile.cs`, `LightTower/PathTile.cs`, `LightTower/Path.cs`
- `PathChunk.cs`

**Map Generation (~8 files)**
- `MapGenerator.cs`, `PathGenerator.cs`, `GroundGenerator.cs`, `GroundChunk.cs`
- `EnvironmentGenerator.cs` (786 lines — GOD OBJECT)
- `LevelData.cs`, `LevelController.cs`, `LTLevelController.cs`

**Fog of War (~4 files)**
- `FogOfWarController.cs`, `FogOfWarArea.cs`

**Placement (~3 files)**
- `PlacementComponent.cs` (if not in P0), `PlacementDirectionArrow.cs`

**Map Objects base (~4 files)**
- `MapObject.cs`, `Source.cs`, `Resource.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Procedurally generated map with tiles, paths, and terrain. Grid visible with placeable cells. Fog of war obscures unexplored areas. Sources (wood, stone) scattered on the map. No buildings or enemies yet — empty world with resources.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
`EnvironmentGenerator` (786 lines) spawns all map objects and is the most complex god object in this phase. `PathGenerator` creates procedural paths that enemies will follow. Grid system is the spatial foundation everything builds on.

---

## Phase 2 — Character & Combat Framework (~35 files)

### File List

**Character Base (~6 files)**
- `Character.cs`, `Controller.cs`, `MovementComponent.cs`
- `AnimationComponent.cs`, `TeamComponent.cs`

**Combat Core (~5 files)**
- `CombatComponent.cs`, `TowerCombatComponent.cs`, `EnemyCombatComponent.cs`

**Ability Framework (~12 files)**
- `Ability.cs` (abstract), `ActiveAbility.cs` (abstract), `PassiveAbility.cs`
- `AutoAttack.cs` (abstract), `AbilityManager.cs`, `AbilityQueue.cs`
- `QueuedAbility.cs`, `FActiveAbilityInputData.cs`
- `AbilityUnlockCondition.cs`, `AUC_LocalAbilityUsed.cs`

**FSM (~5 files)**
- `FSMComponent.cs`, `FSMState.cs`, `FSMCondition.cs`, `FSMTask.cs`, `FSMTransition.cs`

**Perception (~5 files)**
- `PerceptionAI.cs`, `PerceptionSense.cs`
- `PerceptionSense_Sight.cs`, `PerceptionSense_Hearing.cs`, `PerceptionSense_Proximity.cs`
- `PerceptionNoise.cs`

**Gameplay Effect Base (~2 files)**
- `GameplayEffect.cs` (abstract), `GameplayEffectData.cs` (abstract, ScriptableObject)
- `GameplayEffectsComponent.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Characters can be instantiated with stats, take/deal damage, die. FSM drives state transitions. Perception system detects targets within range. Ability queue processes attack commands. No specific tower or enemy behavior yet — generic character skeleton.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
The ability pipeline (`AbilityManager` → `AbilityQueue` → `ActiveAbility` → `AutoAttack`) is the critical chain. `TowerCombatComponent` extends `CombatComponent` with tower-specific damage events (`onDamageEnemy`) that 10+ GE_ classes subscribe to. Getting this event wiring right is essential.

---

## Phase 3 — Tower System (~45 files)

### File List

**Tower Core (~6 files)**
- `Tower.cs` (409 lines — complex), `TowerController.cs`
- `TowerAnimationComponent.cs`, `TowerAutoAttack.cs` (abstract)
- `PlayerTower.cs`, `PlayerTowerHealthBar.cs`

**Auto-Attack Variants (~8 files)**
- `AutoAttack_collisionCheck.cs`, `AutoAttack_directDamage.cs`
- `AutoAttack_overTime.cs`, `AutoAttack_overTime_laser.cs`
- `AutoAttack_projectile.cs`, `AutoAttack_projectile_splash.cs`

**Target Providers (~10 files)**
- `TowerTargetProvider.cs` (abstract)
- `TTP_Nearest.cs`, `TTP_HighestHealth.cs`, `TTP_LowestHealth.cs`
- `TTP_FastestSpeed.cs`, `TTP_SlowestSpeed.cs`
- `TTP_NearestToPosition.cs`, `TTP_FarthestFromTower.cs`
- `TTP_FirstTarget.cs`, `TTP_LastTarget.cs`, `TTP_Random.cs`

**Projectile System (~10 files)**
- `Projectile.cs`, `ProjectileMovement.cs` (abstract)
- `ProjectileMovement_Forward.cs`, `ProjectileMovement_Homing.cs`
- `ProjectileMovement_Parable.cs`, `ProjectileMovement_ToTarget.cs`
- `DirectDamageParticles.cs`

**Area Effects (~6 files)**
- `AreaEffect.cs`, `AreaEffectModule.cs` (abstract)
- `AreaEffectModule_damage.cs`, `AreaEffectModule_effect.cs`
- `AreaEffectModule_slow.cs`

**Tower Data (~5 files)**
- `TowerData.cs` (ScriptableObject), `TowerUpgradeData.cs`
- `TowerLevelUpData.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Towers placed on grid, detect enemies via perception, select targets using priority strategies (nearest, highest HP, etc.), fire projectiles or deal direct damage. Projectiles fly using various movement patterns. Area effects splash damage. Towers level up and can be upgraded to new tiers.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Tower system is the primary gameplay mechanic. `TowerController` orchestrates perception → targeting → ability use. 10 target provider variants and 6 attack variants make this phase the most variant-heavy. `Tower.cs` (409 lines) manages experience, obelisk buffs, and upgrade chains.

---

## Phase 4 — Gameplay Effects (~35 files)

### File List

**Stat/Modifier Effects (~6 files)**
- `GE_StatModifier.cs`, `GE_StatModifierData.cs`
- `GE_Slow.cs`, `GE_SlowData.cs`
- `GE_SlowResistance.cs`, `GE_SlowResistanceData.cs`

**Damage Effects (~8 files)**
- `GE_Dot.cs`, `GE_DotData.cs`
- `GE_Splash.cs`, `GE_SplashData.cs`
- `GE_SplashDamage.cs`, `GE_SplashDamageData.cs`
- `GE_LifeBasedDamage.cs`, `GE_LifeBasedDamageData.cs`

**Chain/Bounce/Split (~6 files)**
- `GE_ProjectileBounce.cs`, `GE_ProjectileBounceData.cs`
- `GE_ProjectileSplit.cs`, `GE_ProjectileSplitData.cs`
- `GE_DirectDamageChain.cs`, `GE_DirectDamageChainData.cs`

**Applicator Effects (~6 files)**
- `GE_GameplayEffectApplier.cs`, `GE_GameplayEffectApplierData.cs`
- `GE_SplashGameplayEffectApplier.cs`, `GE_SplashGameplayEffectApplierData.cs`
- `GE_StacksConsumer.cs`, `GE_StacksConsumerData.cs`
- `GE_StacksConsumerDamage.cs`, `GE_StacksConsumerDamageData.cs`

**Utility Effects (~6 files)**
- `GE_Heal.cs`, `GE_HealData.cs`, `GE_HealLightTower.cs`, `GE_Heal_VFX.cs`
- `GE_GiveResources.cs`, `GE_GiveResourcesData.cs`
- `GE_DisableTower.cs`, `GE_DisableTowerData.cs`

**Building/Tower Effects (~4 files)**
- `GE_GiveEffectToBuilding.cs`, `GE_GiveEffectToBuildingData.cs`
- `GE_GiveEffectToEnemy.cs`, `GE_GiveEffectToEnemyData.cs`
- `GE_AddGemSlot.cs`, `GE_AddGemSlotData.cs`
- `GE_UnlockBuilding.cs`, `GE_UnlockBuildingData.cs`

**Misc (~3 files)**
- `GE_AreaEffectSpawner.cs`, `GE_AreaEffectSpawnerData.cs`
- `GE_Descriptor.cs`, `GE_DescriptorData.cs`
- `ResourceActivatedGEData.cs`

**Gems (~3 files)**
- `GemsComponent.cs`, `GemData.cs`, `GemUI.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Towers apply gameplay effects when hitting enemies: slows, DoTs, splash damage, projectile bounces/splits, chain lightning. Stacking effects accumulate and can be consumed for burst damage. Gems add GE slots to towers. Buildings can receive/give effects. Visual feedback for heals, damage chains, splashes.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
Each GE_ pair (runtime + data) follows a consistent abstract pattern: `GameplayEffectData` (ScriptableObject config) → `GameplayEffect` (runtime behavior). Most are 50-150 lines. The challenge is the event subscription wiring — many GE_ classes subscribe to `TowerCombatComponent.onDamageEnemy`.

---

## Phase 5 — Enemy & Spawner System (~45 files)

### File List

**Enemy Core (~8 files)**
- `Enemy.cs`, `EnemyData.cs` (ScriptableObject)
- `LightTower/EnemyMovement.cs`, `LightTower/EnemyController.cs`
- `EnemyAnimationComponent.cs`
- `EnemyHealthBar.cs`, `EnemyHitVFX.cs`, `EnemySpawnVFX.cs`, `EnemySpawnVFX_mushroom.cs`
- `EnemyGameplayEffectUI.cs`

**Enemy Abilities (~6 files)**
- `EnemyAbility.cs` (abstract, extends AutoAttack)
- `EnemyAbility_applyGE.cs`, `EnemyAbility_spawnEnemies.cs`, `EnemyAbility_targeting.cs`
- `EnemyAICondition.cs` (abstract), `EnemyAICondition_health.cs`, `EnemyAICondition_proximity.cs`

**Spawner Framework (~10 files)**
- `Spawner.cs`, `SpawnerConfig.cs` (ScriptableObject), `SpawnerConfigAutoConfig.cs`
- `FSpawnerConfigAutoConfigData.cs`
- `SpawnerPosition.cs` (abstract), `SpawnerPosition_Circle.cs`, `SpawnerPosition_Line.cs`
- `SpawnerPosition_PathTile.cs`, `SpawnerPosition_FixedPositions.cs`
- `BaseSpawnerData.cs`, `GridBasedSpawnerData.cs`, `CircleBasedSpawnerData.cs`, `PathBasedSpawnerData.cs`

**Wave/Round System (~8 files)**
- `WaveSpawner.cs`, `WaveSpawnerConfig.cs`, `WaveEnemyData.cs`
- `WaveSpawnerConfig_OLD.cs`, `WaveSpawner_OLD.cs`
- `LTSpawner.cs` (round-based spawner)
- `LevelSpawners.cs` (ScriptableObject)
- `TimeSpawner.cs`

**Cycle Management (~5 files)**
- `CyclesManager.cs`, `CycleSpawners.cs` (737 lines — GOD OBJECT, ScriptableObject)
- `SpawnersManager.cs` (singleton)

### What It Looks Like When Running
Enemies spawn in waves/rounds according to cycle configuration. Enemies walk along generated paths toward the player base. Bosses spawn at cycle intervals. Enemies have abilities (apply GE, spawn minions). Health bars visible, hit VFX on damage. Spawner auto-balances difficulty via LpS (Loot per Second) system.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
`CycleSpawners` (737 lines) is a god object that auto-generates spawner configurations with weighted random selection and difficulty balancing. `SpawnersManager` coordinates multiple spawner types (round, wave, time). The LpS auto-balancing formula is the most complex algorithm in the spawner system.

---

## Phase 6 — Factory & Economy (~40 files)

### File List

**Sources & Resources (~4 files)**
- `Source.cs`, `Resource.cs` (if not in P1)
- `ResourceData.cs` (if not in P0)

**Extractors (~6 files)**
- `Extractor.cs`, `AreaExtractor.cs`
- `ExtractorAudioComponent.cs`, `ExtractorResourceUI.cs`
- `ReachableSourceIndicator.cs`

**Processors (~6 files)**
- `Processor.cs`, `EssenceFactory.cs`
- `ProcessorAudioComponent.cs`, `ProcessorResourceUI.cs`
- `Recipe.cs`, `RecipeElementUI.cs`
- `Producer.cs`

**Conveyor Belt System (~14 files)**
- `ConveyorBelt.cs`, `ConveyorBelt_straight.cs`, `ConveyorBelt_curve.cs`, `ConveyorBelt_storage.cs`
- `ConveyorBeltCombiner.cs`, `ConveyorBeltSplitter.cs`
- `ConveyorBeltCrossing.cs`, `ConveyorBeltUnderground.cs`
- `ConveyorBeltGroup.cs` (409 lines — complex)
- `ConveyorBeltSystem.cs` (singleton)

**Idle Detection (~6 files)**
- `IdleDetector.cs`, `IdleDetector_extractor.cs`, `IdleDetector_processor.cs`
- `IdleManager.cs`, `IdleDetectorButton.cs`, `IdleDetectorUI.cs`

**Player Economy (~4 files)**
- `PlayerData.cs` (447 lines — GOD OBJECT)
- Storage_ResourceData (player inventory)

### What It Looks Like When Running
Complete factory automation loop: extractors mine resources from sources, conveyor belts transport resources through the map (straight, curved, split, merge, underground), processors convert inputs to outputs, storage holds surplus. Idle detection highlights underperforming buildings. Player inventory displays current resources. This is the "Factory" half of "Tower Factory."

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
`ConveyorBeltGroup` (409 lines) manages resource transport along connected belt chains with loop detection, merge/split logic, and storage integration. `PlayerData` (447 lines) is a god object tracking all player state. The conveyor belt auto-connection logic in `BuyModeInputMode` (P9) adds cross-phase complexity.

---

## Phase 7 — Special Buildings & Map Objects (~35 files)

### File List

**Beacons (~10 files)**
- `Beacon.cs` (abstract), `CircleBeacon.cs`, `ConeBeacon.cs`, `SquaredBeacon.cs`
- `Beacon_01.cs`, `Beacon_02.cs`, `Beacon_03.cs`, `Beacon_04.cs`

**Crystal/Altar (~4 files)**
- `CrystalAltar.cs`, `CrystalFinder.cs`

**Obelisk (~2 files)**
- `Obelisk.cs`

**Perk Beacon (~4 files)**
- `PerkBeacon.cs`, `PerkBeaconPerk_UI.cs`, `PerkBeaconResourceUI.cs`

**Snowfall (~6 files)**
- `SnowfallBeacon.cs`, `SnowfallBeaconResourceUI.cs`
- `SnowfallController.cs`, `SnowfallControllerUI.cs`
- `SnowfallEffectUI.cs`, `SnowfallVFX.cs`

**Chests (~4 files)**
- `Chest.cs`, `ChestLootTableData.cs`
- `GemsChest.cs`, `GemsChestLootTableData.cs`
- `GoldenCoinsChest.cs`

**Trader (~3 files)**
- `Trader.cs`, `TraderElement.cs`, `TraderElementUI.cs`

**Other (~2 files)**
- `EnemyTower.cs`, `LightBomb.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Map populated with special buildings: fog-clearing beacons (circle, cone, square shapes), crystal altars that grant resources when activated, obelisks that buff adjacent towers, perk beacons that consume resources for upgrades, snowfall beacons that counter weather debuffs, traders that sell items on a cycle timer, chests with gem/gold loot, enemy towers that must be destroyed. Light bombs are throwable damage items.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
Most special buildings follow a consistent pattern: `GameplayObject` or `MapObject` subclass with `PlacementComponent` events. `SnowfallController` is moderately complex (tracks intensity per cycle, applies tiered debuffs). Beacons are the only deep hierarchy (4 shape variants × 4 named variants).

---

## Phase 8 — Game Manager & Flow (~30 files)

### File List

**Game Manager (~6 files)**
- `GameManager.cs`, `LTGameManager.cs` (666 lines — GOD OBJECT, abstract)
- `LTGameManager_Campaign.cs`, `LTGameManager_Endless.cs`
- `LTFunctionLibrary.cs` (500 lines — service locator)

**Match & Level (~5 files)**
- `MatchInfo.cs` (singleton), `MatchSettings.cs` (ScriptableObject)
- `LevelData.cs`, `LTLevelController.cs`

**Time & Cycles (~4 files)**
- `TimeManager.cs`, `CyclesManager.cs`
- `DayNightCycle.cs` (405 lines)

**Victory/Defeat (~4 files)**
- `VictoryAnimation.cs`, `BossVictoryAnimation.cs`, `GameOverAnimation.cs`

**Progression (~5 files)**
- `PlayerUpgrade.cs`, `PlayerUpgradesManager.cs` (singleton)
- `LevelsProgressionManager.cs` (singleton)
- `BossReward.cs`, `BossReward_PlayerUpgrade.cs`

**Persistence (~6 files)**
- `SaveSystem.cs` (692 lines — GOD OBJECT)
- `SaveProfile.cs`, `SessionChecker.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Full game loop: main menu → level select → match start → day/night cycles → enemy waves → victory/defeat. Campaign progresses through levels, endless mode runs indefinitely. Day/night transitions change lighting/fog. Time manager controls game speed. Save/load preserves progression. Boss kills grant player upgrades. `LTFunctionLibrary` serves as the central service locator.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Three god objects: `LTGameManager` (666 lines — game state, economy, tower upgrades), `SaveSystem` (692 lines — reflection-based serialization with BinaryFormatter), `LTFunctionLibrary` (500 lines — facade for ~20 singletons). The save system's use of `BinaryFormatter` + `[Savable]` attribute + `ISavable` interface is the most complex serialization pattern.

---

## Phase 9 — Player & Input (~30 files)

### File List

**Player Controller (~4 files)**
- `PlayerController.cs`, `LTPlayerController.cs` (844 lines — GOD OBJECT)
- `CursorController.cs`

**Input Modes (~4 files)**
- `InputMode.cs` (abstract), `StandardInputMode.cs` (524 lines — GOD OBJECT)
- `EditModeInputMode.cs`, `BuyModeInputMode.cs` (428 lines — GOD OBJECT)

**Camera (~8 files)**
- `IsometricCamera.cs`, `TopCamera.cs`, `SpringArmCamera.cs`
- `PlayerCamera.cs`, `LTCamera.cs`
- `CineMachineArea.cs`, `CineMachineCamera.cs`, `CineMachineSpringArm.cs`
- `CameraShake.cs`, `CameraShakeAnimationEvent.cs`, `ShakeCameraController.cs`

**Hotbar (~5 files)**
- `HotbarController.cs`, `HotbarAction.cs` (abstract)
- `HotbarAction_building.cs`, `HotbarActionUI.cs`, `HotbarUI.cs`

**Wind (~1 file)**
- `WindController.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Player controls isometric camera with zoom/pan/rotate. Three input modes: Standard (select, inspect, sell), Edit (rearrange buildings), Buy (place new buildings with preview + rotation + conveyor auto-connect). Hotbar at bottom with building shortcuts. Cursor changes based on mode/context. Camera shake on explosions.

### Difficulty: ★★★ Hard
Three god objects: `LTPlayerController` (844 lines — largest file in the project), `StandardInputMode` (524 lines), `BuyModeInputMode` (428 lines). The input mode system is the most user-facing code. `BuyModeInputMode` handles conveyor belt auto-connection which cross-references P6 (Factory) heavily.

---

- `PauseUI.cs`, `PauseUI_tutorial.cs`
- `InGameSettingsUI.cs`, `ControlsSettingsUI.cs`, `AudioVideoSettingsUI.cs`, `GameplaySettingsUI.cs`
- `CreditsMenuUI.cs`, `ProfileMenuUI.cs`, `ProfileUI.cs`, `EmptyProfileUI.cs`

**Upgrades UI (~6 files)**
- `UpgradesMenuUI.cs`, `UpgradesMenuTowerInfo.cs`, `TowerUpgradeElementUI.cs`
- `InGameUpgradesMenuUI.cs`, `PlayerUpgradeUI.cs`

**End Game (~4 files)**
- `EndGameUI.cs`, `EndGameInfoUI.cs`, `EndGameAnimationUI.cs`, `GameOverUI.cs`

**Game Info UI (~10 files)**
- `CycleTimeUI.cs` + sub-components
- `PlayerDataUI.cs`, `PlayerMoneyUI.cs`
- `InventoryUI.cs`, `InventoryElementUI.cs`
- `ThermometerUI.cs`, `TimeUI.cs`, `TimeControlButton.cs`

**Utility UI (~16 files)**
- `ModalWindow.cs`, `DefaultModalWindow.cs`, `InputModalWindow.cs`
- `FillBar.cs`, `StatBar.cs`, `DemandFillbar.cs`, `SteppedSlider.cs`
- `HealthBar.cs`, `WorldHealthBar.cs`, `WorldObjectUI.cs`
- `UIList.cs`, `UIListElement.cs`
- `DamageFXUI.cs`, `DirectionalMover.cs`, `DirectionalMoverUI.cs`
- `NotificationsManager.cs`, `NotificationUI.cs`
- `ButtonAnimation.cs`, `SpoilerButton.cs`, `DisableScrollViewDrag.cs`
- `GameVersionLabelUI.cs`, `LoadingIconUI.cs`, `FPSDisplay.cs`
- `ContextWindowUI.cs`

**Tutorial (~6 files)**
- `TutorialInfoUI.cs`, `TutorialQuestData.cs` + variants

**Save UI (~2 files)**
- `SaveProfileLabelUI.cs`

### What It Looks Like When Running
Full UI: HUD with resource display, cycle timer, hotbar. Store panel for buying towers/extractors/processors. Click any object for detailed selection panel (stats, upgrade options, gem slots). Tooltips on hover. Pause menu, settings, profile management. End-game screen with stats. Tutorial quest system guides new players. Notifications for important events.

### Difficulty: ★★☆ Medium
High file count but most UI files follow a consistent pattern: subscribe to data events in OnEnable, update display in callbacks, unsubscribe in OnDisable. `SelectableUI_tower` (482 lines) is the only god object — it shows tower stats, upgrade UI, gem management, and target provider selection in one panel.

---

## Phase 11 — Audio & Polish (~30 files)

### File List

**Audio System (~8 files)**
- `AudioSystem.cs` (456 lines — GOD OBJECT), `AudioData.cs`
- `LTAudioSystem.cs`
- `MusicManager.cs`, `MainMenuMusicManager.cs`, `AmbienceManager.cs`

**Settings (~4 files)**
- `SettingsController.cs` (singleton), `SettingsElement_keyRebind.cs`, `KeyRebindingGroup.cs`

**Stats & Steam (~4 files)**
- `GameStatsManager.cs` (singleton)
- `SteamAchievements.cs` (or equivalent)

**VFX Helpers (~6 files)**
- Various animation event handlers, particle helpers

**Third-Party (read-only, ~8 files)**
- `HighlightPlus/` highlight effects
- `LayerLab/` theme
- `TMPro/` example

### What It Looks Like When Running
Background music transitions between day/night cycles. SFX for all interactions (tower attacks, enemy hits, building placement, UI clicks). Ambient environmental sounds. Steam achievements track player milestones. Settings persist across sessions. Audio source pooling prevents allocation spikes.

### Difficulty: ★☆☆ Easy
`AudioSystem` (456 lines) is the only complex file — it manages source pooling, mixer control, and 2D/3D playback. The rest are event subscribers and wrappers. Steam integration is mostly achievement triggers. Third-party code is read-only.

---

## Cross-Phase Modification Table

| Phase | Modifies | What Changes | Why |
|-------|----------|------------|-----|
| P3 (Tower) | P2 (Combat) | `TowerCombatComponent` adds `onDamageEnemy` event | Tower-specific damage hooks for GE_ subscriptions |
| P4 (Effects) | P3 (Tower) | GE_ classes subscribe to `TowerCombatComponent.onDamageEnemy` | Effects trigger on tower damage events |
| P4 (Effects) | P2 (Combat) | `GameplayEffectsComponent` modifies `StatsComponent` | Effects apply stat modifiers |
| P5 (Enemy) | P2 (Character) | `Enemy` extends `Character`, `EnemyController` extends `Controller` | Enemy specialization |
| P6 (Factory) | P0 (Core) | `Storage_ResourceData` extends `Storage<T>` | Typed resource storage |
| P6 (Factory) | P1 (Grid) | Conveyor belts reference `GridCell` for placement | Belt snapping to grid |
| P7 (Special) | P4 (Effects) | Beacons/obelisks apply `GameplayEffect` instances | Special building GE application |
| P7 (Special) | P6 (Factory) | `PerkBeacon`, `SnowfallBeacon` consume resources | Resource-activated buildings |
| P8 (Flow) | P3–P7 | `LTGameManager` coordinates buy/sell/upgrade across all systems | Economy orchestration |
| P8 (Flow) | P0–P7 | `SaveSystem` serializes all `ISavable` implementations | Persistence touches everything |
| P9 (Input) | P6 (Factory) | `BuyModeInputMode` auto-connects conveyor belts | Placement logic references belt system |
| P9 (Input) | P8 (Flow) | `LTPlayerController` calls `LTGameManager` for buy/sell | Player actions route through game manager |
| P10 (UI) | P0–P9 | UI subscribes to events from every phase | Display layer for all game systems |
| P11 (Audio) | P0–P9 | Audio triggers on events across all phases | Sound feedback for all actions |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/twFactory/StructureMap.md ==== -->
# StructureMap — Tower Factory (twFactory)

## Naming Conventions

| Prefix/Suffix | Meaning | Example |
|---------------|---------|---------|
| `SO_` | ScriptableObject data container | `SO_GameplayObjectData`, `SO_LevelData` |
| `Field_` | Management config field | `Field_EStats`, `Field_ResourceType` |
| `W_` | Runtime wrapper (SO → runtime) | `W_GameplayEffect`, `W_SpawnerConfig` |
| `_-System/` | Folder for 3+ tightly coupled files | `_-TowerSystem/`, `_-ConveyorSystem/` |
| `DataService` | Pure C# logic extracted from MonoBehaviour | `DS_ConveyorTransport`, `DS_SpawnerBalance` |
| `I` | Interface | `ISelectable`, `ISavable`, `ISampleableData` |
| `GE_` | Gameplay effect (preserve existing prefix) | `GE_Splash`, `GE_Dot`, `GE_ProjectileBounce` |

---

## Phase 0 — Core Framework & Data

### `_-StatsSystem/` (8 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| Stat.cs | Stat.cs | Single stat value with modifiers |
| StatConfig.cs | SO_StatConfig.cs | ScriptableObject stat definition |
| StatModifier.cs | StatModifier.cs | Runtime modifier on a stat |
| StatsComponent.cs | StatsComponent.cs | MonoBehaviour stat container, fires `onStatChanged` |
| StatConfigVariable.cs | StatConfigVariable.cs | Variable-based stat config |
| StatConfigObject.cs | StatConfigObject.cs | Object-based stat config |

**DataService candidates:**
- `DS_StatCalculator` — Extract base + modifier math from `StatsComponent` into pure C#. Handles `ModifierOperation` (Add, Multiply, Override) evaluation.

### `_-StorageSystem/` (4 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| Storage.cs | Storage.cs | Generic `Storage<T>` (Odin SerializedMonoBehaviour) |
| Storage_ResourceData.cs | Storage_ResourceData.cs | Typed resource inventory |
| StorableData.cs | StorableData.cs | Data wrapper |
| Storable.cs | Storable.cs | Storable item interface |

### `_-GameplayObjectBase/` (4 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| GameplayObject.cs | GameplayObject.cs | Base class for all placeable buildings |
| GameplayObjectData.cs | SO_GameplayObjectData.cs | ScriptableObject definition per building type |
| GameplayObjectDataGroup.cs | SO_GameplayObjectDataGroup.cs | Groups of related building data |
| PlacementComponent.cs | PlacementComponent.cs | Grid placement + events (`onPlace`, `onUnplace`) |

### Standalone files (remaining P0)
- `Cost.cs`, `ResourceData.cs` — data containers (no system needed)
- `FDamageData.cs`, `FDamageReport.cs` — combat data transfer objects
- `FunctionLibrary.cs` — static utility (GOD OBJECT — split candidate)
- `ExtensionMethods.cs`, `RectExtensions.cs` — extension methods
- `Timer.cs`, `RandomizableFloat.cs`, `RandomizableInt.cs`, `WeightedRandomSelector.cs` — utilities
- `LTAssetsReferences.cs` — asset ID registry singleton
- All enum files — standalone
- Serialization surrogates — standalone
- `SavableAttribute.cs`, `ISavable.cs`, `ISelectable.cs`, `ISampleableData.cs` — interfaces
- `SaveComponent.cs` — persistence helper

**DataService candidates:**
- `DS_FunctionLibrary` — Extract pure math/physics from `FunctionLibrary.cs` (586 lines). Physics queries stay in MonoBehaviour wrapper; math/text/validation become pure C#.

**Interface candidates:**
- `ISelectable` — already exists, used by towers, enemies, buildings, map objects
- `ISavable` — already exists, used by save system
- `ISampleableData` — already exists, used by tower data display

---

## Phase 1 — Grid & Map Generation

### `_-GridSystem/` (6 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| Grid.cs | Grid.cs | Grid container + cell lookup |
| GridCell.cs | GridCell.cs | Individual cell data |
| LightTower/Tile.cs | Tile.cs | Base tile class |
| LightTower/PathTile.cs | PathTile.cs | Path tile (enemy movement) |
| LightTower/Path.cs | Path.cs | Connected path segments |
| PathChunk.cs | PathChunk.cs | Path generation chunk |

### `_-MapGeneratorSystem/` (5 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| MapGenerator.cs | MapGenerator.cs | Top-level map generation orchestrator |
| PathGenerator.cs | PathGenerator.cs | Procedural path layout |
| GroundGenerator.cs | GroundGenerator.cs | Terrain chunk generation |
| GroundChunk.cs | GroundChunk.cs | Individual ground chunk |
| EnvironmentGenerator.cs | EnvironmentGenerator.cs | Map object spawning (GOD OBJECT) |

**DataService candidates:**
- `DS_PathLayout` — Extract path generation algorithm from `PathGenerator.cs` into pure C#. Input: seed + params → Output: tile positions.
- `DS_EnvironmentPlacement` — Extract spawn probability/position logic from `EnvironmentGenerator.cs` (786 lines). Keep Unity instantiation in MonoBehaviour shell.

### `_-FogOfWarSystem/` (2 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| FogOfWarController.cs | FogOfWarController.cs | Singleton: render texture + visibility |
| FogOfWarArea.cs | FogOfWarArea.cs | Area reveal component |

### Standalone files (remaining P1)
- `LevelData.cs` → `SO_LevelData.cs` — ScriptableObject
- `LevelController.cs`, `LTLevelController.cs` — level lifecycle
- `MapObject.cs` — base for traders, altars, obelisks
- `Source.cs`, `Resource.cs` — resource nodes on map

## Phase 4 — Gameplay Effects

### `_-GameplayEffects/` (~30 files — keep as single folder)

All GE_ files follow identical pattern: `GE_XxxData.cs` (ScriptableObject) + `GE_Xxx.cs` (runtime). No sub-grouping needed — the `GE_` prefix is the organizing convention.

| Category | Files | Role |
|----------|-------|------|
| Stat modifiers | GE_StatModifier, GE_Slow, GE_SlowResistance | Modify stats (speed, damage, etc.) |
| Damage over time | GE_Dot | Periodic damage ticks |
| Splash/AoE | GE_Splash, GE_SplashDamage | Area damage on hit |
| Chain/Bounce | GE_ProjectileBounce, GE_ProjectileSplit, GE_DirectDamageChain | Multi-target damage propagation |
| Applicators | GE_GameplayEffectApplier, GE_SplashGameplayEffectApplier | Apply other GEs on trigger |
| Stacks | GE_StacksConsumer, GE_StacksConsumerDamage | Consume stacks for burst |
| Healing | GE_Heal, GE_HealLightTower | HP restoration |
| Economy | GE_GiveResources, GE_UnlockBuilding | Resource/unlock rewards |
| Building | GE_GiveEffectToBuilding, GE_GiveEffectToEnemy, GE_DisableTower | Cross-entity effect transfer |
| Utility | GE_AddGemSlot, GE_AreaEffectSpawner, GE_Descriptor, GE_LifeBasedDamage | Misc effects |

### `_-GemSystem/` (3 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| GemsComponent.cs | GemsComponent.cs | Gem slot container on towers |
| GemData.cs | SO_GemData.cs | Gem definition ScriptableObject |
| GemUI.cs | GemUI.cs | Gem slot display |

**DataService candidates:**
- `DS_EffectStacking` — Extract stack counting, duration refresh, and max-stack capping logic into pure C#. Currently in `GameplayEffectsComponent`.

---

## Phase 8 — Game Manager & Flow

### `_-SaveSystem/` (3 files)
| Current | Becomes | Role |

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/twFactory/SystemPortabilityMap.md ==== -->
# SystemPortabilityMap — Tower Factory (twFactory)

## Classification

- **L0** — Fully portable: Pure C# logic, no Unity dependencies. Can be extracted as standalone library.
- **L1** — Mostly portable: Uses Unity types (Vector3, Quaternion, Color) but no MonoBehaviour lifecycle. Needs thin Unity adapter.
- **L1+** — Partially portable: Uses MonoBehaviour but logic can be separated. Requires refactoring to extract DataService.
- **L2** — Unity-bound: Deep MonoBehaviour/Component dependency. Must remain in Unity project.

---

## Phase 0 — Core Framework & Data

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| All enums (EStats, DamageType, etc.) | L0 | Pure enum definitions |
| Stat, StatModifier | L0 | Pure math (add/multiply/override operations) |
| StatsComponent | L1+ | MonoBehaviour container; stat math extractable as DS_StatCalculator |
| StatConfig, StatConfigVariable | L1 | ScriptableObject data; config data is pure |
| Cost, ResourceData | L0 | Pure data containers |
| FDamageData, FDamageReport | L0 | Pure data transfer objects |
| Storage<T>, Storage_ResourceData | L1+ | Odin SerializedMonoBehaviour; dictionary logic is pure |
| GameplayObjectData | L1 | ScriptableObject — data is pure, needs SO adapter |
| GameplayObject | L2 | MonoBehaviour with PlacementComponent, lifecycle |
| PlacementComponent | L2 | MonoBehaviour with grid/collider interaction |
| FunctionLibrary (pure math) | L1 | Uses Vector3/Quaternion but algorithms are pure math |
| FunctionLibrary (physics queries) | L2 | Raycasting, overlap checks — Unity-bound |
| ExtensionMethods, RectExtensions | L0 | Pure C# extensions |
| RandomizableFloat/Int | L0 | Pure random range |
| WeightedRandomSelector | L0 | Pure weighted random algorithm |
| Timer | L0 | Pure time tracking |
| SavableAttribute, ISavable | L0 | Pure attribute/interface |
| Serialization surrogates | L1 | Uses Unity Vector3/Quaternion types |
| LTAssetsReferences | L2 | MonoBehaviour singleton, asset lookups |

## Phase 1 — Grid & Map Generation

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Grid, GridCell | L1+ | Grid data structure is pure; rendering/interaction is Unity |
| Tile, PathTile, Path | L1 | Position data + adjacency graph — uses Vector3 |
| PathChunk | L1 | Path segment data |
| PathGenerator (algorithm) | L1 | Procedural generation algorithm; uses Vector3 positions |
| GroundGenerator, GroundChunk | L2 | Mesh generation, Unity terrain |
| EnvironmentGenerator | L2 | Unity instantiation, scene manipulation |
| MapGenerator | L2 | Orchestrates Unity generators |
| FogOfWarController | L2 | Render texture, camera — deeply Unity-bound |
| FogOfWarArea | L2 | Collider-based area — Unity-bound |
| LevelData | L1 | ScriptableObject config data |
| LevelController, LTLevelController | L2 | MonoBehaviour lifecycle |
| MapObject | L2 | MonoBehaviour base |
| Source, Resource | L2 | MonoBehaviour with colliders |

## Phase 2 — Character & Combat Framework

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Character | L2 | MonoBehaviour base with components |
| Controller | L2 | MonoBehaviour with Update loop |
| MovementComponent | L2 | Transform/NavMesh manipulation |
| AnimationComponent | L2 | Unity Animator control |
| TeamComponent | L0 | Pure team enum/ID |
| CombatComponent (damage math) | L1+ | Damage calculation is pure; HP tracking is MonoBehaviour |
| TowerCombatComponent | L2 | Tower-specific events + MonoBehaviour |
| EnemyCombatComponent | L2 | Enemy-specific + MonoBehaviour |
| Ability, ActiveAbility, PassiveAbility | L1+ | Abstract logic could be pure; MonoBehaviour for lifecycle |
| AutoAttack | L2 | Requires Unity physics/timing |
| AbilityManager | L1+ | Queue logic is pure; execution needs Unity |
| AbilityQueue, QueuedAbility | L0 | Pure priority queue data structure |
| FActiveAbilityInputData | L0 | Pure data DTO |
| FSMComponent | L1+ | State machine logic is pure; MonoBehaviour for ticking |
| FSMState, FSMCondition, FSMTask, FSMTransition | L0 | Pure state machine definitions |
| PerceptionAI | L2 | Physics overlap checks — Unity-bound |
| PerceptionSense variants | L2 | Collider/raycast-based |
| GameplayEffect (abstract) | L1 | Logic can be pure; needs time adapter |
| GameplayEffectData | L1 | ScriptableObject config |
| GameplayEffectsComponent | L1+ | Container logic is pure; MonoBehaviour for lifecycle |

## Phase 3 — Tower System

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Tower | L2 | MonoBehaviour with heavy component references |
| Tower XP math | L0 | Pure: XP threshold, level scaling |
| TowerController | L2 | MonoBehaviour orchestrator |
| TowerAutoAttack variants | L2 | Physics/instantiation/raycasting |
| TowerTargetProvider (selection logic) | L1 | Distance/HP comparison is pure math |
| TowerTargetProvider (execution) | L2 | Needs Unity collider references |
| Projectile | L2 | MonoBehaviour with physics |
| ProjectileMovement variants (trajectory math) | L1 | Pure: parabola, homing, forward math |
| ProjectileMovement (execution) | L2 | Transform manipulation |
| AreaEffect | L2 | Unity collider overlap |
| AreaEffectModule variants | L2 | Damage application needs Unity |

## Phase 4 — Gameplay Effects

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| GE_StatModifier | L0 | Pure stat modification math |
| GE_Slow, GE_SlowResistance | L1 | Pure speed calculation; needs time |
| GE_Dot | L1 | Pure periodic damage math; needs time |
| GE_Splash, GE_SplashDamage | L2 | Physics overlap for splash radius |
| GE_ProjectileBounce, GE_ProjectileSplit | L2 | Target finding needs Unity |
| GE_DirectDamageChain | L2 | Needs Physics.OverlapSphere |
| GE_StacksConsumer, GE_StacksConsumerDamage | L0 | Pure stack counting + burst math |
| GE_GameplayEffectApplier variants | L1+ | Application logic could be pure; target access needs Unity |
| GE_Heal, GE_HealLightTower | L0 | Pure HP restoration math |
| GE_GiveResources | L0 | Pure resource addition |
| GE_DisableTower | L2 | Needs MonoBehaviour enable/disable |
| GE_GiveEffectToBuilding/Enemy | L2 | Needs Unity object references |
| GE_AddGemSlot, GE_UnlockBuilding | L1 | Logic is pure; needs reference adapter |
| GE_AreaEffectSpawner | L2 | Unity instantiation |
| GE_LifeBasedDamage | L0 | Pure % HP calculation |
| GemsComponent | L1+ | Slot management is pure; MonoBehaviour for lifecycle |
| GemData | L1 | ScriptableObject config |

## Phase 5 — Enemy & Spawner System

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Enemy | L2 | MonoBehaviour character |
| EnemyData | L1 | ScriptableObject config |

| Spawner | L2 | MonoBehaviour instantiation |
| SpawnerConfig | L1 | ScriptableObject config |
| SpawnerPosition variants | L2 | Need Unity positions/colliders |
| WaveSpawner | L2 | Coroutine-based spawning |
| WaveSpawnerConfig | L1 | ScriptableObject config |

| CyclesManager | L1+ | Cycle counting is pure; coroutine timing needs Unity |

## Phase 6 — Factory & Economy

| System | Level | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|

---

## Summary

| Level | Count | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **L0** | ~40 systems | Pure C# — directly extractable |
| **L1** | ~30 systems | Needs thin Unity type adapter |
| **L1+** | ~20 systems | Requires DataService extraction |
| **L2** | ~100+ systems | Unity-bound — stays in project |

### Highest-Value L0 Extractions

1. **DS_ConveyorTransport** — Belt chain traversal, resource distribution, loop detection (from ConveyorBeltGroup)
2. **DS_SpawnerBalance** — LpS auto-balancing formula, weight distribution, duration calculation (from CycleSpawners)
3. **DS_Economy** — Cost evaluation, refund math, resource sufficiency (from LTGameManager)
4. **DS_StatCalculator** — Stat modifier stacking with operation order (from StatsComponent)
5. **DS_Serializer** — Reflection-based [Savable] field discovery, binary encode/decode (from SaveSystem)
6. **DS_RecipeEvaluator** — Input matching, output quantity calculation (from Processor)
7. **DS_TowerExperience** — XP threshold lookup, stat scaling per level (from Tower)
8. **DS_EffectStacking** — Stack counting, duration refresh, max-cap logic (from GameplayEffectsComponent)
9. **DS_PlacementValidator** — Grid occupancy, adjacency rules, belt connectivity (from BuyModeInputMode)
10. **DS_PathLayout** — Procedural path generation algorithm (from PathGenerator)
11. **DS_WaveComposition** — Wave enemy composition, weight selection, boss interval (from CycleSpawners)
12. **DS_SnowfallIntensity** — Per-cycle intensity calculation, tier thresholds (from SnowfallController)

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/twFactory/surfer.md ==== -->
# surfer — Tower Factory (twFactory)

## Prompt 1: Initial source analysis

**Date:** 2025-07-22

**Action:** Full project initialization and source analysis for Tower Factory (twFactory).

**Source Scope:**
- 19,643 total project files (1.568 GB) — from .stub
- 554 game code files in Assembly-CSharp (~49,000 lines)
- 48 compiled DLL assemblies
- 4 scenes: GameScene, LoadingScreen, MainMenu, Tutorial

**Key Findings:**
- Game is Tower Factory — Tower Defense + Factory Automation hybrid
- Unity 6 engine with URP, Input System, Addressables, Localization, Cinemachine
- 11 singletons (GameManager, AudioSystem, SaveSystem, SpawnersManager, FogOfWarController, SettingsController, PlayerUpgradesManager, LevelsProgressionManager, GameStatsManager, ConveyorBeltSystem, MatchInfo)
- Central service locator: `LTFunctionLibrary` — static facade for ~20 manager singletons
- 0 FindObjectOfType calls — clean architecture using singleton registry + LTFunctionLibrary
- 10 god objects (>400 lines): LTPlayerController (844), EnvironmentGenerator (786), CycleSpawners (737), SaveSystem (692), LTGameManager (666), FunctionLibrary (586), StandardInputMode (524), LTFunctionLibrary (500), SelectableUI_tower (482), AudioSystem (456)
- Event-driven architecture using C# delegates/Action<T> (no UnityEvents in game logic)
- 25+ gameplay effect types following consistent GE_ paired pattern (Data SO + Runtime)
- Conveyor belt system with 8 belt variants, group-based resource transport
- 13 tower types × 3 upgrade tiers, 27 enemy types, 10 target provider strategies
- Procedural map generation: PathGenerator → GroundGenerator → EnvironmentGenerator
- Save system uses BinaryFormatter + [Savable] attribute + ISavable interface (security concern noted)

**Documents Generated:**
1. ARCHITECTURE.md — dependency graph, singletons, coupling, events, data flow, inheritance, assets
2. PhaseMap.md — 12 phases with file lists, difficulty ratings, cross-phase modification table
3. StructureMap.md — per-phase system folders, DataService candidates, interface candidates
4. GOAL.md — architecture rules, naming conventions, lifecycle rules, testing strategy
5. Estimate.md — ~585h total (440.5h code + 145h overhead) across 12 phases
6. SystemPortabilityMap.md — L0/L1/L1+/L2 classification, 12 highest-value DataService extractions
7. surfer.md — this file

<!-- ==== FILE: LEARN/wrngfloor/ARCHITECTURE.md ==== -->
# ARCHITECTURE — wrngfloor

## Source Overview

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Wrong Floor (wrngfloor) — first-person horror game |
| **Engine** | Unity (HDRP pipeline) |
| **C# Language** | 7.3 (netstandard2.0) |
| **Total Game Scripts** | 44 `.cs` files in `Assembly-CSharp/` |
| **Skippable Scripts** | 4 (saU.cs, shitok.cs — empty; Readme.cs — SO; Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs — metadata) |
| **Third-Party Scripts** | 2 (Jemeza/SFASFX/SoundPlayer.cs, UnityTemplateProjects/SimpleCameraController.cs) |
| **Actionable Game Scripts** | 38 |
| **Total Project Files** | 11,211 (from .stub) |
| **Total Project Size** | 765.65 MB |
| **Scene** | 1 main scene (174 MB — `Assets/SceneHierarchyObject/2.glb`) |
| **DLL Assemblies** | 94 (engine + framework, in `Assemblies/`) |
| **Framework Script Folders** | 3 (Mono.Security/, System/, netstandard/ — all skipped) |

### Folder Structure (Scripts/Assembly-CSharp/)
```
Assembly-CSharp/
├── Assembly-CSharp.csproj
├── Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs          (metadata — skip)
├── Jemeza/SFASFX/SoundPlayer.cs        (third-party SFX player)
├── UnityTemplateProjects/SimpleCameraController.cs  (Unity template — skip)
├── pl.cs           cam.cs          fl.cs           dver.cs
├── lift.cs         liftkn.cs       menu.cs         hide.cs
├── wfcode.cs       wfcode1.cs      wfzap.cs        wfkill.cs
├── wfscare1.cs     wfscare2.cs     wfscare2v2.cs   wfsatr.cs
├── wfcolis.cs      wfdver1.cs      wfpodsk.cs      wfplavn.cs
├── wftryaska.cs    Wftpscam.cs     mirror.cs       nm.cs
├── vkl.cs          vklt.cs         lampa.cs        key.cs
├── klych.cs        sound.cs        tr.cs           lookat.cs
├── addforcescr.cs  audpitch.cs     trigscare.cs    tryaska.cs
├── golova.cs       kadr25.cs       f.cs            f2.cs
├── veter.cs        opt.cs          opttrig.cs      saU.cs
└── shitok.cs       Readme.cs
```

---

## Dependency Graph

### Core Layer (depended on by most scripts)
```
pl ← cam, fl, hide, menu, wfcode, wfzap, wfkill, wfscare1, wfscare2, wfscare2v2,
     mirror, audpitch, wfplavn, Wftpscam, wfcode1, opt, veter, golova, key, wfpodsk
```

### Interaction Layer
```
cam → dver (SendMessage), vkl (GetComponent), any named component (GetComponent by name)
fl  → pl (sens, MY, MX)
```

### Door/Switch Layer
```
dver     ← cam, wfcode, wfcode1, key, trigscare, mirror
vkl      ← cam, trigscare, tr
vklt     (standalone switch, no script deps)
lampa    (standalone, bone rotation)
```

### Elevator Layer
```
lift   ← liftkn
lift   → pl, cam, tryaska
liftkn → lift
```

### Horror Event Layer
```
wfkill    → pl, fl, nm (NavMeshAgent)
wfscare1  → pl, cam
wfscare2  → pl, cam, lampa
wfscare2v2→ pl, fl
wfcode    → pl, fl, dver
wfcode1   → pl, dver
wfzap     → pl, fl
trigscare → vkl, cam
addforcescr → cam (Camera)
nm        (standalone NavMesh agent)
hide      → pl, cam
golova    → pl, lookat
```

### Utility Layer
```
lookat     (standalone)
tryaska    → fl
wftryaska  (standalone shake)
mirror     → pl, dver, fl
opt        (standalone room culling)
opttrig    → opt
audpitch   → pl
veter      → pl (Transform)
kadr25     (standalone camera swap)
Wftpscam   → pl
wfplavn    → pl (one-shot destroy)
```

### Full Adjacency (who → depends on whom)
| Script | Direct Script Dependencies |
|---|---|
| `pl` | — (root) |
| `cam` | `dver`, `vkl` (runtime GetComponent) |
| `fl` | `pl` |
| `dver` | — |
| `vkl` | — |
| `vklt` | — |

---

## God Objects

No file exceeds 200 lines. Largest files doing 2+ things:

| File | Lines | Responsibilities | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lift.cs` | ~200 | Elevator movement, door animation, audio, lighting, NPC reveal, floor transitions (state machine x=0..23) | **Split candidate** — state machine + door anim + audio should separate |
| `pl.cs` | ~120 | Player movement, mouse look, running, FOV zoom, footstep audio, camera bob animation | **Split candidate** — movement vs camera vs audio |
| `menu.cs` | ~100 | Pause, settings (sensitivity/audio/language), cursor lock, camera toggle | Minor — typical menu |
| `hide.cs` | ~95 | Player hiding, camera transitions, input during hide, exit coroutine | Acceptable |
| `wfcode.cs` | ~100 | Combination lock input, cylinder rotation, door unlock, camera transition | Acceptable |

---

## Singleton Registry

**No formal singletons exist.** All scripts communicate via serialized `public GameObject` references assigned in the Inspector. There are no `static Instance` fields, no `DontDestroyOnLoad`, no service locators.

---

## Coupling Hotspots

### 1. `cam.cs` — Universal Dispatcher via SendMessage + Name-based GetComponent
```csharp
gameObject.GetComponent(gameObject.name).SendMessage("use");
```
This looks up a component **by the GameObject's name** and calls `use()` via SendMessage. Every interactable object must have its script name match its GameObject name. Extremely fragile.

### 2. Direct cross-domain references everywhere
- `lift.cs` directly reads `pl.GetComponent<pl>().cam.GetComponent<tryaska>()` — three levels deep
- `hide.cs` reads `pl.GetComponent<pl>().cam`, `.camm`, `.MX`, `.MY`, `.MZ`
- `wfscare1.cs` writes to `pl.GetComponent<pl>().MX` and `pl.GetComponent<pl>().MY`
- `trigscare.cs` calls `v.GetComponent<vkl>().use()` and `c.GetComponent<cam>().dist`

### 3. Uncached GetComponent calls in Update
Almost every script calls `GetComponent<>()` inside `Update()` or `FixedUpdate()` rather than caching in `Start()`. Examples:
- `dver.cs`: `base.gameObject.GetComponent<AudioSource>()` — called 8+ times per frame
- `cam.cs`: `GetComponent<vkl>()`, `GetComponent<dver>()` per raycast

### 4. Tag-based string comparisons
- `"Player"` tag checked in: cam, addforcescr, trigscare, wfscare1, wfcolis, wfsatr, opttrig, wfkill
- `"slomokno"` tag in cam
- `"wflook"` tag in wfscare2

### 5. `.active` property (deprecated)
Multiple scripts use `.active` instead of `.activeSelf`:
- `fl.active` in wfcode, wfzap
- `aud.active` in wfcode1
- `p.active` in wfcolis

---

## Event System

**None.** The project uses zero events — no `UnityEvent`, no `System.Action`, no `delegate`, no custom event bus.

All communication patterns:
1. **Direct reference + method call**: `l.a()` (liftkn → lift)
2. **SendMessage**: `gameObject.SendMessage("use")` (cam → any interactable)
3. **Direct field read/write**: `pl.GetComponent<pl>().MX = -90f` (wfscare1 → pl)
4. **Coroutine chains**: `StartCoroutine(end())` for delayed sequences

---

## Data Flow

```
Inspector-assigned references (public GameObject fields)
         │
         ▼
   MonoBehaviour.Update()
         │
         ├── Player input → pl (movement/look) → Rigidbody.AddForce
         │
         ├── cam raycast → SendMessage("use") → target script.use()
         │
         ├── Trigger/Collision → OnTriggerEnter → direct calls
         │
         └── State integers (x, on, a) → drive sequential logic
```

- **No ScriptableObjects** for runtime data
- **No data containers** — all state is in-field on MonoBehaviours
- **No save/load system**
- **State machines** use raw `int` fields (e.g., `lift.x`, `wfcode1.on`, `wfscare2.a`)

---

## Inheritance Chains

**Flat hierarchy.** Every script directly extends `MonoBehaviour`. No custom base classes, no interfaces, no abstract classes.

| Class | Base |
|---|---|
| All 38 game scripts | `MonoBehaviour` |
| `Readme` | `ScriptableObject` |
| `SimpleCameraController` | `MonoBehaviour` (Unity template) |

---

## External Packages

| Package/Plugin | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **Unity HDRP** | `Unity.RenderPipelines.HighDefinition.Runtime.dll`, HDRP shaders in Assets, `Volume` component usage in lift.cs |
| **TextMeshPro** | `Unity.TextMeshPro.dll` |
| **Timeline** | `Unity.Timeline.dll` |
| **VFX Graph** | `Unity.VisualEffectGraph.Runtime.dll` |
| **Jemeza SFASFX** | `Jemeza/SFASFX/SoundPlayer.cs` — third-party sound effect player |
| **NavMesh** | `UnityEngine.AIModule.dll` — used by nm.cs, wfkill.cs |

---

## Asset Inventory (from .stub)

### Audio Clips (~60 files, ~90 MB)
Ambient loops, door sounds, footsteps, scares, elevator SFX, heartbeat, breath, siren, dog bark, wind, code beeps. Mix of `.wav` and `.ogg`. Many Russian-named files (dver = door, shagi = footsteps, zvyk = sound, ysilenie = amplification).

### 3D Meshes (~130+ files, `.glb`)
Characters (body, head, eyes, jaw, clothes), furniture (cabinet, stool, shelf, bowl), doors (multiple types including SCP-432 themed), elevator parts, pipes, fire extinguisher, flashlight, tools (crowbar, wrench, axe, hammer, saw), props (cola can, cement bag, radio, bottles), architectural elements. Russian-named meshes present (Дверь = door, Шкаф = cabinet, Куб = cube).

### Textures (~200+ files, `.png`/`.tga`, ~300+ MB)
Albedo, normal, roughness maps for all mesh assets. UI textures (buttons, sliders, toggles). Horror textures (blood, gore, body parts). Game-specific (wanted poster — rozisk, warning signs, panel textures). Largest textures are character body/clothing normals (~6-10 MB each).

### Shaders (~80 files)
Primarily HDRP built-in shaders. HDRP Lit, Decal, deferred tile shading, atmospheric scattering, temporal AA, sky shaders. No custom shaders identified.

### Scenes
Single main scene: `Assets/SceneHierarchyObject/2.glb` (174.77 MB) — entire game in one scene.

### UI Assets
Font: "Perfect DOS VGA 437" (retro CRT aesthetic). Standard Unity UI sprites. Custom button/toggle skins (GUISkin-style).

### Materials & Config
Audio mixers (2), PhysicMaterial (1), NavMesh data, InputManager (legacy input), HDRP quality/graphics settings, PlayerSettings (26.9 KB — full build config).
